docs.text: Birdsite ¶ This is a semi-navigable archive of the random twitterings of one @kfitz between 2008 and 2022. One key note: timestamps claim to be clickable but are not; the account to which they linked is now deleted. As are many of the accounts with which these posts were in dialogue. Such is the way of things.

docs.text: Just joining up with this Twitter thing, fer reals. Sat Apr 05 06:19:56 +0000 2008 Is it true that any press is good press? Or do reductive, misleading reviews just suck? Sat Apr 05 13:41:14 +0000 2008 Everywhere I go, there's a parade. Sat Apr 05 17:54:51 +0000 2008 Waiting for my flight; $71 to fly standby on the earlier flight lost out to $20 to drink in the airport bar. Sat Apr 05 22:11:45 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @BryanAlexander: thanks for dragging me in. Happy flying! Sun Apr 06 04:10:30 +0000 2008 Getting up after my first really decent night's sleep in about two weeks. If I could just have three more of those, I'd be set. Sun Apr 06 15:41:36 +0000 2008 Replying to @calamityjake god yes -- as if my new BFTV isn't smart enough to figure out the signal and adjust accordingly... Sun Apr 06 23:12:50 +0000 2008 Replying to @lblanken i'm waiting anxiously for the next airing -- forgot to set the dvr before leaving for SF! Sun Apr 06 23:14:59 +0000 2008 Replying to @michaeldroy Yes, precisely! Now to catch up on the last three seasons... Sun Apr 06 23:49:35 +0000 2008 Two tasks down, forty-seven to go. Mon Apr 07 16:34:52 +0000 2008 full of tacos, and now trying to stay awake Mon Apr 07 20:36:22 +0000 2008 spending roughly half the day trying to force myself awake, and about a third trying to force myself to sleep. Tue Apr 08 15:03:24 +0000 2008 testing the twitter plugin I'm installing on the blog... Tue Apr 08 18:24:12 +0000 2008 New blog post: Pardon the Dust http://tinyurl.com/6moyay Tue Apr 08 18:25:54 +0000 2008 Tinkering when I really ought to be doing something more productive... Tue Apr 08 18:56:53 +0000 2008 Still trying to get the plugin working. In other words, procrastinating like a big dog. Tue Apr 08 20:14:01 +0000 2008 This is it. I'm stopping now. Really, I'm not kidding. Tue Apr 08 20:42:04 +0000 2008 Curses. Foiled again. Tue Apr 08 20:44:41 +0000 2008 Replying to @chutry Maybe we should set up an exchange; you read mine and I'll read yours. That way they'll all be new and different! Wed Apr 09 03:24:16 +0000 2008 Wishing I had something more interesting to report than that I got a good night's sleep. But really, I feel like a whole new person. Wed Apr 09 14:27:01 +0000 2008 New blog post: Twitterings http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/twitterings/ Wed Apr 09 14:46:52 +0000 2008 Classes went well, faculty meeting was survivable, and now the last minute of office hours is ticking to a close. Wednesday, complete. Wed Apr 09 23:01:17 +0000 2008 How does one go about thickening one's skin, exactly? Thu Apr 10 18:56:05 +0000 2008 Ikea sez a new sofa may be all I need. If only it were so. Thu Apr 10 20:36:39 +0000 2008 Testing out the code for excluding twitterings imported to my blog from my RSS feed... Fri Apr 11 18:14:42 +0000 2008 New blog post: A Twittering Update http://tinyurl.com/6ljkge Fri Apr 11 18:23:06 +0000 2008 New blog post: Future and Past http://tinyurl.com/5qe6nm Mon Apr 14 23:53:35 +0000 2008 Staying home in your pajamas on a Tuesday morning feels like playing hooky, even if you're doing it in order to read a senior thesis. Tue Apr 15 17:09:17 +0000 2008 New blog post: Response to "Electronic Media, Identity Politics, and the Rhetoric of Obsolescence" http://tinyurl.com/6cgxl9 Wed Apr 16 22:35:34 +0000 2008 Fighting off a technicolor sinus infection. Yum. Fri Apr 18 18:20:05 +0000 2008 Contemplating ripping all my CDs and then carting them off to the used record store. When's the last time I listened to a CD, anyhow? Sat Apr 19 17:12:36 +0000 2008 New blog post: Further Tinkering http://tinyurl.com/5lm3rz Sat Apr 19 17:15:23 +0000 2008 New blog post: Twitterings on 15 Apr 2008 http://tinyurl.com/65jv7s Sat Apr 19 17:56:06 +0000 2008 New blog post: Twitterings on 11 April 2008 http://tinyurl.com/6a2lqv Sat Apr 19 18:04:06 +0000 2008 New blog post: Twitterings on 10 April 2008 http://tinyurl.com/6ek6zw Sat Apr 19 18:07:11 +0000 2008 New blog post: Twitterings on 9 April 2008 http://tinyurl.com/6gr4pz Sat Apr 19 18:08:32 +0000 2008 New blog post: Twitterings on 8 April 2008 http://tinyurl.com/6llnh8 Sat Apr 19 18:14:45 +0000 2008 Obviously tinkering with twitterings on the blog; rilly didn't mean to produce such spam. Sat Apr 19 18:17:46 +0000 2008 New blog post: Well, That Was Fun http://tinyurl.com/3kpgq6 Mon Apr 21 05:49:27 +0000 2008 Two and a half weeks. Two and a half weeks. Two and a half weeks. Perhaps if I say it enough, it'll start to sink in. Mon Apr 21 16:24:24 +0000 2008 New blog post: Paranoia http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/paranoia/ Wed Apr 23 02:05:59 +0000 2008 Flying with a sinus infection is no fun. Just in case you were wondering. Wed Apr 23 22:24:19 +0000 2008 PS: iPhone corrects "incase" to read "invade." Be careful when authorizing laptop sleeve purchases. Wed Apr 23 22:26:49 +0000 2008 Dare I hope? Could the middle seat really be empty? Wed Apr 23 22:36:23 +0000 2008 I dare! The door is closed! The full breadth of armrest is mine! Wed Apr 23 22:43:21 +0000 2008 New blog post: Dreaming You're Awake http://tinyurl.com/5usntt Thu Apr 24 22:58:24 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @lblanken: So many possibilities! My favorite would be Richard Powers, Galatea 2.2 Fri Apr 25 15:26:57 +0000 2008 Very, very happy to be home. That is all. Sat Apr 26 06:51:32 +0000 2008

docs.text: Replying to @kfitz @calamityjake: Whoo-hoo, on all counts! Thu May 01 18:29:43 +0000 2008 New blog post: This is the Post http://tinyurl.com/65c5nt Thu May 01 19:54:18 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: Congrats -- fantastic news! Thu May 01 20:50:22 +0000 2008 New blog post: Tonight, A Conversation about the Future of Technology http://tinyurl.com/6a2llk Sat May 03 16:02:41 +0000 2008 The most awesome thing about Twittering now is having yet another publishing option that I can fail to update often enough. Sat May 03 16:11:04 +0000 2008 Just tripped and fell for the second time this year. Wearing the same shoes both times. Damn you, Keen, and your big rubbery toes. Sun May 04 19:14:59 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @calamityjake: back atcha -- good luck with the last stuff there, and the next stuff to follow! Mon May 05 05:25:28 +0000 2008 Using stupid wi-fi you have to pay for because my mother's internet is broken. The price one pays to be a good daughter... Fri May 09 15:36:52 +0000 2008 All senior grades have been filed. The big class is done. Now: the grad class and the little class. And then, reports, reports, reports. Fri May 09 16:02:33 +0000 2008 Sitting in a Community Coffee House, trying to finish up the grading. Thanks, local place, for the free wi-fi! Sun May 11 19:46:44 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @ghw_twits: Great video, not to mention the excellent LSU product placement! Sun May 11 19:51:23 +0000 2008 New blog post: Summer http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/summer/ Fri May 16 16:08:20 +0000 2008 Recovering from two days of graduation festivities spent in 100-degree heat. Happily, ice-cold beverages are just what I want. Sun May 18 22:47:52 +0000 2008 What am I forgetting? What am I forgetting? What am I forgetting? Thu May 22 04:48:53 +0000 2008 Sitting on the plane, waiting for takeoff for Paris. Au revoir, Etats-Unis! Thu May 22 23:17:49 +0000 2008 After a pretty amazing first night's sleep, sitting down at the computer with a cup of coffee and getting to work. The summer has begun! Sat May 24 08:00:56 +0000 2008 New blog post: On Reviens http://tinyurl.com/5mwq5u Sat May 24 10:18:25 +0000 2008 New blog post: Planned Obsolescence, Scholarly Publishing, and Peer Review http://tinyurl.com/6zrphr Sun May 25 08:23:05 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Key http://tinyurl.com/62hxp6 Mon May 26 08:57:53 +0000 2008 New blog post: This Is Scholarship http://tinyurl.com/5uor9p Tue May 27 07:55:25 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Blob http://tinyurl.com/6cnq6f Wed May 28 11:57:28 +0000 2008 has done away with the last outstanding annoyances; now nothing's in the way of the big project. Of course, that means no excuses. Hmm. Fri May 30 08:48:04 +0000 2008 has just answered her first two telephone calls in French, and only butchered the first one. C'est un peu terrifiant! Fri May 30 09:05:26 +0000 2008

docs.text: A brilliant morning of writing and the markets. Now to stay awake for the rest of the afternoon, before the Sunday evening falafel. Sun Jun 01 12:10:14 +0000 2008 New blog post: Pipe Dream http://tinyurl.com/5wpcj7 Mon Jun 02 06:25:04 +0000 2008 New blog post: Métrique http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/metrique/ Mon Jun 02 14:38:45 +0000 2008 New blog post: Time-Shifting http://tinyurl.com/682rl4 Wed Jun 04 06:52:58 +0000 2008 Slowly but surely knocking things off the to-do list and out of the inbox. I only hope I haven't tempted the busywork demons by saying so. Thu Jun 05 10:21:38 +0000 2008 New blog post: Planned Obsolescence: The Proposal http://tinyurl.com/62jklw Mon Jun 09 08:12:10 +0000 2008 Took much of the day off today, to let the synapses cool down a bit. The work's going well, but it's exhausting... Thu Jun 12 20:50:08 +0000 2008 New blog post: Mr. Jobs, Tear Down this Wall http://tinyurl.com/5djxpm Fri Jun 13 13:44:09 +0000 2008 Last night's BSG \(torrent\) was really alarming, until Google told me it wasn't the last episode *ever*, just the last until 1/2009. Sheesh. Sat Jun 14 20:47:31 +0000 2008 I am completely and totally without focus today. That is all. Tue Jun 17 10:53:30 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Golden Notebook \(s\) http://tinyurl.com/6g4p4l Thu Jun 19 07:26:48 +0000 2008 New blog post: Whoops, Vol. VI http://tinyurl.com/6ods32 Thu Jun 19 07:47:58 +0000 2008 New blog post: On Elite Education http://tinyurl.com/6labp3 Sat Jun 21 09:32:52 +0000 2008 About to head out for La Fête de la Musique. I'll be the one sitting at the cafe drinking a beer. Sat Jun 21 14:41:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: Surely you make up for it in purple-and-gold decor? Sat Jun 21 19:43:38 +0000 2008 Testing twhirl. Got nothing but errors yesterday; seems to be working now. Still not sure whether I like it, but it's good for killing time. Sun Jun 22 09:17:45 +0000 2008 Back in the thick of the project -- or at least beginning to slog along again. Mon Jun 23 08:39:03 +0000 2008 New blog post: I'm Getting in the Plane http://tinyurl.com/6ajuay Mon Jun 23 14:52:05 +0000 2008 New blog post: Stuart Moulthrop, "After the Last Generation" http://tinyurl.com/59gpv6 Tue Jun 24 12:40:56 +0000 2008 Would much appreciate it if someone could tell me what good sinuses are supposed to do me. Thx. Wed Jun 25 18:44:49 +0000 2008 Someone snuck in during the night and filled my head with rubber cement. Very clever. Thu Jun 26 09:40:35 +0000 2008 New blog post: Three Steps Forward, Two Steps Back http://tinyurl.com/62znud Thu Jun 26 11:59:39 +0000 2008 Best part of Macworld's "Empty Your Inbox" advice is keeping mail out in the first place. I've been madly unsubscribing for two days. Thu Jun 26 14:47:13 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: Amen. My response: starting a blog. It was the cutting edge in instant gratification! One can only imagine if there'd been Twitter. Thu Jun 26 20:16:26 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Future of Citations http://tinyurl.com/6enqsr Fri Jun 27 09:51:10 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Bolter Principle http://tinyurl.com/56ejwg Sun Jun 29 16:01:47 +0000 2008 Following the leaders: testing out Friendfeed, which at least so far remains under capacity! \(Also, the comment function is nice\.\) Sun Jun 29 17:18:33 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz Also, while the 140-character thing sometimes produces something haiku-like, it's nice to be able to finish my sentence. Sun Jun 29 18:41:14 +0000 2008

docs.text: New blog post: Future Writing, Take Two http://tinyurl.com/5o8pu6 Wed Jul 02 09:51:54 +0000 2008 After a couple of weeks of utterly annoying sunshine, it's finally raining in Paris again -- a tremendous relief! Wed Jul 02 12:09:35 +0000 2008 Happy 4th to everybody back there. Fri Jul 04 07:02:37 +0000 2008 New blog post: Yay. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/yay/ Sat Jul 05 09:10:27 +0000 2008 New blog post: Global Networks http://tinyurl.com/6jfw9h Tue Jul 08 09:49:30 +0000 2008 New blog post: Random Thoughts, Friday Morning http://tinyurl.com/5ekbjd Fri Jul 11 06:21:05 +0000 2008 My iPhone 2.0 is working swell, but where's my MobileMe? I hate the name, but I want the farking update, thx. Don't tell me I'm up to date. Fri Jul 11 20:47:00 +0000 2008 New blog post: Le Quatorze http://tinyurl.com/5rw9w9 Mon Jul 14 09:43:04 +0000 2008 New blog post: Service http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/service/ Fri Jul 18 09:39:25 +0000 2008 Marveling at the omnipresence of this dark knight fella in everybody's updates. Something happen over there while I was eating a baguette? Sat Jul 19 20:40:37 +0000 2008 New blog post: Versioning http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/versioning/ Sun Jul 20 07:17:52 +0000 2008 New blog post: Insert Nippular Pun of Your Choosing Here http://tinyurl.com/5wo9em Mon Jul 21 17:16:11 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @wmrandth, thanks for the shout. @lblanken, there are more at http://machines.pomona.edu, but I think not what you're looking for... Mon Jul 21 20:40:19 +0000 2008 ...that being a college-hosted blogging service? Mon Jul 21 20:40:40 +0000 2008 Which now that I think about it, machines is. Except that I'm a very restrictive administrator. Like getting past the bouncer at a hot club. Mon Jul 21 20:54:33 +0000 2008 The more I think about that analogy, though, the less it works. Because it would be a club where dancing was required. And graded. Mon Jul 21 21:11:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @lblanken: Ew. Not what I intend at all. Blame it on too much late-night free-association. \(FWIW machines is class blogs, not open service\.\) Mon Jul 21 21:20:12 +0000 2008 Really, really wishing that the folks drilling into the other side of the wall would finish their project already and knock it the hell off. Tue Jul 22 07:34:23 +0000 2008 New blog post: Click http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/click/ Tue Jul 22 11:54:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @2xlp c'est dommage que tu n'est pas ici; on peut aller à l'as du fallafel. Mais bien sûr, on a déjeuné il y a huit heures... Tue Jul 22 18:47:23 +0000 2008 Coffee, baguette, updates. Trying to get the morning started. Wed Jul 23 06:21:21 +0000 2008 Realizing that the google-alertedness of my newest post \(http://www\.plannedobsolescence\.net/iphonery/\) prolly ought to extend here. Delete! Wed Jul 23 12:39:56 +0000 2008 New blog post: Deadwood, Take Two http://tinyurl.com/5c8zgf Mon Jul 28 11:49:55 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @halavais: Because you are being held hostage by the baby-industrial complex, man. Tue Jul 29 13:20:54 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @calamityjake: Hope you're metaphysically alright. Apparently it was practically under Claremont; the college has been txting nonstop... Tue Jul 29 20:46:03 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: I'll take hurricanes over earthquakes any day. Tue Jul 29 21:24:12 +0000 2008 New blog post: Collaboration http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/collaboration/ Thu Jul 31 07:13:13 +0000 2008

docs.text: Replying to @kfitz @calamityjake: Congrats on being done! Fri Aug 01 10:09:23 +0000 2008 New blog post: Adventures in Wi-Fi http://tinyurl.com/6oh4at Sun Aug 03 11:40:43 +0000 2008 New blog post: Heh http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/heh/ Mon Aug 04 09:06:13 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @academicdave: I'd follow up @ghw_twits rec with Adrian Johns, The Nature of the Book, for an argument countering EE's techno-determinism. Mon Aug 04 14:59:39 +0000 2008 Just woke up from the first teaching-panic dream of the season. Thanks, August. Tue Aug 05 05:34:44 +0000 2008 New blog post: Hrmph http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/hrmph/ Tue Aug 05 12:30:52 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkgold: Echoing all the congrats, and also the request to hear about the project! Tue Aug 05 20:20:24 +0000 2008 Why is it that having researched and written something on the order of 70 pages of new prose this summer feels like not having done enough? Fri Aug 08 13:44:11 +0000 2008 Stop the presses! Edwards admits having affair! World obviously such otherwise perfect place that this warrants news cycles! Fri Aug 08 22:10:47 +0000 2008 Honestly, my first thought was that the haircut would be going to waste if he weren't. But maybe that's just me. Fri Aug 08 22:11:24 +0000 2008 New blog post: On the Other Hand http://tinyurl.com/62x6j5 Sat Aug 09 08:23:45 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkirschenbaum: Well, you know, there's sensitive information in there! I mean, if it fell into just anyone's hands... Sun Aug 10 22:16:01 +0000 2008 New blog post: Rentrée http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/rentree/ Tue Aug 12 08:31:25 +0000 2008 Off plane in Houston. How utterly bizarre it is to be surrounded by people speaking English. Thu Aug 14 22:23:21 +0000 2008 I liked being able to tune out most of the chatter around me. My French is good enough for eavesdropping but not for passive bombardment. Thu Aug 14 22:29:18 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: I thought it was a bit crowded in here... Thu Aug 14 22:57:09 +0000 2008 The upside is a return to unlimited iPhone data usage. Yes, I'm looking for anything. Thu Aug 14 23:00:24 +0000 2008 From the window of our landing plane, California sure looked... brown. Fri Aug 15 02:49:57 +0000 2008 Replying to @mkirschenbaum @nowviskie: he spent last year with us; we're really going to miss him. Tue Aug 19 16:08:10 +0000 2008 New blog post: Eating the Elephant http://tinyurl.com/66h3j3 Wed Aug 20 15:57:02 +0000 2008 Having to get the car smogged so I can renew my registration for which I am paying a $15 late fee because the DMV didn't correct my address. Thu Aug 21 21:04:05 +0000 2008 And no, iPhone autocorrect, I did not mean slogged. Thu Aug 21 21:06:34 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @ghw_twits: only a little. It is online \(http://www\.mlajournals\.org/loi/pmla \-\- inst\. access required\) , but the accusation still bears out. Tue Aug 26 14:23:14 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Contract http://tinyurl.com/5lmybz Tue Aug 26 14:49:28 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @ghw_twits: Thanks! That's a serious vote of confidence. Now I just have to get it written. Thu Aug 28 19:48:14 +0000 2008 Watching Gustav bearing down on the fam. Not happy about it. Sun Aug 31 16:56:08 +0000 2008

docs.text: Replying to @kfitz @chutry: gah! This year, I'm chairing a panel on the 29th at 1.45pm, which feels like primetime. Last year's panel was during lunch. Tue Sep 02 14:42:50 +0000 2008 New blog post: The Work Ahead http://tinyurl.com/5ptymc Wed Sep 03 14:58:54 +0000 2008 New blog post: The News from Baton Rouge http://tinyurl.com/625gr6 Thu Sep 04 03:26:10 +0000 2008 New blog post: Bringing It Home http://tinyurl.com/5fr7fp Thu Sep 04 19:29:48 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: mom's only sent me one blurry photo of the tree down in their driveway. Really wishing the 'rents were a bit more techy. Thu Sep 04 20:19:39 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: thanks -- it's hard being this far away and feeling the world so silent about it all. Thu Sep 04 20:21:00 +0000 2008 New blog post: But Where's the Minotaur? http://tinyurl.com/6alv8z Fri Sep 05 06:52:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: Thanks -- the announcement went out to faculty late last night; I didn't know it had gone out more widely yet! Fri Sep 05 14:10:15 +0000 2008 The announcement: as of 1 July 09, Pomona Media Studies will be converted from an interdisciplinary program into a full-fledged department. Fri Sep 05 14:14:59 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @calamityjake: I'm pretty amazed. I'd love to trace the network by which the word spread. \(Appropriate for such an announcement\!\) Fri Sep 05 14:19:19 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @2xlp: it's a pretty unmitigated good - this gets us dedicated faculty, dedicated space, and a proper budget. There's very little downside. Fri Sep 05 15:31:09 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @wmrandth: Thanks -- we're ecstatic. This enables us to better support the students we've got, as well as the ones we hope to bring in. Fri Sep 05 15:32:50 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @2xlp: but the disciplinary aspects allow us to branch out internally, to be unmired and coherent. You should check out the current major. Fri Sep 05 15:44:38 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: sending good water-calming vibes your way... Fri Sep 05 18:37:48 +0000 2008 New blog post: Department http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/department/ Fri Sep 05 18:54:57 +0000 2008 New blog post: Has the Large Hadron Collider Destroyed the World Yet? http://tinyurl.com/58htlh Thu Sep 11 01:14:52 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mattbucher: Not terribly well, but the general gist is right. Sun Sep 14 05:31:52 +0000 2008 This has been a very very hard day. That is all. Sun Sep 14 05:38:21 +0000 2008 New blog post: Placeholder http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/placeholder/ Sun Sep 14 05:52:11 +0000 2008 New blog post: In Memoriam http://tinyurl.com/6ztfmn Tue Sep 16 18:57:27 +0000 2008 Happy to have reached the end of an utterly ghoulish week. Fri Sep 19 22:38:04 +0000 2008 Slept in, wrote some, ran errands, and then ran. A good day of rejuvenation. Now I'm making gumbo. Sat Sep 20 23:56:11 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @xina: I suspect that what @mattbucher heard may have been http://www.onthemedia.org/transcripts/2008/09/19/05, for better or for worse. Sun Sep 21 20:09:09 +0000 2008 Mmm. Chile verde. Sun Sep 21 21:02:47 +0000 2008 Bizarre thing: came home yesterday and woke the MacBook Air to find that all Safari cookies had been flushed. Something you want to tell me? Tue Sep 23 14:34:51 +0000 2008 The definition of depressing: when your weekend ends on Friday. *sigh* Fri Sep 26 20:43:42 +0000 2008
docs.title: 2008 09 01 tweets

docs.text: Good grief; it's been a week since I've even been able to come up with 140 characters about anything. Sat Oct 04 00:07:46 +0000 2008 What little of today actually belongs to me will hereby be spent lying around reading a novel. Sat Oct 04 16:52:18 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: Hey, totally looking forward to seeing you there! Mon Oct 06 01:28:45 +0000 2008 Traveled today for the first time since getting home from Paris. TSA has new uniforms. Continental has new planes. Service remains the same. Thu Oct 09 01:03:11 +0000 2008 It does not surprise me that the AT&T Conference Center has very nice phones. It does surprise me that they're made by Cisco. Thu Oct 09 01:17:10 +0000 2008 Grading. Sat Oct 11 14:46:46 +0000 2008 Just wrapped up Flow with very genial pub after-party. Now packing for heinously early flight back to SoCal. \(Then maybe one more beer\.\) Sun Oct 12 02:47:55 +0000 2008 Digging my way out of an avalanche of paperwork. Surely there should be a Saint Bernard with a little keg of brandy somewhere nearby? Wed Oct 15 17:15:39 +0000 2008 On the plane \(headed out to give a talk at MITH\) . Mon Oct 20 18:14:26 +0000 2008 At the hotel. The kitchen closed 30 minutes before I got in, but the bartender took pity on me and let me take a beer back to my room. Tue Oct 21 04:03:41 +0000 2008 Going to give this going to sleep thing a shot. It's 10 pm in SoCal, so I'm hopeful that it will work... Tue Oct 21 05:05:44 +0000 2008 Talk at MITH completely rocked, if I do say so myself. Now sitting in the lounge at IAH, about to go catch the next flight home. Wed Oct 22 01:29:01 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkirschenbaum: Hey, thanks! And thanks again for having me out there. Do you know if the podcast survived, or did Audacity fail? Wed Oct 22 15:18:23 +0000 2008 Oui, on peut: http://tinyurl.com/5u376q Wed Oct 22 19:21:55 +0000 2008 Just out of curiosity: how do you negotiate the politics of unfollowing? Do you ruthlessly drop the overtweeters, or do you suffer through? Wed Oct 22 22:32:45 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @noradio: Heh. I like the image of everybody scrambling to see if I'm still there. \(Honestly, though, this is nonspecific; just curiosity\.\) Wed Oct 22 22:57:13 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @amandafrench: That's my sense as well. But I'm curious whether there's been much in the way of study \(as on unfriending\) . Wed Oct 22 23:02:20 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @calamityjake: Hey, thanks! I didn't realize the review was up. It's a great book -- my favorite of Lessig's, I think... Fri Oct 24 16:31:33 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @academicdave: Complete agreement. I use Pages, which can open .doc files fine, but converting from .docx breaks all the formatting. Sat Oct 25 16:28:09 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkirschenbaum: Woo-hoo! But trying to get mine in today, anyhow. Mon Oct 27 20:31:14 +0000 2008 Got a coupon in the mail from Jiffy Lube; familiar-looking design offering me "an oil change we can believe in." Opportunism or endorsement? Thu Oct 30 20:33:25 +0000 2008 What's the word you use when you're talking about all the people affected by an organization rather than just customers? Not clientele but - Thu Oct 30 23:48:40 +0000 2008 *Stakeholders.* Sheesh. Thu Oct 30 23:51:46 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @profbfromwv: yes, it's the word I was trying to remember, but as it turns out not the right word for the context. Fri Oct 31 14:55:57 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @profbfromwv: was trying to find a term for the folks whose work is published by university presses. Finally went with "authors." Fri Oct 31 14:56:52 +0000 2008 New blog post: Coming Back http://tinyurl.com/6ajqkc Fri Oct 31 16:54:50 +0000 2008 New blog post: 2001 http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/2001/ Fri Oct 31 21:24:17 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: weird. I think I'm going to have to retitle the post; WP seems not to like the numeric thing. One sec! Fri Oct 31 21:33:23 +0000 2008 Try this: http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/twothousandone Fri Oct 31 21:34:33 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: of which there were none, which is part of the point of the post. So maybe you were right about the postmodern/meta thing. Fri Oct 31 21:42:13 +0000 2008
docs.title: 2008 10 01 tweets

docs.text: Track record yesterday not good: in foul mood, unproductive, exhausted; quick nap turned into 2.5 hrs sleep -- right thru an appointment. Sat Nov 01 16:02:39 +0000 2008 Yesterday was a hard day. Today will be better. Sat Nov 01 16:04:02 +0000 2008 Submitted abstract for DH2009. The waiting commences. Sat Nov 01 19:08:25 +0000 2008 Thunder! The cats are Freaking Out. Sun Nov 02 00:51:35 +0000 2008 Ready to vote NOW. The waiting is making me crazy. Mon Nov 03 23:09:27 +0000 2008 Today's productivity projections: sinking fast. Tue Nov 04 16:03:21 +0000 2008 #votereport #91711 Waited about 20 mins at Claremont Manor - longest wait I've had there by a factor of 4 Tue Nov 04 16:53:43 +0000 2008 Yes, yes, yes, no way, yes, no, no, fuck no, no, no, um, hrm, and yes. Tue Nov 04 17:30:52 +0000 2008 Today's events: Voting - 20 min wait; 2 min action. Doctor - 45 min wait; 3 min action. Guess which I'd do again, in a heartbeat. Tue Nov 04 21:35:56 +0000 2008 New blog post: Tuesday http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/tuesday/ Tue Nov 04 22:50:30 +0000 2008 NBC says McCain has called Obama to concede. Wed Nov 05 04:15:50 +0000 2008 Teaching Gilroy and Gates this morning. We certainly ought to have a lot to talk about. Wed Nov 05 17:43:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @xina: total serendipity. Even better: student who brought in the "sentence of the day" took it from Obama, & led into a conv abt signifyin. Wed Nov 05 19:47:10 +0000 2008 r/t @tigerbeat: writ petition filed asking CA Supreme Court to strike down Prop 8: http://aclu.org/lgbt/relationships/37706prs20081105.html Wed Nov 05 21:00:36 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: Whoa -- that's amazing! Many congratulations! Mon Nov 10 00:12:32 +0000 2008 Diet coke. Right in this vein right here, please. Wed Nov 12 22:37:42 +0000 2008 New blog post: http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/msa Thu Nov 13 13:55:48 +0000 2008 Replying to @georgeonline, @chutry and @jbj @briancroxall: thanks! Thu Nov 13 17:38:53 +0000 2008 Freezing. The temperature is dropping outside, apparently, but the hotel seems not to have figured it out. Fri Nov 14 19:40:12 +0000 2008 About to listen to Fred's keynote, in the same room mine will be in tomorrow. It's BIG. I am working on not freaking out. Fri Nov 14 23:24:20 +0000 2008 T-minus three hours. At this point, the talk is what the talk is. All that remains is keeping myself distracted and calm as long as I can. Sat Nov 15 16:35:29 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @2xlp: I think you're onto something here. Spectacle! I suspect, however, that I'll have to settle for overcaffeination. Sat Nov 15 16:47:08 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @chutry: Thanks. I've definitely been enjoying it to this point; here's hoping the rest of it goes well... Sat Nov 15 16:47:46 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @2xlp: Eek! I usually do fairly well when overcaffeinated, except that my usual tendency to speak too quickly gets amplified... Sat Nov 15 16:53:14 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @bighandsome: you have a habit of heading to Claremont when I'm out of town. Something I should know? Sat Nov 15 18:44:48 +0000 2008 Ten minutes, people. Places! Sat Nov 15 19:24:44 +0000 2008 Talk went fabulously, if I'm to believe the reports. Went to two panels afterward. Now trying to unwind with a glass of bad champagne. Sun Nov 16 01:34:31 +0000 2008 \(PS: I choose to believe the reports\.\) Sun Nov 16 01:35:05 +0000 2008 Just got paid $300 to get in an hour later. Thanks, Continental! Sun Nov 16 21:08:59 +0000 2008 No good deed goes unpunished: my flight was overbooked, so I took the rebook offer; rebooked flight was stupidly delayed. Thanks, Delta. Mon Nov 17 17:06:30 +0000 2008 New blog post: Post-Conference Post http://tinyurl.com/5okm6u Mon Nov 17 20:45:47 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkirschenbaum: we've had carpenters in our place doing the same all week. It's been fantastically exciting watching them take shape! Wed Nov 19 16:20:11 +0000 2008 Guys here finishing the bookshelves: trim and shoe moldings, and then the stain begins. Sat Nov 22 21:38:28 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkirschenbaum: no, they're doing it for us. It's the sort of problem I'd rather throw money at than try to deal with myself. Sat Nov 22 21:41:48 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @halavais: yay! Congratulations to all, and good wishes for continued good things. Sun Nov 23 22:25:27 +0000 2008 Halfway to Hawaii \(aka LAX\) . Rest of the way to maitais tomorrow. Tue Nov 25 05:59:37 +0000 2008 New blog post: Hawaii, Day 1. http://tinyurl.com/6at374 Wed Nov 26 18:35:19 +0000 2008 Progress on new chapter: have a clear sense of what needs to be done but haven't quite figured out how to do it. Research in a nutshell. Thu Nov 27 17:02:09 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @xina: tried to post earlier, but twitter failed me. Now no doubt too late: space shuttle sonic boom. Sun Nov 30 22:11:51 +0000 2008 At HNL waiting for flight home. Seems greedy to be melancholy about leaving, but I am nonetheless. Mon Dec 01 04:33:17 +0000 2008
docs.title: 2008 11 01 tweets

docs.text: Dozed a little on plane. Landed LAX at 4.45am. In Claremont by 6 am: diner breakfast, failed attempt at an hour's sleep. Dopey beyond belief Mon Dec 01 18:40:36 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @xina: I am. And my head is just spinning. But I warned my 11 am class of my delirium, and they totally carried things. Excellent session. Mon Dec 01 20:25:47 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @cgbrooke: Happy birthday, .0137 in advance! Mon Dec 01 20:26:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @xina: I'll be keeping a good thought for you! Sat Dec 06 00:17:35 +0000 2008 I vow to be really, really lazy today. That is all. Sat Dec 06 16:26:37 +0000 2008 Why, oh why, must we have sinuses? Sun Dec 07 22:00:35 +0000 2008 Wondered earlier whether the ability to breathe was necessary to the project of teaching. Turns out intermittent ability is enough. Mon Dec 08 20:28:33 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @2xlp: in this case, Stoppard's Arcadia, which is differently amusing when read with a code id your dose. Mon Dec 08 20:32:47 +0000 2008 Replying to @2xlp yes! The impossibility of the project of historical criticism, the imminent heat death of the universe -it's right up your alley. Mon Dec 08 20:42:50 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mattbucher: "they" meaning the rest of her family, who have enough evidence to suggest that it can't possibly end well? Mon Dec 08 20:53:19 +0000 2008 Replying to @mattbucher Good grief. I'm glad to hear everyone's okay! Tue Dec 09 15:10:12 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @zephoria: Woo-hoo! Congratulations! Tue Dec 09 19:53:03 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @partsnpieces: Woo-hoo! Congratulations! Wed Dec 10 23:47:39 +0000 2008 Grading like a fiend. Determined to be done with this semester and move onto other projects posthaste. Fri Dec 12 17:25:21 +0000 2008 New blog post: New Toys http://tinyurl.com/6gc2du Fri Dec 12 21:21:13 +0000 2008 Alternately trying really hard to distract myself from the task at hand, and trying to keep myself from getting distracted. Sat Dec 13 18:58:32 +0000 2008 Flight from ONT delayed due to frost on plane. We have to wait for the sun to come up and melt it. I love SoCal. Sun Dec 14 13:55:50 +0000 2008 I'm pretty sure they're lying to us; a Delta flight just started taxiing to the runway. Sun Dec 14 14:42:27 +0000 2008 And an American flight just took off. Sun Dec 14 14:43:14 +0000 2008 Just boarded my 6 am flight at 8.05. Have gone from 2 hrs btw flights to 10 mins. And that's assuming we actually take off now. Sun Dec 14 16:08:21 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @xina: No dice. Ran from C44 to C40, arriving just as they closed the door. Happily next flight is in a little over an hour & I'll be on it. Sun Dec 14 19:44:04 +0000 2008 New blog post: Firsts in Travel http://tinyurl.com/57fdhm Sun Dec 14 19:51:38 +0000 2008 Replying to @briancroxall Excellent! Mon Dec 15 22:50:58 +0000 2008 Replying to @academicdave Keep me posted on #MLAtweetup plans; I'm conducting interviews, but they have to let me out of the hotel room sometime. Tue Dec 16 05:39:59 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @therefore: welcome back to town! Fri Dec 19 17:21:01 +0000 2008 New blog post: DEVONthink http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/devonthink/ Fri Dec 19 23:23:54 +0000 2008 Woke up at 4 am, on the first day in months I could genuinely sleep in. Why? \(No, I'm really asking\. \*Why?\*\) Sat Dec 20 13:28:23 +0000 2008 Turned in the last grade. Making a pot of coffee. Think I'll sit here and read. \(Why does that seem like such a radical concept?\) Sat Dec 20 14:04:06 +0000 2008 New blog post: Transitions http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/transitions/ Sat Dec 20 14:53:34 +0000 2008 Dear heater: I recognize that it is 38F outside, and you are unused to this. But this situation is your raison d'être. Pls warm the fuck up. Sat Dec 20 16:48:30 +0000 2008 Nervously watching giant green mass on radar image descending toward us. Good news: only going down to 45 tonight. Frost on wings unlikely. Sun Dec 21 21:05:09 +0000 2008 New blog post: More Fun with Software http://tinyurl.com/a67v55 Sun Dec 21 22:38:39 +0000 2008 Wireless internet at home has slowed to a crawl in recent days. Beginning to wonder if my network-extending Airport Express is dying. Mon Dec 22 00:33:09 +0000 2008 Replying to @academicdave Thanks! @dancohen try http://tinyurl.com/8y38ln Mon Dec 22 21:49:51 +0000 2008 Replying to @bighandsome made it to town with far less drama than your journey. But will still require beverages. Tue Dec 23 02:43:28 +0000 2008 Replying to @academicdave except that YouTube only increments view count if you watch the end of the video... Tue Dec 23 16:02:52 +0000 2008 Dammit, UPS, deliver already! That package was supposed to be here yesterday, and my sister will be annoyed with me if her gift isn't here. Wed Dec 24 18:36:00 +0000 2008 Replying to @therefore I may wind up giving my sister printed pictures of what she might someday receive... Wed Dec 24 18:42:33 +0000 2008 4pm mass, fantastic gumbo at my aunt's, the annual matching pajamas, and now drinks and christmas movies. Not bad even if it 70 degrees out. Thu Dec 25 03:04:36 +0000 2008 I've finally figured out where my holiday blahs come from: zero time alone. Introverts unite \(and then go to your separate rooms\) ! Thu Dec 25 18:22:37 +0000 2008 That having been said: happy holidays, all, and may you get all the things you want, and several you didn't even know you wanted. Thu Dec 25 18:23:15 +0000 2008 Got up at 3 am to make 630 am flight out of NOLA. Insane line to check in, but getting thru. Fri Dec 26 11:16:58 +0000 2008 On the plane to SFO & the MLA. See you there. Fri Dec 26 14:26:26 +0000 2008 http://twitpic.com/wu1m - The view from my hotel room. Front desk manager was seriously nice to us. Fri Dec 26 23:31:15 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @academicdave: I'm going to miss having it this time of year; won't provide the instant xmas escape, and will cut into the January stretch. Sat Dec 27 00:33:27 +0000 2008 Replying to @briancroxall I'll try to be there! Sat Dec 27 17:01:59 +0000 2008 Replying to @academicdave v1: funky glasses frames, leather jacket. v2: reading glasses, tweed. v3: fashion circa 1978. Real clue is reading material. Sat Dec 27 18:53:05 +0000 2008 Spent much of today getting ready for Monday's panels. Tomorrow will be non-stop interviewing. ELO cash bar in a couple of hours. Sat Dec 27 23:29:28 +0000 2008 Heading down Nob Hill toward the Hilton. See you in the bar. Sun Dec 28 00:59:23 +0000 2008 Never really got to ELO meetup - ran into old friend I hadn't seen in two years & couldn't get away. Hope there's a second @mlatweetup. Sun Dec 28 04:33:08 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @mkgold: too briefly, alas - but I hope there'll be another opportunity! Sun Dec 28 06:27:28 +0000 2008 Replying to @academicdave, @mkgold and @briancroxall knock 'em dead! #mla140 Sun Dec 28 16:34:11 +0000 2008 Hey cool! I just got followed by a novelist I teach. Hi, @nicolaz! Sun Dec 28 22:39:52 +0000 2008 Brain is completely overheated from interviewing all day, and needs replacement coolant, preferably martinis. Mon Dec 29 00:07:00 +0000 2008 Still haven't gotten to set foot in a panel -- but will be in three this afternoon. Mon Dec 29 16:49:26 +0000 2008 This morning's first interviewee has shown up -- before the housekeeper is done, before the coffee arrives, before my colleagues... Mon Dec 29 16:50:36 +0000 2008 A busy day of interviews, panels, conversations. Now in my last panel, Joni Mitchell. Tue Dec 30 03:28:16 +0000 2008 Well, that was awful. Tue Dec 30 06:19:49 +0000 2008 Replying to @mlatweetup sorry to have missed the second iteration, too! My 5pm ran right into my 7pm panel. Grrrr. Tue Dec 30 06:21:17 +0000 2008 Replying to @partsnpieces oh my god - I hope you're okay! Tue Dec 30 17:58:03 +0000 2008 Have decided the best way to wash the MLA from your brain is driving down the Coast Hwy with the top down. Better than I've felt in months. Wed Dec 31 00:58:13 +0000 2008 Replying to @kfitz @noradio: how funny! I hope he's doing well. Wed Dec 31 15:34:48 +0000 2008
docs.title: 2008 12 01 tweets

docs.text: Spent the last two days in Monterey, recovering from the MLA, the holiday season, the year. A bit of writing before we head home. Thu Jan 01 18:04:04 +0000 2009 Home sweet home. Just wish I hadn't brought this stupid cold/sinus infection/whatever with me. Fri Jan 02 06:16:19 +0000 2009 New blog post: Retreat, Advance http://tinyurl.com/8hoztg Fri Jan 02 18:02:29 +0000 2009 New blog post: Probably Unrelated Observations http://tinyurl.com/a7q76o Sun Jan 04 17:56:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave and @chutry In Louisiana, it at least used to be true that you could buy beer after noon on Sunday, but not wine or liquor. Sun Jan 04 18:33:39 +0000 2009 New blog post: Edit Scrivenings http://tinyurl.com/8htc95 Mon Jan 05 16:54:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @kfitz @jayrosen_nyu: Twitter uncovers bits of collective unconscious. Three unrelated tweets in a row mention Virginia, or tacos? Something's up. Mon Jan 05 17:13:42 +0000 2009 Just lost a morning's worth of work. And it was no doubt the most brilliant work I've ever done, or will ever do. Tue Jan 06 19:47:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Thanks. I'm searching around to see if there are temp files stashed somewhere, but no dice. I'm too nervous to go forward yet. Tue Jan 06 21:06:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @fearv yeah, I'm working in Scrivener. I have a backup from before I started this morning and am now reconstructing today's work. Tue Jan 06 22:19:40 +0000 2009 ...but it will never be as good. [/whine] Tue Jan 06 22:20:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave That's what I thought. Appears that one rtfd file in package is corrupted - got overwritten in crash by plist data? Tue Jan 06 23:07:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave cont: Posted full story to forum - http://tinyurl.com/8uujts Awaiting response... Tue Jan 06 23:09:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall I'll say this for Scriv.: when Word has eaten a document, Bill Gates has never responded to my query or helped me sort it out. Wed Jan 07 16:20:08 +0000 2009 \(Also, writing is going extremely well in this format\. I'm just becoming a bit more obsessive about creating frequent backups\.\) Wed Jan 07 16:20:53 +0000 2009 Earthquake? Fri Jan 09 03:51:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @zota my cats, by contrast, were in mid-dinner and were sorely perturbed to be so interrupted. Fri Jan 09 03:53:59 +0000 2009 5.0 1 mi s. of San Bernardino, according to USGS. Just as Metrolink passed right behind, so I honestly wasn't sure at first. Fri Jan 09 04:03:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome I am right there with you. Give me a hurricane any day. Fri Jan 09 04:04:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @fearv A plan. Now there's an idea. How was it out there? Was not my favorite moment of the night, and I'm a fair bit further away than you. Fri Jan 09 04:07:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @fearv Same here. Hit just as train passed, but with one really hard jolt and then a couple following behind. Hoping that's the end of it. Fri Jan 09 04:10:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum ooh. Fire pretty. :) Fri Jan 09 22:08:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @halavais if you haven't come up with a copy, let me know; I can probably find one for you. Fri Jan 09 22:11:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @halavais I think not, though it's been a while. I think you dump the CP folder in themes, plugins in plugins, activate & configure... Fri Jan 09 22:26:17 +0000 2009 Mellon workshop on Media Studies in the Liberal Arts is about to begin. All participants seem to have made it to town. So far, so good! Fri Jan 09 23:38:46 +0000 2009 Brilliant discussion of discipinarity v. interdisciplinarity in SLAC media studies curricula this morning in Mellon workshop. Sat Jan 10 19:03:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @xina That's my hope, though probably not until tomorrow afternoon sometime. Sat Jan 10 23:47:34 +0000 2009 First trip to the gym since... eek! November?!? No wonder that felt so awful. I mean, good. No wonder that felt so good. Tue Jan 13 21:49:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I've been unable to load your blog all day in Firefox. Keep getting a connection interrupted/reset error message. Wed Jan 14 20:43:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry I was there briefly last week. It was *dreamy*. Wed Jan 14 21:47:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Mine resolved more or less right after my last tweet. The power of twitter! Thu Jan 15 02:29:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @mamamusings It's VERY annoying. I keep hoping Apple will press service providers into reciprocal agreements. But not holding my breath. :( Fri Jan 16 16:13:54 +0000 2009 Big things are in the works. The problem is that they move slowly and crowd out the little things. Hard to report on big things in progress. Fri Jan 16 17:14:18 +0000 2009 grrr. asst registrar scheduled my 17 student class in a computer lab with 9 machines. glad I checked. Fri Jan 16 23:39:25 +0000 2009 Have almost finished building the course websites -- except for the fact that I haven't finished building one class. Mon Jan 19 04:07:42 +0000 2009 New blog post: Campus Collaborations http://tinyurl.com/ayh4cs Mon Jan 19 15:51:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @shanakimball Fabulous! Thanks for pointing me in the right direction... Mon Jan 19 19:00:12 +0000 2009 Oh. My. God. Tue Jan 20 16:39:05 +0000 2009 I'm completely drained. What a morning. Now a valiant attempt to go back to work... Tue Jan 20 18:08:29 +0000 2009 New blog post: MediaCommons http://tinyurl.com/89d5to Wed Jan 21 14:48:47 +0000 2009 New blog post: Campus-Based Publishing http://tinyurl.com/ap4kq3 Thu Jan 22 18:12:25 +0000 2009 New blog post: Media in Transition http://tinyurl.com/cfgyqz Thu Jan 22 18:25:59 +0000 2009 New blog post: While I'm At It http://tinyurl.com/arrayd Thu Jan 22 18:27:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore Thanks! I'm pretty fired up about it. Thu Jan 22 18:34:14 +0000 2009 Yes, I really did post three times in fifteen minutes. Very happy to have lots of small ideas again, but sorry they're coming in flurries. Thu Jan 22 18:34:45 +0000 2009 Feeling excited about the semester, despite the usual yucky first day of classes in which I blather since I have no content to discuss yet. Thu Jan 22 20:14:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @johnmjones Windex = ant kryptonite. No kidding. Doesn't prevent them, but discourages them mightily. And less stinky. Thu Jan 22 22:29:04 +0000 2009 New blog post: CommentPress http://tinyurl.com/d99k23 Mon Jan 26 17:20:30 +0000 2009 New blog post: CFP: MLA 2009 http://tinyurl.com/bweqv6 Tue Jan 27 15:26:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattbucher Good god. Tue Jan 27 18:31:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattbucher I have to admit, I've never been a fan, but U&I almost turned me around. Tue Jan 27 18:39:25 +0000 2009 I'm teaching "Certainly the End of Something or Other, One Would Sort of Have to Think" tomorrow, and now I just don't know what to say. Tue Jan 27 18:40:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Ugh! Planned Obsolescence or MediaCommons? Thanks for the heads-up... Tue Jan 27 19:40:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @xina I know, right? I can't decide how good a thing that is, though. Tue Jan 27 19:41:05 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Hmmm. It's up for me. I'll have to see if I can figure out what's going on! Tue Jan 27 19:48:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Okay, I'm clueless; no reported outages, & everything's working on my end. What error are you getting? I'll report to host... Tue Jan 27 19:57:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Whew. Gremlins, I guess. Tue Jan 27 19:58:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench and @dancohen One up side is that name won't make it appear that the program is limited to journals... Wed Jan 28 19:09:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @dancohen Which I really hope leads to the beginning of a robust digital monograph distribution program... Wed Jan 28 19:13:43 +0000 2009 Survived class #1 today. Wish it had been a bit better than that. Heading into class #2, and hoping for good conversation... Wed Jan 28 22:33:58 +0000 2009 New blog post: Commenting Policy http://tinyurl.com/c7wadq Thu Jan 29 18:36:58 +0000 2009 At workshop on the future of scholarship at USC, about to give big project talk. Fri Jan 30 22:12:29 +0000 2009 In the new Lucas building. Which is vibrating ominously. And looks like a very shiny version of a creepy old Hollywood hotel. Fri Jan 30 22:13:28 +0000 2009 Survived the talk. Now trying to survive the post-adrenaline headache. Sat Jan 31 00:37:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Whoa. You officially rock. Sat Jan 31 16:52:21 +0000 2009 New blog post: More Complaints http://tinyurl.com/ck8npc Sat Jan 31 17:07:35 +0000 2009 Laziest day ever. Slept in, goofed around on computer, read. Two hour nap. Tonight: more of the same. Except with a glass of wine. Sun Feb 01 00:09:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp It's a Paso Robles Cab/Syrah blend - inexpensive TJ's grab. Sun Feb 01 04:03:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @xina I'm sorry I didn't grab that drink with you last night - headache sent me home, but 1.5 hrs on the 10 might have been better spent! Sun Feb 01 04:05:03 +0000 2009
docs.title: 2009 01 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @joeymornin That's bizarre. "Here's a POP setting: but don't use it, or else!" Mon Feb 02 20:50:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry Oh my am I having this problem this semester, teaching an entire course on a late colleague, forcing myself not to first-name him... Tue Feb 03 15:29:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall http://machines.pomona.edu -- you can follow the links from there! Tue Feb 03 15:56:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry I agree, but worry in my particular case about first naming resulting in massive intentional fallacizing/biographical criticism. Wed Feb 04 22:37:26 +0000 2009 New blog post: Digital Peer Review http://tinyurl.com/cdgamb Fri Feb 06 19:45:08 +0000 2009 Back into the project today: re-read the chapter thus far, figured out what's ahead. Now reading SPARC report on publishing collaborations. Sun Feb 08 18:40:09 +0000 2009 @amandafrench I'll throw Pages into the mix, if you're a mac user; nice feature set \(incl a writeroom\-like mode\) but much cleaner than word Sun Feb 08 19:09:51 +0000 2009 @academicdave @j_l_R @chutry I wanna go! Sun Feb 08 22:31:55 +0000 2009 In fact, am hoping to be at DH09 just beforehand... Sun Feb 08 22:33:11 +0000 2009 New blog post: MediaCommons Blogs http://tinyurl.com/d9z426 Sun Feb 08 23:11:15 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Huh. Whaddayano. Mon Feb 09 00:30:44 +0000 2009 Just registered for @thatcamp. Very excited about the possibilities! Mon Feb 09 15:11:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Yes! Mon Feb 09 16:35:42 +0000 2009 New blog post: Grrrr http://tinyurl.com/d2s6q9 Tue Feb 10 18:56:24 +0000 2009 The best part of that blog post is that the last part of the url resolves to "grrrr-2"; apparently I've grrrr'd before. Tue Feb 10 18:58:33 +0000 2009 Hi, @amandafrench 's class! Thu Feb 12 01:03:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @shanakimball and @moncia I'll look forward to hearing about it, as will the rest of @mediacommons! Thu Feb 12 17:10:54 +0000 2009 Don't Click: http://tinyurl.com/amgzs6 Thu Feb 12 18:32:15 +0000 2009 Well, that's really annoying. Thu Feb 12 18:36:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Ooh. I had a prof in grad school whose lectures were read off yellowed, curling typescript, and whose seminars were lectures. Thu Feb 12 21:53:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave And when she asked a question, it had a concrete factual answer. Srsly: once asked a class that had just read The Bostonians-- Thu Feb 12 21:54:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave "what dichotomies do you see in the text?" The answer: north vs. south. All the interesting responses were ignored. Thu Feb 12 21:55:32 +0000 2009 New blog post: Media Studies and Literary Studies http://tinyurl.com/cqyt53 Fri Feb 13 18:10:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @jwolman I feel your pain. kfitzpatrick needs to figure out her own address, too. Sat Feb 14 19:12:49 +0000 2009 Replying to @cgbrooke More or less exactly what I said after finishing the index on mine. I'll at least farm out a first pass at the next one... Sun Feb 15 00:50:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry There's something to be said for doing it once. Mine took WAY longer than I expected, though, and was pretty painful in the process. Sun Feb 15 00:51:51 +0000 2009 New blog post: My New TOS http://tinyurl.com/ce6c6q Mon Feb 16 17:39:20 +0000 2009 Wow. Skip reading your twitter stream for a day and you miss a lot! Thu Feb 19 18:10:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @alechosterman Yeesh. I can't even imagine. In no small part because of my completist fetish I'd never be able to catch up. Thu Feb 19 18:31:50 +0000 2009 Eating spicy Thai potato chips. The package reads "ginger with attitude." They are not just saying. Thu Feb 19 22:48:18 +0000 2009 I have had a twitch in my left lower eyelid for the last three days and I am seriously READY FOR IT TO STOP. NOW. Thu Feb 19 23:21:27 +0000 2009 Not least because I fear that everyone around me thinks I'm winking at them. Or giving them the stink-eye. Thu Feb 19 23:22:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp You mean other than every single day of my entire life? Fri Feb 20 00:05:46 +0000 2009 Attn everyone: please stop talking about the new HBO series. I would like the song from 'Smokey & the Bandit' to leave my head, immediately. Fri Feb 20 16:14:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @fearv Crap. We got a long way to go, and a short time to get there! See what you've done? Fri Feb 20 16:46:10 +0000 2009 New blog post: Teaching Carnival, TK http://tinyurl.com/con7qw Sun Feb 22 22:04:49 +0000 2009 Replying to @jbj Hey, cool! Tue Feb 24 00:20:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Congratulations!!! Can you say which one? Or is it still under wraps? Tue Feb 24 00:51:42 +0000 2009 New blog post: Teaching Carnival 3.2 http://tinyurl.com/dbtdm4 Tue Feb 24 15:33:04 +0000 2009 New blog post: Digital Humanities Roundup http://tinyurl.com/a9vh2n Wed Feb 25 18:35:32 +0000 2009 It is hard to prepare your digital media studies course when the freaking internet is down. I'm just saying. Wed Feb 25 19:51:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattbucher Wow, that'd be a weird thanksgiving dinner. Wed Feb 25 22:39:05 +0000 2009
docs.title: 2009 02 01 tweets

docs.text: Really hard few days, trying to get caught up after "retreat" weekend. Now deep in a painful writing task I wish I didn't have to do. Wed Mar 04 05:10:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore This was our biennial faculty-trustee retreat. The scenery was gorgeous, that much I'll say. Wed Mar 04 05:29:21 +0000 2009 Transferring data from the MBAir to my spankin new iMac. Trying not to be impatient, and failing. Fri Mar 06 04:19:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Completely! Fri Mar 06 04:30:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @culturecat If that has happened to you, living where you do, the world is too too cruel. Sun Mar 08 01:21:26 +0000 2009 Grading. Horribly behind. What more is there to say? Sun Mar 08 17:14:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @GeorgeOnline The "check for updates" should be able to keep you on top of new releases. Mine may be malfunctioning, though. Tue Mar 10 15:41:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @veek Hmmm. Annotations are about background, comments are interpretative? I'm just guessing here... Tue Mar 10 21:13:52 +0000 2009 Replying to @veek I get it. I was thinking about two different kinds of footnotes in an edition of Shakespeare, for instance: the gloss and the analysis Tue Mar 10 21:19:19 +0000 2009 Spring break began yesterday. And not a moment too soon. Trying to remember what my book project is about again. Fri Mar 13 16:26:01 +0000 2009 Why is it that my students seem to forget everything they know about paper writing when they start writing a thesis? Fri Mar 13 18:10:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore Apparently the humanities are not expected to recover. Fri Mar 13 23:17:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore keep me posted on that! I'll look forward to hearing how it goes. Sat Mar 14 02:34:10 +0000 2009 Replying to @spikenlilli I *totally* want to take that class. Mon Mar 16 20:31:35 +0000 2009 Completely rocking day in which I got a bunch of annoying small stuff done - but not enough on the Big Thing. Ah well - back at it tomorrow. Mon Mar 16 23:29:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @davidmsilver Woo-hoo! \(Not that there was ever any doubt\.\) Congrats! Wed Mar 18 16:41:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I want to say Gopher! UseNet! Lots of stuff! But all I'm coming up with right now is SecondLife. Thu Mar 19 16:50:37 +0000 2009 Adjusting to my new glasses. The world looks *really* different when you can perceive depth! Thu Mar 19 21:57:30 +0000 2009 New blog post: Getting Serious About the Online Part of Research Online http://tinyurl.com/dx5fxp Fri Mar 20 18:33:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @shanakimball Thanks for the shout-out! I'm honored to be in that company. Fri Mar 20 19:50:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @cforster and @GeorgeOnline I actually know someone who sent an iPhone through the washer & dryer, and it came out okay. Sat Mar 21 16:35:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @cforster and @GeorgeOnline Have also heard tales of placing soaked iPod/iPhone in canister of rice to dry out. Sat Mar 21 16:36:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @partsnpieces My coverage began with my first day of employment \(assuming I'd done the paperwork in advance, which I had\) . Sun Mar 22 15:54:32 +0000 2009 New blog post: University Press + University Library = The Future of University Publishing? http://tinyurl.com/cywmel Mon Mar 23 14:50:13 +0000 2009 Have the feeling that folks focusing on Michigan's shift to mostly-digital publishing are missing the bigger story... Mon Mar 23 16:25:06 +0000 2009 Shift in format is less important than press being freed from bottom-line orientation. Press now free to experiment with form: that's key. Mon Mar 23 16:26:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore Thus far, most experimentation I've liked has been outside uni press structure. Wark's Gamer Theory is key model. Mon Mar 23 17:01:54 +0000 2009 Or alongside: Wardrip-Fruin, Expressive Processing. I hope more such experiments \(esp\. ones that seem strange and scary\!\) are forthcoming. Mon Mar 23 17:03:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore Absolutely. That's the kind of thing I'm hoping for - that we can finally stretch the boundaries of what a scholarly "text" is. Mon Mar 23 17:34:40 +0000 2009 Replying to @halavais Having to stay within a budget is a pretty different thing from having to recover costs; could produce braver choices. Mon Mar 23 18:27:43 +0000 2009 Day 5 of full-time glasses wear. The first day that the weight of them has annoyed me. Also the first day of teaching in them. Related? Tue Mar 24 00:14:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline A semi-weekly edited collection of the best web-based writings on pedagogy? Tue Mar 24 14:32:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @halavais Hey, excellent! I'll be looking forward to it. Fri Mar 27 14:24:28 +0000 2009 I am writing a check. To put in an envelope. With a form. Registering for a conference. Why does it smell like 1995 in here? Fri Mar 27 15:48:21 +0000 2009 Not for nothing, but the conference in question has a website - on which all information is posted in .doc files. A website you download. Fri Mar 27 15:49:08 +0000 2009 The punchline is that my panel is on digital scholarship. Ba-dum-bum. Fri Mar 27 15:49:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome You're more than welcome! And the nerves were unnecessary - you were fabulous. Sat Mar 28 19:45:11 +0000 2009 Trying to finish sched for rest of term in Intro Digital MS. Combining 2wks on remix/fan cult into 1. But now I need a new topic! Ideas? Mon Mar 30 17:22:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @stephanietomlin Seems the obvious choice, no? I think I'm going to make the week "new forms," and look at Twitter + other developing tools Mon Mar 30 18:30:17 +0000 2009
docs.title: 2009 03 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @kfitz @shanakimball: No. Wed Apr 01 20:00:01 +0000 2009 Why does NeoOffice think the font is Garamond Premier Pro, while OpenOffice thinks it's Garamond Premr Pro? Neither can see the other. Grrr. Thu Apr 02 23:08:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @dylanborgman I hadn't thought of that. Brilliant suggestion! Thu Apr 02 23:38:39 +0000 2009 One example of a text message you do not want to receive from your partner who's traveling might be "This plane hit a bird." I'm just saying Fri Apr 03 02:34:24 +0000 2009 @mkirschenbaum "we didn't vaccinate kids against polio in the past -- why bother now?" Fri Apr 03 22:17:11 +0000 2009 @mkirschenbaum Or how about this one: "we didn't need laws against texting while driving in the past; why do we need them now?" Fri Apr 03 22:18:35 +0000 2009 Would really love to hear from Evernote users: How do you use it? What systems does it replace, and what do you use it coupled with? Fri Apr 03 23:22:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @gselmer Would love to hear more! I can sort of imagine its usefulness, but am not translating that into clear workflow... Sat Apr 04 11:54:17 +0000 2009 At Haverford for Mellon 23 workshop on Digital Archivalism; starting discussion about how technology is changing how the humanities work Sat Apr 04 13:52:35 +0000 2009 What new modes of scholarship might the digital inspire? curatorial work, data mining, etc #digitalarchivalism Sat Apr 04 14:33:40 +0000 2009 Challenges in digital classroom work lie less in instruction in tools than in creation of models. #digitalarchivalism Sat Apr 04 14:35:48 +0000 2009 Tech raises questions about nature of the humanities - anxieties that aren't raised for colleagues in other areas #digitalarchivalism Sat Apr 04 14:46:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare Absolutely! Statement should have been in quotation marks; was part of another participant's comment. Sat Apr 04 16:38:39 +0000 2009 #digitalarchivalism has been amazing. But: Longest. Day. Ever. \(Which is mostly the jetlag speaking\.\) Sun Apr 05 01:35:58 +0000 2009 About to hear from @JenServenti at #digitalarchivalism Sun Apr 05 14:04:16 +0000 2009 Fantastic overview of ODH/NEH program from @JenServenti - exciting possibilities! #digitalarchivalism Sun Apr 05 14:39:50 +0000 2009 Just found out #IR10 proposal was accepted. Very kind peer reviews, particularly for an abstract challenging the future of peer review! Mon Apr 06 17:29:07 +0000 2009 I hate waking up with a headache. Never seems to bode well for the day that follows. Tue Apr 07 14:13:15 +0000 2009 Okay, Twitterific. I keep getting RTs of folks I'm following, but haven't seen the originals. What gives? Tue Apr 07 17:43:52 +0000 2009 Diss proposal defense this morning; senior project crits this afternoon. Fun to see what my students are working on independently! Tue Apr 07 18:13:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @shinyalice Do bring it over - I want to see it! Tue Apr 07 21:45:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @fearv Oh, man - I wish I'd known Carl was out here! I'd have invited him by... Wed Apr 08 14:55:25 +0000 2009 Getting ready for a faculty panel on Harry Potter. I'm talking about it in rel. to anxieties about decline of reading. Other suggestions? Thu Apr 09 21:41:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Wow. Yes, absolutely - I'll add that in. Thanks! Thu Apr 09 21:45:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas Hey, excellent - I'd forgotten all about that. \(I tend to put Bloom out of my mind as quickly as I can\.\) Thu Apr 09 22:11:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore That's a BIG part of what I'm up to; how does HP inspire reading to turn into writing... Thu Apr 09 22:13:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @rachaelsullivan Thanks! I hadn't seen that one - I'd mostly focused on Reading at Risk & To Read or Not to Read http://bit.ly/CUsKu Thu Apr 09 22:23:20 +0000 2009 Note the new report, though - Reading on the Rise - which sure hasn't gotten the same kind of press coverage as the other two... Thu Apr 09 22:23:59 +0000 2009 Thanks again @academicdave @mattthomas @therefore and @rachaelsullivan for the HP ideas. Panel went fabulously: 200+ students showed up... Fri Apr 10 05:31:32 +0000 2009 ...and said "aw!" when the moderator ended the event after 1.5 hours. Fri Apr 10 05:32:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore Sadly, no; it's now lost to the ages. I'm actually sorry about that - it would be worth sharing! Fri Apr 10 05:37:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie Congratulations - how exciting! Fri Apr 10 14:23:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Thanks for the link to the UMaine criteria; that'll be *really* helpful... Fri Apr 10 14:26:20 +0000 2009 Preparing for Sophie demo/lab this afternoon. Fri Apr 10 16:23:40 +0000 2009 Replying to @xina So glad to hear! Send good vibes your way... Fri Apr 10 16:29:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @stephanietomlin I was glad to see you there! It was terrific fun - and what a turnout! Fri Apr 10 17:39:18 +0000 2009 @davidmsilver I echo everyone else: wow. It is indeed the end of an era. But I look forward to seeing what you do next! Sat Apr 11 14:10:40 +0000 2009 My address gets an 88 on walkscore.com - which is pretty awesome for suburban SoCal... Sun Apr 12 02:58:18 +0000 2009 The last batch of midterm grading. Four weeks until finals. Sun Apr 12 15:13:47 +0000 2009 Whoa. Just put the contact lenses in for the first time since getting the glasses; I'm super-dizzy. Bizarre. The world is once again flat. Sun Apr 12 21:22:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare My inclination would be to say PHP/MySQL would be most useful, as that combo underwrites much common web software today... Mon Apr 13 19:36:21 +0000 2009 @d_kompare But Java's an excellent starting point for teaching basic coding principles \(our intro CS courses focus on Java\) Mon Apr 13 19:37:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare I'd probably say that Java needs more than a few weeks, but Javascript wouldn't, and many of the principles are the same. Mon Apr 13 20:10:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore and @briancroxall I completely agree. I imagine an accidental hard drive reformatting might do it. Mon Apr 13 21:42:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Probably wise. It's a battle I've been fighting here, trying to persuade IT that my computer = my lab, not a tool they lend me Tue Apr 14 00:18:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @mikhailg Much of that expectation is created by the genre itself, which gets huge mileage out of setting up the unfortunate to fail. Wed Apr 15 19:36:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @rachaelsullivan Hell yeah. Something about that muscular toucan has me very freaked out. Thu Apr 16 00:23:03 +0000 2009 RT @dancohen at #digdil09 on peer review: Lynch: new forms just emerging; but clear that quality of existing peer-review is declining a lot Thu Apr 16 15:58:53 +0000 2009 I'm an hour-fifteen behind, but catching up! Thu Apr 16 15:59:12 +0000 2009 RT @dancohen Lynch: Scholars should care about doing research that's important, not research that's peer reviewed. #digdil09 Hear, hear. Thu Apr 16 15:59:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Assuming that's both important and peer reviewed, sure, but peer review may function better in non-traditional forms. Thu Apr 16 21:43:42 +0000 2009 New blog post: Not Dead Yet http://tinyurl.com/ce8loe Sun Apr 19 16:28:17 +0000 2009 New blog post: The Wages of Mouthing Off http://tinyurl.com/cdxn67 Mon Apr 20 23:09:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I rather thought so my own self. Thanks! Mon Apr 20 23:51:53 +0000 2009 Got my @thatcamp acceptance last night, but didn't want to spoil it for sleeping eastcoasters. See you all there! Tue Apr 21 13:24:42 +0000 2009 I find it alarming that "prepare #MiT6 presentation" is currently no. 3 on my list of things to do. Saturday looms! Tue Apr 21 15:24:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @shanakimball My fear is that if there were two of me, there'd just be more demands. Work expands to fill the space it's allowed... Tue Apr 21 15:36:50 +0000 2009 To clarify: #MiT6 paper \(\=chapter\) is complete. Need, however, to carve 15 mins out of 40 pages. Would almost be easier to start fresh. Tue Apr 21 15:38:36 +0000 2009 Is anyone successfully using multiple accounts with the free version of #tweetie? Authentication keeps failing on my secondary account. Tue Apr 21 16:37:58 +0000 2009 Found very odd fix for multiple account problem: http://bit.ly/1kbar - comment 1. Tue Apr 21 16:45:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @cforster My sense is that students are usually required to turn papers in to turnitin - hence the name and the supposition of guilt Tue Apr 21 20:29:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy You can! Double-click your icon, then click the star. Voilà! Tue Apr 21 21:46:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Yes! :) Tue Apr 21 23:08:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @johnmjones That's a riot. D'you think maybe "sharperfx.com" did it intentionally? The bit with the globe bouncing into hands is brilliant. Tue Apr 21 23:11:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Only if "history" = forwards as well as backwards. \(No doubt I'm too late with this response, but such is temporality\.\) Wed Apr 22 04:50:47 +0000 2009 Wondering why some of the new posts in my #tweetie stream are dimmed, as though I've already selected them when I clearly haven't... Wed Apr 22 21:28:49 +0000 2009 IAH, on my way to #MiT6. CO outdid itself in the crap food category: "turkey bbq bake." Pretzel dough injected with bbq flavor goo. Thu Apr 23 23:01:00 +0000 2009 Presence of turkey in bbq-flavor goo was pretty doubtful. Thu Apr 23 23:01:26 +0000 2009 made it to the hotel five minutes before the bar closed. have beer. sleep soon. uneventful but long travel day. Fri Apr 24 04:17:17 +0000 2009 Ugh. East coast time is painful. Coffee & a little work before wandering over to #MiT6. Big thx to organizers for not starting at 9am. Fri Apr 24 12:42:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare Good morning! I'll look forward to saying hi in person once I'm able to face the world. Good luck with the defense. Fri Apr 24 13:01:48 +0000 2009 Unexpected effect of the iPhone: it has radically changed my perspective on paying for overpriced hotel internet access. Fri Apr 24 13:24:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @gselmer It's made me more resistant and resentful. Used to shrug and say, oh well, cost of doing business. Now I use the iPhone & say ha! Fri Apr 24 13:31:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @jwolman Given some hotel business models that change could go either way; lower prices to draw more in or raise them to milk those who pay. Fri Apr 24 13:40:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @alicejrobison I'm in that slot, too! I'm hoping that I'll have adjusted a bit, but I'm dubious... Fri Apr 24 13:59:50 +0000 2009 First plenary discussion of #MiT6: John Foley, Lisa Gitelman, Rick Prelinger, Ann Wolpert; Peter Walsh moderating. Fri Apr 24 16:26:00 +0000 2009 New blog post: MiT6 http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/mit6/ Fri Apr 24 16:31:38 +0000 2009 First question to John Foley: survival of oral culture; parallels between oral traditions and emerging internet cultures? Fri Apr 24 16:35:56 +0000 2009 First question to John Foley: survival of oral culture; parallels between oral traditions and emerging internet cultures? #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 16:36:29 +0000 2009 Foley: oral tradition survives through rule-governed flexibility; ability to morph with morphing society; power to navigate network #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 16:41:05 +0000 2009 Next q Lisa Gitelman: physical archives primary material of history; how might digital change not just archives but history itself? #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 16:47:36 +0000 2009 Gitelman: all but absent from digital archives is labor that produced it; can search Google Books for words, but not for fingers #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 16:53:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry Actually, was question posed to Lisa Gitelman, & the topic of her talk. \(Panel structured around such questions\.\) Fri Apr 24 16:54:10 +0000 2009 q to Rick Prelinger: archive in past served small audience; as archives move to internet, how to serve vastly expanded audience #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:02:24 +0000 2009 Prelinger: must reconceive archive as repository rather than workshop; not collection but place of transaction #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:04:10 +0000 2009 Prelinger: want to see movement from archive/library being host of solitary research spaces to venue for collaboration #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:04:54 +0000 2009 q to Anne Wolpert: given malleability, is archive of digital archives still an archive? #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:06:17 +0000 2009 Wolpert: threats to persistence of archival material were once about preservation: rodents, decay, theft, etc. #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:09:02 +0000 2009 Wolpert: persistence in digital world does not happen by luck; happens by intentional investment #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:10:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @j_l_r Not even remotely! I have jetlagged archival-discussion fever. :) #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 17:49:57 +0000 2009 Classroom for next session has power outlets by each seat. Seats are just a hair too low for table, though. Power, yes; body, no. Hmm. #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 18:23:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Long of body, short of legs? Like Cajuns. A connection that wouldn't have occurred to me. Fri Apr 24 18:26:51 +0000 2009 M Manoff: false binary between library need to "remake themselves to support the new, or the stick with the old and face obsolescence" #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 18:38:13 +0000 2009 M Manoff: libraries bear some responsibility for crisis facing monograph; just now recognizing need to resist journal monopoly pricing #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 18:48:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare and @loganpoppy No kidding: mine's palatial by comparison, if built for six-foot tall men. Fri Apr 24 19:02:03 +0000 2009 Digital Libraries & Archives session was 2/3 archives, 1/3 libraries. All great papers, but I crave more uni library issues. #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 19:28:11 +0000 2009 Great Q for Marlene Manoff about nightmare scenario: if JSTOR disappeared, would libraries be prepared? A: not as much as we'd like #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 19:40:42 +0000 2009 Seems to be a body/mind issue after all: this room has me-sized chairs/tables, but one lone power outlet. Onto which I have glommed. #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:08:02 +0000 2009 Dennis Downey, textflows.com - project creating dynamic text; slower reading process, but transforms text into game, painting #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:35:00 +0000 2009 TextFlows turns prose into poetry, poetry into performance; screen as proscenium #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:36:58 +0000 2009 Next up: @dkompare, "Time Vortex: Versioning and the Fluid Text" #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:45:20 +0000 2009 \(Not "vortext," as the program had it\. Nice typo, though\.\) #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:45:58 +0000 2009 @dkompare rethinking notion of repetition from "ReRun Nation"; in transmedia era, logic has become that of versioning #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:49:35 +0000 2009 @dkompare: "cult television" as term foregrounds affective relations with users #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:50:41 +0000 2009 @dkompare: text, work, brand: work is material, arbitrarily fixed, has copyright status; versioning of texts creates new works #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 20:57:05 +0000 2009 @dkompare: fetishistic remastering of original works coupled with addition of extensive new CGI; complex logic of versioning #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 21:01:45 +0000 2009 Jason Mittell emailed me from one row up to tell me that the bldg# for my panel w/@halavais may be typo'd in the program: E51, not E15 #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 21:29:03 +0000 2009 \(You got to love the email from three feet away, but we need to figure out how to make Jason Twitter\.\) #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 21:32:41 +0000 2009 Super curious why the robot video camera up front was pointed at the audience a minute ago. \(And a little self\-conscious, too\!\) #MiT6 Fri Apr 24 23:30:04 +0000 2009 Have that awful head-filling eyes-burning scratchy-throat feeling. Cannot get sick today. No no no no no. Fri Apr 24 23:50:35 +0000 2009 Talking on the MIT campus at 9:00, building E51 room 376. Drop by if you're around. #MiT6 \(via @halavais, with whom I'm speaking\) Sat Apr 25 12:49:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @j_l_r Sorry about the starting on time thing; we've got a very efficient moderator, not steeped in five-minutes-late time of uni #mit6 Sat Apr 25 13:28:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @austinds Have fun in NOLA! Sat Apr 25 13:31:24 +0000 2009 Failed to mention MediaCommons URL in my talk: http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org - actively seeking participation, so come join! #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 14:03:00 +0000 2009 Love these rooms with the power outlets at the seats, but still feel like I need to be about six inches taller to make it work. #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 19:23:34 +0000 2009 Jennifer Holt on migration of television to digital space; suspicious claims that TV is still dominant viewing format #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 19:26:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare Totally believe that! Sat Apr 25 19:27:15 +0000 2009 Miranda Banks: technical changes redefine nature of "work"; tv's move to digital must produce new means of thinking about labor #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 19:45:01 +0000 2009 Banks: 1988 WGA strike failed in part b/c TV writers failed to see why they should care about VHS residuals. Oops. #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 19:53:40 +0000 2009 Good god: anybody here from UGA? http://www.uga.edu #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 20:04:03 +0000 2009 Abstracts with authors after G have gone poof; makes it hard to decide what panel to go to next! #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 20:40:13 +0000 2009 Plenary conversation: The Future of Publishing, with Gavin Grant, Jennifer Jackson, Bob Miller, and Bob Stein #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 22:55:22 +0000 2009 Q to Gavin Grant: how is the rise of new publishing and promotion technologies affecting the way you acquire and publish your titles? #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 23:01:48 +0000 2009 A: mixed; acquisition and preparation remain the same. Publicity is a bit more wired; social media help books to find right readers #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 23:03:55 +0000 2009 Grant: Amazon is the evil heart of publishing. #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 23:04:33 +0000 2009 Miller: dark side of ebook publishing? "downward pressure on prices." Sheesh. #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 23:26:16 +0000 2009 Grant: Kindle as 1970s technology released now; "it's like Texas Instruments designed an ebook reader" #MiT6 Sat Apr 25 23:28:33 +0000 2009 Bea Arthur, TV Battle-Ax? For shame, NYTimes, for shame. Sun Apr 26 05:02:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @synch101 Yes, thanks - finally got a bit of sleep. I don't handle jet lag anywhere near as well as you! Sun Apr 26 13:12:01 +0000 2009 Jean Burgess: in whose interest is it for us to understand user-created content as cat videos? #MiT6 Sun Apr 26 13:33:19 +0000 2009 Very cool series of video essays on Strangers on a Train being presented by Chris Keathley, Andrew Miller, and Eric Faden #MiT6 Sun Apr 26 15:08:45 +0000 2009 Faden: what would scholarship look like if it weren't a paper? if it used the tools we're studying? #MiT6 Sun Apr 26 15:09:45 +0000 2009 At the airport, on my way home from #MiT6. Amazing weekend, all the way around. Now I have to go pay for it - work piles up in my absence. Sun Apr 26 19:04:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @xina aw, man! I wish I'd been around! On my way home from Boston... Mon Apr 27 02:30:34 +0000 2009 Still trying to catch up at home, post-#MiT6. Why is it that the real payment for a great conference always comes due afterward? Tue Apr 28 16:28:22 +0000 2009 Is it bad of me to note that reading senior theses is more fun after a margarita or two? \(Probably\.\) Thu Apr 30 03:10:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall and @loganpoppy Good points, both of you. May as well enjoy it! Thu Apr 30 03:21:22 +0000 2009 Not to mention that I hear tequila & citrus are good virus preventatives. Thu Apr 30 03:22:30 +0000 2009 On Time's 100, T. Boone Pickens writes for Ted Turner, and Turner writes for Pickens. I miss the New Yorker's "Logrolling in Our Time." Thu Apr 30 18:41:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I sense a recurring theme! Thu Apr 30 19:34:42 +0000 2009
