docs.title: 2009 06 01 tweets

docs.text: Hey, happy birthday @amandafrench! Good luck with Project Caffeine Withdrawal -- as somebody else said, you're braver than I am. Wed Jul 01 14:04:59 +0000 2009 Though I'm sure he's offline right now, sending good thoughts @briancroxall's way... Wed Jul 01 14:09:40 +0000 2009 Trying to decide whether I want to spend my birthday on planes or in a country where I know no one. This is not the most ideal choice. Thu Jul 02 14:57:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @stoton CommentPress for Drupal is pretty much tops on my wishlist... Thu Jul 02 15:47:31 +0000 2009 Booked the flights. Birthday will be spent alone abroad but at least in a city I love. \(With a nice excuse to upgrade the hotel room a bit\!\) Thu Jul 02 15:50:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @rachaelsullivan Yeah, things could be a lot worse! Thu Jul 02 16:19:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @sshreeves That's great. Alas, I'd be flying east-to-west, so not only would I lose 9hrs of the day, but I'd spend 6am to 10pm in transit. Thu Jul 02 16:21:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall I'm a big fan of Meridian http://bit.ly/wfIjN - multiple clocks, alarms, timers, and stopwatches, with flexible displays. Thu Jul 02 18:35:23 +0000 2009 @briancroxall PS: I hope the small one is well soon, and that mom & dad hold up in the meantime. Thu Jul 02 18:36:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @superba Congrats! And six reviews already! My book's been up there for almost three years and not ONE review. Stupid scholarly publishing. Thu Jul 02 19:14:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @superba That's an awfully nice offer! The book's available here http://bit.ly/191OwD but I'd be happy to send you one. Thu Jul 02 19:39:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @superba Wow, thanks! I'll look forward to hearing what you think. Thu Jul 02 20:07:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @veek I use Google Reader for feeds & added Safari icon to home screen on the iPhone. It isn't perfect but you can star items to read later. Thu Jul 02 20:53:52 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench See, now *there's* something I could give up coffee for. Thu Jul 02 21:04:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @alogemann Cool, thanks! Thu Jul 02 21:56:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley, @dancohen and @naypinya My sense? OA, yes. Author-pays, no. Publishing must be paid for, but not enough research is grant-funded. Fri Jul 03 02:46:52 +0000 2009 @brettbobley @dancohen The majority of labor that goes into academic journals is un- or under-paid. What if \(1\) we took that labor back?... Fri Jul 03 02:52:04 +0000 2009 @brettbobley @dancohen and \(2\) gave grants directly to non-author-pays OA journals, as does SSHRC? Fri Jul 03 02:53:22 +0000 2009 @brettbobley @dancohen As Billy Bragg would say, "start your own revolution and cut out the middleman." Fri Jul 03 02:53:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay I look forward to it! Fri Jul 03 03:03:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley Hey, but no pressure, right? Fri Jul 03 03:08:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay Exactly. We do the majority of editing and review for free. They provide imprimatur and distribution. We can handle the first... Fri Jul 03 03:24:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay and there's that interweb thing I've heard about for the latter. Fri Jul 03 03:24:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay Hey, excellent. I'll look forward to seeing the result! Fri Jul 03 03:30:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay Sometimes I get the feeling we're watching that already. But I still hope the film comes sooner. Fri Jul 03 03:39:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench That's excellent! I love the unexpectedly extended birthday celebration. Fri Jul 03 14:02:16 +0000 2009 New blog post: Why I Want Google Wave NOW, Please http://bit.ly/18w5p3 Fri Jul 03 17:22:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @superba Me, too, but no, it started from this report http://is.gd/1lYpB and a Q about whether humanities OA can support author-pays pubs. Fri Jul 03 17:36:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @superba My inner bomb-thrower said we're already self-funding most research; why should we pay to publish? Better to start our own journals Fri Jul 03 17:38:16 +0000 2009 Whoa. RT @jmittell: just discovered that OpenOffice docs can drag into WordPress and retain all formatting/footnotes. #openoffice FTW! Fri Jul 03 18:08:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @barbarahui It would be tremendous fun! Unfortunately, I don't have room in my schedule even for UN-conference planning. :( Fri Jul 03 19:33:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @superba I already ordered it, I'm happy to say; I'm really looking forward to reading it! Fri Jul 03 19:34:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @barbarahui Hmmm. I wonder if the folks at IML would be interested... Fri Jul 03 19:46:52 +0000 2009 "The announcement stirred speculation that she would focus on a bid for the 2012 Republican nomination for president." She needs 3 years? Fri Jul 03 20:04:33 +0000 2009 "By leaving office early, [she] will be able to travel... more freely and not have to deal with the constraints of being a governor." !!! Fri Jul 03 20:08:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay As long as you're looking that way, could you keep an eye on Putin, in case he rears his head? I'm worried no one's watching now. Sat Jul 04 00:46:30 +0000 2009 Proud to be an American, where at least I know I'm free to take an hour and a half nap after breakfast if I feel like it.* Sat Jul 04 19:00:04 +0000 2009 *Weekends and holidays. Some restrictions may apply. Sat Jul 04 19:00:29 +0000 2009 Am unreasonably happy at having beaten a couple of Rayman Raving Rabbids games that had me totally stymied. Sun Jul 05 00:08:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @barbarahui That's awesome! Sun Jul 05 03:59:23 +0000 2009 Three sets of fireworks visible from my balcony. Happy 4th, SoCal. Sun Jul 05 04:08:53 +0000 2009 ~@j_l_r Been following this; it's an interesting experiment, though not really p2p as I imagine it - not open, not discussion-centered. Sun Jul 05 21:46:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Poor thing! I hope you're all holding up... Mon Jul 06 18:30:44 +0000 2009 New blog post: Blegging: Preservation http://bit.ly/2HQCKQ Tue Jul 07 14:46:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Fantastic! I'm so happy to hear it. Tue Jul 07 14:58:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @jasonrhody Isn't The Scarlet Letter "found" in the Custom House preface? Tue Jul 07 16:11:59 +0000 2009 New blog post: Against Anonymity http://bit.ly/AT996 Thu Jul 09 15:09:36 +0000 2009 MLA members: would you be willing to pay xtra w/ registration for wireless during conference? How much? \(Imagine $ for single\-user pass\.\) Thu Jul 09 17:59:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @g_gerg That's what I'm asking - if it were to be included with reg fee, how much more would conf attendees be willing to pay? Thu Jul 09 18:12:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas Should but don't. If MLA's response to why they can't provide wi-fi is $, the ? is how much of a hike in reg fees we'd accept... Thu Jul 09 18:21:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @g_gerg It's a conference with ~15K attendees annually, so there are serious limitations on venue already \(don't get me started on those\) . Thu Jul 09 18:44:10 +0000 2009 Replying to @g_gerg Problem 2 is the org doesn't see tech as a requirement, which is what I'm trying to imagine a way around. Thu Jul 09 18:44:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @billwolff I couldn't agree more - but am not holding my breath. If, however, $5-10 more in reg fees made the difference...? Thu Jul 09 19:33:03 +0000 2009 After four days doing little other than eating in Baton Rouge, am fat, dumb, and happy. Emphasis on dumb, alas. Five weeks left: not enough. Sat Jul 11 00:59:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @mamamusings I will definitely work on that! Sat Jul 11 02:14:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @dlnnewman Sadly, no; it was a very quick trip to see the folks & meet up with Rick. I'll let you know next time I'm back! Sun Jul 12 16:23:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @davidmsilver Congratulations, David - and all best to you and Sarah! Sun Jul 12 16:24:44 +0000 2009 Beginning a hardcore press to get this book finished; off the grid for most of the week. See you soon, internets. Mon Jul 13 17:53:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @dancohen Yes, LSUP budget goes from 500 to 400K but with no cover for overage, which was 1M last year. Tue Jul 14 02:31:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @dancohen The saddest part is that LA is actually 1 of 2 states doing *well* in this economy; the cuts are pure opportunism. Tue Jul 14 03:18:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @dancohen The previous is personal opinion unadulterated with any factual basis other than speculation of family living in LA Tue Jul 14 03:19:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Yes. :) Tue Jul 14 14:15:11 +0000 2009 Have turned the corner on this section of this chapter. Now to be able to say that about the chapter. And then about the book... Wed Jul 15 00:01:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Man, so do I. :) Wed Jul 15 18:03:53 +0000 2009 TEI folks: P4 guidelines indicate a required DOCTYPE declaration http://bit.ly/vNW8H but I can't find the req in P5. Does it still exist? Wed Jul 15 20:48:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid Si! Va! Si! Va! \(I hope I can call on you for return cheers in a month\.\) Thu Jul 16 02:18:34 +0000 2009 New blog post: Please Reply This Email Immediately and Provide the Following Information Above http://bit.ly/BKRbQ Thu Jul 16 16:07:37 +0000 2009 New blog post: Obsolete http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/obsolete/ Thu Jul 16 16:54:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @partsnpieces Boy, that's nervy. Pretty near as nervy as asking a woman when she's due. Totally possible, but with major potential badness. Thu Jul 16 19:02:05 +0000 2009 Twice this morning I read tweets about "Emmy noms" as comments on their yumminess. I think I need to stay away from the LOLcats. Thu Jul 16 23:53:42 +0000 2009 New blog post: On Teaching Infinite Jest http://bit.ly/1zhKZR Fri Jul 17 15:15:40 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Thanks, for this and for the #infsum shoutout! Fri Jul 17 16:49:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @jasonrhody Not that I've used it, but the Public Knowledge Project's Open Conference Systems: http://pkp.sfu.ca/?q=ocs Fri Jul 17 20:24:17 +0000 2009 New blog post: RIP, Walter Cronkite http://bit.ly/fxUCz Sat Jul 18 00:28:54 +0000 2009 New blog post: Okay, AT&T, You're On Notice http://bit.ly/FBb1g Sun Jul 19 17:00:19 +0000 2009 New blog post: The Cost of Peer Review and the Future of Scholarly Publishing http://bit.ly/lIGDf Mon Jul 20 16:16:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @paoloman This is part of what I argue; the most impt aspect of peer-to-peer review may be review of the reviewers. Full chapter TK soon! Mon Jul 20 16:40:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @unsworth agreed; it's a problematic sample, not least as those journals have paid staffs as opposed to most hum journals... Mon Jul 20 19:53:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Hi there, #dhtaos! Tue Jul 21 01:59:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay Absolutely! Also, that authority = exclusivity. Otherwise, my colleagues won't take my work seriously. That one's bugging me today. Tue Jul 21 21:51:44 +0000 2009 If your colleagues don't take your work seriously, you need to show them why they should. Hrmph. Tue Jul 21 21:52:16 +0000 2009 Replying to @barbarahui Calls for a hearty round of antidisestablishmentarianism. Tue Jul 21 21:53:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie Argh! I clearly need to catch up with the stream before replying. :( Tue Jul 21 21:54:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Haven't gotten to read it yet, but if @sramsay's on target, I'm totally with him. I'll look forward to reading your post! Tue Jul 21 21:56:04 +0000 2009 Trying to figure out what I have to say about metadata. Other than "it's important! Have some!" *sigh* Wed Jul 22 16:10:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @jwolman I have one that lasts a bit longer: "Tour de France" where "France" is pronounced all Englishy. Wed Jul 22 16:11:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @lblanken Yeah, that's what I'm trying to focus on, but am still having trouble saying more than "yay, metadata!" :P Wed Jul 22 16:23:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline That's exactly the kind of thing I'm after: extensibility! Wed Jul 22 16:23:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline and @barbarahui Oh, that's brilliant. Metadata as interpretive act, implicit statement of data's significance. Wed Jul 22 16:41:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Yes! Thanks - that's just what I needed. We know some things better than others do, but can't know as much as everyone knows. Wed Jul 22 16:42:53 +0000 2009 Now, how to cite these crowdsourced ideas about metadata? Need meta-meta-metadata. :) Wed Jul 22 16:45:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave One would think there would be a better way, but not so much, I guess. Come on, Google Wave API! Wed Jul 22 17:54:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I just literally LOLd. \(And I think this represents the first time I've ever typed that acronym\.\) Wed Jul 22 17:57:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @amcunningham ACK! It honestly never even occurred to me. #nottooswooftwiththehashtags Wed Jul 22 18:09:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave That's the problem, right? B/c mine are linked to my blog, I have my end archived, but not the other participants. Wed Jul 22 18:27:37 +0000 2009 Following the lead of many others, and trying out http://backupmytweets.com Wed Jul 22 20:16:48 +0000 2009 backupmytweets.com backed up 1000 tweets. I thought they just rounded off, until I checked; my last post \(about them\) was 1001. Wed Jul 22 20:19:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline YouTube, my friend! Every one that I remembered, and several I'd forgotten. Wed Jul 22 21:33:32 +0000 2009 The thing the #Gates incident brings up for me is why we accept a world in which "never talk back to the police!" is an unquestioned truth. Thu Jul 23 15:05:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Sure. Which leads to situations like this one http://bit.ly/Yw02Z in which the officer's feelings take precedence over all else. Thu Jul 23 15:39:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @matthewbaldwin God, this just made me remember: his first year here, I went to a match with Dave. How could I have forgotten that? #infsum Fri Jul 24 00:11:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @shanakimball Seal it in a canister of rice, at least overnight. Should dry it out fine! Fri Jul 24 00:25:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattbucher and @skylightbooks Ooh: Joni Mitchell, "Cold Blue Steel and Sweet Fire" \(addiction \+ Canadian bonus\!\) #infinitejestplaylist #infsum Fri Jul 24 19:31:20 +0000 2009 While the metadata chapter hasn't yet killed me, it hasn't exactly made me stronger, either. Fri Jul 24 21:24:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @jilltxt My insurance is pretty good: $50 copay for an ER visit \(waived if results in hospitalization\) and $15 for regular dr visits Sat Jul 25 21:58:27 +0000 2009 Replying to @jilltxt But \(a\) I'm the exception, and \(b\) my employer and I pay a lot for that coverage. Sat Jul 25 21:58:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @j_l_r I just followed you; I don't know if that gives you a link to follow back or not, but I'm at http://bit.ly/11r2J1 Sun Jul 26 20:37:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall That's great, Brian - I'm glad you know about this year, and are making plans for next. Sending you all good thoughts... Mon Jul 27 15:25:05 +0000 2009 @academicdave: I'm not getting your @ replies at all, even when I'm following the person they're sent to. Is that intentional? Mon Jul 27 15:26:38 +0000 2009 Just figured that out over the last two days, seeing only one side of convos between @academicdave & someone else I follow. Mon Jul 27 15:27:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave See, for instance, I didn't get your reply to ME, even; had to go into your user page to see it. What gives? Mon Jul 27 15:29:26 +0000 2009 @academicdave I take that last one back; the reply just took a minute to show up. And I'm seeing your non-replies, so you're not blocked... Mon Jul 27 15:34:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave I'm def following you - got this one. I also get your reg posts, but don't get replies to others, even if I'm following them. Mon Jul 27 16:06:32 +0000 2009 New office is surrounded by classrooms, so will need to keep door shut once students are back. Need a sign: The Professor Is In/Not In/Busy. Mon Jul 27 18:00:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave No doors is brave. I'd never be able to get anything done. But I like mixed-use buildings with classroom/office proximity. Mon Jul 27 18:43:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Yeah, it's probably a pipe dream. This one gets ignored, after all: http://yfrog.com/70gjrj Mon Jul 27 18:46:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @captain_primate Are you experiencing generally, or only with some folks? I think the issue with @academicdave may have been his app Mon Jul 27 19:23:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @captain_primate As @academicdave points out, I unfollowed and refollowed, and all seems well now! Mon Jul 27 19:31:29 +0000 2009 Just watched "The Yes Men Fix the World," which was a surreal experience since I know one of them from a past life. Tue Jul 28 03:59:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @lnakamur I was in a masters program with Jacques Servin a million years ago. It's always bizarre seeing him in this context! Tue Jul 28 15:17:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry That doesn't sound good... Wed Jul 29 02:51:37 +0000 2009 \(no doubt too late with this, but nonetheless\) The Lion, the Witch, and the Walk-in Closet. #failedchildrensbooktitles Wed Jul 29 02:54:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @zachwhalen Hey, and the book's from Vandy! I had a great experience with them; I hope you did, too. Congrats! Thu Jul 30 16:23:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @dancohen I'm curious how Wave will blur the lines between email/IM/Twitter/etc; which services will disappear in its, um, wake? Thu Jul 30 21:07:30 +0000 2009 I took the day off on Tuesday. This is worth announcing because I think it's the first full day off I've taken in a couple of months. Thu Jul 30 21:08:56 +0000 2009 Result: weirdly disoriented yesterday. But insanely productive today. Maybe I should look into some kind of weekly downtime or something. Thu Jul 30 21:10:54 +0000 2009 I could call it the weekly pause. Or the seventh-day breather. Something like that. Thu Jul 30 21:12:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @dancohen a real issue! If everything cloudifies, will there be any archives? \(Says she with email dating back to 1992 on her hard drive\.\) Thu Jul 30 21:14:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Ooh, catchy! Thu Jul 30 21:47:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome Now if it were full of awesome like this, it's be worth reporting! http://bit.ly/3xT1O Thu Jul 30 23:20:38 +0000 2009 FInished. The. Chapter. \(Now to chapterize the article, draft the conclusion, polish the intro, and make sure the whole thing makes sense\!\) Fri Jul 31 17:08:15 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Should that have been "chapterificate"? I *am* easily confused right now. Fri Jul 31 19:25:52 +0000 2009 Wow. Searching the @chronicle website allows you to see the future! \(Srsly: sorting results by date shows articles posted a week from now\.\) Fri Jul 31 19:41:47 +0000 2009 A phrase I really need to work on eliminating from my writing: "into the future." Seriously: as opposed to what? Moving into the past? Fri Jul 31 21:15:12 +0000 2009
docs.title: 2009 07 01 tweets

docs.text: I'd really like a better term than "multimodal" to say the thing we used to mean when we said "multimedia." Any suggestions? Mon Aug 03 18:23:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @veek That's *awesome*. Mon Aug 03 19:46:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley My sense is while multimedia can include static uses \(like illustration\) the multimodal pushes user engagement beyond reading. Mon Aug 03 20:15:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @ericbot Except that the book is mediated, too; it's a little too generic to designate change. Mon Aug 03 21:06:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @partsnpieces 85 spf sounds like a serious sun-blocking weapon! And daily brushing is an important weapon in the fight against tooth decay. Tue Aug 04 17:40:53 +0000 2009 So, the March 2009 issue of PMLA just landed in my inbox. I get that print moves slowly, but sheesh. Wed Aug 05 19:12:49 +0000 2009 Rats. Revising the article into chapter 3 is going to be a lot harder than I'd hoped. Harder than nine days, I fear. Ratsratsratsrats. Wed Aug 05 20:30:35 +0000 2009 My thoughts about chapterizing keep coming back to the eggs & omelets thing; you have to break the article in order to make a chapter. Thu Aug 06 17:37:27 +0000 2009 I have a very hard time breaking stuff that works. The problem is that it doesn't work in this context. So: il faut craquer des oeufs. Thu Aug 06 17:38:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Not yet, but soon, I hope! Thu Aug 06 20:34:35 +0000 2009 #SCMS tweeps: seeking participant for a workshop on the future of conferencing, esp. w/an interest in economic/ecologic issues. DM or @ me! Fri Aug 07 19:08:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave More like how to be more ecological by having conferences online. On a space like, I dunno, @mediacommons. For instance. :) Fri Aug 07 19:14:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @jonbecker Very cool -- thanks for the link! Fri Aug 07 19:20:07 +0000 2009 NeoOffice is crashing repeatedly this morning. Beginning to think it doesn't like my book. Tue Aug 11 15:53:18 +0000 2009 Gah. Press wants endnotes not footnotes. OO/NeoO can either do footnotes or one big endnote dump at end of document w/o chapter divisions. Tue Aug 11 16:03:52 +0000 2009 Figured out how to place endnotes after each chapter, but it's causing both NeoO and OO to crash repeatedly. Surely there's a better way. Tue Aug 11 16:04:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave You and me both. I'm about to dig my heels in on this issue and tell the press that I have to deliver the ms with footnotes. Tue Aug 11 16:16:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall I hope I can get footnotes in the printed text, but the issue now is what I have to deliver to them. Which, ugh. Tue Aug 11 16:43:11 +0000 2009 Honestly, just let me loose in InDesign and I'll typeset the stupid thing myself. Tue Aug 11 16:43:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Les Mechanisms sont très importants; il faut les bien comprendre! Tue Aug 11 17:01:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmaxsfu Wow - that's super cool. Probably more than I can contemplate right now, but I want to learn more! Tue Aug 11 18:14:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @kwazana Not quite. About 5 miserable pages to go, plus conclusion. Was trying to distract myself with formatting... Tue Aug 11 19:27:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @kwazana Ugh, no. Right now all I have to say is "so now you see, digital scholarship, fight the power, the end." Will prob write it later. Tue Aug 11 19:38:55 +0000 2009 Sheesh. Twitter goes down for half an hour and already there's a post on the NYT about it? http://bit.ly/eFrpQ I think I know who's outraged Tue Aug 11 19:51:18 +0000 2009 Hey, so: I pretty much have a draft. \(At least of all but the conclusion, which I may not write until after I put the thing out for review\.\) Tue Aug 11 22:20:15 +0000 2009 Not sure what to make of the fact that the chapters I wrote this summer are the part I like the least. Perhaps I left the spinach for last. Tue Aug 11 22:28:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Hey, so do I! This is like metaphorical spinach, not the good stuff. Either that or I didn't work as well this summer as last. Tue Aug 11 22:43:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos grazie! Tue Aug 11 22:48:29 +0000 2009 I really need a WP plugin that produces pop-up annotations/footnotes. Something Snap-like, but with my text. And not ugly. Any ideas? Tue Aug 11 23:08:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas It's quite nice but I'm really hoping to get away from the bottom-of-page model. Esp since I argue against the "page" metaphor! Tue Aug 11 23:23:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid That's what I was thinking. Also that the conclusion can be as up-to-date as possible that way. Tue Aug 11 23:27:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Hmm. I'll ponder. The problem is I'm publishing the text for discussion in CommentPress, which runs in WPmu, so I may be stuck. Tue Aug 11 23:28:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @noahwf Fabulous news - congratulations! Wed Aug 12 02:46:28 +0000 2009 Wondering if the answer to my popup annotation question is really as easy as using the acronym tag. It's ugly, but it may do the trick. Wed Aug 12 16:56:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Problem is using JavaScript in a WP post is a major pain, unless there's been some new development while I wasn't looking. Wed Aug 12 17:04:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @thepalephantom Yes, I've seen this, and it's great - but I'm trying to break away from the bottom-of-the-page model. Wed Aug 12 17:10:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Hmm. That may be a solution. I was looking at something like overLIB, but was stuck on the js issue... Wed Aug 12 17:21:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattbucher Interesting! Very "Host." Problem is that the main text is going to be in CommentPress, so I'm not sure whether this would work. Wed Aug 12 17:23:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @clioweb That would be fabulous! Wed Aug 12 19:03:42 +0000 2009 In case you didn't know, @clioweb is a supergenius & has solved my footnote issue. Now I really look forward to foisting the ms on you all. Wed Aug 12 23:04:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore Not at all! I don't have a pub date yet, but expect pre-pub CommentPress release to start in a week or two. Will keep you posted. Thu Aug 13 00:28:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @nickmirzoeff Yes, thanks! Am working on a draft today; will share when ready. Thu Aug 13 16:24:22 +0000 2009 It's awesome that things are so well landscaped around here, but ENOUGH WITH THE FREAKING WEED WHACKERS ALREADY. You're killing me, guys. Fri Aug 14 15:29:02 +0000 2009 Aaaand: the manuscript is submitted. Fri Aug 14 17:39:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome Thanks! Fri Aug 14 17:45:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave If all goes according to plan, as early as the end of next week... Fri Aug 14 17:46:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Really! I've got CP ready to go. Once the sysadmin installs WPmu and I can get the data in \(and get my editor's ok\) , it's on. Fri Aug 14 17:55:44 +0000 2009 New blog post: Um... Is This Thing On? http://bit.ly/QiCQu Fri Aug 14 17:56:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall "Relax." I seem to have heard this word somewhere before, but I'm not placing it. Fri Aug 14 17:58:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave My Intro Dig Media Studies course in the spring was entirely paper-/bookless; so nice to have everything available online. Sat Aug 15 18:47:40 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave A physicist calculated that if he pushed all the way to the end of his 9.69 Olympic race he'd have finished in 9.55. Amazing. Sun Aug 16 22:56:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave That's crazy. Sun Aug 16 23:12:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Holy crap. Sun Aug 16 23:47:24 +0000 2009 My morning began with me dropping a full glass of juice on my desk, where it shattered & sprayed juice & glass everywhere. How's yours? Mon Aug 17 14:30:40 +0000 2009 Replying to @jbj Ugh! My only one-up here is the presence of all my computer equipment in the juice trajectory. Otherwise, tomatoes are worse. Mon Aug 17 14:46:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay Yes. And now you can finally start speaking your mind, at last! :) Mon Aug 17 15:50:02 +0000 2009 @sramsay \(p\.s\. congratulations\) Mon Aug 17 15:50:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston Can you post it online and say in the syllabus that students are responsible for reading and complying with it? Mon Aug 17 15:51:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston Yes. We can set our own policies, but I hated having it in the syllabus. I do walk through it carefully on the first day. Mon Aug 17 16:00:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @triproftri Joke! Big joke! Mon Aug 17 16:01:03 +0000 2009 If I could reinvent grad school, the first thing I'd do would be to require all first-years to take a course in grant/fellowship writing. Mon Aug 17 16:37:31 +0000 2009 Which is to say: how can I have gotten this far into my career and still suffer like this every time I have to write an application? Mon Aug 17 16:38:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave While you're at it, vote for mine; Hacking the Ivory Tower w/@MediaCommons & @FlowTV folks: http://bit.ly/iScwx Mon Aug 17 17:11:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @annehelen Excellent! I'll get the MediaCommons vote out for us, too. Mon Aug 17 17:38:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Aw, man. I was really hoping it was possible to get better at this. It's misery right now. Mon Aug 17 19:31:12 +0000 2009 RT @FlowTV Two Flow-based panels up at SxSW-interactive panel picker. Please vote & retweet! http://bit.ly/36azAf and http://bit.ly/VBYhC Mon Aug 17 21:44:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry Thanks! Have voted for yours as well. Sounds like a great panel. Mon Aug 17 21:48:18 +0000 2009 New blog post: The Opposition http://bit.ly/2iEUps Tue Aug 18 12:38:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos I'm a big #dork and I'm reading it right now! Tue Aug 18 19:30:45 +0000 2009 Now boarding for Amsterdam. See you when there's wifi. #stupidat&tintlroamingcharges Tue Aug 18 19:54:49 +0000 2009 Raging ass who spilled a drink on himself & then complained b/c airline wouldnt provide him with spare pants just taken off plane. Thank god Tue Aug 18 20:35:54 +0000 2009 In AMS after a blissfully raging-ass-free flt. Dude was bonkers, if you hadn't guessed, insulting staff & threatening lawsuit. Wed Aug 19 06:54:39 +0000 2009 Gave flight attendant & service mgr my card in case. They handled it all way more professionally than I'd have been capable of doing. Wed Aug 19 06:56:33 +0000 2009 Now to keep myself awake for 2.5 hours until next flight boards. So paid for wifi, but most of you are asleep & can't help amuse me. Wed Aug 19 06:59:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @ritaraley No, he pretty much kept it to yelling for spare pants. Doesn't measure up to recent public crazy, but makes for a bad 9hr flight. Wed Aug 19 07:13:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Wow. You mean there's a mobile provider even stingier than AT&T? Wed Aug 19 07:52:16 +0000 2009 3 hrs sleep Monday night. Breakfast between ONT & IAH. Dinner, 2 hrs sleep, & breakfast between IAH & AMS. So how can it be Wednesday? Wed Aug 19 09:06:00 +0000 2009 Which is to say: I'm delirious. Let's board this flight already so I can sleep a little more. Wed Aug 19 09:07:02 +0000 2009 Okay, okay, I'm awake. #jetlag Wed Aug 19 15:33:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @clioweb No kidding. And they haven't even launched yet! Wed Aug 19 17:57:41 +0000 2009 Okay! Took an hour nap yesterday once in the hotel, and then slept 9 solid hours last night. Feel fabulous \(but expecting the 3pm crash\) . Thu Aug 20 06:43:22 +0000 2009 Weird sudden optical effect had me worried about oncoming migraine. Turns out a very small bug was crawling around on my glasses lens. Thu Aug 20 06:59:25 +0000 2009 Things that don't go well together: jet-lag, monster headache, rock festival in the street outside... [1/2] Thu Aug 20 19:53:28 +0000 2009 ...and the discovery that I have to fill 60-90 minutes in tomorrow's defense. [2/2] Thu Aug 20 19:53:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @JenServenti Thanks; I'll take that. Or, rather, some non-virtual Advil. Thu Aug 20 20:30:54 +0000 2009 So here's something I love about Norway, or at least the two Norwegian hotels I've stayed in thus far: gently heated bathroom floors. Thu Aug 20 21:16:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley Very cool project; I look forward to hearing/seeing more... Thu Aug 20 21:42:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley Okay, wow. I knew I loved this place. Fri Aug 21 06:34:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @halavais Boy, you just want the world, don't you? Next thing you'll be asking for customer service to actually serve customers. Sheesh. Sat Aug 22 17:38:10 +0000 2009 Major up-side of a birthday spent in Europe: having it last from 12.01 CET to 11.59 PDT. You still have 86 minutes to get your wishes in. :) Mon Aug 24 05:35:08 +0000 2009 New blog post: On the Run http://bit.ly/285Llk Mon Aug 24 07:16:45 +0000 2009 Okay, today's travel is seriously kicking my ass. But I'm four hours from home. Tomorrow morning, pre-semester panic begins. Mon Aug 24 22:20:00 +0000 2009 Home. Ahhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh. Tue Aug 25 03:43:04 +0000 2009 The MLA is now tweeting: RT @mlaconvention: Stay tuned to mlaconvention for news about the MLA's annual mtg in Philadelphia. Wed Aug 26 21:54:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @triproftri Dunno, but it's Rosemary Feal who's doing the tweeting. Very, very cool. Wed Aug 26 22:00:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @johnmjones That's a very good question. Program cmte meets in a couple of weeks; I hope to press the issue... Wed Aug 26 22:07:05 +0000 2009 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm glad you're here - it's a great step! Wed Aug 26 22:13:16 +0000 2009 Replying to @mlaconvention and @johnmjones An informal poll of folks following me suggests most are willing to pay a modest amt more in conv fees for wifi. Wed Aug 26 22:25:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @mlaconvention My sense is many people would happily pay $10, and some a bit more. They'd also like MLA to negotiate w/conf ctrs for access. Wed Aug 26 22:38:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @mlaconvention Digital comm \(like Twitter\) is an increasingly important part of the conference experience. It's becoming a necessity. Wed Aug 26 22:39:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall I spotted that and almost said something myself. It's beginning to look like a new MLA. Thu Aug 27 00:48:22 +0000 2009 Whenever I need roof access \(e\.g\. for a/c service\) , I have to give $50 dep to HOA for a key. Currently have a key, and am tempted to copy. Thu Aug 27 20:42:38 +0000 2009 @mariabustillos Hey- finished Dorkismo on trip to Norway & LOVED it. Posted rev on Amazon. Can't wait to find out what the next book holds. Fri Aug 28 16:54:20 +0000 2009 New blog post: Senioring a Young Field http://bit.ly/EOi2k Fri Aug 28 18:02:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @TheRepoRat Of course! \(and she is\) Fri Aug 28 18:22:15 +0000 2009 Need a good essay exploring tension btw series & seriality in television \(esp\. recent tv\) , and know I'm missing something. Any suggestions? Fri Aug 28 18:31:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell That may be the right thing - but I can't remember: how close is it to the "Telling Television Stories" chapter in TV & Am Cult? Fri Aug 28 18:49:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell I've got a copy, so no worries -- thanks! Fri Aug 28 18:53:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ Absolutely! Fri Aug 28 20:26:32 +0000 2009 Mac geek cred: my pre-ordered copy of Snow Leopard just arrived via FedEx. Now the dilemma: finish the syllabus, or stop & upgrade? Fri Aug 28 20:38:54 +0000 2009 Hey, does Tweetie not allow you to block someone directly from the app? Do I really have to log in on the web to block a spammer? Fri Aug 28 20:41:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Am backed up out the wazoo. Do need to check on application compatibility, though... Fri Aug 28 20:53:52 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Insanely so. And even the web interface doesn't make it easy \(the link is easy to overlook\) . Fri Aug 28 20:59:27 +0000 2009 Okay, still need to do some scanning and may shift some things around, but the TV Authorship class is close to ready: http://bit.ly/QEjau Fri Aug 28 21:04:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @fuzzycontext Thanks, I'll take a look. I think I've got a couple of good things now, though. Fri Aug 28 21:24:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @johnmjones Wait, what? That's... unexpected. Fri Aug 28 21:24:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @ErikaJL Two essays from the same issue of VLT \(58\.1\) , in fact: @jmittell's and @mznewman's, both of which are perfect for the class. Fri Aug 28 22:12:54 +0000 2009 Okay, all backed up; have checked for app compatibility & updated a couple. Am about to begin upgrade process. Wish me luck. Fri Aug 28 22:13:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @ErikaJL It's called Television Authorship; http://bit.ly/10UI12 Fri Aug 28 22:31:19 +0000 2009 Okay, first load & restart seems good; msg says 33 mins to go. Fri Aug 28 22:32:43 +0000 2009 Fan running like crazy, but otherwise seems okay. 14 mins to go. Fri Aug 28 22:42:55 +0000 2009 Post-installation restart. Fingers crossed. Fri Aug 28 22:53:53 +0000 2009 Hmm. "Where is System Events?" http://yfrog.com/2ajo0j Fri Aug 28 22:59:15 +0000 2009 I hit "cancel." Mac appears to have shrugged & said whatevs. Fri Aug 28 23:06:25 +0000 2009 A bit of syncing weirdness \(keychain issue on a remote computer, I think\) but all seems okay. Fri Aug 28 23:18:46 +0000 2009 But OS9 called & wants its keyboard viewer icon back. http://yfrog.com/27pvkp Fri Aug 28 23:20:20 +0000 2009 Okay, wow - Exchange 2007 support is INSANELY easy: enter email address & password, & Mail autodiscovers. And that's from home! Fri Aug 28 23:24:10 +0000 2009 Hmm. At some point I'd synced contacts on my Mac to the Exchange server so now they're in both places. But now can sort work & life. Fri Aug 28 23:29:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell That would be fantastic - let's chat! Sat Aug 29 06:00:51 +0000 2009 Snow Leopard installation 1 went far easier than 2 and 3 is now messing with me bigtime. Guess I got cocky & Apple's showing me who's boss. Sun Aug 30 04:06:38 +0000 2009 Most serious Snow Leopard incompatibility so far: Quicksilver. I mostly use it for keystroke program/file launching, but boy, do I miss it. Sun Aug 30 05:45:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @bantic Yeah, if I were any more reliant on it, I'd absolutely have waited. The good news is it sorta half works but preferences are borked. Sun Aug 30 07:02:26 +0000 2009 Wow- lots of responses while I slept! RT @josh_braun The Internetz have grown a solution to the Quicksilver problem http://bit.ly/LV8Mr Sun Aug 30 16:35:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Probs with some plugins, plus prefs don't work and QS gets attached to the first space you launch it in. But b54 is ok for now Sun Aug 30 16:49:46 +0000 2009 Just so it's been said: publishing a 260+ page manuscript in readable form online takes a LOT of time. Must find better system. Sun Aug 30 21:41:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline As a prof of mine once said "disabled" is the one marginalized group that all of us who live long enough will eventually join. Sun Aug 30 23:35:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @captain_primate I'm having no trouble at all with the 3.0 beta of 1Password. Mon Aug 31 14:23:07 +0000 2009
docs.title: 2009 08 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @midwinter_ I got mine a couple of weeks ago; took it to Norway with me. Super convenient! Tue Sep 01 05:25:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ I'd pre-ordered from Amazon - I can't imagine it wouldn't be shipping from Kensington! Tue Sep 01 15:02:18 +0000 2009 The air here is filled with fine ash - 30 miles from the fires. Tue Sep 01 15:07:21 +0000 2009 Just asked by dean's office whether a post on a serious academic blog should be included among faculty publications. I'm so proud. #headway Tue Sep 01 20:52:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @TimMcDonaldPhD By my estimation \(a rough one fer sure\) , one primarily focused on the field or other professional concerns of the author \(s\) . Tue Sep 01 21:06:35 +0000 2009 @alechosterman My dean doesn't; I was presented with a specific case that happened to be a serious academic blog. Tue Sep 01 22:30:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Sure, though the question here was about a cv-level inclusion of a specific blog post as comparable to an article. Tue Sep 01 22:30:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I hope you & all the other east coasters will forgive me if I say I don't want to hear about it? [1/2] Wed Sep 02 02:11:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Forecast has been only 3 more days over 100 for 8 days now. Was 109 on Saturday; a mere 100 today. With a light rain of ash. Wed Sep 02 02:14:10 +0000 2009 Wow, that sounded pissy. Wasn't going for pissy, but for "la la la, I can't hear you!" \(I blame the heat & smoke for my irritableness\.\) Wed Sep 02 02:19:10 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare Don't worry about me - we're 20 miles from the nearest fire - but the smoke has permeated the area & apparently made me whiny. :) Wed Sep 02 03:49:49 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry Hey, you write about movies, they sell machines on which you can watch movies. What's not to love? Thu Sep 03 01:47:54 +0000 2009 Looked up thinking, how long until noon meeting? Answer: 24 minutes. And I was still at home in pajamas. Showered & out the door in 13 mins. Thu Sep 03 19:11:57 +0000 2009 Which is to say, I rock. Thu Sep 03 19:12:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @veek Hey, cool! I'd absolutely dig it... Fri Sep 04 00:54:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Me! http://panelpicker.sxsw.com/ideas/view/2890 Fri Sep 04 14:41:16 +0000 2009 SXSW PanelPicker voting closes today; please vote for Flow and MediaCommons, "Hacking the Ivory Tower" http://bit.ly/2RDfjD Fri Sep 04 14:43:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie and @briancroxall I stayed there last time it was in Philly, and it was great. The only downside was having to carry a coat around. Fri Sep 04 20:28:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @wpwend42 Ooh, I should have sent you an invitation - we'd both get extra space free. Sat Sep 05 00:37:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @wpwend42 Done! Sat Sep 05 14:59:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @sramsay I've been using Tweetie, which has been behaving fine in SL. Sat Sep 05 15:31:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Coming right up! Sat Sep 05 16:59:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @kintopp Can't confirm that WPMU is affected, but can tell you that someone tried \(and failed\) to get into my WPMU 2.8.4 admin user account. Sat Sep 05 18:33:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @jasonrhody In the spirit of belt&suspenders, I use Blackblaze and Dropbox: Bb for automatic remote backup; Db for syncing across machines. Sun Sep 06 19:35:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @jasonrhody If you want to test out Dropbox, let me know and I'll send you a referral. 2GB acct free, plus a little extra space for the ref. Sun Sep 06 19:36:16 +0000 2009 Replying to @jasonrhody Sent invite to your gmail. Let me know if you have questions! Sun Sep 06 20:08:03 +0000 2009 New blog post: Ick http://bit.ly/JHQ5O Mon Sep 07 15:21:32 +0000 2009 After a sleepless night spent coughing in LA, I coughed my way across the country and now hope to stop coughing long enough to sleep in NYC. Thu Sep 10 03:46:36 +0000 2009 Which reminds me: dear fellow passengers on CO50 today: I swear it's a bronchial infection, not H1N1. You can all relax. Thu Sep 10 03:47:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Better than I expected, thanks! Spent the morning in a series of mtgs at NYU & was sorry not to have time to pop in & say hi. Thu Sep 10 18:03:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Hey, cool! How late do you think you'll be there? I've got a 6pm dinner, but if we're done early enough I'd love to swing by. Thu Sep 10 19:38:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench don't know if you're still at the bar - just wrapping up dinner in union sq, so would be abt 30 mins... Fri Sep 11 00:24:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench have a great one. since my last post I figured out I'm getting sicker, so I'm going to go crash. will try to catch you tmw. Fri Sep 11 00:37:50 +0000 2009 Ugh. Sick. And feeling pretty whiny about it, actually. Fri Sep 11 01:58:15 +0000 2009 Replying to @koralesky Thank you! I could certainly use it. Fri Sep 11 11:31:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Hope you guys had a great time last night. I collapsed & am feeling even rougher this am. Here's hoping for recovery today... Fri Sep 11 11:33:24 +0000 2009 Day 1 of MLA Program Comm meeting was fabulous \(if with much coughing from me\) . Aside from wifi, anything you'd like to see at future conv? Fri Sep 11 11:55:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave You'll be happy to know that formal panels at night are ending! \(And everybody expects the interview aspect is on the decline\) Fri Sep 11 12:10:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Video is a great idea, but I can imagine that with that many panels, it would be $$$. Maybe we shd start grassroots flip mvmt. Fri Sep 11 12:13:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @amndw2 Great idea! I hope there'll be more of that forthcoming. Fri Sep 11 13:17:53 +0000 2009 Shameless self-promotion: I'm in Humanities Magazine this month, being questioned impertinently: http://bit.ly/fMXru Fri Sep 11 13:29:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Oh, no! I'm so sorry to hear you're sick, too. Probably just as well I didn't make it; we would have just wheezed together. Fri Sep 11 13:30:29 +0000 2009 WTF, AT&T? I've got 5 bars on 3G and all my calls are failing! Fri Sep 11 16:24:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @triproftri and @briancroxall Lots of cool stuff coming - very exciting conversation today. Yay for a 21st century MLA! Fri Sep 11 20:42:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @nickmirzoeff Me too! Thanks for the visit, and I'll look forward to keeping you posted on our progress. Sat Sep 12 02:34:54 +0000 2009 At the point of seriously hoping that my cough has turned into acute bronchitis and not pneumonia. :( Sun Sep 13 01:02:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @alechosterman Got appt w/doc for Tues afternoon, but only by freaking out on the phone. Thank goodness we don't have socialized medicine! Sun Sep 13 01:10:29 +0000 2009 Also starting to wonder whether it's irresponsible of me to try to teach Monday. It's not H1N1, but should I still keep my germs to myself? Sun Sep 13 01:12:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I hope to spend tomorrow that way. And may whether I want to or not. Sun Sep 13 01:40:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde energy level is near zero. I'm going to spend today pondering alternatives from a prone position. Sun Sep 13 15:03:21 +0000 2009 Okay, still with the cough and the shortness of breath, and now low-grade fever and abdominal cramps? I guess it's time for urgent care. Sun Sep 13 18:24:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I've got a call in; waiting for a reply. Thank goodness we don't have to deal with all that rationing that you guys put up with. :P Sun Sep 13 18:52:30 +0000 2009 On-call dr says go to urgent care/er. Now to figure out which one will actually take my insurance. *sigh* Sun Sep 13 19:03:48 +0000 2009 I think it's me & one other dude in the urgent care. Slightly creepy it's so quiet. Through initial screening & waiting on the doc. Sun Sep 13 19:39:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I'll only tweet long enough so that someone knows I'm there. Sun Sep 13 19:51:23 +0000 2009 And I'm out. Xray shows no pneumonia, but acute bronchitis. And PA thinks I may have H1N1 too. Nice. Scrip for zpak & codeine. Back to bed. Sun Sep 13 20:11:05 +0000 2009 PS: I'll begin my official apologies to everyone I've come in contact with over the last three days shortly. Except whoever gave me this. Sun Sep 13 20:12:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde No kidding. Though there's a small part of me that's thinking, well, at least I'll have it over with... Sun Sep 13 21:07:39 +0000 2009 FWIW, if this is H1N1, it sucks. I never run a fever - my temp is usually low if anything - and I'm now over 100. I hate this. Sun Sep 13 23:05:55 +0000 2009 And am sorry to be whining to you poor people, but... well, here I am, and there you are. Sun Sep 13 23:06:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym Ugh, that's awful. Okay, I'm waving the white flag - serves as a convenient handkerchief besides! Here's hoping I'm up soon. Sun Sep 13 23:27:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley Perversely, that is precisely what I'm hoping for at this stage. Wish me luck! Mon Sep 14 00:13:27 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston Should know by Thurs at the latest, sooner if it's +. Have also been told I'll be contacted by "federal officials" if it's +. Mon Sep 14 00:35:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston So unless they drag me off to the camps, I'll let you all know as soon as I do. Mon Sep 14 00:35:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston Indeed! Net-based instruction, here I come. Mon Sep 14 00:41:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @nickmirzoeff Yes, the deadline's tomorrow. I think the app itself has gone in; your ltr can follow. No problem! Mon Sep 14 15:56:53 +0000 2009 Sickbed update: sweated out the fever overnight; now back to my usual 98.2. Breathing better. Makes me think it's just bronchitis after all. Mon Sep 14 16:24:17 +0000 2009 Go, zpack, go! Mon Sep 14 16:24:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos I'm hoping so, too - though I've got Skylight down for next Tuesday. Am I wrong? Mon Sep 14 17:00:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Don't get me started on this. I have an essay coming out in a volume on teaching w/tech that introduces "weblogs" to lit profs Mon Sep 14 19:36:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Wrote the essay, what, four years ago? Based on a class taught three years before that? Mon Sep 14 19:37:27 +0000 2009 About to begin my first distance-ed experience - though not the way I might have expected it would happen. Mon Sep 14 20:10:59 +0000 2009 Classes seem to have gone well. So grateful to my colleagues for all their help yesterday & today. Mon Sep 14 23:42:19 +0000 2009 Gee, NBC4 is reporting that Jay Leno's first show was a "rousing success." I'm stunned. Thanks for the hard-hitting critical take. Tue Sep 15 01:15:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare Ah, it's NBC4 LA, conveniently located in Burbank. In the same complex as... hey... Tue Sep 15 01:53:13 +0000 2009 Hey, lazytwitter: I need a WP plugin that will allow me to see comments by user, like the user page allows you to see posts by user. Ideas? Tue Sep 15 04:28:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @inactinique Wow - that *is* cool! #zotero Tue Sep 15 07:19:32 +0000 2009 @zotero 2b7 Mac is gorgeous- but a sync error: Component returned failure code: 0x804b000a \(NS\_ERROR\_MALFORMED\_URI\) [nsIXMLHttpRequest.open] Tue Sep 15 16:22:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @inactinique Aha! Thank you! Tue Sep 15 17:50:41 +0000 2009 @inactinique Also, the update deleted my WebDAV settings for attachment backup; had to reinstate those. #zotero Tue Sep 15 17:56:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @inactinique Interesting - I wonder why my WebDAV settings disappeared. Anyhow, all seems to be working now. Tue Sep 15 18:31:08 +0000 2009 Good grief, but I feel terrible. I keep feeling like I'm getting over this thing, but every time I think I'm out, it pulls me back in. Wed Sep 16 01:25:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Thanks so much; I could use it! Wed Sep 16 03:09:13 +0000 2009 Day 2 of teaching via Sakai's chatroom client. My students are together in our classroom, and so it's like peeking through a keyhole for me. Wed Sep 16 20:24:38 +0000 2009 Which is to say, not entirely satisfying, and yet I'm grateful to get any glimpse of what's going on I can. Wed Sep 16 20:25:05 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Heh. The fever's gone, though the rest of the symptoms are lingering. I'm mostly at home out of a desire not to infect everyone... Wed Sep 16 20:59:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @emilynussbaum Super cool. I'll look forward to reading more. Wed Sep 16 21:08:36 +0000 2009 Wow - awesome line from my class's online discussion: "the entire history of authorship is about fan fiction." Wed Sep 16 22:48:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde It's an updating of the Roland Barthes line: Every text is a tissue of quotations. The specific ref: Chauc/Shakes Troilus&Cressida. Wed Sep 16 22:53:44 +0000 2009 ~@plragde But so many other possibilities. I love supplanting Bloom's anxiety of influence with fan fiction... Wed Sep 16 22:55:09 +0000 2009 ~@plragde I'm less sure there's that great a divide between borrowing/quotation and other forms of appropriation... Wed Sep 16 23:03:46 +0000 2009 ~@plragde A Thousand Acres/Clueless are exactly the kind of thing I'm trying to get at. There's a deep relationship btw Clueless & fanfic... Wed Sep 16 23:05:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde But that's a pretty fine distinction. The point is that the "author" is no more original than the writer of fanfic... [1/2] Wed Sep 16 23:30:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde All authorship is borrowing, in some form or another. [2/2] Wed Sep 16 23:30:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym That's fantastic -- I hadn't heard that. Thanks for passing it on! Wed Sep 16 23:35:26 +0000 2009 @kfitz Michael Chabon has said all his writing is fan fiction. Another reason to love him. \(via @nancybaym\) Wed Sep 16 23:35:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @mikejolk That's just plain awesome. Thu Sep 17 01:05:02 +0000 2009 re fanfic: http://bit.ly/15O0Ad Next Tarantino Movie Homage To Beloved Tarantino Movies Of Director's Youth \(via @mikejolk\) Thu Sep 17 01:06:04 +0000 2009 ~@annehelen methodology = nil; have you read the description of my blog? These lists are just link farms trying to produce google juice... Thu Sep 17 16:51:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @nickmirzoeff Thank you so much! I'm increasingly excited about the possibilities... Thu Sep 17 16:52:03 +0000 2009 H1N1 results are back - negative. So we're back to bronchitis, which is not getting better. I feel, in fact, like I cracked a rib coughing. Sat Sep 19 18:46:06 +0000 2009 And not to whine, but: all this, and no freaking immunity to show for it? Sat Sep 19 18:46:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Yup. Everything hurts, in one very specific spot. Sat Sep 19 18:51:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @wpwend42 Glad it's working for you. I absolutely love it - the one service I cannot do without. #dropbox Sat Sep 19 19:11:27 +0000 2009 The one on Hollywood Blvd? Sounds good! Sat Sep 19 21:56:23 +0000 2009 Sheesh. Nothing to see here; moving on. Sat Sep 19 21:58:24 +0000 2009 New blog post: The Flu and You http://bit.ly/ruP1L Sat Sep 19 22:21:14 +0000 2009 Texas Tech needs a new chin strap contractor. I'm just saying. Sun Sep 20 03:35:17 +0000 2009 @nowviskie I send recovery & immunity wishes your way, as one who hasn't recovered or cashed in on the immunity. :( Sun Sep 20 18:40:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie No, no - what's the internet for, if not whining? Just sending you wishes for a speedy recovery, and wishing I'd gotten immunity! Sun Sep 20 19:00:51 +0000 2009 New blog post: Teaching When \(You Think\) You Have the Flu http://bit.ly/17nS4q Sun Sep 20 21:14:15 +0000 2009 Replying to @brettbobley Thanks! It was an awful lot of fun & a great audience to get those ideas in front of. And my answers have been called "cheeky"! Mon Sep 21 14:15:19 +0000 2009 @CapitolClio Somebody did -- and it worked! Mon Sep 21 14:51:56 +0000 2009 Home from a return trip to the urgent care. Bruised rib is excruciating. Got steroids plus more codeine to STOP THE COUGH. Wish me luck. Tue Sep 22 03:59:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym They did an xray so at least I know it's not cracked, but I think the treatment is the same: wait six weeks. :( Tue Sep 22 04:13:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @kwazana Yup. It's been pretty awful. Hopefully on the mend, though! Tue Sep 22 04:55:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I think that's where it all started - irritation from the smoke led to bronchial infection, etc. etc. Tue Sep 22 14:33:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Laudanum would be just the thing, as I'm feeling mightly consumptive... Tue Sep 22 17:56:20 +0000 2009 In Los Feliz for dinner and the end of Infinite Summer at Skylight Books. #infsum Wed Sep 23 00:54:15 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Are we going to be 3? and inside or out? I'll get us a table. Wed Sep 23 01:06:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos okay, inside. see you soon! Wed Sep 23 01:12:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos cool beans. I'm here. Wed Sep 23 01:19:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @xina and @mariabustillos I heard the squealing tires and looked back to you see jump out of the way - awful! Wed Sep 23 14:18:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos A lovely and memorable night - thanks so much! Wed Sep 23 14:19:12 +0000 2009 RT @performaddict: Yes Men's Andy Bichlbaum arrested after fake NY Post \(We're Screwed\! headline\) and SurvivaBall stunt. http://bit.ly/NcLr4 Wed Sep 23 14:22:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @CatinStack hahahahahahahaha! Wed Sep 23 14:23:39 +0000 2009 New blog post: Things I Love About Things http://bit.ly/3oxXwi Wed Sep 23 14:58:11 +0000 2009 Fulbright referee instructions: "At several stages of the application process, reviewers only have access to the Online Application...[1/2] Thu Sep 24 22:15:26 +0000 2009 ...and electronically submitted materials, which should be signed by the referee across the back flap." I think my head just exploded. [2/2] Thu Sep 24 22:15:49 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell Where do I put the stamp? Thu Sep 24 22:25:16 +0000 2009 Replying to @twel Is all I'm sayin'. Thu Sep 24 22:37:29 +0000 2009 Testing out a new Twitter tool. Nothing to see here, move along. Fri Sep 25 19:50:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @skylightbooks My pleasure - thanks for putting on such a great event! Fri Sep 25 21:20:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @billiehara Man, you really do have the worst travel luck. Take care of yourself! Sat Sep 26 02:55:46 +0000 2009 New blog post: Something's... Not... Right... http://bit.ly/3k4I5 Sun Sep 27 16:43:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ First round of account invites should be going out in three days. I am trying not to salivate. Sun Sep 27 20:54:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ According to Mashable, 100K invites on the 30th... http://bit.ly/2FJVjF Sun Sep 27 21:13:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ I hope so! I'll look forward to finding out. Expect to see me here begging if I didn't make the first cut. Sun Sep 27 21:57:37 +0000 2009 New blog post: Planned Obsolescence: Now Online http://bit.ly/HFN4W Mon Sep 28 13:36:04 +0000 2009 Care about the future of scholarly publishing? Planned Obsolescence is live online for open peer review - please RT! http://bit.ly/zjXhV Mon Sep 28 14:02:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @Monosyllabicism How funny! It was a great \(if mildly surreal\) event. Hope all's well with you... Mon Sep 28 14:11:30 +0000 2009 Wow. I haven't had a migraine in two years, and I've got serious migraine-aura this morning. Stress much? So glad I have one last Maxalt... Mon Sep 28 14:30:04 +0000 2009 Going back to bed until I can see again. Back to Planned Obsolescence shortly, I hope. Mon Sep 28 14:43:52 +0000 2009 Can mostly see out of both eyes again, though with the Maxalt it kinda feels like I'm seeing out of someone else's eyes. But it's a start! Mon Sep 28 15:29:49 +0000 2009 You can follow the discussion on Planned Obsolescence via the comments RSS feed: http://bit.ly/DZwlT Mon Sep 28 18:50:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @TheRepoRat Thanks so much! This morning has been nuts, but I'm looking forward to reading your comments & continuing the conversation... Mon Sep 28 19:25:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Hey, I forgot to tell you about that at dinner! Thanks for the reminder, @mattbucher! Mon Sep 28 19:44:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos It was the perfect epigraph for the project - how could I not use it? Mon Sep 28 19:45:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @james3neal Thanks so much! We're very excited, needless to say. Mon Sep 28 20:00:22 +0000 2009 New blog post: A Very Brief Note to Tenured Radical http://bit.ly/ufKaN Tue Sep 29 14:38:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Or, is there is a tuition benefit, why it should be universally given to faculty rather than only to those with children. Tue Sep 29 14:54:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave \(And therefore why it shouldn't be off\-limits in times of budget cuts\.\) Tue Sep 29 14:58:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare But this isn't about universal child education; it's about a differential benefit - a huge difference! Tue Sep 29 15:43:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Thank you - for the comments and for this! Tue Sep 29 16:37:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Yay! Tue Sep 29 18:24:22 +0000 2009 Good grief, it's delightful outside. How long has it been since I've been able to say that? Tue Sep 29 19:01:34 +0000 2009 Replying to @TheRepoRat Thanks so much for taking the time! Still playing catch-up today, but look forward to continuing the conv. Will def. follow up. Tue Sep 29 20:47:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @jwolman Me neither. But it's only 7.19 am Google time, so there's still hope. I hope. Wed Sep 30 14:20:10 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym I'll be there! -- #ir10 roll call: Who's going to be there? RT! Wed Sep 30 14:21:15 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Completely made my day yesterday! #dropbox Wed Sep 30 14:29:08 +0000 2009 So, it's 8.30am on the west coast. Anybody gotten a Wave invite yet? \(More to the point: to whom should I direct my begging?\) Wed Sep 30 15:35:00 +0000 2009 New blog post: Digital Campus http://bit.ly/EeVmS Wed Sep 30 16:10:40 +0000 2009 Just finished listening to @digitalcampus - thanks to @amandafrench for making Planned Obsolescence a pick of the week! http://bit.ly/37LV7P Wed Sep 30 16:57:00 +0000 2009 Thanks to @inactinique for the Brizzly invite. The first 10 people to use this code can try it, too. http://bit.ly/t2SzS Wed Sep 30 18:35:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @inactinique I haven't heard rumors of any floating around out there yet, but I'm hoping soon! Wed Sep 30 18:39:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ If not us, then whom? Whom, I ask you? Wed Sep 30 19:09:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym I think you should be the most popular girl on Twitter anyway. & I'm not just sucking up b/c you might someday have invites. :) Wed Sep 30 20:07:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @dancohen You & me both! I wonder if the same culprit gave it to us? \(And I hope you recover more quickly than I have; it's been a month\.\) Wed Sep 30 23:24:10 +0000 2009 New blog post: Planned Obsolescence Community Blog http://bit.ly/Ek7iU Wed Sep 30 23:54:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie Hmm. @profhacker and viruses, you say? could be something quite sinister going on... docs.text: Replying to @thfondak Have one coming, thanks! Like the concept \(many social media streams in one place\) ; not sure I like the execution. Fri Oct 02 18:22:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @BryanAlexander Thanks for the RT. I hope all's well with you! Fri Oct 02 22:36:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @billiehara Sadly, yes. But this I will promise you: the first year is the worst. It gets better. Much better. Why did I set my big class up this way? #thinkahead Sat Oct 03 15:47:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym Oh, right. That's it! Sat Oct 03 15:52:45 +0000 2009 By the by, I'm really excited about the way discussion is building on Planned Obsolescence; come by and join in! http://bit.ly/zjXhV Sat Oct 03 16:01:00 +0000 2009 Ugh. Doc gave me an antibiotic meant to be taken on an empty stomach, but it makes me seriously nauseated. Cure may be worse than disease. Sat Oct 03 16:43:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @d_kompare Have fun! I'll look forward to hearing your thoughts when you get a chance. Sat Oct 03 16:44:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I've got 33 5-page scene analyses to comment on, and no TA. Topic is fun, tho, even if commenting isn't. Just wish I had more time. Sat Oct 03 17:11:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @kwazana This is a bit too intense for ginger, I'm afraid, but happily it's short-lived; I'm okay about half an hour after I eat. Sat Oct 03 21:11:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @kwazana Exactly \(though diet Coke, of course\) ! Sat Oct 03 21:27:01 +0000 2009 ARGH! I apparently just fried one of my external hard drives, unplugging it without realizing it was mounted. Now it won't mount at all... Sat Oct 03 22:23:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench My understanding is that the team is \(a\) in Australia, and \(b\) pushing the invites out one at a time, by hand. Soon, I hope... Sun Oct 04 16:38:16 +0000 2009 New tack with antibiotic today: 1 saltine before, drown capsule with water, 2 saltines after. Screw "empty": I will not napalm my stomach. RT @mkgold: Late on these, but congrats to @ProfHacker team on the @chronicle article and to @chutry on the First Monday piece. Unfortunately, that's as far down as I can get it right now. But it nonetheless feels better... Tue Oct 06 23:47:44 +0000 2009 Looking forward to DH2010: first time I'll have been back to King's College since the Most Bizarre Conference in the History of Conferences. Wed Oct 07 04:25:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Heh heh heh. Part 2 of Int'l Pynchon Week in 1998. Involved impostors, breaking and entering, and embezzlement. Good times. Wed Oct 07 04:38:49 +0000 2009 The Most Bizarre Conference in the History of Conferences: http://bit.ly/35BilY I shit you not. Wed Oct 07 04:45:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I was indeed there. Alas, that post is about as close to memoir as I think there is \(and it was four months after the fact\) . Wed Oct 07 14:16:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I think everyone was a bit dumbfounded - how could someone actually manage to pull that off? Wed Oct 07 14:17:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde That would be interesting: The Professor and the Madman as a Jekyll/Hyde kinda deal. Plus Pynchon. Wed Oct 07 14:40:57 +0000 2009 At ONT on my way to #ir10. Wed Oct 07 22:59:53 +0000 2009 Landed at IAH & pulled up to gate only to be told there'd been a security breach in the airport & we could be stuck for hours. Took 10 mins. Thu Oct 08 05:16:44 +0000 2009 A little breakfast before heading to #ir10. Looking forward to the conf \(and happy to be out of the ashtray\) . Thu Oct 08 14:35:48 +0000 2009 Did we break Twitter? I'm curious how it would work for collaborative conference notetaking/backchannel. Thu Oct 08 20:46:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @janelle_ward My invites are gone, alas - and not one of my invitees has actually been granted access yet. :( I keep hoping, though... Thu Oct 08 20:52:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @Sarah_M_Ford Ping me when you do get set up. Thu Oct 08 21:03:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @janelle_ward Indeed! I keep hoping they'll step up the invites; it's only going to be useful once the folks I want to wave at are on. Thu Oct 08 23:51:38 +0000 2009 Grr. Keep forgetting to add hashtag. #ir10 Thu Oct 08 23:52:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @acavender Yay! Just added you to a wave from last week, so you can grab some other contacts. Fri Oct 09 15:21:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid Seems to me that situation requires even better karma! Do I need to add them to the catalog? Fri Oct 09 21:34:41 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Not right this second. But I will be again in about 25 minutes. Fri Oct 09 21:35:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @Sarah_M_Ford Done. Also sent you a ping. I'm in as kfitz47 at gmail, but you might try kfitz47 at googlewave if that doesn't work. I haven't taken my jacket off all day, and my feet are numb thru wool socks. Fri Oct 09 21:56:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Actually, yes - it's been a great conference. \(And the reception will be warming, too, when I can get hands on a glass of wine\.\) Fri Oct 09 22:07:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Hmph. I wonder if it's a post-SL issue. I hope that there's an update before long; B56a7 is still a bit unstable for me. Fri Oct 09 22:18:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Just found it! Fri Oct 09 22:19:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Rats, I was hoping someone might take the project over. \(What I found was the pdf, which has the same iCal info, alas\.\) Fri Oct 09 22:28:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @cscannella Whew. Fri Oct 09 22:51:50 +0000 2009 You know what I hate? Wintergreen. Sat Oct 10 04:21:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @zephoria Aaaauugh! Sat Oct 10 05:31:06 +0000 2009 good morning #ir10! come hear about writing at 8:30 - @Sarah_M_Ford on LJ vs. FB, me on peer review, and Wisktrom & Olin-Scheller on fanfic! Have repeatedly uninstalled and reinstalled; still crashes. Makes me srsly unhappy. Sat Oct 10 16:47:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @cliotropic Did you switch to something else? \(Google QSB, LaunchBar, etc?\) Sat Oct 10 16:52:12 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid LOVE IT. Has entirely changed my relationship to taking the computer with me; it's practically weightless & super fast. Sat Oct 10 17:19:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas and @sivavaid Yes: when I bought the weight diff was huge; it's less now. Also, the new MBP battery is better. But I still love it. Had numb fingertips! #ir10 Sat Oct 10 18:12:05 +0000 2009 Replying to @snurb_dot_info But it wasn't bashing; it was pointing out the contradiction between "we're just here for you!" and "you do the work." #ir10 Sat Oct 10 18:14:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @snurb_dot_info Fair enough; I can see that. I like it so far! #tweetie2 Sun Oct 11 19:14:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @be_b Thank you so much -- it was a great discussion, and I hope we can do it again sometime soon. [2/2] Fri Oct 23 18:06:58 +0000 2009 Just had the very creepy experience of hearing someone talking right outside my hotel room window. I'm on the 11th floor. Haven't played with digress.it tho so can't compare. Wed Oct 28 20:09:17 +0000 2009 Following @s2ceball, I'm building my promotion dossier online. Thu Oct 29 02:11:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @profsyn Eep! I didn't ask & should have. They've been great with previous online stuff; I suspect they'll say yes, but I should be polite! Thu Oct 29 02:29:16 +0000 2009 Somebody tell me to stop working and go watch Top Chef. Thu Oct 29 04:13:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @digitalhumanist If you insist! :) Thu Oct 29 04:19:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench That just completely made my day. Thu Oct 29 04:55:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Very cool - I'd love to see what you've got! Thu Oct 29 04:56:52 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos Done and done! It's nice to know, but I'm so sorry you had to find that out! Thu Oct 29 17:25:03 +0000 2009 Okay - my iMac was just hijacked by Front Row. I hadn't invoked it, and I could NOT get out of it. WTF? Thu Oct 29 23:04:07 +0000 2009 Working on page 4 of the cv. The thing's due tomorrow. The good news is that the stuff I can/need to document now stops in a page or two. Thu Oct 29 23:28:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp I think it's cmd-esc; I hit it trying to cmd-~ to another document, and because FR is so slow in SL, it seemed unprovoked. But yeah. Thu Oct 29 23:53:33 +0000 2009 So what day is it? And what time am I supposed to beg for someone to tell me to stop working and go watch television? \(And what, exactly?\) Fri Oct 30 01:45:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @captain_primate So now, I guess? Yay! Fri Oct 30 01:53:51 +0000 2009 So apparently the iMac has decided to make me quit working by completely locking up. This is what I get for failure to immediately comply. Fri Oct 30 02:29:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @kwazana Dude, I'm teaching The Wire; there's always something good on! Fri Oct 30 04:05:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Indeed. And I don't get the value added in "following" a list, especially when I'm already following the folks on it. It'll fill with stuff for the internal dossier \(teaching, etc\) , but now...? Fri Oct 30 23:35:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @j_l_r Thanks! I'm pretty pleased with it, on the whole, and look forward to populating it with the rest of my info between now and January. Sat Oct 31 00:09:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell Maybe I'll start a click-to-publish dossier service. Wordpress.com for dossiers. Wow. That's actually not a bad idea. Sat Oct 31 00:11:13 +0000 2009 ~@jmittell has inspired me to imagine a click-to-publish dossier service, like Wordpress.com or MediaCommons for dossiers. I'm doing it. Sat Oct 31 00:13:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell That's a fantastic idea. My head is swimming. Time to go domain name shopping. Sat Oct 31 00:14:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell You got it. It's a SoCal embarrassment of riches this spring... It would be awesome if someday I could find these things less painful to produce. Sat Oct 31 20:15:27 +0000 2009
docs.title: 2009 10 01 tweets

docs.text: I need a WP plugin that lists comments by user on the dashboard, just as the user panel gives you access to posts by user. Any suggestions? Sun Nov 01 22:23:02 +0000 2009 I am precariously close to caught up. This whole 25-hour day thing ROCKS. #yesiposteditfromthewrongaccountthefirsttime Mon Nov 02 00:48:39 +0000 2009 Remembering why I love the first weeks of standard time: got up at 5.30 and am now down to inbox=4 and 4 items to go on today's to-do list. Mon Nov 02 17:11:14 +0000 2009 Looking for folks whose institutions have outsourced faculty email to MSLive/Gmail. Would like to hear up- and downsides of your experience. Mon Nov 02 17:23:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @jbj I'm going to be at MSA in Montreal, too. Mon Nov 02 18:17:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @ddchamberlain Gmail? or other? And by "in-between period" do you mean the actual transfer or having students/fac-staff on diff services? Mon Nov 02 18:19:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Yeah, I think so. *sigh* Mon Nov 02 18:19:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @ddchamberlain Gotcha - that's good to know. I can send you one. It'll take a while to process, but at least it'll be on the way. My jaw dropped when Mail autodiscovered my account from my address after I upgraded to SL. Mon Nov 16 15:04:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @mznewman I've done it - 1.5+ years now. NeoOffice rulez. Tue Nov 17 04:03:38 +0000 2009 Bringing high-end networking to liberal arts colleges! Guess they found something more interesting to write about. Wed Nov 18 02:25:40 +0000 2009 Man, I have a day full of meetings and come home to discover that there's a new DH journal & I'm on its editorial board: http://bit.ly/5GnAy Thu Nov 19 06:39:00 +0000 2009 Which is to say: you rock, @dancohen. Go @dhnow! Fri Nov 20 17:06:16 +0000 2009 Gah! Xmas+MLA plane tickets are going to be $900+, and I've got no institutional support for the trip. You're killing me, Continental! Fri Nov 20 18:48:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Can't work: it's ONT-MSY-PHL. No JetBlue out of ONT anymore, no Virgin anywhere, cheapest SW is just under $900. So I'm just screwed. Fri Nov 20 19:19:12 +0000 2009 Being way less productive and focused working at home today than I need to be; time to head to the coffee house down the block, I think. Fri Nov 20 19:30:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @stephanietomlin Are they lost on the server or on your computer? I had to rearrange things b/c of a hacker... The big one is really too big, but I'm too much of a softie. Fri Nov 20 22:00:31 +0000 2009 @mmwwah About. Freaking. Time. Fri Nov 20 22:10:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym Speaking of #aoir, I find myself unreasonably annoyed by this: http://bit.ly/7sm21b - as though no one's been doing it for 10yrs! Fri Nov 20 22:34:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @alisap27 There's the genius of the digital-only syllabus. What error? 0:-) Fri Nov 20 22:35:21 +0000 2009 So is The Postal Service's "The District Sleeps Alone Tonight" back in rotation? I've heard it twice in two days, in two different places. Fri Nov 20 23:46:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome "We still deliver on Saturday - nyah nyah"? Sat Nov 21 00:12:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome I'm imagining whole new campaigns now. "FedEx: When it absolutely, positively, has to get there. Period." Sat Nov 21 00:51:59 +0000 2009 Two things, the first cool: just got a new Magic Mouse, and it's really as amazing as I thought it would be. Sun Nov 22 01:45:00 +0000 2009 The second creepy: someone on the college network is searching my blog for keywords like married, husband, boyfriend, sex, and so forth. Sun Nov 22 01:46:11 +0000 2009 \(Though at least after coming up empty on "husband" and "boyfriend," they searched "girlfriend\."\) Sun Nov 22 01:47:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Probably something like that, yeah. It just comes as enough of a surprise that I leapt to creepiness. Sun Nov 22 06:52:21 +0000 2009 Made an awesome batch of Tuscan white bean & greens soup yesterday, which I'll be eating all week, until I make the Thanksgiving gumbo. Yum! Sun Nov 22 19:18:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline I started from this http://bit.ly/6RDfCn but added smoked sausage and used heartier greens \(a turnip/mustard/collard mix\) . YUM Sun Nov 22 19:57:09 +0000 2009 New blog post: Planned Obsolescence Updates http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/planned-obsolescence-updates/ Tue Nov 24 14:39:43 +0000 2009 The updates: Planned Obsolescence \(http://bit\.ly/zjXhV\) is now running in CommentPress 3.1, just released by if:book \(http://bit\.ly/5FxyOa\) Tue Nov 24 14:46:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Hear, hear. I absolutely cannot function without #1Password anymore. Here's hoping they add #chrome support soon. Tue Nov 24 23:55:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @1Password Thank you for the reply! No timeframe necessary; I'll be patient, I swear. :) Wed Nov 25 01:08:58 +0000 2009 @unsworth How awful, for everyone. I'm so sorry. Wed Nov 25 05:54:40 +0000 2009 Thankful to have R. here this weekend. Also thankful for the long weekend and the light I begin to glimpse at the end of the tunnel of work. Thu Nov 26 16:44:46 +0000 2009 Gumbo is gumbificating. Happy Thanksgiving, all. Thu Nov 26 23:20:38 +0000 2009 My first external HD was 100MB, and cost something like $200, IIRC. I just bought a 1.5TB drive for $100. Mind, blown. http://bit.ly/7KP2Qd Sun Nov 29 16:25:37 +0000 2009 Have finally finished commenting on the term paper drafts. Please, please, please let this make final grading easier. Mon Nov 30 02:04:31 +0000 2009 RT @openhumanities: Interesting discussion re Open Access versus Open Process http://bit.ly/7t9tcY Mon Nov 30 13:29:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @jbj I'm with you; my students have invariably checked out after Thanksgiving. Mon Nov 30 14:43:13 +0000 2009 OUP Journals: your system keeps rejecting my credit card as expired when I have clearly put in a 2012 expiry date. Did this last year, too. Mon Nov 30 15:17:17 +0000 2009 I just generated a #TweetCloud out of a year of my tweets. Top three words: post, blog, thanks - http://w33.us/3qqa Mon Nov 30 16:23:24 +0000 2009 For better or for worse, that seems about right. Mon Nov 30 16:24:57 +0000 2009 This morning did not begin well. And I am now way more cranky and short-tempered than I ought to be. #hopingisnapoutofit Mon Nov 30 16:45:39 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym You got it. After all, the term paper drafts are done and I have a fun week of teaching ahead. What could be so bad? #stupidemail Mon Nov 30 16:51:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @nancybaym I am as the surface of a calm sea. Nothing can affect my mood unless I choose to let it. #om Mon Nov 30 17:09:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos And you are far too kind! Mon Nov 30 17:33:39 +0000 2009 Love, love, love that Tweetie 2.1 now shows you breaks in the timeline and lets you fill them in. #tweetierocks Mon Nov 30 19:58:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @ryancordell Congratulations!!!
