docs.text: Replying to @emarsh For my $2, absolutely. It's got a ton of new functionality, and "pull down to reload" is just nifty. Tue Dec 01 14:12:04 +0000 2009 New blog post: December! http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/december/ Tue Dec 01 16:04:06 +0000 2009 Oh, brilliant - congratulations! RT @footnotesrising: congrats to @digitalhumanist for new job at @UMD_MITH! http://bit.ly/90VLy5 Tue Dec 01 16:07:54 +0000 2009 The end of an era: the last releases from @RCCSreviews. Thanks, @davidmsilver, for everything. Tue Dec 01 17:02:54 +0000 2009 The end of an era: the last release from @RCCSreviews. Thanks, @davidmsilver, for everything. /This time with the link! http://bit.ly/6AStu6 Tue Dec 01 17:12:10 +0000 2009 Woo-hoo! RT @brettbobley: Congrats @mkirschenbaum on MLA First Book Prize for Mechanisms. http://bit.ly/6TkNTV Tue Dec 01 21:53:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Indeed! Jobs and prizes for everyone! Tue Dec 01 22:55:43 +0000 2009 RT @mkgold: ...and on this day of celebration, a sadder note: @davidmsilver retires RCCS http://tinyurl.com/y9q3bnj Tue Dec 01 23:20:18 +0000 2009 1.5TB is sure a lot smaller and sexier than it used to be. \(That is, I guess, assuming you find small sexy\.\) http://yfrog.com/1eurjj Wed Dec 02 03:22:05 +0000 2009 Replying to @therefore \(Which makes me want to make an "it's hollow\.\.\. it goes on forever\.\.\. and\.\.\. my god\.\.\. it's full of stars" joke\.\) Wed Dec 02 03:50:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp I am totally backed up out the wazoo, trust me \(two local, two remote\) . It is not possible to be more data paranoid than I am. Wed Dec 02 03:52:54 +0000 2009 Why, yes, I did in fact wake up at 3.30 this morning! Wed Dec 02 12:08:50 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality A little Poi Dog Pondering in the morning can be a good thing - it's certainly upbeat in its total demoralization. Wed Dec 02 15:17:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality They passed through BR every year or so when I was at LSU, so I saw and heard a lot of them then. But can it be 20 yrs? Eek! Wed Dec 02 16:27:41 +0000 2009 Halle-freaking-lujah: Quicksilver update. http://bit.ly/T8846 \(via @lifehacker\) Wed Dec 02 19:41:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @boonebgorges So far, so good. And MASSIVE speed improvements. Wed Dec 02 20:10:20 +0000 2009 Replying to @mmwwah Woo-hoo! Wed Dec 02 23:57:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @boonebgorges Please be sure to tweet that when you do! Thu Dec 03 00:00:39 +0000 2009 Oh! How nice to come home and be reminded by Twitter that there's Top Chef a-waitin' on the DVR! Thu Dec 03 05:03:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @daveheal In this case, no; all I got was a "oooooooooooh Top Chef." Thu Dec 03 05:08:51 +0000 2009 Dear UT Austin graduate admissions: please either allow file uploads for LORs or provide a text entry box larger than three lines high. Thx. Thu Dec 03 16:13:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @annehelen and @crsbecker Sadly, I am not surprised by any of this. Thu Dec 03 16:27:18 +0000 2009 Hey, the MLA volume on Teaching Literature and Language Online is out! Thu Dec 03 17:59:36 +0000 2009 (On a snarkier note: I wrote my article for the volume during summer 2006... [1/2] Thu Dec 03 18:02:52 +0000 2009 ...which might lead one to think that publishing essays about teaching online might be better done online.) [2/2] Thu Dec 03 18:03:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy I hear you. My chapter is on a course I taught in 2004 using this thing called a "weblog." You may have heard of them. Thu Dec 03 19:02:26 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell My assumption is that it's about where the RTs come from; clients that don't support the new style prob produce old style RTs. Thu Dec 03 19:43:41 +0000 2009 Excellent column by Barbara Fister in Library Journal on me and Rupert Murdoch. \(Really\!\) http://bit.ly/50gkaY Thu Dec 03 21:58:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @mmwwah Not much, dude. I'm beside myself! Thu Dec 03 22:22:52 +0000 2009 New blog post: Gee, Time Warner, Thanks for Asking http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/gee-time-warner-thanks-for-asking/ Fri Dec 04 16:20:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @shanakimball Wonderful news -- congratulations, Maria! Fri Dec 04 16:42:14 +0000 2009 RT @shanakimball: Maria Bonn has been named AUL for Publishing at the University of Michigan Library. http://bit.ly/7zedKt Fri Dec 04 16:48:17 +0000 2009 Have taken article draft from 15,500 words to 11,200. Still have to get down to 8000. *sigh* Fri Dec 04 17:49:17 +0000 2009 Excellent week of posts and discussions on In Media Res, focusing on "Books as Screens." http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/imr/ Fri Dec 04 18:16:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @antoniomarcos Thanks so much; very nice to hear! Fri Dec 04 20:03:53 +0000 2009 It is 62 degrees in my third-floor apartment. Have been waiting for heater repairman for 1.5 hours. Wishing I had fingerless gloves. Fri Dec 04 21:18:03 +0000 2009 But I don't *want* to spend another three hours agonizing over which 3000 words can still be cut from this article! Fri Dec 04 21:37:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Somebody told me to disemvowel it - but alas that only hits character rather than word count. Maybe I cut all articles & prepositions? Fri Dec 04 21:55:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Of course I got it. But editing like this makes me literal. Fri Dec 04 22:03:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid I was thinking of an every-third-word strategy. Fri Dec 04 22:03:49 +0000 2009 62 degrees doesn't seem all that cold until you've been sitting in it for 2.5 hours waiting for the heater repair guys. Also I'm a cold wimp Fri Dec 04 22:26:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas WANT. Fri Dec 04 22:35:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @nirak The irony, of course, is that it's 67 outside. Am wearing two sweaters and a scarf, but neither hat nor gloves. Fri Dec 04 22:36:03 +0000 2009 Hey, only 2888 words to go. Fri Dec 04 22:36:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @nirak See: cold wimp. Also, being SoCal, my condo is uninsulated, so outdoor temp very quickly = indoor temp. Lo tonight: 41. Heat, please. Fri Dec 04 22:58:16 +0000 2009 Good news: I have heat. Bad news: broken heat was my fault. Good news: was a quick fix. \(It's been that kind of week\.\) Fri Dec 04 23:40:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Go ahead, punk. Make my day. Sat Dec 05 00:01:56 +0000 2009 Made it down to about 10K words before caving. Will return tomorrow and see if I can find another 2K that can go. Sat Dec 05 00:55:27 +0000 2009 Replying to @wedaman You mean to say the US government is actively promoting the terrorist fist jab? Sat Dec 05 00:56:03 +0000 2009 Have returned home to discover it's finally warm enough inside. Now to turn down the heat to go to sleep... Sat Dec 05 08:40:48 +0000 2009 I wish I could begin to convey the degree to which I do not feel like working today. Sat Dec 05 20:11:43 +0000 2009 Just back from taking the big cat to the emergency vet. The little cat and I are very freaked out, and a whole lot broker. Mon Dec 07 00:40:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @ksmoker Thanks - I'm sure he will be. But it's scary nonetheless! Mon Dec 07 01:22:40 +0000 2009 Just picked up the big cat from the emergency vet; now waiting in parking lot for regular vet to open. Please let web info be correct! Mon Dec 07 15:45:19 +0000 2009 Little cat just figured out big cat still isn't home; went tearing all over the house and then ran up to me meowing her head off. Mon Dec 07 19:02:34 +0000 2009 Posted from wrong account the first time, obvs. Mon Dec 07 19:02:49 +0000 2009 Replying to @mmwwah I'm not sure if she figured that part out, but it definitely hit her that he wasn't back. Mon Dec 07 19:47:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni We're very excited about the panel; more stuff will be coming to that site soon. Mon Dec 07 19:48:43 +0000 2009 Lunch sounds great - I'm pretty clear next week except for Tuesday... Mon Dec 07 19:57:44 +0000 2009 Carry on; nothing to see here. Mon Dec 07 20:01:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Who knew Tweetie had a "reply all" issue? Mon Dec 07 20:01:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos That's amazing - congratulations! Tue Dec 08 01:47:12 +0000 2009 38 degrees out this morning, and this view from my balcony. Leaves in foreground, snow in back, the way I like it. http://yfrog.com/4iixwhj Tue Dec 08 16:46:18 +0000 2009 Hey, the Chrome extensions gallery is also open! https://chrome.google.com/extensions Most desired extensions \(by me\) : @1Password & @zotero. Tue Dec 08 19:26:40 +0000 2009 Hey, folks with Wave invites: I'm using it with my spring classes & will need about 30 total. I've got 14, but maybe save an extra for me? Tue Dec 08 20:18:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @boonebgorges Many thanks! I'll check in with you around January 15. Tue Dec 08 20:23:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ Thanks! I'll check in with you around Jan. 15 & let you know if I'm still in need. Tue Dec 08 20:23:49 +0000 2009 Wow - thanks all for the offers of Wave invites. I'll DM as I need them, probably starting around Jan. 14. Tue Dec 08 22:59:37 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston Classes don't start until the 21st so shd be plenty of time. But I sent someone an invite Mon & she was active 3hrs later! Tue Dec 08 23:06:26 +0000 2009 Picking up the big cat from the vet. Seems he's going to be okay, tho the vet said "he was a very sick kitty!" Tue Dec 08 23:44:03 +0000 2009 WHOA. The Google Groups I belong to just popped up in my Wave contacts. This is getting interesting. Wed Dec 09 00:41:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @billiehara, @captain_primate, @jmcclurken and @billwolff I'm in. See you there. Wed Dec 09 01:16:21 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ and @mmwwah My god, the irony. :) Wed Dec 09 01:28:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @midwinter_ That's my sense as well - I'm mostly wondering if Google is beginning to shut the groups down by starting this migration. Wed Dec 09 01:37:18 +0000 2009 Keep thinking something smells funny in here - something burning? - before remembering that I bought the big cat a new pheromone diffuser. Wed Dec 09 02:05:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Yeah, it's the Feliway. Poor dude has had such a bad couple of days, I figured he could use all the pheromones he could get. Wed Dec 09 02:13:12 +0000 2009 Oh, and thanks again, all, for the offers of Wave invites, but I just got another 25, so I ought to be totally covered now. Wed Dec 09 02:14:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @christateston Weird! I wonder if that'll happen to me - I had a wave with 14 invites left, and then a separate one popped up with 25... Wed Dec 09 02:26:20 +0000 2009 Life as I knew it is over. The big cat now has a pricey prescription canned diet, and the little cat wants it too & is refusing the dry. Wed Dec 09 02:58:38 +0000 2009 So, the choice: lock the little cat up with her dry food twice a day until she eats, or cave and give everybody the pricey canned stuff? Wed Dec 09 03:15:48 +0000 2009 This is what I'm thinking about instead of grading. Wed Dec 09 03:16:00 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Actually, that's brilliant - why didn't I think of that? Wed Dec 09 03:19:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @sgerald A very, very good point. Wed Dec 09 03:19:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni The vet did tell me it was fine to give her the same stuff, but I may test the fake-out routine... Wed Dec 09 03:25:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas Thanks - that's extremely helpful. Does make me wonder how I could have gone this long without knowing any of this... Wed Dec 09 04:24:10 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattthomas This was a full-on blockage, with emergency catheterization. Poor little guy - I wish I'd known! Wed Dec 09 04:39:02 +0000 2009 A prize to whoever can tell me why I've got "Rocky Top" stuck in my head. \(Two prizes to whoever can make it go away\!\) Wed Dec 09 19:27:25 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench, @jcmeloni and @acavender My usual anti-earworm is a chorus of "Lonesome Loser" - drives out the other song but doesn't stick. Wed Dec 09 19:36:58 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmcclurken Why, thank you! I'll look forward to reading your thoughts. Thu Dec 10 05:24:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @mariabustillos and @ksmoker Had the exact same thing happen. Then woke up with Rocky Top again this morning. Am happy to trade 4 Tainted Love. Thu Dec 10 17:53:40 +0000 2009 Okay, I know you people have been talking about this, but can I just say the new FB privacy settings make no freaking sense? Thu Dec 10 18:18:39 +0000 2009 Why am I being given a choice between "old settings" and "everyone" without being told what my old settings were? That's not a choice. Thu Dec 10 18:20:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @stevewhitaker Trust me, I am. But it's still a bizarre pairing of options, isn't it? Thu Dec 10 18:25:21 +0000 2009 Very excited about new UM DH Series and the associated UM/HASTAC Pub Prize \(and honored to be in such great company\) ! http://bit.ly/8Y7tkk Thu Dec 10 18:36:43 +0000 2009 New blog post: UM/HASTAC Publication Prize http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/umhastac-publication-prize/ Thu Dec 10 18:51:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @mmwwah Not my point. The point is the combination of sneakiness and bad pairing of options. Thu Dec 10 19:04:03 +0000 2009 Sitting at the coffee house down the block, trying to force myself to focus on #grading. 7 term papers down; 39 to go. Fri Dec 11 16:42:48 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie I followed @sivavaid's lead yesterday and deauthorized/disallowed/removed as many apps as possible. Still not enough, though. Fri Dec 11 20:05:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline I use BackBlaze; $5/mo or $50/yr for unlimited storage. Works pretty seamlessly, though initial backup can be slow. Fri Dec 11 20:25:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie Exactly what I mean - even after disabling everything possible, I still feel potential overexposure - and yet want some presence. Fri Dec 11 21:33:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline, @jcmeloni and @jasonrhody I am this paranoid: lap/desk sync via Dropbox, plus two local TimeMachine backups and one more remote. Fri Dec 11 22:28:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline I have a paid account, so the vast majority of my data \(pretty much everything except music & movies\) is backed up there, too. Fri Dec 11 22:48:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline & yes, super-paranoid: what if thieves got keys to both my home and office & cleaned me out? What if there were an earthquake? Fri Dec 11 22:50:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @GeorgeOnline \(Recognizing that in that latter case, I'd probably have worse problems to worry about than data loss\.\) Fri Dec 11 22:51:17 +0000 2009 Replying to @billiehara I'm so sorry - just having been through this with one of my cats I know how awful leaving them there is! Fri Dec 11 23:03:33 +0000 2009 14 down, 29 to go \(and 4 yet to be turned in\) . #grading Fri Dec 11 23:06:05 +0000 2009 Online Christmas shopping: nieces & nephews, aunt & uncle, done. Parents, almost done. Sister and grandmother still to go. Sat Dec 12 19:34:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @billiehara I'm so sorry; sending all my best thoughts and wishes. Sat Dec 12 19:48:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @billiehara Billie, I'm so sorry for your loss - thinking of you... Mon Dec 14 01:16:54 +0000 2009 Was really fuzzy-headed this morning, so consumed a lot of caffeine. Now have a thin layer of super-wired on top of a fuzzy-headed center. Mon Dec 14 19:33:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Yes, exactly. And just that productive. Mon Dec 14 19:45:16 +0000 2009 Just discovered that Fluid implements 1Password. Rawk! Tue Dec 15 17:19:27 +0000 2009 On the dual mode of review of Planned Obsolescence, including external reviews by @lisaspiro, available for discussion! http://bit.ly/7dtQ8b Wed Dec 16 19:27:05 +0000 2009 New blog post: Peer Review as Dialogue http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/peer-revew-as-dialogu/ Wed Dec 16 19:50:30 +0000 2009 Hey, cool! // Conversation with Kathleen Fitzpatrick, NITLE Summit Keynote Speaker http://bit.ly/7AqgNc /via @Mikeynitle Thu Dec 17 19:34:52 +0000 2009 Class one? Done! Independent study? Done! Class two? Waiting... on... one... last... paper. 1 hour & 12 minutes before I pull the plug. Thu Dec 17 23:48:27 +0000 2009 Fall semester is done. Now to figure out how to cram ten weeks of work into five. \(With time out for holidays and MLA\.\) Fri Dec 18 15:56:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @kfitz WHOA. Congrats, @scottrettberg, who just got a million euros from HERA for research on electronic literature and creativity! /via @jilltxt Fri Dec 18 19:06:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid I'd argue that it does matter beyond academia, insofar as there are doubtless NYT readers looking at the crap economy... [1/2] Fri Dec 18 19:10:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @sivavaid ...and encouraging their soon-to-graduate kids to go to grad school, mistakenly thinking it provides job security. [2/2] Fri Dec 18 19:11:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Sending good warm thoughts from the land of sunny and 75. \(Where I would actually love to have a bit of weather, frankly\.\) Sat Dec 19 02:03:03 +0000 2009 Replying to @BryanAlexander Congratulations! Very much looking forward to a preview at Pomona in May... Sat Dec 19 15:30:18 +0000 2009 Taking the excuse of feeling mildly puky to spend the day in bed with laptop & books. If it weren't for the puky part this would be awesome. Sat Dec 19 15:33:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @jwolman I got snowed in in DC once, and was stuck for days. Turns out there was only one working plow in the entire district. Sat Dec 19 16:21:44 +0000 2009 Replying to @jwolman This was back in the Marion Barry days; the rest of the plows had been cannibalized for parts to keep that one running. Sat Dec 19 16:23:26 +0000 2009 Icons are randomly disappearing from the dock/app switcher in Snow Leopard. Permissions repair/reboot have done nothing. Grrr. Sat Dec 19 18:24:49 +0000 2009 Double grrr: fixing disappearing icons required getting rid of dock prefs - and that hosed my spaces prefs. Sat Dec 19 18:46:51 +0000 2009 Replying to @mmwwah Yeah, I complain, but it's actually pretty nice - the post-semester freedom to be sick & geeky. Sat Dec 19 18:58:07 +0000 2009 New blog post: Not Exactly the Tip of the Tongue http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/not-exactly-the-tip-of-the-tongue/ Sun Dec 20 17:05:52 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy Haven't taught with Twitter, but got same complaints about blogging until I made explicit that posts were like reading responses... Sun Dec 20 17:55:36 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy i.e., turning "post if you have something interesting to say" into "come up with something to say about this week's material." Sun Dec 20 17:57:07 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy The community aspect of the blog is key - I want students writing for the class, not just for me. It's really all about comments... Sun Dec 20 18:01:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy I see the potential for Twitter to build that community, absolutely, but I hope they can connect that community to their writing. Sun Dec 20 18:06:14 +0000 2009 Warning you now: I'll be seriously pissed if it turns out Brittany Murphy's cardiac arrest was caused by complications from anorexia...[1/2] Sun Dec 20 21:14:40 +0000 2009 She was amazingly cute before the industry convinced her that she had to become a skeleton to be a star. Such a waste in so many ways. [2/2] Sun Dec 20 21:14:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @alechosterman Yes, absolutely: cf. Karen Carpenter. Who, not incidentally, was also 32 when she died. Sun Dec 20 21:36:59 +0000 2009 Replying to @dancohen It's a bad headline for a great column - worth reading if you haven't. \(Tho I'd be just as happy never to hear that name again\.\) Sun Dec 20 23:27:24 +0000 2009 Trying to get convo going again on Planned Obsolescence; will be starting revisions soon, and could use any & all feedback. RT my next? Mon Dec 21 16:08:53 +0000 2009 Need something to read while snowbound? Care about scholarly publishing's future? Join the conv at Planned Obsolescence! http://bit.ly/zjXhV Mon Dec 21 16:09:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Heh. You've already been an enormous help. Go grade, and build that presentation, and I'll see you in Philly. Mon Dec 21 16:39:14 +0000 2009 Replying to @daveheal Thanks, Dave! Mon Dec 21 16:40:09 +0000 2009 Thanks, all, for the RTs; I appreciate the help! Mon Dec 21 16:50:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @sgillies Thanks. Would love to hear more abt yr reading of Handle/URL; would you comment on the text? Mon Dec 21 18:56:27 +0000 2009 Inbox 1. In three different accounts. Sorely tempted just to delete the last message in each. Mon Dec 21 19:52:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @ncecire I agree, tho as I say in the "locators" section, there's an awful irony in having reached a state at which less info seems better. Mon Dec 21 20:37:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @ncecire \(See fn 4\.22: http://bit\.ly/6BPH5I\) Mon Dec 21 20:37:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell Indeed. How hard would it have been?!? Mon Dec 21 23:54:23 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell Hey, there's a thought. \(I'll spare you my more cynical thoughts about whether such suggestions will actually be heard\.\) Tue Dec 22 00:01:46 +0000 2009 Replying to @jmittell Yeah, no kidding. What little disciplinary org time I've got right now is all going to the MLA, but once that's done, perhaps... Tue Dec 22 00:13:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench, @briancroxall and @barbarahui Just awful, all the way around. I'm so sorry, and will keep hoping for good things soon. Tue Dec 22 00:22:10 +0000 2009 Preparing all the electronics for travel. At some point I suppose I should pack some clothes, too. Tue Dec 22 03:07:19 +0000 2009 On the plane, headed to BR to see the fam, before #MLA09. Tue Dec 22 14:23:46 +0000 2009 #MLA09 tip: if you're talking to someone more junior/less famous than you, keep scanning the room over her head - someone better's coming! Tue Dec 22 17:41:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @2xlp Philly. Whoo-hoo! Tue Dec 22 17:51:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @kylebeachy Hey, awesome. Thanks! \(Do have some holiday fun, too, though\.\) Thu Dec 24 17:18:33 +0000 2009 Up \(relatively\) early for a few quiet minutes before the family nuttiness resumes. It's times like these that make me look forward to #MLA09 Fri Dec 25 14:19:36 +0000 2009 Breakfast with the fam, packing, and then off to #MLA09. See you at @mlatweetup, if you're there. Sat Dec 26 15:08:47 +0000 2009 MSY > IAH > PHL. Won't make it to the Marriott before midnight. At least I can sleep in tomorrow, before #MLA09 gets going. Sat Dec 26 20:23:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome Yes, but b/c it means they're combining two half full flts into one. Did that to me on the way to BTR. Sat Dec 26 22:43:45 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome So sorry! Were you eastbound or westbound? I'm at IAH now, headed to PHL, and a little worried. Flt before mine is 1.5hrs late. Sat Dec 26 23:04:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @bighandsome Yeeks. Definitely the wrong direction. Good luck with getting there! Sat Dec 26 23:12:57 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde I do not know whether to be pleased, embarrassed, appalled, or somewhat sorry that the actual experience doesn't quite measure up. Sun Dec 27 00:26:30 +0000 2009 On the plane for PHL, flight 1776, because Continental's cute that way. Sun Dec 27 00:50:27 +0000 2009 If you're on CO1776 & headed to #MLA09, I'm the one in the funky glasses and black leather jacket. Can't miss me! Sun Dec 27 00:52:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum My mission as an emissary of cool has only begun. #MLA09 Sun Dec 27 01:04:09 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I plan to be there, assuming the guys in the hall yelling about cheesesteak at 3am found what they needed & will let me sleep. Sun Dec 27 17:08:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Yikes! I'm glad you're safe. Sun Dec 27 17:21:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Yes, though often opportunistically; Tulane shut down many of its humanities PhDs post-Katrina, for instance. Sun Dec 27 18:11:32 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Hey, wait - so am I! #MLA09 Sun Dec 27 18:12:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Heh. Or I could. I look like my avatar, except with glasses & a black jacket/scarf. Sun Dec 27 18:19:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Okay, did the dorky stand-n-scan, but don't think I see you. I'm up at a table facing the bar, kinda hiding behind the fountain. Sun Dec 27 18:26:11 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Heh heh heh heh. Sun Dec 27 19:13:06 +0000 2009 @mlatweetup #MLA09 anyone available for the tweetup Mon 28th btw 5-7? // seconding suggestion by @amandalicastro Sun Dec 27 19:15:03 +0000 2009 After a brief absence, I'm again ensconced in the Marriott lobby, this time at the bar facing the fountain. Say hi if you're around. #MLA09 Sun Dec 27 19:58:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @chutry Cool; I'm still by the fountain in front of the bar... Sun Dec 27 20:40:38 +0000 2009 Replying to @kfitz Hey, @mlaconvention: thx for all the awesome tweets! If you add #MLA09, tho, more folks will see & they'll be archived http://bit.ly/5cSzNt Sun Dec 27 21:03:01 +0000 2009 Replying to @jbj Hey, are you around? @chutry and I are waving hello. Sun Dec 27 21:18:35 +0000 2009 Trying to remind myself that it's only the first day, but I've never seen the main convention hotel lobby as empty as this one is. #MLA09 Sun Dec 27 21:31:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave I'm about to run to a session, but will pass back through the Marriott bar around 6-6.30; @chutry will as well. Sun Dec 27 21:50:56 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave heading down from session - will walk with if you haven't left yet. Sun Dec 27 23:45:42 +0000 2009 Replying to @jasonrhody Very sorry Media Studies & Dig Scholarly Present is up against Getting Funded in the Humanities. They must think we're a threat. Mon Dec 28 04:48:44 +0000 2009 So, at what point do I acknowledge that I'm awake and just order breakfast already? Mon Dec 28 10:00:28 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie That's just not fair! Mon Dec 28 10:39:37 +0000 2009 Reasonably strong wi-fi signal \(PCCA Free\) in Liberty Ballroom Salon A. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 13:27:12 +0000 2009 Okay, I lied: wi-fi fail. Will try to tweet from iPhone. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 13:37:13 +0000 2009 Dene Grigar on the credentialing of new media programs & faculty. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 13:38:18 +0000 2009 Grigar: various efforts to create standards and practices for reviewing and credentialing work in digital media: MLA, ACLS, CAA, ELO. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 13:53:07 +0000 2009 Tabbi: we should circulate annotations as new mode of public scholarly discourse online. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:03:58 +0000 2009 Tabbi: should not look to internet for future of those forms that were born & thrive in print; will not satisfy online. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:06:47 +0000 2009 Fairly sure I don't agree. Those forms may not be satisfying online yet, but that won't necessarily always be so. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:09:18 +0000 2009 And what of those forms \(like the scholarly book\) born but struggling in print? #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:09:24 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Tabbi was specifically talking about novels/poetry, as opposed to born-digital lit. Still not convinced. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:16:44 +0000 2009 Benzon: focus on digital residue/ephemerality as means of reconceiving the web more generally. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:19:13 +0000 2009 Douglass on datamining in literary studies, using Aarseth, McCloud, Moretti. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:23:21 +0000 2009 Douglass: pushback against comput. models of lit analysis: there's no discovery - we only find what we already know to be interesting #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:33:06 +0000 2009 Douglass: we know we can build models to defamiliarize a huge space such as genre - but why do we need to? As impt a ? as how. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:36:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I agree. And the argument that b/c we don't yet have pleasant means of reading a novel online, we won't, is just wrong. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 14:43:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Alas, session ended before your tweet. Would love to prod him though... Mon Dec 28 14:49:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Which is precisely the issue I had with Grigar's presentation; I get the need for credentialing, but trad models not nec best. Mon Dec 28 14:55:14 +0000 2009 In the great hall @ the conv ctr, haunted by memories of conferences past, esp SCMS, conducting Skype conf w/if:book over bad wi-fi. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 15:05:40 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall iSlate iSlate iSlate. \(Which is to say, please god, not the Kindle\.\) Mon Dec 28 15:08:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Zombies! #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 15:10:05 +0000 2009 Was about to post my annoyance at use of my term for a process that I wouldn't say is peer-to-peer review, but then got credited. Oops. Mon Dec 28 15:24:06 +0000 2009 Cheryl Ball presenting Kairos at e-journal poster session. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 15:26:30 +0000 2009 RT @mlatweetup: #mla09 tweetup 5pm Monk's 16th & Spruce Mon Dec 28 15:40:36 +0000 2009 #mla09 tweetup 5pm Monk's 16th & Spruce /via @mlatweetup Mon Dec 28 15:41:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Details of what the standards were was a bit light, but the real issue I had was the determination to institutionalize... Mon Dec 28 16:09:08 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni ...rather than revolutionize. Mon Dec 28 16:09:28 +0000 2009 Back upstairs to recharge my batteries, literally & figuratively. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 16:36:31 +0000 2009 Replying to @jcmeloni Aha - that kind of standards! Yes, agreed! Mon Dec 28 16:38:52 +0000 2009 Meredith McGill on the matter with book history & what it can learn from the digital. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:37:29 +0000 2009 McGill: re-reading Darnton's communications circuit; developments in book history since; institutional success of field. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:39:35 +0000 2009 McGill: what's the matter with book history? marginalization of bibliography in lit studies depts. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:44:12 +0000 2009 McGill: shift from bibliography to material book history results in minimization of text, meaning, interpretation. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:47:07 +0000 2009 McGill: lit scholars working on book history often feel like franchisees in a field owned by historians. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:50:52 +0000 2009 McGill: lit critics need a book history that can cope with analysis as well as materiality. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:51:55 +0000 2009 McGill: rise of new media / media studies offers opportunity to reintegrate textual with material analysis. \(YAY, media studies\!\) #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 20:54:42 +0000 2009 HOLY COW. Image on cover of "Reading at Risk" is stock photo of the photog's young son in library in Bologna "reading" a 13th c ms! #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 21:06:03 +0000 2009 Fantastic paper from Patricia Crain on the erasure of children from history of books and readers. #MLA09 Mon Dec 28 21:15:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @plragde Only intermittently, but sometimes the tweeting helps me process/take notes... Mon Dec 28 21:30:26 +0000 2009 Heading to lobby; if you're walking to @mlatweetup, wait for me! Mon Dec 28 21:56:40 +0000 2009 @mlatweetup relocating to Tangier \(18th & Lombard\) Mon Dec 28 22:29:05 +0000 2009 Replying to @academicdave Just got back from 5pm @mlatweetup. Rats! Tue Dec 29 00:26:47 +0000 2009 Today's academic read http://bit.ly/5qdX9H @briancroxall on academic labor. Required reading 4 all higher ed folks #MLA09 /via @academicdave Tue Dec 29 13:06:56 +0000 2009 In meetings today, more or less all day. Keep me posted on what's going on in the awesome DH panels today! #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 14:46:58 +0000 2009 Two great meetings so far today; going into number 3 shortly. #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 16:37:59 +0000 2009 Met a little while ago with Bonnie Wheeler, pres of CELJ, who talked about Planned Obsolescence yesterday! http://bit.ly/5drT3P #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 16:39:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @newkidonthehall Had a great conv with her! \(Also, I meant to reply to your comment yesterday & failed\.\.\. will have to revisit\.\) Tue Dec 29 16:50:54 +0000 2009 .@jbj: for most faculty \(contingent, non\-R1\) doing DH exacerbates issues of local resources & isolation #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 19:03:16 +0000 2009 .@jbj: social networks as lightweight collaboration systems. #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 19:04:06 +0000 2009 You know who rocks? @nowviskie Giving us the low down on the tough issue of intellectual property and DH. #MLA09 /via @academicdave Tue Dec 29 19:24:25 +0000 2009 @jasonrhody talking about NEH support for/challenges in/lessons learned from collaborative projects. #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 19:41:40 +0000 2009 .@jasonrhody: desire overall is to model generosity in scholarship. \(Hear, hear\!\) #MLA09 Tue Dec 29 19:47:32 +0000 2009 New blog post: #MLA09 http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/mla09/ Tue Dec 29 21:03:47 +0000 2009 Up early this morning \(after way too little sleep\) for the Legacy of David Foster Wallace, followed by program committee session. #MLA09 Wed Dec 30 12:51:16 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie Absolutely - it's on my list! Wed Dec 30 13:16:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench Sadly, I won't be able to tweet during the panel, but will hope someone else there might... #MLA09 Wed Dec 30 13:17:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @mattbucher Forthcoming, as soon as I can get out of the program committee mtg & online. Short version: awesomeness. #MLA09 Wed Dec 30 16:13:48 +0000 2009 Heading to the airport - thanks, all \(& especially @mlaconvention\) , for an amazing #MLA09. See you in LA for #MLA11! Wed Dec 30 17:07:33 +0000 2009 Just realized I'm coming away from #MLA09 with no purchases, and no schwag! Don't quite know how that happened. Wed Dec 30 17:35:59 +0000 2009 Was teased about being "huggier than usual" last night - must be a sign of how much I enjoyed #MLA09. Wed Dec 30 17:51:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie, @amandafrench and @mkirschenbaum Indeed. As sorry as I am to be leavin the conf, it's nice to feel it ongoing... Wed Dec 30 18:31:06 +0000 2009 Replying to @nowviskie and @mkirschenbaum Yes! I kept being surprised to realize @amandafrench wasn't actually in Philly; connection was almost as vibrant. Wed Dec 30 18:34:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Hey, thanks for coming to the program committee session; it was really productive. Looking forward to more innovation! Wed Dec 30 18:53:53 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Back atcha - looking forward to doing it again soon. Wed Dec 30 19:03:27 +0000 2009 @mlaconvention: Just got off the phone with WYYY reporter; very nice conversation. I hope good things come out of it! Wed Dec 30 19:21:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @mlaconvention WHYY, of course. Will look forward to hearing what they pull from the conv, if anything. Lots about DH excitement at MLA. Wed Dec 30 19:30:13 +0000 2009 @jcmeloni Happy birthday, and here's to greater world domination! Thu Dec 31 00:59:19 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Sure - but I think may be less the Year DH Broke than the Year DH Got Recognized as Being at the Center of the H. Thu Dec 31 01:23:22 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum The trick now will be avoiding the calcification that such narratives of hegemony can work to produce... Thu Dec 31 01:24:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Shhhhh! Thu Dec 31 01:25:27 +0000 2009 New blog post: The Legacy of David Foster Wallace http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/the-legacy-of-david-foster-wallace/ Thu Dec 31 01:38:30 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall and @mkirschenbaum But on some level that would be part of the point, right? Digital scholarly communication = where it's at? Thu Dec 31 02:02:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Of course, but it's no accident that the scholarly use of new social technologies is taking root first in DH and rel fields. Thu Dec 31 02:18:04 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall Which is to say it's not the same thing, but in the early stages, it's evidence of a way of working... Thu Dec 31 02:18:47 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Is all DH necessarily computational? If so, I'm not sure I'm really all that digital in my humanities. Which seems odd to me. Thu Dec 31 02:19:55 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality So is the use of Twitter not-DH/soft DH, but an argument promoting the use of Twitter hard \(er\) DH? Thu Dec 31 02:22:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality That makes perfect sense to me; I'd support that definition. Thu Dec 31 02:27:54 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Where one of those goals might, for instance, be using digital tools to improve scholarly communication... Thu Dec 31 02:29:02 +0000 2009 Replying to @samplereality Yay! \(Thought I was home but wanted to be sure I wasn't wrong\.\) Thu Dec 31 02:35:13 +0000 2009 Replying to @briancroxall and @samplereality I'd say more the latter than the former - self-conscious, critical use/study of tools may be key. Thu Dec 31 02:46:43 +0000 2009 Replying to @jenterysayers There's prob a "who has the bigger umbrella" controversy here of the kind Jeremy mentioned in our panel... [1/2] Thu Dec 31 02:52:33 +0000 2009 Replying to @jenterysayers ...but I'd argue but DH, C&C, and digital MS are all at least cognate/cousin fields... [2/2] Thu Dec 31 02:53:35 +0000 2009 Replying to @amandafrench I would agree with that! \(Jeremy was talking specifically about software/platform/critical code studies \- who contains whom?\) Thu Dec 31 03:14:10 +0000 2009 Replying to @ryancordell Woo-hoo! Congratulations! Thu Dec 31 16:47:18 +0000 2009 Replying to @phdaisy Aw man, I miss Cooter Brown's! Have one for me. Thu Dec 31 23:58:29 +0000 2009 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Yeah, that little award thingy sure doesn't count. Fri Jan 01 01:11:26 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2009 12 01 tweets

docs.text: WANT. RT @nowviskie: Pretty jazzed about design concepts for the XO-3 \(future tablet version of One Laptop Per Child\) : http://is.gd/5IWCT Fri Jan 01 18:44:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Huge, no? Everything from scribbled journal notes to early roughs to more polished drafts. Don't know how complete, but wow! Fri Jan 01 23:47:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum He didn't, but I'd be happy to ask him about it. Sat Jan 02 01:10:57 +0000 2010 @nowviskie On the GCal sharing thing: do you have one core calendar that you share? Or can your embed include multiple cals? Sat Jan 02 17:08:18 +0000 2010 @nowviskie Will confess that I miss the one calendar/multiple categories mode of organization. Sat Jan 02 17:09:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Aha. I'm Mac, too, and w/Exchange, so using an Exch cal in iCal, which then syncs to GCal! So prob. 1 calendar would be best. Sat Jan 02 17:26:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Makes sense. Had focused cals around areas of responsibility \(teaching/research/admin\) but "availability" is key issue. Sat Jan 02 17:39:50 +0000 2010 @nowviskie I liked the visual clue about the kind of work I was doing at any point in the day, but hell, it's all work. Sat Jan 02 17:40:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattthomas I've been using BusySync to manage the iCal > gCal sync, but am not yet a fan of BusyCal, which strikes me as too, well, busy. Sat Jan 02 17:55:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara and @mattthomas What I'm hoping is to embed a version of my gCal in my main faculty webpage, so my students can see my availability. Sat Jan 02 18:42:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara and @mattthomas Or, more to the point, the lack thereof. @nowviskie has this working on her site, which was my initial inspiration. Sat Jan 02 18:43:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @Kylewarneck Hey, is this *our* reunion? Because I'll make sure there's a reception if you'll make sure to come to it. Sun Jan 03 02:58:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @ryancordell So completely annoying. Had a long interview w/reporter in which I gave her a way to combat that, but obvs didn't make the cut. Mon Jan 04 15:32:49 +0000 2010 The shorter me: none of us do all our scholarship in 140char or less - but we do draw our community's attention to impt arguments elsewhere. Mon Jan 04 15:37:54 +0000 2010 Also on writing in public: until we get over fears of talking w/the broader culture, we'll never convince them that what we do is important. Mon Jan 04 15:40:39 +0000 2010 So scholarly use of Twitter isn't just cool internally; it has potential to demonstrate what we do and why the culture should care. #MLA09 Mon Jan 04 15:43:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold YAY! Please keep me posted, and let me know if I can be of help, informally or in my Prog Cte hat. #MLA11 Mon Jan 04 15:44:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline I'm in. Annoyance will drive me to commit to more work! #MLA Mon Jan 04 15:46:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @profsyn Yes! Belittling Twitter in that sense is not unlike dismissing poetry for failing to contain fleshed-out narratives. Genre, people. Mon Jan 04 15:49:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Exactly. I have a better sense of what folks in my field are reading and arguing about now than ever before. Mon Jan 04 15:50:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton I guessed that in mid-interview; could hear mad typing. She said of the public-outreach thing, though... [1/2] Mon Jan 04 15:53:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton ...that it helped her make a point she hadn't yet - so I assumed would be in there! Wonder how much she got edited down. [2/2] Mon Jan 04 15:54:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton Done & awaiting moderation! Mon Jan 04 16:36:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @nancybaym If it's at all possible, hire someone to do the first draft. It's a miserable process & will take you way longer than it's worth Mon Jan 04 16:51:13 +0000 2010 Read annoying article. Got mammogram. Bought expensive prescription cat food. How many times do I need to be crushed? #trivial Mon Jan 04 16:57:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Is all I'm sayin. Mon Jan 04 18:10:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic and @clioandme Yes - and at least the potential to help persuade those publics about why they might care. Mon Jan 04 18:16:20 +0000 2010 Beginning revisions of Planned Obsolescence; would still love your input! http://bit.ly/zjXhV Mon Jan 04 18:18:12 +0000 2010 O: & for those who followed my GCal embed ?s - found the ability to embed multiple cals, plus use Tungle for appt reqs: http://bit.ly/5frhTY Mon Jan 04 18:20:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic and @clioandme Precisely. And having one of our prof pubs refer to such online dissemination as "trivial" is a case in point. Mon Jan 04 18:26:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmaxsfu Hey, excellent! Mon Jan 04 18:43:27 +0000 2010 Have decided to blog some of the revisions as I go forward. Here, Zombies! http://bit.ly/7FMd4E Mon Jan 04 19:14:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmaxsfu Yikes - password prompt?! That ain't right. Mon Jan 04 20:38:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj Thank @academicdave, who originally turned me on to it! Tue Jan 05 01:15:13 +0000 2010 Working on personal statement for promotion review file. Getting seriously tired of explaining myself. Want simply to say: "see blog." Wed Jan 06 17:10:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Not envious. Not at all. Wed Jan 06 17:59:43 +0000 2010 Okay, I think the dossier's complete, including the personal statement. Would love feedback if you're of a mind. http://bit.ly/7PWW7U Thu Jan 07 17:13:17 +0000 2010 My primary worry is that early drafts of previous personal statements have sounded awfully defensive, as if justifying flaws in the file... Thu Jan 07 17:14:10 +0000 2010 This time out, I'm fairly confident - and hey, it's a much lower-stakes review. But I still worry about tone. Thu Jan 07 17:15:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @wpwend42 Hey, cool. I bet that was a great class! Thu Jan 07 17:16:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Does it really? I'm alarmed to say I hadn't even checked. One project for spring: mobile-ize all stylesheets... Thu Jan 07 17:18:28 +0000 2010 Ugh, forgot to write the bit about use of technology in teaching that I left a note-to-self to include... not done yet after all. :( Thu Jan 07 17:21:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @phdaisy Good point; thanks! Thu Jan 07 17:32:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Not down here! Thu Jan 07 18:25:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Wait, what? Your salary *decreased*? And your workload went up. Hey, um... congratulations? Thu Jan 07 23:33:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde I'll admit that we do have a tendency toward the prolix in our personal statements; I don't know if that's my school or my field. Thu Jan 07 23:34:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Are there particular spots where you see the defensive tone? I'd really love to root that out... Thu Jan 07 23:35:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde I can't imagine how it conceivably could. I'm already chair & unprotected from committee service. I've been surprised before, tho. Thu Jan 07 23:54:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Such a cheery portrait of promotion you paint! Things are a bit different around here; a huge load falls on associates. Fri Jan 08 00:18:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde In fact, fairly heavy service is an expectation for promotion here. Fri Jan 08 00:20:26 +0000 2010 What's up with the new FB iPhone contact sync? Claims to send FB friend links and photos to contacts, but does it send contact info to FB? Fri Jan 08 03:29:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yikes. Just realized I didn't send @ProfHacker my paragraph. Basically it's my comment on IHE. Will send now... Fri Jan 08 15:52:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Done! Fri Jan 08 16:01:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid and @jmittell Both Zotero and 1Password. Otherwise I'd be all Chrome in a heartbeat. Fri Jan 08 21:06:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @s2ceball The thinking is that a panel "belongs" to its speakers until it takes place, & then ownership transfers to the public. #mla09 Sat Jan 09 17:00:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @s2ceball So speakers/organizers can comment before, but others can only comment after. #mla09 Sat Jan 09 17:01:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @s2ceball Yeah, it would be - I think we're aiming for that next year! #mla11 Sat Jan 09 17:09:49 +0000 2010 Hey folks, for a short article: what's the single most important misunderstanding about open access publishing you'd like to see corrected? Sun Jan 10 14:59:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Yeah, that's the main one I'm trying to debunk! Sun Jan 10 15:06:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Aha - a myth about institutional open access initiatives? Good one! Sun Jan 10 15:12:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Indeed! Sun Jan 10 15:18:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Aha, that's an important one; thanks! Sun Jan 10 15:33:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @lisaspiro Ooh, excellent. Thanks! Sun Jan 10 15:34:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @sshreeves Gah! That's awful. It's currently 6.25 PM ET. TUAW has powers beyond the ordinary blog. docs.text: Replying to @rachaelsullivan Yes! Calling out untenured faculty on their caution is a form of victim-blaming. That said, change can & shd percolate up. Which is awesome and all, but I'd be happier with sanity. Mon Feb 01 19:50:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Completely stolen from @jbj, I think, at @ProfHacker. Tue Feb 02 16:06:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Actually, looking back, it's @jbj's absence policy I stole, and @drnels's late work policy. Go @ProfHacker! Why do you ask? \[checks email\.\.\.checks email\.\.\.checks email\] Tue Feb 02 18:38:02 +0000 2010 Ask and ye shall receive. Letter 1 just walked in the door. Tue Feb 02 18:45:49 +0000 2010 Am absolutely blown away. Tue Feb 02 19:41:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @rachaelsullivan and @phdaisy Absolutely good! I'm stunned by the generosity of my colleagues and students. It's a bit overwhelming. Tue Feb 02 19:51:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Happiness. 100%. It's pretty overwhelming. Tue Feb 02 21:22:04 +0000 2010 At the vet with cat # 2, who has lost 25% of her body weight. Running tests. Poor little thing. Tue Feb 02 23:29:17 +0000 2010 Okay, no Twitter for me until Lost airs on the west coast, apparently. You east coasters are trouble. Fri Feb 05 01:35:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah The shorter letter # 2: "We Have Never Done It This Way Before." Fri Feb 05 01:36:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Hence the possible hotel stay. :) Fri Feb 05 01:53:36 +0000 2010 The shorter letter # 2: "We Have Never Done It This Way Before." Fri Feb 05 16:54:33 +0000 2010 Hey, it's #snowpocalypse out there, and it's... like... sorta... drizzling here! See, we have weather, too! Apply one chorus of Sister Christian! Sat Feb 06 23:04:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @alicejrobison I'll be there, but alas, only Saturday, as I'll be flying back from a talk at Michigan... Sun Feb 07 01:21:10 +0000 2010 Just purged my Gmail account of an enormous amount of old listserv mail. Feel megabytes lighter. Sun Feb 07 21:24:19 +0000 2010 WHODAT!!! Mon Feb 08 02:32:26 +0000 2010 Oh. My. Freaking. God. They're going to do this! #whodat!!! Mon Feb 08 02:47:12 +0000 2010 Dear DC: I am coming to visit Thursday, and live in a place without weather. Would be great if you could clear that snow out by then. Thx! Mon Feb 08 15:22:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @CapitolClio Which is most directly and immediately the problem: no snow boots. So completely not prepared for this... Mon Feb 08 16:01:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley All I'm really asking for is a dry non-slip pathway from the Henley Park Hotel to the Old Post Office. The rest I can sort out. Mon Feb 08 16:59:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @footnotesrising Ugh, yes. Will do. Mon Feb 08 17:48:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Hi from Southern California! Mon Feb 08 20:42:58 +0000 2010 Help, tweeps! When Said mentions a "conception of the text as existing entirely within a hermetic, Alexandrian textual universe"... [1/2] Tue Feb 09 00:47:09 +0000 2010 ...what does "Alexandrian" signify? Is it the school of theology that's being referenced? What about it? How does Alexandrian=hermetic? Tue Feb 09 00:48:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman Ha! Tue Feb 09 00:54:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Possibly. The closest ref I'm coming up with is the Alexandrian poets, who used masses of allusions in their work, but I dunno. Tue Feb 09 00:55:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @jsench I was wondering if it was that, but couldn't quite parse it. The following phrase is "having no connection with actuality"... [1/2] Tue Feb 09 01:13:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @jsench which made me think it was Alexandrian poets & their propensity for allusion. And perhaps less the New Testament? But I dunno. [2/2] Tue Feb 09 01:15:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde It's one of 3 possibilities I can see: the library, the poets \(who loved allusion\) , or the text-type of some New Testament mss. Tue Feb 09 01:16:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Perhaps. But is hermeticness really the thing we associate with the library of Alexandria, rather than its destruction? Tue Feb 09 01:41:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde From the Alex. school: "their style is often bad, and great patience is required to clear up their numerous and obscure allusions" Tue Feb 09 01:42:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Oh, you have no idea how much I wish I could. Must annotate for anthology. Sigh. Tue Feb 09 01:52:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde A medievalist colleague of mine insists it's abt Clement of Alex, who believed Bibl reading must be done in retreat from the world. Tue Feb 09 04:12:06 +0000 2010 Ahhh. Nothing scheduled before office hours at 4 tomorrow. Means I might actually make my Wednesday deadlines. And get to sleep past 5.30. Tue Feb 09 05:16:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman Buzz looks interesting \(and awfully familiar \*cough\*twitter\*cough\*\) , but I'm waiting for them to merge Gmail and Greader already. Tue Feb 09 18:44:58 +0000 2010 @jwolman Okay, well, there it is: Buzz can pull Greader and Twitter into its stream... Tue Feb 09 18:48:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Where's mine? \(Oh, wait\. I bet that's not what you meant\.\) #buzz Tue Feb 09 19:35:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui It hailed here on Saturday - absolutely bizarre. It's not raining here yet today, but the clouds over the mtns look ominous. Tue Feb 09 20:25:02 +0000 2010 Is it bad to be at the point where I hope the snow is bad enough back east that my trip is cancelled rather than just a massive headache? Tue Feb 09 21:44:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Ugh. And then dump, what, 10-15 inches more on top of it tonight? This does not sound good. Tue Feb 09 21:54:47 +0000 2010 Hey you back east: enough with the LOST live tweeting. Some of us don't have the option of watching when you do. :) Wed Feb 10 04:07:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton Hey, great minds think alike! Wed Feb 10 04:08:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara Ooh, sorry - I really wasn't aiming at you! You weren't revealing anything, but others were. :/ Wed Feb 10 04:35:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton Completely fascinating. Part of it's the WTF factor, I think. Wed Feb 10 04:36:28 +0000 2010 I am four grant apps away from meeting two impossible same-day deadlines - the last of the insane deadlines I set myself up for in Jan-Feb. Wed Feb 10 04:38:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley This does not instill great confidence in me in this week's trip actually taking place. Wed Feb 10 04:39:56 +0000 2010 Trip to DC cancelled. Which means I get a snow day on Friday. In SoCal. Which sounds exactly perfect to me. Wed Feb 10 14:50:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell In a bit, sure - need to get some coffee in first! Wed Feb 10 15:07:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @BidenPC Alas, I'll probably work. Just on my own stuff instead of everything else... Wed Feb 10 15:08:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais Mine popped up this morning. It'll arrive shortly, I'm sure. Wed Feb 10 15:46:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @betajames They're adding post-to-Twitter soon, they said. Wed Feb 10 16:51:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenServenti Actually, I have snow! It's just safely tucked up on the mountains five minutes north of here. http://yfrog.com/334giyj Wed Feb 10 16:54:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @chutry I'm here - http://www.google.com/profiles/kfitz47 - and now following you. Wed Feb 10 17:50:22 +0000 2010 Tinkering with Buzz today. So far, I like it; far better signal to noise control than FB. But then, there aren't many of us there yet. Wed Feb 10 17:52:08 +0000 2010 I'm kfitz47 on Google if you care to follow along. Wed Feb 10 17:52:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I'm sure it'll be there soon; mine popped up this morning. I'll be on the lookout! Wed Feb 10 17:54:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenServenti Yes, indeed. I find that prettiness and closeness are more or less inversely proportional, when it comes to snow. Wed Feb 10 18:01:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara Yup! Send me addresses by DM & I'll happily add them. Wed Feb 10 18:11:32 +0000 2010 Up side: no travel tmw. Down side: a day of working on planes, unbothered by anything but travel, immediately turned into a day of meetings. Wed Feb 10 18:52:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @acavender Weird! But because his billing address is here in CA, the florist assumed he'd gotten it backwards & delivered them to him in BR. Sun Feb 14 19:10:49 +0000 2010 Some yelling straightened it out, and I now have lovely roses. But in the meantime a little sample of the indignities of the commuting life. It's also clearly an end run around increasing interest rates; they'll waive the fee if you spend $2400/yr with them. Wed Feb 17 02:39:51 +0000 2010 @citibank: "The reason we are making this change is to maintain the quality of our service amid the rising cost of doing business." Wed Feb 17 02:44:11 +0000 2010 Ha. Ha ha. Hahahahahahahahahahaha. Ha. Hm. Wed Feb 17 02:45:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ah, just amusing myself at the expense of big banking. Well, not "expense," really. If only I could. Wed Feb 17 02:59:13 +0000 2010 Called to cancel @citibank card. Agent asked if I'd received the notice of the new $60 annual fee. Yup. Account terminated, after 24 years. Wed Feb 17 02:59:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @lnakamur Oh, yes - so do I! Let's definitely grab one Saturday afternoon/evening... Wed Feb 17 03:12:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @alicejrobison Back atcha! Wed Feb 17 03:13:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Dunno - looks that way. They waive the fee if you spend more than $2400/yr with them, but it's a secondary card for me, so no. Wed Feb 17 03:24:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @ambrose_mensch I think they may just be counting how many people are cancelling because of the fee; he didn't try to sell me on staying. Wed Feb 17 03:28:57 +0000 2010 DirectTV + winter Olympics = mighty pleasant flight! Wed Feb 17 21:45:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Gallagher & Greenblatt on the potato in the materialist imagination. Wed Feb 17 22:04:56 +0000 2010 Can toothpaste go bad? Thu Feb 18 13:09:54 +0000 2010 Awesome turnout for my UM talk, about to begin! Thu Feb 18 21:59:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @moncia I totally used that on a slide in my talk at U-M today \("the hopey\-changey part"\) . Thu Feb 18 23:46:46 +0000 2010 Sorry to have missed yesterday and today at #dml2010, but my visit to UMich totally rocked, if I do say so myself. See you tomorrow! Thanks for coming out, especially on such a busy day. Sat Feb 20 19:24:08 +0000 2010 Fantastic lunch with @lnakamur, now awaiting session on race, ethnicity, class and digital media practices. Sat Feb 20 22:03:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais Instead of doing the relaunch thing, let the bar run; it took a couple of minutes for mine to flip on. Sat Feb 20 22:14:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais Not at all? How annoying! I wonder if UCSD is suggesting that it's time for the conference to wrap up? Sat Feb 20 22:42:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek It's a Twitter extension - displays your stream in a popup, signals you when you've got new tweets... Mon Feb 22 21:08:00 +0000 2010 I'm so scattered right now that I can't even gather the pieces enough to make a list of everything I need to do, much less actually do it. Tue Feb 23 15:37:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @phdaisy Thanks - you too! Tue Feb 23 16:06:18 +0000 2010 People are absolutely killing me today. #stresslevelsthroughtheroof Tue Feb 23 21:39:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Hey, fantastic - that's wonderful news! Thanks for showing it! Tue Feb 23 21:46:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara Somebody decided our mascot sounded girly, so we're Imaginary-Male-Mascot-Animal-Variants and Mascots. Tue Feb 23 23:08:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Gah! Because finding and reading sources is the hard part of humanities research?!? Tue Feb 23 23:12:08 +0000 2010 "keep in mind that every newly minted Ph.D. has already done most of the research for his or her book when the tenure clock starts..." [1/2] Tue Feb 23 23:15:34 +0000 2010 "It’s just a matter of revising the dissertation into a book." Oh, is that all. #sigh [2/2] Tue Feb 23 23:16:01 +0000 2010 http://nyti.ms/c1lYNW, in case you were wondering. /via @dancohen Tue Feb 23 23:18:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde and @xina Most R1s in the US have a 1-book-and-significant-work-toward-2 requirement for tenure in the humanities; promotion=2nd book. Tue Feb 23 23:35:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Reminds me of a grad st. I heard some years back saying she'd done all the research for her diss; now she just had to write it up. Tue Feb 23 23:37:56 +0000 2010 @dancohen Something in that "just" made me feel very very stabby. Especially considering we were sitting in the expository writing program. Tue Feb 23 23:42:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek She was in English Ed, actually. Which disheartened me even further. Tue Feb 23 23:45:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah You said it, sister. Wed Feb 24 20:40:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic Why not allow knowledgeable users - hell, librarians - to help them correct the errors in ways that can improve the algorithm? I don't use Word, so no, but I did integrate it into NeoOffice, where I think it can be removed... I'm 1 for 1, but nobody will pay me for anything, at least at the moment. I'm preparing some serious begging. Thu Feb 25 03:38:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Good lord. Misplaced floor mats did that? Are they magnetic floor mats? Thu Feb 25 03:55:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality and @brettbobley I would suggest that if they've lost you six years of data they owe you six years of refund. Unbelievable. Thu Feb 25 04:08:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I mean, "100% Lifetime Satisfaction Promise" seems, well, not to have been met. All NeoOffice for me. docs.text: Listening the new Gorillaz album pre-release, thanks to NPR. http://bit.ly/aptYqk Mon Mar 01 16:41:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah The word "fan" overstates the case just a bit. Sat Mar 27 02:43:35 +0000 2010 Have spent much of today recovering from the awesomeness of #NITLE. Will now spend the rest of the day doing the same. Sat Mar 27 18:37:01 +0000 2010 NOLA Marriott seriously dimmed the light for Earth Hour - both atmospheric & impressive! Sun Mar 28 02:33:32 +0000 2010 Over the last week, I've migrated 17 years worth of archived email to Gmail, and still have tons of space remaining. Amazing. Sun Mar 28 13:56:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay My personal impulse would be to call it the Birkerts, but that may be too field specific. http://bit.ly/dxHK82 Sun Mar 28 14:03:07 +0000 2010 Am having one of those WHY DO I HAVE CATS??? moments. It will pass once I figure out whether the damage to the floor is permanent. Sun Mar 28 19:14:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy Apparently. Hmph. Sun Mar 28 19:52:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Mine will be forgiven. But they wreaked a little more havoc this weekend than was necessary. Mon Mar 29 01:39:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @tmcphers Mine knocked over their water bowl on my wood floors while I wasn't home; water sat there long enough to get under the finish. Mon Mar 29 04:14:37 +0000 2010 A new blog post, about The New Everyday: http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/the-new-everyday/ Mon Mar 29 14:49:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Now *there's* a spectator sport. Mon Mar 29 15:20:08 +0000 2010 RT @mkgold: Registration now open for The Digital University conf at CUNY GC \(NYC 4/21\) . @sivavaid keynotes http://is.gd/b5cI4 #du10 Pls RT Mon Mar 29 16:55:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @pdahlgre WHOO-HOO!!! Mon Mar 29 20:10:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @davidmsilver Wow, that's fantastic! Mon Mar 29 23:43:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mariabustillos First, WOW! Congrats! Second, difficult choices ahead... Mon Mar 29 23:47:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Fantastic news -- congrats! Tue Mar 30 03:40:42 +0000 2010 On getting myself back to work, 30 minutes at a time: http://bit.ly/aWKABM Tue Mar 30 15:20:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Thanks - that really means a lot to me. It's scary to have that out there, and hard to realize how much I still wrestle with. Tue Mar 30 15:49:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @pmhswe My pleasure! I hope it works for you; so far, it's working well for me. Tue Mar 30 18:46:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Congratulations! Fantastic news! Tue Mar 30 20:09:37 +0000 2010 Maybe I'm not reading the press release right - but was there really only *one* FDHC fellowship given by the NEH this year? Tue Mar 30 20:57:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @sarah_haskins Absolutely loved it! Fantastic project - thanks for making it available! Wed Mar 31 02:35:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @mariabustillos Whoa - fantastic! As fabulous as Emerson is, I'm all pro-small liberal arts college. Wonderful news. Wed Mar 31 06:08:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman Not even an allowance for time zones? Really, give us on the west coast a fighting chance. Wed Mar 31 15:34:20 +0000 2010 New blog post: This Is Just to Note http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/this-is-just-to-note/ Thu Apr 01 02:24:03 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 03 01 tweets

docs.text: New blog post: Revisions http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/revisions/ Thu Apr 01 15:14:27 +0000 2010 When I first read this I thought it said "propose." The reality is less fun, but at least I know it's not April Fools. http://bit.ly/d1xJS6 Thu Apr 01 15:40:56 +0000 2010 "in those days the Mac was derided as a toy, a media poseur's plaything and a shallow triumph of style over substance" http://bit.ly/9UOkW5 Thu Apr 01 16:42:36 +0000 2010 "The iPhone, like the Mac, was derided upon introduction as a plaything, but it transformed the smart-phone landscape" http://bit.ly/csiUH9 Thu Apr 01 16:44:57 +0000 2010 Point being: I don't know what people will do with the iPad yet, but don't count them out. Engaging devices produce unexpected results. Thu Apr 01 16:49:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @wedaman Exactly. That you might not program ON the iPad doesn't mean people won't be inspired to program FOR it. Play is inspiring. Thu Apr 01 16:50:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman No kidding. Hard not to imagine what he'd have done with it. Thu Apr 01 16:56:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattthomas I saw that exchange, tho not until this morning. It may be a device for consumption, but I think it will inspire new production. Thu Apr 01 16:59:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave That I also agree with: if computers become appliances, we'll have a problem. But I remain cautiously optimistic. Thu Apr 01 17:21:42 +0000 2010 I love Thursdays: the one day all week I don't have to be on campus for anything. Today has thus far been spent working in my pajamas. Ahhh. Thu Apr 01 20:04:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I walked away from Firefox pretty much entirely. I lament not having easy access to @zotero, but it's not worth the pain. Thu Apr 01 21:16:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell I was telling my students just yesterday about the impossibility of making the Mellon workshop folks here last January go inside. Thu Apr 01 23:19:31 +0000 2010 Gmail wishlist: I have a "to do" label that I use to collect things, well, to do, as a means of getting them out of my inbox. I wish [1/2] Fri Apr 02 00:31:35 +0000 2010 that there were a means of making them reappear in my inbox on a certain date, if they haven't been otherwise dealt with. [2/2] Fri Apr 02 00:32:17 +0000 2010 Good grief. "Don't Stop Believing" just came on in the cafe I'm in. Horrible dissonance: do I flash into The Sopranos or Glee? Fri Apr 02 00:33:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @KelliMarshall Ha! Before those two appropriations, that would absolutely have happened, but I think it's been overcome by events. Fri Apr 02 00:37:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton Oh, no. If anything there was too much Journey. Perhaps it's the desire to repress the memories that has solidified the link. Fri Apr 02 00:54:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Now that would be worth seeing. Fri Apr 02 00:54:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @SopranosBlog I wouldn't think your memories of that song would be particularly good, either. Fri Apr 02 00:55:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @SopranosBlog I mostly meant in your Tony persona, for whom that song would be a bummer. But yeah, perfect ending. Fri Apr 02 01:33:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay and @nowviskie pwd Fri Apr 02 03:17:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah http://www.xkcd.com Fri Apr 02 03:33:58 +0000 2010 In which I admit to excitement about the fact that I'll be picking up my iPad at 9am tomorrow. http://bit.ly/afaEzI Fri Apr 02 17:31:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @anniedreshfield Absolutely! Fri Apr 02 17:50:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @synch101 Absolutely! Fri Apr 02 19:14:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality That's a reasonable question & a very different one from most that are being raised. As I work on the future of reading [1/2] Fri Apr 02 19:21:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I can justify it \(and write it off\) . But if it weren't for that I'd probably at least wait until 2G. [2/2] Fri Apr 02 19:21:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Promise! Sat Apr 03 02:04:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Thanks! Seems to me only natural. Sat Apr 03 02:05:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmcclurken Thanks! Sat Apr 03 02:06:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Promise to do so as soon as I get my hands on mine. About which more in a sec. Sat Apr 03 16:19:17 +0000 2010 Curses! Foiled again. Apple did not get the iPads to the bookstore by 9am as promised; manager is hoping they'll arrive by 10ish. #impatient Sat Apr 03 16:20:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Was funny in my head, too. Sat Apr 03 16:52:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ On Google Docs: you can access & read but apparently not edit, unless I'm just not finding the button. Sigh. Sat Apr 03 18:19:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Me! Sat Apr 03 19:10:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Still setting up at this point. Too engrossed to tweet. But will hope to have commentary soon! Sat Apr 03 19:22:36 +0000 2010 Loading iPad up with stuff. Haven't done much as yet, but so far I'm pleased; wifi/Safari is fast. More reports as I can tear myself away. Sat Apr 03 19:42:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @therefore Have been having fun setting mine up, tho wish there were an easier way to get app data from iPhone to iPad. Sat Apr 03 21:32:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention You only have to pay for the connection with the 3G model; I got the wifi only. Sat Apr 03 21:54:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Yes! All my apps transferred, and those that had released iPad native/universal versions \(like Instapaper\) updated w/no prob. Sat Apr 03 23:12:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Only issue so far is transferring data for apps that store it on the iPhone rather than on a server \(like Stanza\) . Sat Apr 03 23:13:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell and @loganpoppy What about the private schools \(like my own\) that are need-blind, resulting in more socio-economic diversity not less? Sun Apr 04 03:43:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Pomona's different that way; MUCH less prep school here than east-coast SLACs get. Sun Apr 04 03:49:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy Not exactly; in our usage, need-blind means we commit to funding the full need of any student who's admitted. And "merit" isn't- Sun Apr 04 03:51:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy just about grades & SATs, but other stuff too. 75% of students are on financial aid; avg. package is 50%. 25% 1st gen college. Sun Apr 04 03:52:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy And we've stuck to the no-loan policy, even in budget retraction. Sun Apr 04 03:53:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy Oh, absolutely; it's not perfect. But it's WAY more diverse than you'd expect. Sun Apr 04 03:54:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @therefore Yes, anxiously awaiting the case. Sun Apr 04 03:55:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @therefore None yet; they hadn't come in to our bookstore. Did you get one? Sun Apr 04 05:37:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Oh, I intend to do much showing off in the coming days! Sun Apr 04 16:50:32 +0000 2010 Have just had to launch Firefox for the first time in ages in order to run a cranky website. When exactly did such a good browser go wrong? Sun Apr 04 17:32:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy I've completely dropped it other than instances like this, and using Zotero. And even that's become painful. Sun Apr 04 17:40:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Chrome, 95% of the time. Safari about 4%. And Firefox when I absolutely have to. Sun Apr 04 17:52:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Which does make me worry a bit for Chrome, but so far I haven't hit a problematic extension. Sun Apr 04 17:58:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @KelliMarshall and @loganpoppy Haven't had any difficulty with any of my WP installs, but I know others have. Not sure what that's about at all. Sun Apr 04 18:07:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @therefore That's the one I'm thinking of getting - pretty simple protection for the device plus a little lift for typing. Sun Apr 04 22:11:41 +0000 2010 I think I just totally felt the shit out of this: http://yfrog.com/j3ch9p Whoa. Sun Apr 04 22:44:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @christinielsen A 6.9 in Baja. Criminy. Sun Apr 04 22:45:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jokeisup God, me too. That was something. Particularly for being that far away. Sun Apr 04 22:54:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Afar is the right place to watch from. Shee. Sun Apr 04 22:54:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Oh, that was no metaphor, mon ami. Sun Apr 04 23:10:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Totally felt it, but everybody's fine. Except for the cats, who need tranquilizers. Sun Apr 04 23:37:22 +0000 2010 Baja quake now upgraded to 7.2. Thank god it was deep. #needabeer Sun Apr 04 23:52:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Not a good introduction to the area! I hope she's okay... Sun Apr 04 23:53:21 +0000 2010 Gumbo is bubbling. Rice is cooking. Classes for tomorrow are more or less ready. Not a bad Sunday, all told. Mon Apr 05 00:10:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Yeesh; that's terrifying. It was a pretty slow roller by the time it got up here, but still enough to make everything sway. Mon Apr 05 00:11:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @JessieNYC and @cscannella That one looks good! I tend to do chicken & sausage, following a group of recipes in a gumbo/soup ckbk I got yrs ago. Mon Apr 05 00:24:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde No, metaphor implies conscious association, at least to me. That was just Freaking Out. Mon Apr 05 04:28:34 +0000 2010 New blog post: Preliminary Thoughts on the iPad http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/preliminary-thoughts-on-the-ipad/ Mon Apr 05 16:39:24 +0000 2010 So if you had a site that you must read that won't run on a modern Mac browser \(Chrome, Safari, and Firefox are all out\) what would you use? Mon Apr 05 18:20:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Talan Memmott's Lexia to Perplexia. Apparently runs on Windows machines but not on Mac. Including Opera, which I've now tried too. Mon Apr 05 18:27:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @sgsinclair Brilliant idea, but sadly, it doesn't work for this site. Rats. Mon Apr 05 18:43:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Or Netscape 4. Or just a Windows machine. :( Mon Apr 05 18:43:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Alas, the browsers it emulates are actually too old! I need the Netscape 4/IE 6 sweet spot. Mon Apr 05 18:44:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Won't run on my Intel-based Mac! I go in search of a Windows machine. :-/ Mon Apr 05 18:57:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp http://bit.ly/d0916D Asking for IE4/NN4. Sheesh. And http://www.dejavu.org/emulator.htm won't work. Mon Apr 05 19:24:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Ooh, cool. How do I make it pretend to be NN4? Mon Apr 05 20:05:45 +0000 2010 Gah! Cultured Code! I srsly love Things, but really: $34.95 for the desktop, plus $9.99 for the iPhone, & now $19.99 more for the iPad? Rly? Mon Apr 05 23:38:52 +0000 2010 Yes, Things for iPad looks nifty. Better than the iPhone version. But asking for $20 more from me may drive me to another task manager. Mon Apr 05 23:40:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Wise man. Or wise guy. Not sure which is more applicable. Mon Apr 05 23:45:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost I'm the kind of obsessive that needs to be able to find out what I'm procrastinating on NOW. Mon Apr 05 23:46:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni You & me both. One tires of "2.9.13." Onward, already. Tue Apr 06 03:56:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate and @jcmeloni Not I. Tue Apr 06 04:07:40 +0000 2010 New blog post: The Late Age of Print, Audio Edition http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/the-late-age-of-print-audio-edition/ Tue Apr 06 15:42:57 +0000 2010 RT @ProfHacker: New at ProfHacker: Initial Thoughts about the iPad - by @kfitz http://bit.ly/cLGg2m Tue Apr 06 18:58:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall You go, Brian! Wed Apr 07 21:20:34 +0000 2010 Thanks, all; my students were suitably impressed. \(Not least by the two unconnected "rockstar" comments\!\) Wed Apr 07 21:36:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @bighandsome You mean "department." Of course, we're still in the basement, but we do have better toys now. Wed Apr 07 21:38:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @goodbyewaffles Hey there! I didn't know you were on here. Wed Apr 07 21:38:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Alas, it was a pretty brief window \(it was late in the class\) . Wed Apr 07 21:53:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @anniedreshfield I was surprised by that, too! Wed Apr 07 22:05:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @mikejolk Indeed. My male cat was being mean & my vet told me to stop free-feeding. When food wasn't always available, he became much nicer! Wed Apr 07 22:18:39 +0000 2010 My Writing Machines wants to know whether any of Shelley Jackson's "words" have received a copy of the story as yet. Any words out there? Wed Apr 07 22:45:36 +0000 2010 My Writing Machines *class* wants to know, that is. Wed Apr 07 22:49:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Thanks, G; the FAQ was ambiguous. Sat Apr 10 00:05:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Surprisingly enough, I think Apple has one it would be willing to sell you. Sat Apr 10 00:10:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde On the other hand, there's this: http://lifehacker.com/5513520/make-a-cheap-stylus-for-ipad-and-other-touchscreen-devices Sat Apr 10 00:25:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Eek - does this mean Tweetie's going to get saddled with the native RTs in the desktop app, too? Sat Apr 10 04:20:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Actually, I've read reports that say that Google's aware the problem is on their end, and that they're working on it. Sat Apr 10 04:22:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Unitarians? Sun Apr 11 16:05:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @j_l_r Thanks! Sun Apr 11 19:57:06 +0000 2010 Have spent much of the last nine hours resisting the onset of operations of the stomach virus that I fear has got me. Ugh. Mon Apr 12 16:38:09 +0000 2010 Now trying to decide whether to go ahead and cancel the day or to keep fighting. Mon Apr 12 16:44:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Got confirmation from a Droid user that GDocs doesn't work on the Android mobile browser either; it's a Google-side issue. Mon Apr 12 20:48:48 +0000 2010 FYI: the iPad is thus far the world's greatest invention for the day home sick in bed. That is all. Mon Apr 12 23:08:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @noradio Typing gets quicker, quickly. Still some glitches, & I hate that the apostrophe is on the # screen, but with a little practice... RT @ebertchicago: Ken Nordine turns 90. The voice. http://j.mp/aArEm2 Tue Apr 13 18:01:21 +0000 2010 Since this has apparently become the moment to say so, knock 'em dead tomorrow, @jcmeloni. \(Not literally\. You know\.\) Tue Apr 13 18:50:17 +0000 2010 RT @brettbobley: Digital Humanities Symposium at Dartmouth, May 14th: http://bit.ly/9VoWcm // see also http://bit.ly/9VwVbT - I'll be there! Tue Apr 13 18:51:35 +0000 2010 You know what's amazing? Signing up to do something for free, because it's awesome, and then finding out afterward that they're paying you! Tue Apr 13 20:17:56 +0000 2010 You know what's way less awesome? Finding a huge project you'd forgotten about in your inbox. And it's due in two weeks. Tue Apr 13 20:29:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah i.e., have not blown oneself up yet. Tue Apr 13 22:53:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @s2ceball YAY! Looking forward to seeing you there! Wed Apr 14 23:05:00 +0000 2010 Seriously contemplating dumping a professional organization membership; haven't been to that conf in years & am unlikely to go soon. Wed Apr 14 23:57:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri If you are uncomfortable enough to ask, yes. Thu Apr 15 00:19:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @joguldi A relationship between the last two tweets?
