Happy to do anything I can to help. Fri Apr 16 19:11:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @anniedreshfield I can take zero credit; thank whoever produced the theme I'm using. I only wish I were that cool. Sat Apr 17 06:04:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @patrickgmj and @jcmeloni I almost left a comment on your post earlier today saying a slightly cooler variant of "Ooh! Me! Ooh! Me!" So I'm in. Sat Apr 17 06:06:33 +0000 2010 My delete key has stopped working in text input boxes in Chrome. What gives? Sat Apr 17 14:48:49 +0000 2010 And, like that, it's back. Go figure. Sat Apr 17 14:53:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattthomas, @mkgold and @jcmeloni Was in Palm Springs today so just got to see this thread, which is made of awesome. I may change my name. Sun Apr 18 17:54:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ AAAARGH! Sun Apr 18 20:46:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Two further thoughts: \(1\) Weird that it wouldn't work on Android, then, and \(2\) Have you tried Opera? Sun Apr 18 21:08:44 +0000 2010 Just took the first run/walk I've done in ages. Did you know that running is harder than walking? I'll check it out, definitely being one of those. Sun Apr 18 22:59:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Dude, I medalled in that. More on Planned Obsolescence to come during the panel this afternoon. #du10 Wed Apr 21 14:47:55 +0000 2010 @mediacommons user profile system built with the support of an NEH Digital Start-up Grant. #du10 Wed Apr 21 14:48:39 +0000 2010 Avi demoing Shakespeare Quarterly Open Review project at MediaCommons: http://bit.ly/askUFo #du10 Wed Apr 21 14:50:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @s2ceball Yup! Have published a little about the issue in ISQ but more forthcoming in print version's conclusion. http://bit.ly/aQEZtF #du10 Wed Apr 21 14:59:56 +0000 2010 doesn't understand why tenure and peer-review should continue as gatekeeping...doesn't seem a useful model to continue #du10 \(via @s2ceball\) Wed Apr 21 15:01:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Is the idea filtering instead of gatekeeping? Not that there's no assessment of quality, but that it's not done pre-pub. #du10 Wed Apr 21 15:03:21 +0000 2010 CHNM projects being presented by @foundhistory #du10 Wed Apr 21 15:04:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @ricksva gatekeeping = pre-pub review that determines what gets published, vs. filtering = post-pub review of quality/importance Wed Apr 21 15:06:34 +0000 2010 .@foundhistory: not just interested in technical platforms, but also hackability of social platforms \(conference, journal, library\) #du10 Wed Apr 21 15:12:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @ricksva Yes, but peer review is now a gatekeeping process, and I favor turning it into a post-pub filtering process, tenure entirely aside. Wed Apr 21 15:19:13 +0000 2010 Ben Vershbow on the history of the Institute for the Future of the Book's projects, incl Gamer Theory. http://bit.ly/90cwtX #du10 Wed Apr 21 15:24:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @ricksva Probably best to read the peer review chapter; it's a complex argument. http://bit.ly/37LV7P Wed Apr 21 15:32:29 +0000 2010 reviewing feed for #du10 #academicauthority panel: the underlying issue w digital t&p is lack of transparency. /via @s2ceball [Hell, yeah.] Wed Apr 21 16:00:00 +0000 2010 About to begin plenary panel "A Digital Future?" with @epistemographer, Phil Pochoda, & Ana Laura Martinez \(via Skype from Paraguay\!\) #du10 Wed Apr 21 17:37:41 +0000 2010 \(And me, in case that isn't clear\.\) #du10 Wed Apr 21 17:38:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @kfitz Thanks, @jcmeloni ! Wed Apr 21 17:43:31 +0000 2010 You're giving away trade secrets, @epistemographer. #du10 Wed Apr 21 17:44:04 +0000 2010 87 slides in 15-20 minutes; think I can do it? #du10 Wed Apr 21 17:45:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley I think you meant to say "Fitzpatrick style." I put a Creative Commons license on it, so Larry could remix it. Wed Apr 21 17:47:38 +0000 2010 Ana Laura Martinez was of course in Uruguay, not Paraguay. Stupid pre-presentation jitters. #du10 Wed Apr 21 18:07:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde 90% are 1-2 words. Wed Apr 21 18:09:15 +0000 2010 @epistemographer on "some library stuff that hopefully resonates" #du10 Wed Apr 21 18:09:44 +0000 2010 @epistemographer: need to find ways to combine internal/expert knowledge with external/crowdsourced knowledge. #du10 Wed Apr 21 18:14:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @mickimcgee Thanks! Wed Apr 21 18:52:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @aaronknoll Thanks! I agree- tho a UC study shows that the most conserv sector of academia now is tenuretrack asst profs \(too much to lose\) . Wed Apr 21 19:00:05 +0000 2010 In case you're keeping score: 87 slides; 19:47. Heh. #du10 Wed Apr 21 19:02:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri First question was abt the fate of online version of my project; second was which \(online/print\) would be authoritative. #du10 Wed Apr 21 19:09:59 +0000 2010 Q from @brettbobley for Phil Pochoda about press budget vs library budget for journal acquisitions. #du10 Wed Apr 21 19:10:38 +0000 2010 Phil Pochoda: there's money enough in unis to solve the crisis, but change won't come without a disaster first. #du10 Wed Apr 21 19:13:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenServenti Any Hershey's Dark? #du10 Wed Apr 21 19:50:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @mac734 Thank you! Wed Apr 21 19:50:41 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid's keynote at #du10 starting: "'The Classroom is Sacred: 21 Lessons About Digitization Without Commercialization" \(via @mkgold\) Wed Apr 21 22:41:14 +0000 2010 @sivavaid playing grumpy old man for purposes of tonight's talk #du10 Wed Apr 21 22:42:50 +0000 2010 @sivavaid: focusing on teaching; valuing both tradition and experimentation #du10 Wed Apr 21 22:43:58 +0000 2010 @sivavaid: The wrong reason to teach online is to save costs \(the right reason is to reach those students who can't reach you\) . #du10 Wed Apr 21 23:15:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Thank YOU, Matt; it was an amazing day. #du10 Thu Apr 22 04:56:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ooh! Did you write one on PHP? Learning it/MySQL properly is one of my summer projects! Thu Apr 22 12:07:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Eep. I should search before I ask! Was a knee-jerk resp bc I've enjoyed TYHTMLCSS so much that I want you to teach me more. Thu Apr 22 12:26:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Totally did not take it as snark; in fact, I thought "crud, should have searched" as soon as I hit send. :) Thu Apr 22 12:50:36 +0000 2010 Dear people who think you know how to do my job: I'd prefer it if you'd just go ahead and do it for me. Thanks! Fri Apr 23 21:59:34 +0000 2010 No really, I wasn't kidding. #grrrr Fri Apr 23 22:16:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @annehelen Hey, cool - what's the panel, and who's the presenter? Fri Apr 23 22:21:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara Take healthy fruit-based smoothie; add vodka. Blend. :) Fri Apr 23 22:22:43 +0000 2010 @billiehara PS: Send me one. Fri Apr 23 22:25:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah GRRRRRRR. Fri Apr 23 22:25:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @annehelen Oh, wonderful. So sorry not to be there! Fri Apr 23 22:33:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @billiehara You think there's an NEH SUG in that? Fri Apr 23 22:34:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara A very long straw. Fri Apr 23 22:37:31 +0000 2010 Okay, whatever it was I was so aggravated about yesterday, it was nothing that 9.5 hours of sleep couldn't cure. Or at least make me forget. Sat Apr 24 14:52:04 +0000 2010 The utterly insane Wednesday deadline has been turned into a manageable Monday dl, a reasonable Wednesday dl, and a crazy Sunday dl... [1/2] Sun Apr 25 19:46:04 +0000 2010 ...which, almost having met tomorrow's deadline, makes me want to take the afternoon off and watch last week's episode of Lost. [2/2] Sun Apr 25 19:46:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm still plugging along but the plugging is slowing a bit. Of course, my end of the web crisis was spent sitting & waiting. Sun Apr 25 20:22:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Must all begin to feel like déjà vu after a while! Sun Apr 25 21:02:33 +0000 2010 \(mega\) blogged: "Uninvited Guests:" http://is.gd/bHFKv \(regarding twitter at invitation\-only academic events\) /via @nowviskie Sun Apr 25 22:40:44 +0000 2010 Three to go, and then I'm talking a break. Phew. Sun Apr 25 23:28:33 +0000 2010 Taking a break, that is. But I'm talking about how much I need a break, I guess... Sun Apr 25 23:29:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Our pioneer forebears had these devices that required etched vinyl in order to produce sound. How quaint! Sun Apr 25 23:39:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Right there with you; would be a real game-changer. Sun Apr 25 23:42:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Yeah, trust me. Thank goodness for that spreadsheet. Mon Apr 26 00:21:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm all for your interpretation! Mon Apr 26 00:31:10 +0000 2010 You know what's completely awesome? When you forgot you promised someone you'd do something & then it lands on your desk this week. Sigh. Mon Apr 26 18:44:51 +0000 2010 I think this may be a recurring theme. Mon Apr 26 18:45:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Have I told you today how much you rock? I think I have failed to do so. Tue Apr 27 01:15:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni That probably seemed out of context; I was catching up & spotted you being encouraged in your retrain the world program! Tue Apr 27 01:38:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench, @captain_primate and @jcmeloni The heck with it. If the next week goes as it ought, the first round's on me. Tue Apr 27 04:04:22 +0000 2010 Simplenote now comes in an iPhone/iPad universal version. This is HUGE. Tue Apr 27 14:50:23 +0000 2010 Deadline 2 of 3 met. Deadline 3 is the killer. Wish me luck, everyone. Wed Apr 28 01:44:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Awesome. I really needed that! Wed Apr 28 01:48:01 +0000 2010 Catching up on FlashForward. Hard to suspend disbelief in the Hong Kong episode, since the airport is actually Ontario, 10 miles from here. Wed Apr 28 04:57:39 +0000 2010 Good grief. I thought I was doing well with this task, but I just did the math & figured out I'm precisely one item over halfway done. Wed Apr 28 14:05:49 +0000 2010 With effectively three days to go. Must. Move. Faster. Wed Apr 28 14:07:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality "old-fashioned iPods" is perhaps the best phrase I've read this morning. Wed Apr 28 15:32:15 +0000 2010 @amandafrench If the form you were working on was the macronym, what would you call the recursive acronym, e.g. GNU, WINE? Wed Apr 28 19:50:12 +0000 2010 223 down; 158 to go. And now I go teach for six hours. [/whinge] Wed Apr 28 20:07:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I shall crash the machinery of scholarly publishing through sheer overload. Mwa-ha-ha! Wed Apr 28 20:32:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Ooh! Would you mind sending it to me, too? My library doesn't carry it and I'm too impatient for ILL. Thu Apr 29 06:38:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Saw that in the middle of the Kindle book I was reading last night on my iPad - completely freaked me out. No idea it was there. Thu Apr 29 16:38:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Congratulations! Fri Apr 30 03:54:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Perhaps we should call them the "Oh god please let there be a" conferences. #futureofjournalism Fri Apr 30 16:49:39 +0000 2010 "Black Students Experience More Online Bias Than Whites." You don't say, @chronicle. http://bit.ly/awLRlC Fri Apr 30 20:37:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @Kylewarneck If you're around between 2 and 4, come by the MS reception! Fri Apr 30 20:39:46 +0000 2010 The season I refer to as "Death By Reception" begins in 3... 2... Fri Apr 30 20:52:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde There was at this one. Many of them, alas, are lemonade-only. And the same lemonade at all of them. Fri Apr 30 23:09:52 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 04 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @annehelen and @crsbecker All of mine are doing this, too, & makes me nuts. Has made me wonder if this is how HS are teaching transition. Sat May 01 15:22:55 +0000 2010 A mere 120 to go. \(One day soon, I hope to have something else of interest to say\.\) Sat May 01 17:58:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley and @jenserventi Dude, I brought my laser last time but they made me leave it outside the Old Post Office. Sun May 02 02:45:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley "It's okay, sir; I'm a digital humanist." I like it. Sun May 02 03:16:03 +0000 2010 Stopped for dinner with 74 to go. There was beer. Suspect I may not make it much further. Beginning to download for the plane. *sigh* Sun May 02 03:46:24 +0000 2010 Waiting to take off for IAH, on my way to NYC for MLA. Have a teeny bit of work to do on the way. Keep the coffee coming, Continental! Sun May 02 13:46:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Writey is definitely good. It's particularly appealing when you're facing moving house, or a stack of thankless admin tasks. Sun May 02 17:56:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Hahahahahahaha. Haha. Ha. Um. Mon May 03 11:11:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench You know, I was thinking of suggesting that to @mlaconvention; would certainly make for a more amusing process. Mon May 03 11:34:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench But then some smart aleck would figure out the URL shortener, and we'd be back where we started. Mon May 03 11:35:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattthomas One IS cognizant of one's privilege, thanks, as I do note in the post. Also, most such leaves are grant/fellowship funded. Mon May 03 19:25:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattthomas And I take that point. But some grad students are on non-teaching fellowships, so it's not a grad/faculty divide. Mon May 03 19:39:02 +0000 2010 If someone told you you were in a "d&d free workplace," what would you take that to mean? Mon May 03 23:31:03 +0000 2010 That's what *I* thought! Mon May 03 23:50:22 +0000 2010 Congratulations, @cgbrooke! Collin Brooke wins Computers & Composition Distinguished Book Award for Lingua Fracta! \(via @kakennedy\) Tue May 04 01:09:56 +0000 2010 So, on the "d&d free" issue: I immediately leapt to d20s too, but it turns out that the reference was way more 1980s personal ads than that. Tue May 04 01:13:09 +0000 2010 HA! \(Wish I could elaborate, but can't just yet\. Soon, I swear\.\) Tue May 04 02:25:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Wrong 1980s. Think craigslist, but earlier. Tue May 04 02:34:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell It's even better than that, though it does rely on a delicious ambiguity between vindication and vindictiveness. Tue May 04 02:36:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Unofficially, yes. I'll announce once it's official... Tue May 04 02:58:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Fantastic - yes! I'll look forward to it. \(Alas, I have to leave Dartmouth abt 4 for my flight, but it'll be great to see you\.\) Tue May 04 03:04:54 +0000 2010 Departing LGA for IAH after two exhausting and exhilarating days at the MLA. Now to catch up on everything I let drop for the last 2 weeks. Tue May 04 20:44:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench and @sivavaid It's increased Kindle sales to me by infinity percent. Before iPad, 0; since, 3, all bought bc unavailable in iBooks Thu May 06 07:05:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory That was the very first album I ever bought. I look back on that with amazement, being like 11 & devoid of taste at the time. Thu May 06 15:22:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Actually, I don't find it embarrassing - pretty astonishing to have my first album be one I'd still be willing to listen to. Thu May 06 15:41:44 +0000 2010 Feeling rilly sentimental about the end of the semester. Not just that I'm going on leave - I'll miss these particular classes. A lot. Fri May 07 02:19:29 +0000 2010 Do you know if the order in which schools were admitted to "thefacebook.com" during its first year or so is available anywhere? #lazyweb Fri May 07 19:25:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @billwolff That one's more or less what I know: Harvard, Stanford, Yale, Columbia > Ivies > whoever. I'm curious about the order of whoever. Fri May 07 20:04:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @billwolff But honestly, it doesn't really matter; I'm getting myself bogged down in a side point. :-/ Thanks anyhow! Fri May 07 20:05:52 +0000 2010 Ohmygoodness, whataweek. Sat May 08 06:29:37 +0000 2010 Editing together video for a talk I won't be here to give on Friday. Starting to think I may have made this more complicated than necessary. Sat May 08 21:27:04 +0000 2010 Suddenly feel... what is this bizarre sensation? As though I'm actually on top of all my deadlines? As though panic is not imminent? Weird. Mon May 10 00:03:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Okay, that worked a little better than I hope you actually meant it to. \(Instant brain response: Crap\! Which one?\!?\) Mon May 10 00:05:49 +0000 2010 Am officially at the point in The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo at which I'm starting to drag my heels, not wanting it to end. Mon May 10 02:14:21 +0000 2010 And yes, that means I'm reading a novel tonight. So there. Mon May 10 02:14:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Yes, absolutely. The best kind of work! Mon May 10 04:38:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie We have 0 from dept. All $ is awarded at the college level. In prev yrs, fac got 2 funded trips; this yr, 1. \(I self\-funded 4\.\) Mon May 10 13:43:54 +0000 2010 The semester must be nearly over because I'm thinking about things like the relative merits of DevonThink and Evernote. Mon May 10 15:30:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Your post that mentioned Evernote on the iPad has me thinking about switching. DT is super powerful, but w/too much overhead. Mon May 10 15:51:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @janineutell DT is another info mgt tool, in some ways way more powerful than Evernote \(contextual searching etc\) , but maybe a bit too much. Mon May 10 15:52:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate One word for you: Scantron. \(Seriously, you are a better man than I; there's no way I could manage that\.\) Mon May 10 20:16:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate I get that. I frown on them too, but SHEESH already. Mon May 10 20:25:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate I definitely hear that - I spent 11 yrs split btw 2 depts, and we never get TAs. But we are lucky to have small classes! Mon May 10 20:40:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate I can't imagine having large classes with that much grading and no TA support. I send you speedy grading vibes. Mon May 10 20:41:19 +0000 2010 New blog post: Time Will Tell, But Epistemology Won't: The Richard Rorty Archive http://bit.ly/czqLiT Mon May 10 22:32:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold I finished it last night; it pays off quite well. Will look forward to hearing what you think. Tue May 11 03:34:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold They are, aren't they? I loved that. Tue May 11 03:42:59 +0000 2010 This is why I don't commute to L.A. http://yfrog.com/2g9m1j Tue May 11 15:42:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings I did the same, but finally caved - the iPad version is really nice. But I still resent the triple charge. Wed May 12 14:02:05 +0000 2010 Seriously contemplating a switch from DT to Evernote; like the ease of use & low overhead. But need a fan to tell me: should I go premium? Wed May 12 17:17:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @budtheteacher That's nice to know, thanks! Wed May 12 17:26:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Actually, that's a good point; I'm going to be moving a lot of stuff. And I like the any-file-format feature... Wed May 12 17:27:43 +0000 2010 The one thing that's irritating me so far about Evernote is that txt is a premium-only file format. Really? Wed May 12 17:35:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings I've got THOUSANDS of them. Okay, hundreds. But still. May upgrade just so I can drag & drop. Wed May 12 17:38:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Ooh! Tell me how you like it - I've been contemplating one too! Wed May 12 17:38:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Yes, of course; the challenge is wanting to get my hundreds of txt files *into* Evernote. Wed May 12 17:51:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @michaeldroy DevonThink - amazingly powerful information/document manager, but way more than I really need & w/o Evernote's syncing ability. Wed May 12 18:52:53 +0000 2010 Sigh. That was me: Found nifty txt/rtf-to-Evernote importing script: http://bit.ly/a7IiFZ Wed May 12 19:00:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlmcgill It's more for notetaking than for citation organization... Wed May 12 21:10:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @philnel About the same time "relatable" became a word, I think. *sigh* Thu May 13 04:39:29 +0000 2010 Onboard, awaiting takeoff. Will be my first flight with wifi. Join us in the 21st century, Continental! Um, United. Uninental? Continited? Thu May 13 13:56:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Go, Julie! Thu May 13 14:14:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah That sounds about right. Thu May 13 17:05:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Ooh, let me know if you get good recs. I'm not planning on trading the 12-yr-old Civic in any time soon but wish it were iPodable. Thu May 13 18:53:10 +0000 2010 Have gotten just about everything from one database migrated from DevonThink to Evernote. Some cleaning up to do, but seems good. Thu May 13 18:57:20 +0000 2010 5 hour flight with wifi went by in a flash. 3 hour drive thereafter, a bit less so. Thank goodness it's gorgeous out there. Thu May 13 22:48:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander So sorry to have missed today's session! Hanover says hi, though. Thu May 13 22:49:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Hey there. Yeah, I'm here - let's definitely plan on lunch! Fri May 14 01:10:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Soap operas! Fri May 14 01:10:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @ironmanx28 NO!!! I'm... horrified. Furious! How can this possibly be? I'm so sorry, Steve... Fri May 14 01:12:05 +0000 2010 Dartmouth Digital Humanities symposium beginning. No word of a hashtag. I vote for #dighum, which produces nothing on search right now. Fri May 14 13:05:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell and @mandellc That would work. Or #DartDH, which frees up a few extra characters. Fri May 14 13:07:47 +0000 2010 #DartDH is livestreaming at http://bit.ly/DartDigHumStream - program at http://www.dartmouth.edu/~dighum/program.html Fri May 14 13:11:44 +0000 2010 Mary Flanagan kicking off #DartDH with "The Digital Humanities Today." Fri May 14 13:16:52 +0000 2010 Mary Flanagan on multiplicity of DH: "The day we define it in a lockstep way is the day I'm working in a new field." #DartDH Fri May 14 13:19:53 +0000 2010 @brettbobley Will do! Fri May 14 13:44:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @d_kompare *sigh* Was talking with @jmittell and didn't realize I should have been ready for my closeup! Fri May 14 14:03:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @ninabeth Whoops. Guess I should have realized that that big camera right in front of me might be live. #DartDH Fri May 14 14:04:29 +0000 2010 Tom Streeter up now at #DartDH, talking about his forthcoming book, The Net Effect \(from my friends at @NYUPress\) . Fri May 14 14:10:59 +0000 2010 Streeter: most powerful force maintainin openness of internet is memory of the romantic stories told about the internet's openness #DartDH Fri May 14 14:33:20 +0000 2010 Murray: fears among some that if we permit electronic publishing, it'll lead to general erosion of standards \(peer review, quality\) #DartDH Fri May 14 14:40:45 +0000 2010 Sigh. Remembering why I don't like presenting on a machine other than mine. That was... awkward. Fri May 14 19:19:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Are you watching? Thanks for the kind words. Felt worse than it was, I hope. Fri May 14 19:25:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Thank you. At several points I was quite concerned about that - kept losing my own thread in trying to see my presenter notes! Fri May 14 19:33:32 +0000 2010 On the plane, on my way back to LA. Fantastic day at #DartDH, though, as Echo might say, I wasn't my best. Happily, everyone else rocked. Sat May 15 01:12:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Same for #rorty! If only that two-places-at-once thing really worked. Sat May 15 01:22:11 +0000 2010 Okay, enough with the bumpity. New altitude, please. \(This inflight internet is awesome for playing backseat pilot\.\) Sat May 15 01:29:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus It's probably just as well that I haven't been able to blog/tweet from a plane before now! Sat May 15 01:40:39 +0000 2010 Somewhere over Missouri. This has been a bumpy day. Hanging on for Sunday evening. Sat May 15 03:25:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @philnel About to enter Kansan airspace. So far so good. \(Have a good trip\!\) Sat May 15 03:49:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah This freaking semester, and everything that has gone along with it, will be OVER. Sat May 15 03:51:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Though I had a rather lengthy debate about whether Sunday evening was the demarcator I'm looking for. Really middle of next week. Sat May 15 03:52:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I'm only 15 hours behind on the Twitter stream, but I was required to swear that you'd show up in person for #mla11. Tue May 18 17:11:25 +0000 2010 New blog post: A Little \(Self\-\) Promotion http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/a-little-self-promotion/ Tue May 18 17:33:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Oh, just wait; I'll be complaining about the kids today and their music any second now. :) Tue May 18 17:49:54 +0000 2010 Thanks, all - I'm pretty happy about it, needless to say! As I told the @profhacker crew, first round at @THATCamp is on me. Tue May 18 17:52:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen You like me! You really like me! Tue May 18 17:53:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman You did, indeed. Thanks much! Tue May 18 18:22:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Yeah, you told me about that! Mine should in theory provide an increase in salary, but probably an increase in committee work too. Tue May 18 20:17:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Thanks, Brett; I'm thrilled, needless to say! Tue May 18 20:18:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @fearv And a pipe with a very rich tobacco. Tue May 18 20:22:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @jasonrhody Thanks, Jason! Tue May 18 20:31:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @granolasusan and @jcmeloni I hear that; I marvel at productive folks with kids. Cats are almost more than I can manage, some days. Tue May 18 20:33:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @CapitolClio Thank you - that means a lot to me! Tue May 18 20:34:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui Thanks, Barbara - see you soon, I hope? Tue May 18 20:34:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui Yes, definitely. See you there! Tue May 18 20:47:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui Oh, that's just crazy talk! Tue May 18 20:47:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Oh yes, all three! There will be much celebration... \(And thanks\!\) Tue May 18 22:11:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave, @digitalhumanist and @samplereality My flight gets into DCA at 7pm Friday; if there's Auld Shebeening after 8ish, let me know... Wed May 19 04:56:27 +0000 2010 I know I'm supposed to sneer at Wave, but both of my spring classes used it to very good effect. I'm happy that accounts are now open. Wed May 19 16:35:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Pretty much "it didn't save the world after all; ergo, it sucks." Wed May 19 16:48:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella I think perhaps a @ProfHacker post on just that topic may be in the offing. Wed May 19 17:09:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie I think the iPad has the benefit of Steve's RDF; one you've actually used one, sneering at it becomes much more difficult. Wed May 19 17:10:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @val_forrestal http://mashable.com/2010/02/01/google-chrome-greasemonkey-2/ Wed May 19 21:47:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @val_forrestal LOL. Literally. Wed May 19 21:50:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid I use iAnnotate, which ROCKS. Syncs with a designated folder on your hard drive. Thu May 20 22:24:17 +0000 2010 How much do I hate the IT assumption that they can manage my work computer better than I can? A lot, that's how much. Thu May 20 22:59:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave and @briancroxall I get into DCA at 7ish. Keep me posted on any whereabouts... Fri May 21 02:16:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall and @academicdave Sounds good to me. Haven't a clue where that was, though. Fri May 21 02:29:26 +0000 2010 Trying to decide if I want to try traveling with the iPad only, instead of bringing the laptop, too. Think this might not be the right trip. Fri May 21 05:39:12 +0000 2010 IAH, on my way to DCA. It always annoys me to fly between these airports but at least there's no 7ft rakish statue of ol'whatsisname at DCA. Fri May 21 17:24:53 +0000 2010 I don't suppose that an airport gift shop would have actual cold & sinus meds \(the pseudoephedrine kind, not the weaksauce replacement\) ...? Fri May 21 18:53:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @KelliMarshall The way my sinuses feel right now, it'd be worth it. I'll go hunt them down! Fri May 21 19:08:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Are you sticking around thereafter? Fri May 21 19:09:11 +0000 2010 Rats; no dice. Only the PE stuff. Thanks for messing it up for everyone, meth industry! Fri May 21 19:35:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Rats. Well, I'll wave at you from the Metro. Fri May 21 19:52:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell I'd feel better about the trade if I could breathe while watching it. Fri May 21 19:54:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @kbusse2 and @kellimarshall No kidding. Had an interesting convo w/ a pharm once, who thinks the log issue is attempting to regulate sales [1/2] Fri May 21 19:57:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @kbusse2 and @kellimarshall rather than purchase - that the prob was pharms selling it out the back door & now they have to be accountable. Fri May 21 19:58:51 +0000 2010 At DCA metro, waiting what will apparently be 12min for the next blue line train. Don't drink all the beer before I get there, thatcampers. Fri May 21 23:20:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Excellent. I'm about 5 mins from Vienna; will come out after checking into hotel. Fri May 21 23:55:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlxJrvs You made it! Sorry to hear about your crap travel day. Sat May 22 03:13:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Are you gone? If you can hang on a couple I'll pack up & join you. If not, I'll see you there! Sat May 22 11:36:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Be right there! Sat May 22 11:39:01 +0000 2010 #THATCamp is getting started! @dancohen giving us a bit of history and a bit of looking forward. Sat May 22 13:39:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall and @academicdave Nobody except @alxjrvs, who is stubbornly insisting that he likes his. Sat May 22 13:41:13 +0000 2010 Rules of #thatcamp: no grandstanding, no presentations, no slides, no spectators, no hierarchy. On the positive sides: hugs are approved. Sat May 22 13:43:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @joguldi Are you in the house? If so, yay! #thatcamp Sat May 22 13:45:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Can confirm, except that it was more of a "holy crap" moment. Sat May 22 13:46:56 +0000 2010 Will confess much as I love my iPad that I'm remembering why I still travel with the MBAir: hate not having notes and Twitter open at once. Sat May 22 13:52:09 +0000 2010 Unfortunately, most of what I've written over the last 2 years could qualify for #hackacad; I'm having a hard time deciding what to submit. Sat May 22 14:00:31 +0000 2010 @patrickgmj leading #thatcamp session "Who wants to be a hacker?" Answer: me! A major goal of this sabbatical, in fact. Sat May 22 14:59:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlxJrvs I'm in. #thatcamp Sat May 22 15:05:08 +0000 2010 Only one session of #thatcamp so far and already my head is buzzing. Really looking forward to dreaming up more projects. Sat May 22 15:42:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @johnmjones Whoops! At least you're not having to listen to my head-ringtone. #thatcamp Sat May 22 15:47:31 +0000 2010 Dork shorts at #thatcamp! So far, me, @patrickgmj, @alxjrvs, David Staley. Sat May 22 16:26:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @historying Sadly, no; keyboard cat has been replaced with an iPad dinger. But the interface - it's almost like magic! Sat May 22 16:33:27 +0000 2010 Here comes the @BaronessElsa! #thatcamp Sat May 22 16:38:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Maybe, but it's about a streaming long manhood. What kind of followers is she after? #thatcamp Sat May 22 16:47:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @kimonizer Let's do it! #thatcamp Sat May 22 17:16:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlxJrvs From the design patterns room: 1 iPad \(that I can see\) , 3 netbooks, 8 MacBooks, 5 Windows laptops, 1 Moleskine. #thatcamp Sat May 22 17:36:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Didn't realize you were behind me until I saw your tweet! #thatcamp Sat May 22 17:38:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I've been using Twitterific; really hated the Tweetdeck client. Sat May 22 18:06:10 +0000 2010 Open Peer Review session Google Doc: http://bit.ly/anFtEM #thatcamp Sat May 22 18:48:00 +0000 2010 Trying that Open Peer Review Google Doc link again; should be open now. http://bit.ly/anFtEM #thatcamp Sat May 22 19:05:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Sorry; I'll try harder! #thatcamp Sat May 22 19:42:14 +0000 2010 RT: "For the most liberal part of the academy, we're the most conservative re: evaluating alternative publishing" --@dancohen #thatcamp Sat May 22 19:49:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost Not sure I follow you on "questioning impact." The "we" was humanists, who prefer binary up or down peer rev to the mush of impact. Sat May 22 20:13:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @joguldi Cool! I need to know more about Arcade & how it works... #thatcamp Sat May 22 20:14:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I'll never share my hard-earned #mla09 t-shirt! But I'll let adoring fans, um, adore. Sat May 22 23:27:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @ddchamberlain not immediately; bar first. Come with! Sun May 23 00:11:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @ddchamberlain Thats my plan; the trick will be keeping someone sober enough \(or finding a cab\) . Sun May 23 00:22:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Alas, tonight! Am staying at a hotel in DC. Am at the Auld Shebeen right now, tho. Sun May 23 01:56:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I thought it was just because I'm REALLY fabulous. Sun May 23 01:56:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Indeed! We'll have to find another opportunity to plot, I hope before #mla11. Sun May 23 02:03:03 +0000 2010 Troubleshooting tips from my hotel: if your browser will not display web pages & your laptop has an on/ off switch make sure it's turned on. Sun May 23 04:19:35 +0000 2010 Yay, I have a suitcase! This will make all. the. difference. At least to those around me tomorrow! Sun May 23 04:40:28 +0000 2010 In other news, vaguely certain that the hotel front desk staff think some illicit business is being conducted in my room. Sun May 23 04:42:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Me checking in without a suitcase and then having one delivered to me out front 30 mins later, at 12.45 am. Sun May 23 04:57:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Which is to say there's No Telling what I'm up to in here. Sun May 23 04:58:34 +0000 2010 Like @patrickgmj, could not get to sleep for anything last night. Up until after 3, jangling with the excitement of #thatcamp! Sun May 23 12:51:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @joguldi I'd support that proposition. #thatcamp Sun May 23 16:50:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @joguldi Hmm. Good question; I'll need to look into it! #thatcamp Sun May 23 16:53:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui and @jcmeloni Yes, yes, yes! Sun May 23 17:16:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlxJrvs No worries - will look forward to more soon! #thatcamp Sun May 23 18:17:22 +0000 2010 Safe trips home, #thatcamp-ers. See many of you soon, I hope! Sun May 23 19:32:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana Yeah, was at THATCamp yesterday; today & tomorrow at a meeting in DC. Were you at C19? Sun May 23 19:38:52 +0000 2010 Realizing that I will in fact have to miss the #LOST finale due to a meeting dinner. Will I have to avoid Twitter until I can watch it? Sun May 23 21:11:00 +0000 2010 And how long before ABC will have it available for streaming? Sun May 23 21:11:25 +0000 2010 The new Tantalus: I have a bottle of wine. It has a plastic cork. The crap corkscrew it came with cannot make it budge. Sun May 23 21:39:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen So sorry I had to miss today; yesterday was the best first half of #thatcamp ever. Thanks to you and the rest of @chnm for it! Sun May 23 21:47:21 +0000 2010 Saved by the bellguys downstairs. Have glass of wine and Stieg Larsson for an hour's relaxation. Sun May 23 21:49:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Very sorry you won't be joining us, too! It was a productive day today. I'll look forward to more scheming with you soon. Sun May 23 22:01:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana THATCamp was awesome; journal meeting has been too. Don't fly back to SoCal until Tuesday, tho... Sun May 23 22:09:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Fantastic seeing you, if not enough. I'll look forward to more at #dh2010 and #sci8! Sun May 23 22:10:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana Definitely! Sun May 23 22:21:30 +0000 2010 First submission for #hackacad: "The Stakes of Disciplinarity" http://bit.ly/cqKeY6 Sun May 23 22:22:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall You mean the whiteboard version? #thatcamp Sun May 23 22:40:13 +0000 2010 Okay, off to dinner. Pls to get all of your #LOST live-tweeting out of your system before I come back. Or hope I can watch tonight. Sun May 23 22:56:07 +0000 2010 Back from amazing dinner, waaaaay too late for Lost & even more too early for streaming. Am doomed to avoid Internet for foreseeable future. Mon May 24 02:37:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate Fantastic getting to meet you & spend some time over dinner. I'll hope for more soon! Mon May 24 02:38:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana That's really driven by your doc rather than by Zotero. Word lets you have notes restart numbering in each chapter... Mon May 24 19:11:26 +0000 2010 spending the year at NYU, so we'll definitely catch up then, if not before. Sorry to bag so late! Mon May 24 20:56:03 +0000 2010 Wow, that was awesome. Mon May 24 21:01:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell No, it was the message that started out as a DM and ended as a live tweet. Whoops. \(Am in mid\-huluing of finale now, tho\.\) Mon May 24 21:41:48 +0000 2010 Uncertain when I ate the box of razorblades, or why, exactly, but am pretty sure it wasn't a good idea. #ugh Mon May 24 22:35:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @LookBackMaps Oh, that is so not good to hear. I have to be on a plane tomorrow at noon. Glad to hear you're better, though! Mon May 24 23:03:28 +0000 2010 Hoping you'll all forgive or ignore the following tweet. Tue May 25 01:24:10 +0000 2010 Am seriously hoping that the reverse peristalsis portion of today's festivities is over. But, alas, am not optimistic about it. #iamsick :( Tue May 25 01:25:54 +0000 2010 PS: this is not the best day I've ever spent in a hotel room. And I do not look forward to attempting to fly home tomorrow. Tue May 25 01:27:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm You are so kind! I'm actually in DC, and the roomsvc guys have brought me gingerale. Could be much worse! Tue May 25 01:35:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm thanks so much! Tue May 25 01:47:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen You & me both, I assure you. Tue May 25 01:47:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @TheRepoRat Good grief. I'll stop whining! This is a fiesta by comparison. Tue May 25 01:48:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Thanks so much, Brett! \(Was going to attempt a joke about government in action, but can't quite put it together\.\) Tue May 25 02:16:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Thank you so much! Mostly just hoping to fall asleep now. Tue May 25 02:17:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Hmm. I would be a little worried about what the process might turn the ginger ale into by the time it got approved. Tue May 25 02:23:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @KelliMarshall Never did get the Sudafed. Happily, my sinuses cleared up. Cannot say much for the rest of me. Tue May 25 02:53:49 +0000 2010 Dear digestive tract: awesome D! Way to show those microbes who's boss. But here's the thing, I swear: there is NOTHING left in you. Chill. Tue May 25 04:30:35 +0000 2010 I seem to have survived. Not exactly planning on rushing out and grabbing a cheeseburger, but I think I'll be able to fly home. Tue May 25 13:52:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Alas, yes; I need to watch it again. I think I liked it, and I'm pretty sure the puking was unrelated. But I need to check. :) Tue May 25 13:57:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Me too! I prefer to be left alone when I'm sick, but there's alone and then there's *alone*. Tue May 25 13:58:45 +0000 2010 Have drunk some ginger ale and eaten a cracker. The world has not come to an end, though it was a little iffy there for a minute. Tue May 25 16:44:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Ugh, the mere thought. I'm sticking with the ginger ale and crackers for the time being. But thanks. Tue May 25 16:54:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @jonathanagray No worries there - trust me. Tue May 25 17:39:09 +0000 2010 Have made it all the way home without incident. Nonetheless, flying immediately after so much incident was no fun at all. Do NOT recommend. Wed May 26 03:24:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Would like to propose draft of ch5, "The University," but not sure whether that would work. http://bit.ly/cJqSFv #hackacad Wed May 26 14:35:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @kakennedy Happened to my mother once. 2 mo later, we found a pkg of \(no longer\) frozen broccoli in the trunk -had fallen out of a groc bag. Wed May 26 17:45:12 +0000 2010 Remember yesterday when I said that I'd apparently survived? Spoke too soon. I promise to avoid any further details. Wed May 26 17:53:56 +0000 2010 I am eating rice. Thu May 27 00:58:19 +0000 2010 In other news, I've decided that living alone makes tweeting while sick too tempting, & probably both tedious and icky for one's followers. Thu May 27 01:08:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Cautious, nothing! Me, white rice, and diluted Gatorade; it's a regular party in here. Thu May 27 01:15:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Thanks so much, Liz - I'm hoping I'm really on the mend! Thu May 27 01:21:59 +0000 2010 Whoa, new feature added to recent Chrome build: what you see when your secure connection to a website fails. http://yfrog.com/bgo1pp Thu May 27 01:24:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Whoa. You go. So to speak. Thu May 27 01:32:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana Hahahahahaha! Coke used to do the trick for me, but somehow the acid was a problem this time. Am now on gatorade & sparkling water. Thu May 27 01:33:34 +0000 2010 I am nothing short of astonished by how much better I feel. Go white rice! Thu May 27 04:34:34 +0000 2010 RT @ProfHacker: New at ProfHacker: @kfitz looks around "After the Review": http://bit.ly/cGyn08 Thu May 27 14:17:06 +0000 2010 Out of bed today, and pretty much recovered, but after nearly 72 hours on ginger ale, 4 crackers, and 2 bowls of rice, a little dopey. Thu May 27 17:33:54 +0000 2010 So it's a housekeeping day around these parts, taking care of mindless stuff I've been putting off. Hope to return to the big thoughts tmw. Thu May 27 17:34:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Not many of us use 3G on our laptops. If a certain unnamed smartphone service provider would allow tethering already, maybe. Thu May 27 17:45:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall and @rachaelsullivan Oh my word there has to be a better way. Which is to say I'd rather never fit in my favorite pants again. Thu May 27 17:45:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention 3G is utterly inaccessible in most conf rooms in the Bonaventure, if #mla11 sessions will be there. Other hotels may vary. Thu May 27 18:01:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall SCMS was there in March. Trust me when I say Do Not Want. Much better just to visit - go to the revolving bar up top! Thu May 27 18:06:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Conv ctr/Marriott may not be bad for 3G. And both are on Boingo network for those who subscribe \(aka, me\) . Thu May 27 18:11:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Awful. A case study in "you can't get there from here." It's totally worth seeing, but not a good place to have a conference. Thu May 27 18:13:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall On top of which, I just found it oppressive somehow. Never really had a space piss me off before, but this one did. Thu May 27 18:14:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I wonder what gives you that impression. Thu May 27 18:16:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @zachwhalen Mostly. But the other issue is that there are NO good lines of sight in the hotel; trying to find someone in the lobby is awful. Thu May 27 18:29:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @zachwhalen Requires lots of walking in circles. And if they're walking in circles, looking for you... Thu May 27 18:30:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention See F. Jameson, Postmodernism: a most disorienting hotel, for which one needs a seriously good cognitive GPS device. Thu May 27 19:01:00 +0000 2010 RT @zota: BP lied about the size of the spill. Arco is a BP brand. Just throwing that info out there, west coast gas buyers. Thu May 27 19:01:28 +0000 2010 RT @zota: BP lied about the size of the spill. Arco is a BP brand. Just throwing that info out there, west coast gas buyers. Thu May 27 19:01:52 +0000 2010 Sorry for the double RT; Tweetie lied and said the first one failed. Thu May 27 19:05:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Actually, that was Portuguese, which is even weirder. Fri May 28 01:05:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Brilliant news! #hackacad Fri May 28 15:51:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @Mdigitalculture Congrats, and YAY! #hackacad Fri May 28 15:52:29 +0000 2010 Having one of those mornings in which I want to be working on twelve things at once; not doing well at prioritizing in linear fashion. Fri May 28 15:55:34 +0000 2010 Almost forgot to submit for #hackacad: On Open Access Publishing http://bit.ly/4KKCj1 Fri May 28 15:58:53 +0000 2010 Am taking advantage of this week's forced de-caffeination to undo my coffee habit. White tea this morning, which I forgot how much I love. Fri May 28 16:02:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp No, I haven't, but I've heard good things. I'll have to test it out! Fri May 28 17:05:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @bighandsome So, so sorry to hear that. But know that PO Media Studies loves you! Fri May 28 18:01:57 +0000 2010 Submitted for #hackacad & building on @dancohen and @sramsay: "Open Access Publishing and Scholarly Values \(part 3\) " http://bit.ly/ad4nxX Fri May 28 19:02:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Sounds like a plan - thanks! Fri May 28 19:15:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Thanks! Fri May 28 19:31:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Ooh, one thing, though: it's Fitzpatrick. \(Someday I will convince the world of the superiority of Fitzpatrick over Fitzgerald\.\) Fri May 28 19:46:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay No worries; it's an easy and understandable slip, which I get all the time. Stupid F. Scott. Fri May 28 19:55:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Also Katherine rather than Kathleen, but I consider that anti-Irish bias. Fri May 28 19:56:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @bighandsome Absolutely! Was great to have you out last month, if too briefly, and in the midst of chaos. We'll have to do it again soon. Fri May 28 20:00:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost Related to the substitution of material form for impact that you & I have been talking about, I think. Fri May 28 20:24:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus I agree that junior scholars can/will/should take initiative on OA, etc, but I still think senior folks must take the lead [1/2] Fri May 28 20:48:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus in ensuring that those junior scholars are supported in their work and that their work is read and evaluated appropriately. [2/2] Fri May 28 20:49:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Hey, thanks for the "best of" nomination! Let me know if you have thoughts about what you might want & how I can help. Fri May 28 21:44:40 +0000 2010 Why is the thing I'm most alarmed by in the Gary Coleman story that he was MY AGE? \(And did I expect him to be older or younger? Not sure\.\) Fri May 28 21:57:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @BidenPC Except that it's not "oh my god, he died at 42?" but "oh my god, he was 42?" It's less about mortality than literally his age. Fri May 28 22:10:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri and @nowviskie et al: this is exactly the thing I'm talking about; not that it's unsafe for jr folks to pub in OA/alt venues [1/2] Fri May 28 23:25:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri and @nowviskie but that sr folks like @RayS6 @sramsay et al have to make the argument re the importance of doing so in the rev proc. Fri May 28 23:26:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I'm torn between feeling HUGELY flattered and mildly old. Fri May 28 23:33:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I will happily accept that! Do let me know, though, if you want me to make some selection \(s\) . Fri May 28 23:41:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @BidenPC Don't get me wrong - 42 is *super* young, and I've got a ton more to do. But I was surprised that my shock wasn't about mortality! Sat May 29 00:20:40 +0000 2010 Why do I find it so hard to let the registration on a domain I don't use expire? Sat May 29 15:06:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @ddiamantaras Interesting nonetheless - thanks! Sat May 29 15:14:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @zachwhalen Congratulations! Sat May 29 15:16:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana Damn, it all comes back to that, doesn't it. Sat May 29 17:15:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @tmcphers Actually, the last time I let one go, I needed it again 2 yrs later, and it's been held hostage since. That may be it. Sat May 29 17:54:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Something like that. It's also the fear that I might need it later & not be able to get it back \(as happened with another one\) . Sat May 29 18:04:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Hurts to say: mediacommons. I think I had .org, .net, & .com. But I thot, that name will never work! All are being held hostage now. Sat May 29 18:18:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah No kidding. So electrapress is up for renewal, and tho it's been out of service for 3+ yrs, I'm afraid to let it go. Sat May 29 18:26:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Exactly. I've been debating for a month and can't decide. I think it's going to get flushed, but... ugh. Sat May 29 18:32:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @patrickgmj Fan-TAS-tic! I'm very excited; keep me posted. Sun May 30 01:43:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @patrickgmj Yes, of course! I look forward to testing and expect to have many brilliant if technically impossible suggestions. :) Sun May 30 01:50:11 +0000 2010 Looking at a very complex set of tickets for #dh2010 and #sci8, with apt-hunting time in NYC. This is going to be difficult and non-cheap. Sun May 30 02:26:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @zachwhalen and @patrickgmj Ooh, that's exciting to hear! I have to wait for the NYU developers come back from the weekend before I can play. :( Sun May 30 02:28:02 +0000 2010 "'having two identities...is an example of a lack of integrity' is sentiment of someone who’s never had to code-switch" http://bit.ly/c91co7 Sun May 30 04:03:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah and @kwazana I'm moving back & forth between goofing on the computer and clearing stuff off the DVR... Sun May 30 04:30:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate Poor Sam! Recent experience has made me profoundly empathetic. Here's hoping the rest of the family emerges unscathed. Sun May 30 17:30:07 +0000 2010 Revising is hard. Sun May 30 18:14:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana Certainly like self-surgery. "Okay, if I cut myself open here, and remove this organ, and suture this to that, will I still work?" Sun May 30 18:20:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana A bit too in the thick of it at the moment, but let's do swap soon! Sun May 30 18:36:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Wha? They're already ground! If that can break a garbage disposal, what's a garbage disposal for? Mon May 31 15:15:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @patrickgmj Completely AWESOME. Have left a few comments therein. I am increasingly excited about this. Mon May 31 15:27:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana For doss, I'd go with whatever you find easiest. Prob start at 1 for each chapter, yes. Mon May 31 17:00:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwazana I use NeoOffice, which is an open source ms-office-like suite. It's totally compatible with Word, but yeah, don't switch now. Mon May 31 17:17:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges Super cool! \(Though I keep looking for the button on the video interface that will make everyone stop interrupting you\.\) Mon May 31 21:12:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @billwolff Congratulations! Mon May 31 21:58:22 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 05 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @alexcho47 I can take no credit! It's all Avi and the curators. But thanks for spreading the word. Tue Jun 01 17:48:17 +0000 2010 RT @ProfHacker: New at ProfHacker: @kfitz discusses her experiences "Teaching with Google Wave" http://bit.ly/bCmyz4 Tue Jun 01 22:36:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate Hey, she's just ahead of her time; the original URL was thefacebook.com. http://bit.ly/bcCWnO Wed Jun 02 01:14:27 +0000 2010 Hey, I'm apparently @mkirschenbaum's doppelganger; I'll be addressing the ADE West in 3 weeks on digital scholarship & tenure/promotion. Wed Jun 02 14:42:29 +0000 2010 RT @amandafrench: CFP: Undergraduate \(yes, undergrad\!\) Digital Humanities Symposium at Bryn Mawr/Haverford w/@kfitz: http://bit.ly/bv1mp5 Wed Jun 02 16:00:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah You can still get grandfathered in if you get a plan before the 7th, I think. Wed Jun 02 16:34:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Oh... hey! How about that! :) Wed Jun 02 16:35:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah It's a good q, esp on the iPad. I apparently only used ~67MB on the iPhone in April, but I'm not sure how typical that is of my use. Wed Jun 02 16:57:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah ~135MB in March, ~93MB in Feb. Not sure how much all that means; prob the catch would be tethering. Wed Jun 02 17:00:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @shermandorn Gaaaaaaaahhhh! #hackacad Thu Jun 03 02:28:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @shermandorn That would be the one. **sigh** Thu Jun 03 02:48:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @shermandorn You. Go. Thu Jun 03 03:00:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Cheese. \(I'll always opt for the savory over the sweet\.\) Thu Jun 03 15:57:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @marcparry and @rachaelsullivan Hey, C *is* for cookie; that's good enough for me. Thu Jun 03 16:37:42 +0000 2010 After two years, they've finally discovered the cause of the leak above my sliding glass door. I will declare triumph once it's been fixed. Thu Jun 03 17:03:49 +0000 2010 But a helpful hint: when installing a sliding glass door, caulking around the upper lip and, say, nailing it to the flashing is a good plan.
