Sat Jun 26 05:43:03 +0000 2010 Have almost got a handle on the last chapter, I think. Turned a major corner with it today, at least. Now to finish - & then the conclusion! Sun Jun 27 22:39:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Thanks! Sun Jun 27 23:35:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @therefore Thanks - and good luck finding your way out of the weeds! Sun Jun 27 23:35:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Woo-hoo! Mon Jun 28 20:17:56 +0000 2010 RT @ProfHacker: New at ProfHacker: my latest post, in which I explain the ins and outs of "Moving Your Wordpress Blog": http://bit.ly/ayGwJY Tue Jun 29 15:20:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @nancybaym Whinging? \(As in to whinge?\) Tue Jun 29 20:51:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @nancybaym I've never heard it with an s before, but whinging is a very useful bit of UK English. Tue Jun 29 20:52:32 +0000 2010 Hey, internet - you don't happen to remember where I put my UK adapter plug when I moved in here five years ago, do you? Tue Jun 29 23:04:11 +0000 2010 It's not in that one place with all the stuff. I already checked there. Tue Jun 29 23:04:27 +0000 2010 On the other hand, if any of you need any DSL inline filters, I have about a gross of them. Tue Jun 29 23:06:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Continental I have! Or had two weeks ago, at least, and can probably lay hands on again. UK, not so much. Tue Jun 29 23:07:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @janineutell Aw, man. You have no idea how much I wish I were. Tue Jun 29 23:25:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic Hmmm. Old computer bag might be it. Tue Jun 29 23:26:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie How narrow-minded of them. How about a cache of old deposit slips from an account I closed seven years ago? Tue Jun 29 23:27:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @brettbobley 100% win. Wed Jun 30 02:22:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah If I could borrow one, I'd be much obliged. Wed Jun 30 02:24:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni That puts me out of the running, I guess. Sigh. Wed Jun 30 02:32:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Touché. Wed Jun 30 02:33:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Depends on whether you were serving or receiving, I think. Wed Jun 30 02:38:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Dude, of course. I'm just being old and crotchety. Wed Jun 30 02:43:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ha! Wed Jun 30 02:44:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley If so, I should have two days of non-crotchety left. What happened? Wed Jun 30 03:29:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I'm so sorry... Wed Jun 30 03:50:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah UK telephone? I think I'm okay, other than the adapter. Wed Jun 30 04:02:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Oh, no! I'm so sorry! Wed Jun 30 04:42:40 +0000 2010 Today was a difficult day. Tomorrow will be better. Wed Jun 30 05:31:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall @1Password, dude. My online life utterly depends upon it. Wed Jun 30 16:42:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall and @jbj Yes, I hate that Flickr/Yahoo thing too, but srsly, @1Password is genius. As many different strong passwords as you need. Wed Jun 30 16:49:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Yes, I do. There's info in the support docs about syncing across machines, so... Wed Jun 30 16:51:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I have all of them. Honestly could not work online without. Wed Jun 30 16:51:32 +0000 2010 Inbox = 1. That 1 is a gift certificate. That shouldn't count against me, right? Wed Jun 30 21:13:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni It feels like more of an accomplishment than it probably ought to, given the number of items on my to-do list. Wed Jun 30 21:28:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlecHosterman Yeah, I can't bear unread mail, but I'm also a relentless filer. Wed Jun 30 21:30:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Bust out the champagne then! Inbox = 1 gift certificate! Wed Jun 30 21:31:07 +0000 2010 Okay, Apple, I'm serious: Mail on iOS4 needs *both* a delete and an archive button. *Two* buttons, not one re-assignable one. Wed Jun 30 21:34:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @jasonrhody So if I held the phone in my right hand, it would work? Wed Jun 30 21:43:40 +0000 2010 Pro tip: announcing your very low inbox status is just willing folks to send you stuff to do. Wed Jun 30 22:39:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Primary reason I cut mine off today. And it wasn't all that long! Thu Jul 01 01:12:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar How utterly unlikely. Thu Jul 01 01:15:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm Mine was all about one screen of messages when I used a desktop client. With Gmail, it's about 10 that triggers me. Thu Jul 01 01:18:37 +0000 2010 R's gone, and I'm headed out in the morning. So it's me, leftovers, and whatever's piled up on the DVR tonight. Thu Jul 01 01:41:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlecHosterman That was one of my motives for moving everything to Gmail: robust searching so you can find what you need only when you need. Thu Jul 01 02:08:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Such an awful choice! I'm so sorry you're having to think about things like that now. Thu Jul 01 03:21:34 +0000 2010 Looking forward to a last three hours and thirty-six minutes as department chair. Oh, did I say thirty-six? :) Thu Jul 01 03:26:55 +0000 2010 Okay, major bleg: just had apt in NYC for next academic year pulled out from under me. If you know anyone subletting, maybe let me know? Thu Jul 01 03:39:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp Will do! Thu Jul 01 03:52:38 +0000 2010 Thanks for the RTs, all - I appreciate the help! Thu Jul 01 03:58:54 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 06 01 tweets

docs.text: This has NOT been my day. Thu Jul 01 05:07:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @zaynk I'm sure I'll work it out. It's just way more tiresome than I needed! Thu Jul 01 05:32:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @profhood I really, really hate the "dark side" label. Somebody's got to be chair, and trust me when I say you want it to be someone good. Thu Jul 01 05:37:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @profhood Well, the good news is that I got to do that, too. No course reductions for chairing here. Thu Jul 01 05:45:45 +0000 2010 @jcmeloni My goals for today: getting 17 days worth of stuff in one small suitcase; not missing flight; not having a total meltdown. Thu Jul 01 13:45:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Early August to late June, both flexible. Thu Jul 01 14:24:52 +0000 2010 July is not starting as I'd hoped. But then, June didn't end as I'd hoped, either. Thu Jul 01 17:23:43 +0000 2010 @amandafrench Hey - happy birthday there! I hope it's a great one. Thu Jul 01 17:48:20 +0000 2010 There are days when the best part of getting upgraded is the preflight drink. Hey, it's after noon. Thu Jul 01 19:20:06 +0000 2010 Hey, a quick word of thanks to everyone helping out with the apt crisis. Suspect it will work out. \(Perhaps the preflight drink talking\.\) Thu Jul 01 19:27:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate That one's been pushing my buttons for a while now. However, are we sure that responding is NOT a violation of FERPA? :) Thu Jul 01 23:10:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate Appropriate? Probably not. But hilarious? Certainly. Thu Jul 01 23:22:13 +0000 2010 Thanks, travel goddesses. I'll take that \(utterly unexpected & basically impossible upgrade\) . Thu Jul 01 23:34:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Heh. Thu Jul 01 23:38:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @ryancordell and @captain_primate I think I got a discount on Coda bc I already had Transmit... Thu Jul 01 23:52:34 +0000 2010 This is now officially hilarious: the travel goddesses give and the travel goddesses take away. Fri Jul 02 00:01:49 +0000 2010 Not only bumped out of my upgrade by a computer snafu but bumped out of my good coach seat too. Fri Jul 02 00:02:42 +0000 2010 But have a shiny $50 travel certificate waiting for me at LGA if I'm willing to stick around and ask for it at 11.30 pm. Fri Jul 02 00:04:20 +0000 2010 How shocked am I to discover that the promised travel voucher was not in fact waiting at LGA? Shocked, I tell you! Fri Jul 02 04:10:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @moncia Thanks. See you... um, soon! \(Guess I should go to sleep\.\) Fri Jul 02 05:49:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @mariabustillos Some of it's actually kind of funny. Most of it's just whining. A couple of things really sorta suck. Ah, well. Fri Jul 02 06:08:29 +0000 2010 Ending what has been a difficult day by changing my title on my various websites. Take that! Fri Jul 02 06:11:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Ooh, I like that. Fri Jul 02 12:18:19 +0000 2010 RT @ericzinner: NYUPress in HuffPo: Independence Day: 15 Feisty Small Presses+Books You're Going To Want From Them http://huff.to/caWfia Fri Jul 02 16:53:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I'm so sorry, Mark. Fri Jul 02 18:17:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I'm a big fan of the JQuery rollover note script @clioweb wrote for me to use with Planned Obsolescence... Fri Jul 02 18:19:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Okay, so only 10 hours later, finally in front of a computer again. I'm happy to share if you'd like! Sat Jul 03 04:51:43 +0000 2010 Hey, so, thanks all for your good apt-hunting vibes: problem solved. A most awesome place in the most awesome location. A bit $$, but YAY!!! Sat Jul 03 22:05:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @therefore I've thought exactly the same thing. Also, had the entire staff of a restaurant last night wanting to play with it. Sat Jul 03 22:13:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @TheRepoRat Alas, "a bit $$" is relative to the already insane $$ I was expecting \(i\.e\., this is more\) . Sat Jul 03 22:54:44 +0000 2010 My iMac at home is asleep. My Time Capsule is not. I can remote admin my wifi network via the TC, but I cannot persuade it to wake the iMac. Sun Jul 04 17:44:58 +0000 2010 Massively unfocused today. Have spent hours goofing around with something that did not need goofing around with. Dragging my heels on work. Sun Jul 04 19:09:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde Yeah, which I thought I had on. Apparently not. Sun Jul 04 20:00:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yay! Sun Jul 04 22:19:34 +0000 2010 Had a fantastic dinner tonight with an amazing view of the East River. Of course the fireworks were on the Hudson. But no matter. Mon Jul 05 05:22:17 +0000 2010 Heading to London this evening for #dh2010. Mildly concerned about the fact that after 4 days in NYC I'm still on California time. Mon Jul 05 13:57:45 +0000 2010 There is a certain presentation that I should be busily preparing this morning. I'm hoping motivation will strike shortly. Mon Jul 05 14:25:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp Ambien gives me bad bounceback - if I take one tonight I won't sleep at all tomorrow. But I do have Restoril & am unafraid to use it. Mon Jul 05 14:44:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp Restoril's pretty old - temazepam is the generic. A distant relation of Valium, et al. Best used sporadically to avoid tolerance. Mon Jul 05 15:12:12 +0000 2010 New blog post: On the Road \(Again\) \(and Again\) http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/on-the-road-again-and-again/ Mon Jul 05 16:31:51 +0000 2010 MTA to NJT to AirTrain to EWR to LHR. See you there. Mon Jul 05 19:30:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @nycdlq Dude, if I saw LBJ on the IRT, I'd be pretty darn sure I'd accidentally dropped LSD. Mon Jul 05 20:28:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @ncecire Life in SoCal has made me public transit compulsive; if I *can* use it, I *will* use it. Mon Jul 05 20:29:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Hey, excellent. The weather in NYC has been awful for the last two days, so I look forward to a little SoCal style sunny & 72. Mon Jul 05 21:29:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley \(But with much better curry\.\) Mon Jul 05 21:29:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @nycdlq I did catch the reference, of course! But the image of you listening to it at 7 is pretty priceless. Mon Jul 05 22:47:32 +0000 2010 Sitting in club lounge at Marriott Grosvenor Square, waiting for our room to be ready. 100% delirious. Happy to have a day before #DH2010. Tue Jul 06 09:24:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Thanks! Looking forward to seeing you tomorrow, once I've recovered a bit more! Tue Jul 06 17:56:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Be careful what you ask for, my friend. 105 degrees is just around the corner. Wed Jul 07 05:39:25 +0000 2010 Good morning, London. Coffee has been had. Online catching up has been accomplished. Now perhaps a bit more coffee and some actual work. Wed Jul 07 06:54:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Hey, you too! I mean, have a great night. I think. Wed Jul 07 07:02:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Ooh. Sorry not to be there this morning. If you guys come up with the answer, would you fill me in later today? #thatcamp Wed Jul 07 08:26:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I'm not sure Wave did fail, but if it did the primary reason is mislabeling: it isn't a social app, but groupware. #thatcamp Wed Jul 07 09:03:03 +0000 2010 Ugh, body. You're letting me down. One episode of jet-lag too many? Wed Jul 07 15:03:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley So sorry not to have made it this afternoon; hoping to recover enough for tomorrow morning. #dh2010 Wed Jul 07 16:27:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I am familiar with that word, but not in that context. Wed Jul 07 16:44:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @sherah1918 There's one in my town I see at least twice a week; always makes me think "is this 1982 again?" Wed Jul 07 20:44:57 +0000 2010 Asleep by 10pm; awake at 3am. Out of bed by 5am. Only coffee around: McDonald's. *sigh* Thu Jul 08 04:54:40 +0000 2010 New blog post: Five Years Post-Tribble http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/five-years-post-tribble/ Thu Jul 08 06:55:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Ooh, excellent! Very much looking forward to #sci8, in the meantime. Thu Jul 08 08:10:05 +0000 2010 Have at last made it to #dh2010, just in time for the panel on building the humanities lab. Very, very happy to be here. Thu Jul 08 08:10:47 +0000 2010 Starting out with notetaking etc on the laptop, but will wind up on the iPad before long; MBAir battery life isn't what it used to be. Thu Jul 08 08:30:06 +0000 2010 @nowviskie on founding assumptions/environment of NINES: peer review as "core function" Thu Jul 08 08:32:15 +0000 2010 @nowviskie on founding assumptions/environment of NINES: peer review as "core function" #dh2010 \(this time with hashtag\!\) Thu Jul 08 08:32:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Indeed! Am hoping to play the outlet-search game as little as possible. \(PS: Hi\!\) Thu Jul 08 08:35:42 +0000 2010 Have hit the wall of jet lag. The big question: 40-minute walk or 9-minute tube ride back to my hotel? Thu Jul 08 14:20:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Thank you. Thu Jul 08 14:21:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Ooh, conference nap room. I could certainly get behind that. Thu Jul 08 14:27:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yes, feel better! So sorry that you're sick... Thu Jul 08 20:19:14 +0000 2010 SEVEN HOURS. I slept for seven hours in a row. For the first time in over a week. I feel like I could do ANYTHING. Fri Jul 09 04:53:27 +0000 2010 Think I completely wore myself out with the 40min walk to & from #dh2010 yesterday. Going to do it again today, but carrying less stuff. Fri Jul 09 06:46:25 +0000 2010 Also, dressed more weather-appropriately. Was utterly drenched by the time I got back to the hotel yesterday evening. Fri Jul 09 06:47:01 +0000 2010 In the basement of the Strand Building for session on Teaching/Managing; Anouk Lang on the edu potential of social networking sites. #dh2010 Fri Jul 09 10:02:58 +0000 2010 Incidentally, the basement of the Strand Bldg is the site of the craziest conf I ever attended, involving embezzlement & academic fraud. Fri Jul 09 10:04:56 +0000 2010 Very, very odd being back on the scene. Fri Jul 09 10:05:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Appropriately, it was a Pynchon conference; completely surreal. Fri Jul 09 10:21:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @tla and @mkirschenbaum Except that the pious huffiness is focused on a second-hand mischaracterization of a speaker's point. #dh2010 Fri Jul 09 11:14:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @tla I'm not saying the mischaracterization should stand! In fact, the responses to the initial tweet perpetuated the misreading. Fri Jul 09 11:22:15 +0000 2010 Dying of the heat in K.240.#dh2010 Fri Jul 09 13:45:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @KathrynTomasek Nope; upstairs where the ACH AGM was. At probably 65% capacity, but sweltering. Fri Jul 09 13:49:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I wouldn't either. It's pretty cushy compared with the heat in NYC before I left. Not to mention actually being at #dh2010. Fri Jul 09 13:54:15 +0000 2010 Third presenter's a no-show. Sigh. At least I can escape the room. #dh2010 Fri Jul 09 13:58:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @jasonaboyd If you see @karikraus's most recent tweets, you'll see that that was not at all her point. #dh2010 Fri Jul 09 14:57:02 +0000 2010 Oh, holy moly, am I ready to get back to writing already. Sat Jul 10 09:27:27 +0000 2010 Very interesting analysis of backchannel use at DH conferences being presented by @clairey_ross. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 10:11:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @dougreside Completely agree: Buzz should have been brilliant. Other prob tho was redundancy btw Buzz/Gmail/Google Reader. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 11:10:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @sgsinclair Ooh, interesting q! My sense is that trad review inhibits innovation, promotes safety, but would be fun to explore. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 11:15:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @jamescummings Yes, absolutely. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 11:18:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @dougreside I do like that, but I hate having to mark things as read in 2 places; needs better integration, as @jamescummings said. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 11:19:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @ernestopriego Thank you! Sat Jul 10 11:19:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @sgsinclair Yes! Interpretation of metrics is key; just b/c there might only be a few ppl in a subfield doesn't mean impact is low. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 11:26:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @sgsinclair Also, impact develops over time, which can be taken into consideration in an open process, but not in a closed one. #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 11:27:16 +0000 2010 Yes, I just poured 1/4 cup of sparkling water right on the keyboard of my MBAir. No, I did not do it on purpose. Happily, it wasn't juice. Sat Jul 10 14:00:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @fraistat See you again soon, I hope! Sat Jul 10 14:01:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @filmsnob So far, so good! I once poured Crystal Light on a keyboard, which shorted it out; aspartame is apparently electronics-unfriendly. Sat Jul 10 14:11:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @jamescummings Way to exercise that panopticon! #dh2010 Sat Jul 10 16:12:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Hope you're feeling better! Or starting to. Sat Jul 10 21:18:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Smartassitude is always a good sign. Sat Jul 10 21:27:03 +0000 2010 Thank you to Harold Short and the entire @digihums2010 staff for such a terrific and memorable #dh2010. \(via @brettbobley\) Sat Jul 10 22:01:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Why does this not surprise me? Sat Jul 10 22:01:46 +0000 2010 R. has taken off for airport. I have late checkout & so can loiter here for about 6 hours longer. Might actually try to get some work done. Sun Jul 11 06:42:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni It's also nice to know you're still awake over there; it's odd being on totally the wrong time zone once the conf is over. Sun Jul 11 07:47:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jamescummings and @melissaterras that in fact no one might be watching at all. [2/2] Sun Jul 11 07:49:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @jamescummings and @melissaterras There's also the Zizek claim \(FWIW\) that the real terror of the contemporary is not being watched but [1/2] Sun Jul 11 07:49:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Heh. I was hoping you were actually asleep! Alas, my flight's not for hours yet, but I'm hanging in the hotel and trying to write. Sun Jul 11 09:27:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ugh, so sorry. I hope both sleep and productivity return soon. Sun Jul 11 09:50:04 +0000 2010 You know, "St. Elsewhere" may be totally 2006, but that Gnarls Barkley still provides a mighty snappy writing soundtrack. Sun Jul 11 10:15:41 +0000 2010 It appears I may have to violate my no-MS policy. NeoOffice is making a total hash of saving my manuscript in the required .doc format. Grr. Sun Jul 11 12:29:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell The hash that NeoO is making of .doc is bizarrely amplified in .rtf. I'll try OO and hope that works! Sun Jul 11 12:40:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @magistrahf It's possible but seems unlikely; it's a pretty complexly formatted document. Sun Jul 11 12:44:11 +0000 2010 At Paddington, beginning the journey back to New York, stop 3 of 4 on my ludicrously complex plane ticket. Next stop: Heathrow. Sun Jul 11 13:51:17 +0000 2010 Okay, up and over. See you back Stateside, Twitter. Sun Jul 11 17:27:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Woo-hoo! I'm back now, too. How reasonable this timezone is, tho, I'm not yet sure. Mon Jul 12 02:21:51 +0000 2010 Hey, NYC: you were supposed to fix that heat problem while I was gone. Mon Jul 12 02:22:33 +0000 2010 LGA to EWR to AirTrain to NJT \(where I am now\) to Penn Station to ACE to West 4th to Washington Sq. Not exactly home, but yay nonetheless. Mon Jul 12 02:25:12 +0000 2010 Of course, the first step was LHR to EWR, not LGA to EWR, which would just be dumb. Mon Jul 12 02:39:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @ericzinner Fair enough, I suppose. See you in a bit. Mon Jul 12 15:24:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Oh, good grief. The. Best. You're making me more homesick for Paris than ever. Mon Jul 12 15:28:35 +0000 2010 Oh, man. Slept for 9+ hours, and am still feeling flattened. Could do with a couple more, but have things that must be accomplished today! Mon Jul 12 15:39:07 +0000 2010 Extremely frustrated that neither OpenOffice nor NeoOffice \(nor Pages, etc\) will deal with endnotes the way I need. MS Office, here I come. Mon Jul 12 16:55:39 +0000 2010 Hey, tweeps: did we get a count on how many attendees there were at #DH2010? I thought I saw a number at some point, but can't find it now. Mon Jul 12 21:43:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @saschel Hmm - she says the room seats 250 but that there could be as many as 200 more registered folks in the streaming theatre. Mon Jul 12 22:20:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Hmm. I thought I'd heard higher, like over 400; probably I've crossed it w/ another conference in my head. Where was I last week? Mon Jul 12 22:39:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Okay, whew. I'm not losing my mind after all. Thanks \(and see you soon\) . Mon Jul 12 22:51:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @unsworth That's what I thought; thanks! Mon Jul 12 23:08:24 +0000 2010 Sucked it up & installed MS Office on the MBAir. Word did in 30 seconds what I'd spent two days trying to get the open variants to do. :/ Mon Jul 12 23:39:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar I can't tell if you're kidding from here, but oh, you are so Not Kidding. Mon Jul 12 23:58:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @Scheopner Mine was all about endnotes. Grrr. Tue Jul 13 00:06:36 +0000 2010 I hope all else is well there - any further word from the medical profession? Is there consensus that this is a done deal? Tue Jul 13 00:14:08 +0000 2010 Ignore that last, svp. I'm growing to hate the Tweetie way of dealing with DMs. Tue Jul 13 00:16:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @davidmsilver Whoa, time flies. What a cutie! It looks like it was a happy birthday. Tue Jul 13 00:17:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm Works nicely! Also masks embarrassment well. Tue Jul 13 00:22:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm Eesh, yes, that would be dangerous! Tue Jul 13 00:24:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @brfidler Hey, cool. Tell @therefore I said hi \(actually, I think I just told him\) and I'll look forward to hearing what you're working on! Tue Jul 13 01:09:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @cforster Yay, pictures! I hope everyone's resting well. Tue Jul 13 02:51:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Hey, I had no idea it was your birthday. I hope it was a good one! Tue Jul 13 03:50:33 +0000 2010 Hmm. Thunderstorms predicted for most of the day today. And me on a turboprop this afternoon. Why does this not give me a good feeling? Tue Jul 13 13:16:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Yeah, you know, that would have been a much better idea. Ah, well; I'm sure it'll be fine. Tue Jul 13 13:25:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @msbonn You too! Tue Jul 13 13:57:03 +0000 2010 Yay! // Delighted to see that we're reading part of @kfitz's Planned Obsolescence for CLIR seminar: http://bit.ly/92fB57. /via @briancroxall Tue Jul 13 14:25:30 +0000 2010 Flying through IAD. Dare I trust the airlines \(2, alas\) to properly transfer my bag, or should I gate-check \(& risk a too\-tight connection\) ? Tue Jul 13 15:54:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Usually my instinct, too, but I have a 56-min connection & would have to wait for bag... Tue Jul 13 16:02:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Kinda what I'm thinking. I never check, but I'm thinking it might be the better course this time. Tue Jul 13 16:21:32 +0000 2010 Cab \(hey, it's raining\!\) to Penn Sta to NJT \(presently\) to AirTrain to EWR to IAD to CHO. Wish me luck. Tue Jul 13 16:44:36 +0000 2010 Hi there, @brinkmanship! Tue Jul 13 17:06:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais I'm srsly contemplating bailing on one travel commitment... Tue Jul 13 17:22:36 +0000 2010 Checked my bag. And went through security. And then found out my flight was going to be 3.5 hrs late. And now it's cancelled. All in 15min! Tue Jul 13 17:34:09 +0000 2010 Am rebooked on the next flight; will miss my connection, but have a res on the next flight to CHO. \(Also have vodka, but that's medicinal\.\) Tue Jul 13 17:41:32 +0000 2010 Crap. And now I understand why she didn't actually tell me when the next flight to CHO is - it doesn't leave IAD until 10.20 pm. Sooo happy. Tue Jul 13 17:48:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais I won't say it. But unless I can come up with some college $, I may be stuck. Tue Jul 13 17:49:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Dude, you'd "just drive" to Boston; you're not a reliable guide here. Tue Jul 13 17:50:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais Now *that* would be a feat! Tue Jul 13 17:53:44 +0000 2010 I'm stuck w/this stupid ticket \(they'll cancel my flt home if I bail\) but I so wish I could go back in time & tell myself to take the train. Tue Jul 20 20:49:48 +0000 2010 Ugh; found the info. It's for faulty power supplies, not for failing hard disks. So I may be stuck. Tue Jul 20 20:57:32 +0000 2010 Wait: the TC mounted; whatever its problem, it's not the one throwing errors. TC=disk3s2; errors coming from disk4s2. Which is what disk? Tue Jul 20 21:02:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate Since March 2008, apparently. Tue Jul 20 21:10:21 +0000 2010 Okay, nearly 60GB worth of logs, which popped up in about an hour, deleted. Still can't figure out which disk was throwing the errors, tho. Tue Jul 20 21:38:44 +0000 2010 Hey, remember when I said I didn't want to face the next task on my list, like two hours ago? Guess what? I haven't! Tue Jul 20 21:44:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara WOW - that's some serious walking! Wed Jul 21 03:13:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @ryancordell Have you gotten ahold of a copy? I'd be happy to send you mine. Wed Jul 21 03:14:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @ryancordell Sure; DM me her info. Wed Jul 21 15:56:53 +0000 2010 Okay, so Flipboard is pretty cool, but it sure asks for a LOT of permissions in order to interact with FB. Not sure how I feel about that... Wed Jul 21 23:27:13 +0000 2010 Found out today that my passive-aggressive downstairs neighbor hired the woman who cleans my apt in an attempt to use her to spy on me. Thu Jul 22 00:13:11 +0000 2010 She refused. And of course told me the story. Thu Jul 22 00:14:13 +0000 2010 I am unbelievably tickled by this. A nice bit of information to tuck away for a rainy day. Thu Jul 22 00:15:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Indeed. Thu Jul 22 00:17:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @TheRepoRat Alas, we both own. At least what she wanted to know was totally benign, but that she wouldn't just ask me is a scream. Thu Jul 22 00:24:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah She wanted S to turn on the sound system here to see if the bass was making her ceiling vibrate. That she wouldn't ask me is a riot. Thu Jul 22 00:25:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah But then, she did ask me about it 5 yrs ago, and we picked the subwoofer up off the floor. Now, she says, the vibration's back. Thu Jul 22 00:26:20 +0000 2010 What my neighbor wanted to know: if I'd moved my subwoofer back onto the floor, as she claims our soundsystem is making her ceiling vibrate. Thu Jul 22 00:27:55 +0000 2010 I hadn't. In fact, I picked it up at her behest 5 years ago. That she doesn't want to ask me about it directly is hysterically funny to me. Thu Jul 22 00:28:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Oho. So this whole thing about my subwoofer is really a screen for Wiley's plan to ensure my book never sees the light of day? Thu Jul 22 00:37:37 +0000 2010 Why is AT&T emailing me about their new store in Niles, MI? Thu Jul 22 02:43:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @sherah1918 Definitely begins to sound like a scam. Thu Jul 22 03:01:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @unsworth Nice. It looked phishy, but I couldn't spot the hook. Thanks, John. Thu Jul 22 03:10:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @unsworth It's quite clever; I could easily have fallen for it, as I have a habit of unsubbing from random stuff like that. Thu Jul 22 03:10:53 +0000 2010 As @unsworth points out to me, the AT&T Niles, MI email is phishing; the hook is in the unsubscribe link! Thu Jul 22 03:12:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @unsworth I got that one, too. Is it animus toward MI, or an assumption that MI won't raise suspicions? Thu Jul 22 05:09:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek I got the Angola one overnight! Thu Jul 22 15:06:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Woo-hoo! Need a test subject? Thu Jul 22 15:07:30 +0000 2010 Two days ago I searched for a particular product on the site of a particular internet discounter. [1/2] Thu Jul 22 17:40:57 +0000 2010 Since then, ads for that discounter featuring that item have popped up EVERYWHERE. Perhaps it's time to clear my caches. [2/2] Thu Jul 22 17:41:39 +0000 2010 Today's massive lack of productivity is sending me out to run errands. I may even go to a movie! It's been that kind of day. Thu Jul 22 21:55:46 +0000 2010 Okay, now I feel productive. Got the car smogged, went to the PO, renewed my registration & insurance, & am now at the movies. Much better. Thu Jul 22 23:34:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Inception. Thu Jul 22 23:49:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Actually, what you want is the future anterior, my favorite tense. How great it will be having done it! Fri Jul 23 03:32:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @nickmirzoeff I'm in Claremont, probably until Aug 7 or 8. Finishing up some stuff here & packing. Glad you're enjoying the workshop! Fri Jul 23 03:56:12 +0000 2010 @ryancordell Hey, knock 'em dead, Ryan! Fri Jul 23 13:57:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Yikes! I'm so sorry to hear about this - I hope the kitty's okay. Fri Jul 23 14:46:54 +0000 2010 Rilly, rilly not with the program yet this morning. Or this summer, quite frankly. What say we back the calendary up a bit & take a do-over? Fri Jul 23 14:50:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Gads, the things they can get into. It never fails to surprise me, either. Fri Jul 23 15:17:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall It does still happen! They're just called something like "electronic roundtables." They use laptops w/large external montors. Fri Jul 23 15:40:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Problem is no one knows what an "electronic roundtable" is. I wish we could decide that "poster session" is not a pejorative." Fri Jul 23 15:43:33 +0000 2010 Now wondering if the energy of the DH poster session would be transferable to MLA if we were to create a specific campaign... #mla11 Fri Jul 23 15:47:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall True. But there's also something a bit "horseless carriage" about the construction. #mla11 Fri Jul 23 15:48:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @nickmirzoeff If I can make it, though, I'll let you know! I'll hope to see you, if not here, then next month in NYC. Fri Jul 23 17:20:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @ryancordell Woo-hoo! Congrats! Fri Jul 23 18:25:47 +0000 2010 Twitter's "thanks for noticing" when something has gone wrong technically always strikes me as insincere, if not downright sarcastic. Fri Jul 23 20:39:21 +0000 2010 RT @lizlosh: Rosenbloom says that experimentation may need to be added to analysis in humanities methods #critcode // brilliant! Fri Jul 23 23:18:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @mkirschenbaum And an expansion thereof beyond DH to the humanities-in-general. Fri Jul 23 23:53:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @profhood Ah, you must have missed the joyous reunion. Sat Jul 24 00:07:06 +0000 2010 I will finish that peer review. I will finish that peer review. I will finish that peer review. Um. After I get back from a run. Sat Jul 24 16:05:23 +0000 2010 Okay, what's with Chrome suddenly allowing all these pop-ups even though I've got pop-ups disallowed, huh? Sat Jul 24 17:16:46 +0000 2010 PS: This running thing is starting to kick back into place. So yay. Sat Jul 24 17:17:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Soooo good. It's been eons since I've been able to get ahold of it. Sat Jul 24 18:52:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward I'm so glad to hear that! Sat Jul 24 19:47:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara and @rachaelsullivan Leslie Stahl used to work out at the gym I belonged to in NYC back in the late 90s. She'd be dropped off [1/2] Sat Jul 24 19:49:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara and @rachaelsullivan at 5.30 am by her driver and work out in full 60 Minutes hair and makeup. It was super unnerving. [2/2] Sat Jul 24 19:49:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Happens to me every time I go get my hair cut. I see the gossip rag covers and think "who?" Sat Jul 24 20:54:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall iAnnotate PDF. It's not perfect, but I like the annotation capacity. Sun Jul 25 14:13:22 +0000 2010 The peer review of death is at last done & in. Now beginning the horrific project of cleaning out closets & dressers preparatory to packing. Sun Jul 25 18:04:18 +0000 2010 The piles: to go to Goodwill; to be stored in the garage; to be stored in my office; to stay put; to be shipped to NY; to come with me to NY Sun Jul 25 18:05:46 +0000 2010 I have approximately 10 days to decide which pile EVERY SINGLE ITEM IN MY HOUSE goes into. Sun Jul 25 18:06:21 +0000 2010 Oh, forgot the "direct to dumpster" pile. Sun Jul 25 18:07:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Trust me; odd corners are stacked with piles of old boxes & shopping bags & stuff I "might use someday." Ah, the joy of purging. Sun Jul 25 21:00:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp Yes! Furnished studio on perfect block in west village for only mildly insane $$$. July 4th weekend Craigslist FTW. Sun Jul 25 21:04:33 +0000 2010 RT @academicdave: Significant ruling on DRM: http://bit.ly/b0lHeR // Whoa: LOC allows cracking CSS on DVDs, calls jailbreaking fair use. Mon Jul 26 16:33:42 +0000 2010 Holy cow, if I'm reading that right, not only jailbreaking but unlocking is now considered fair use. Go LOC! http://bit.ly/b0lHeR Mon Jul 26 16:35:40 +0000 2010 Also breaking DRM on "literary works distributed in ebook format" in order to enable read-aloud or to convert to format of screen reader. Mon Jul 26 16:37:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave No, actually, that is new: the previous allowance was strictly for classroom use; this is for *critical* & documentary use. Mon Jul 26 16:38:12 +0000 2010 Breaking CSS now legal "when circumvention is accomplished solely in order to accomplish the incorporation of short portions [1/2] Mon Jul 26 16:39:25 +0000 2010 of motion pictures into new works for the purpose of criticism or comment" - no longer just restricted to classroom use. [2/2] Mon Jul 26 16:39:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave The ruling seems to indicate that cracking for purposes of inclusion in critical commentary falls under fair use! Mon Jul 26 16:49:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @BGCDML Hmm. That doesn't seem to have come up, though cracking DRM on video games for "good faith testing" purposes is now fair use. Mon Jul 26 16:50:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @JeffatSCC Indeed: no longer restricted to film & media studies profs; includes students! Mon Jul 26 16:51:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Yes, good point; "fair use" is nebulous, and this is not: cracking for purposes of critical commentary now legal, period. Mon Jul 26 16:59:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell \(With the non\-insignificant caveat that if there are other measures than cracking available, one has to take them\.\) Mon Jul 26 17:00:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave Exactly. c.f. In Media Res. Mon Jul 26 17:03:49 +0000 2010 New blog post: Fair Use http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/fair-use/ Mon Jul 26 18:19:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Not a bad idea; thanks! Mon Jul 26 18:23:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Excellent! I'll ponder. Mon Jul 26 18:28:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @zota That's certainly what it looks like to me. I wonder why everyone has latched on the jailbreaking clause rather than the unlocking one. Mon Jul 26 19:55:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj But I can't not go out that way! Mon Jul 26 20:02:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges Is that a contented sigh or a cry for help? #oneweek Mon Jul 26 20:23:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges I get it. #oneweek Mon Jul 26 20:26:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @digitalhumanist Hi, Dave's family, from pleasantly sunny Southern California. Tue Jul 27 00:50:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @escapegrace Well, when you put it that way... Tue Jul 27 01:31:10 +0000 2010 Very excited that SQ's open review is getting notice today - though extremely sorry that MediaCommons's role in the process got no play. Tue Jul 27 14:13:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Oh, hey, thanks! Tue Jul 27 14:14:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Just spotted that! Many thanks to @NEH_ODH, for the tweet and for making these experiments possible! Tue Jul 27 14:21:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @pmhswe Oh, I have no worries about that! Katherine's been fabulous to work with. Tue Jul 27 14:30:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara Oh no! I'm so sorry! Tue Jul 27 16:08:27 +0000 2010 New blog post: MediaCommons, Shakespeare Quarterly, and Open Review http://bit.ly/abJR3H Tue Jul 27 16:28:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall and @foundhistory Also the @NEH_ODH funded MC profile system: http://bit.ly/bvbTow Tue Jul 27 16:40:33 +0000 2010 @briancroxall @foundhistory Also the @NEH_ODH funded MC profile system: http://bit.ly/bvbTow #oneweek \(now with hashtag\!\) Tue Jul 27 16:41:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Yes, of course! But there might be a utility \(i\.e\., shared resources\) in building on existing models, no? #oneweek Tue Jul 27 16:47:07 +0000 2010 All of the #oneweek ideas are fantastic; I'm having a hard time finding a preference. Looking forward to watching it unfold... Tue Jul 27 16:52:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench and @foundhistory Ooh, 1 with footnote support would be great; see my comment on that WP-as-book-platform post abt footnote pain. Tue Jul 27 16:53:37 +0000 2010 RT @ProfHacker: New at ProfHacker: guest author @jmittell with "Letting Us Rip: Our New Right to Fair Use of DVDs" http://bit.ly/9dbHCo Tue Jul 27 19:11:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen *exhausted sigh* Wed Jul 28 01:58:48 +0000 2010 I swallowed a camera today! \(And found out I do not have an ulcer, or anything worse\!\) Wed Jul 28 23:49:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander Nice article, Bryan; thanks! Thu Jul 29 14:32:29 +0000 2010 Some thoughts on open access + scholarship in the liberal arts world, current developments, http://bit.ly/91XlbT #NITLE /via @BryanAlexander Thu Jul 29 14:33:07 +0000 2010 Today's discovery: swallowing a camera can leave you with a heck of a sore throat. Thu Jul 29 14:34:27 +0000 2010 I am writing up "notes" \(i\.e\., a renter's agreement\) for my tenants, and sound like a fussy old lady. Thu Jul 29 23:30:01 +0000 2010 Along which lines: what am I forgetting to include? Bad sublet/housesitter experiences I should know about? Thu Jul 29 23:30:43 +0000 2010 Ooh, yes. I've got the "no modifications" clause, but hadn't thought of the "don't touch the boxes" clause. Thanks, all! Thu Jul 29 23:56:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @nirak You and me both. Particularly after my last major housesitter. *shudder* Fri Jul 30 01:46:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @philnel Wow, Phil - that's awfully nice! Thanks so much for the post. Fri Jul 30 14:45:04 +0000 2010 Okay, wow. Just got off the phone with some of the outreach folks from #oneweek. You're really going to love [redacted]. I can hardly wait! Fri Jul 30 18:50:21 +0000 2010 Testing out the free Socialite app from MacHeist. http://bit.ly/calcapp Fri Jul 30 21:10:36 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 07 01 tweets

docs.text: Insta-poll: If you were going on sabbatical for a year and could bring one cookbook with you, which one would it be? Sun Aug 01 18:25:21 +0000 2010 Really excellent cookbook choices! I'm leaning toward the five of you who said some variant of "internet, doofus." I think that's where I am Sun Aug 01 19:37:51 +0000 2010 Though if I were to bring one print cookbook, @edmj and @brettbobley, it would be Bittman. Sun Aug 01 19:38:48 +0000 2010 Okay, back to packing. Sun Aug 01 19:40:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yeah, that's one of my favorite apps, especially standing in the grocery store thinking "what could I do with *that*?" Sun Aug 01 19:41:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @mikejolk Ooh, really? I'll go look for that; thanks! Sun Aug 01 20:48:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley You know, my landlord showed me this weird little alcove in my studio, called it a "kitchen." Thought I'd see how it works. Sun Aug 01 21:37:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @edmj Someone once asked me if I cooked. I said, well, if by that you mean do I heat food in the kitchen, sure. Sun Aug 01 22:35:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @cforster The Oxford Dictionary of English was auto-delivered to the Kindle app on my iPad yesterday. Surprise! Mon Aug 02 13:49:07 +0000 2010 All the small stuff is done. Packing is almost complete. Now, two major writing projects to finish before the summer's over. Go! Mon Aug 02 16:48:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmcclurken Wow, that's scary - glad you're okay! Mon Aug 02 23:53:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar A good rule, that. Mon Aug 02 23:53:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I still haven't found one I really like. Osfoora is better now that it's got the split horiz view, but I'm still looking. Tue Aug 03 01:26:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @NYUpress Hey, thanks for the plug, but commenting on the review experiment is now closed; readers can still see the discussion, though! Tue Aug 03 14:24:25 +0000 2010 RT @anthologize: www.anthologize.org #oneweek // YAY, Anthologize! Tue Aug 03 19:25:02 +0000 2010 New blog post: Anthologize http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/anthologize/ Tue Aug 03 22:43:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Yeah, I had that moment of realizing that I need to start fewer posts with "I've only got a minute for this, but..." Wed Aug 04 13:43:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench I'm planning on putting that on my business cards, you know: "kfitz, Prominent Media Studies Scholar." So watch out! :) Wed Aug 04 13:51:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I'm kinda fond of "The Ivory Tower and the Open Web." Wed Aug 04 15:54:25 +0000 2010 Was in the middle of sthg & thought, "oh, goodness, don't forget to do [sthg else]," but 30 seconds later had already forgotten what it was. Wed Aug 04 16:41:51 +0000 2010 Here's the good news: by the time I get to NYC, all but three of my boxes should already be there waiting for me. So I can get set up ASAP. Wed Aug 04 16:46:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @jsench Yes, totally. Thanks for awakening the wrath of the delivery gods, man. Wed Aug 04 17:10:24 +0000 2010 Have just shut off my land line and my cable. Cable \(or sthg like it\) will return when I do; land line probably won't. Feel oddly liberated. Wed Aug 04 20:27:51 +0000 2010 Amazing day - it appears Prop8 has been shot down! http://bit.ly/dunLDL Wed Aug 04 20:44:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Yeah, over 4th of July weekend, of all times! Wed Aug 04 20:44:43 +0000 2010 RT @jwolman: Glad to hear that California is once again merely financially, as opposed to morally, bankrupt. Wed Aug 04 20:45:57 +0000 2010 In other things that made me happy today, catnip turns even big cats into kittehs. http://bit.ly/97aOeV Wed Aug 04 20:53:02 +0000 2010 New blog post: Justice http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/justice/ Wed Aug 04 21:09:39 +0000 2010 The glee with which Google's announcement \(http://bit\.ly/akHmQU\) that it's canceling Wave development has been greeted depresses me. \(1/2\) Wed Aug 04 21:29:52 +0000 2010 It's an imperfect technology with a bad rollout and an incorrect sense of its niche - but I used it in classes with great success. \(2/3\) Wed Aug 04 21:30:59 +0000 2010 Witness my ProfHacker post: http://bit.ly/abr5Iq. I'll be sorry to see it go. \(3/3\) Wed Aug 04 21:32:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman That would make me quite happy, in fact; as with Buzz, I always wanted to see a better integration with the rest of the Googleplex. Wed Aug 04 21:38:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum As I said, bad rollout & bad sense of the technology's purpose. But still a powerful tool, once you figure out what it's for. Wed Aug 04 21:52:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Very likely you wouldn't. It's really not a personal tool, but rather groupware. Wed Aug 04 21:58:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Of course! But unless your whole group wanted to use it... Wed Aug 04 22:13:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @clioweb I honestly believe that you could solve *any* problem using JQuery. Thu Aug 05 02:28:45 +0000 2010 Oh, good grief: please don't let this attempt to do in net neutrality go overlooked in today's celebrations: http://nyti.ms/c8dhJA Thu Aug 05 03:07:03 +0000 2010 RT @googlepubpolicy: @NYTimes is wrong. We've not had any convos with VZN about paying for carriage of our traffic. We remain committed ... Thu Aug 05 16:11:17 +0000 2010 One hopes that's true. #dontbeevil Thu Aug 05 16:11:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni For real? I guess this means I need to check it out. Thu Aug 05 22:58:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni No, I remain spielless. Thu Aug 05 23:00:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni On it. Thu Aug 05 23:06:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Especially an *overnight* kilt. *That* would be useful. Fri Aug 06 06:08:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Srsly. Cycling \(& baseball\) should start assuming everyone's doping, and then give bonuses to those who can prove they're not. Fri Aug 06 16:58:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality My god, the coincidence. Fri Aug 06 16:59:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yes, you should - inquiring minds want to know. Fri Aug 06 17:05:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Whoa. Fri Aug 06 17:09:04 +0000 2010 Contemplating whether I cd use a Kickstarter campaign as a bribe to get, say, a browser-agnostic version of a most awesome plugin developed. Fri Aug 06 17:13:05 +0000 2010 What would you estimate the annual loss to GNP caused by hours sitting around waiting for the cable/phone/delivery guy to show up might be? Fri Aug 06 18:28:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlmcgill That assessment sounds like it arises from deep experience. Fri Aug 06 19:52:37 +0000 2010 Uh, Chrome? WTF? \(Sudden unprovoked quit, with no "oops, sorry" message\. Hmph\.\) Fri Aug 06 20:30:12 +0000 2010 Hey kids, word to the wise: Dr. Bronner's liquid soap sprayed at high velocity into your eye hurts like hell! Don't do it. Sat Aug 07 00:08:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Which is why I persist in using the stuff. But seriously: eyeball corrosive. Sat Aug 07 00:47:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlmcgill Flying in tomorrow, actually. But yes, email! Would love to come to Rutgers! Sat Aug 07 00:49:24 +0000 2010 So I can see out of the castile/peppermint soap eye again. Things could be worse. Sat Aug 07 01:41:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Morning! Sat Aug 07 11:44:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Not yet; up early to head to eastern time, though. Sat Aug 07 11:57:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ha. The funny thing is that I have to check a bag. But I have all the essentials in my carryon! Sat Aug 07 12:08:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Will bear that in mind. Sat Aug 07 12:11:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @acavender You go! Sat Aug 07 17:43:52 +0000 2010 They're offering a $300 voucher to take the next flight - but today, I just want to get there already. LGA, here I come. Sat Aug 07 17:50:58 +0000 2010 Huh. Same crew IAH to LGA as was on ONT to IAH. Less fancy plane, though. Sounds about right. Sat Aug 07 17:58:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Dude, that sounds like being a runner to me. Sat Aug 07 17:58:36 +0000 2010 See you in 3 hours, NY. Sat Aug 07 18:21:49 +0000 2010 Hello, LGA. Sat Aug 07 21:52:31 +0000 2010 I just earned the Sightseer Pin on @gowalla! http://gowal.la/r/21iQV Sat Aug 07 21:57:38 +0000 2010 My suitcase showed up! Sat Aug 07 22:24:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni No kidding. Wouldn't *that* have been embarrassing? Sat Aug 07 23:32:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri OW! Take care! Sat Aug 07 23:33:37 +0000 2010 I have an apartment, and have even put some food in it. Now to begin unpacking. Sat Aug 07 23:34:26 +0000 2010 Okay, everything that was here when I arrived, or that arrived with me, is unpacked and put away. Three more boxes due on Monday. Phew. Sun Aug 08 03:23:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Sorry - I crashed right after I finished! I'm in the west village, in a fantastic \(if tiny & $$\) studio. Sun Aug 08 12:40:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Hi Vika! A very small if lovely studio in the west village. Super happy with it so far! Sun Aug 08 12:58:49 +0000 2010 Rechargeable batteries in my mouse just died. And the extras plus the charger are in one of the three boxes that's not here yet. Sigh. Sun Aug 08 16:15:11 +0000 2010 In better news, took a run in Hudson River Park. Absolutely gorgeous out there today. I'm still in lousy shape but have inspiration now. Sun Aug 08 16:16:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @nancybaym I'm in NYC on sabbatical, for the academic year. I still can't believe it. Sun Aug 08 16:21:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp I wound up not following it; the spring went a little more haywire than I'd hoped. Perhaps now! How would you alter it? Sun Aug 08 16:45:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus I haven't, actually; I'll have to do that this week. Would be good to force me to think through what exactly I'm going to do! Sun Aug 08 17:01:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp Whoa. That's pretty amazing. But I think I'd need an app to handle the calculations for me. Sun Aug 08 19:16:23 +0000 2010 Okay, wrote a little, ran a little, cleaned up, ran errands, ate lunch. I think all that justifies an afternoon of lazy reading, no? Sun Aug 08 19:39:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I always knew I liked you. Sun Aug 08 19:54:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @writinginpublic Hey, are you in town? Or still on the continent? Sun Aug 08 20:42:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @writinginpublic Yes, please! I'll look forward to it. Sun Aug 08 20:54:51 +0000 2010 WP gurus: is there a way to ignore a theme update notice \(ie, make it go away w/o updating\) ? One I use has an update that breaks everything. Sun Aug 08 22:25:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Not spotting anything related to update-checking in the admin file... Sun Aug 08 22:34:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges That would do it, though I was hoping for theme-by-theme ignoring. May just learn to live with the notice. Sun Aug 08 22:53:09 +0000 2010 Huh. I've never managed to crash a cable box before. Sun Aug 08 23:35:19 +0000 2010 Made a most amazing dinner in my postage-stamp sized kitchen. Really, it's the kitchen for which "-ette" was invented. But dinner rocked. Mon Aug 09 00:28:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde That sounds about right. Mon Aug 09 00:40:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @lincolnmullen Brilliant - that did it. Many thanks! Mon Aug 09 00:59:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Ooh, just let me know when; I've been following #cunypie with great envy...! Mon Aug 09 02:18:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Broccoli, kale, and bell peppers sauteed in olive oil with sea salt, crushed chipotle peppers, and cashews, over brown rice. YUM. Mon Aug 09 11:06:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah I hope the trip is going well so far! Mon Aug 09 11:07:06 +0000 2010 Not quite as awake as I thought I was when I got out of bed. Mon Aug 09 11:39:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench and @sivavaid Wow, if only I were in New York and could go with you. Hey, wait!
