Fri Aug 27 15:26:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jtheibault But that's precisely why it's so dismaying. To have pioneered in one area but reflexively reject experimentation in another? Fri Aug 27 15:30:37 +0000 2010 Someday I will not screw up the double DM reply. Stupid Tweetie. Fri Aug 27 16:07:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @tanyaclement Congratulations! Fri Aug 27 20:55:32 +0000 2010 @jcmeloni Just home from a lovely dinner with colleagues in visiting department; now going to finish Shteyngart's Super Sad True Love Story. Sat Aug 28 02:21:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost Number 1: footnote/endnote support, or the failures thereof. It's sad; they undoubtedly won't fix it. Sat Aug 28 15:10:56 +0000 2010 There are days where things conspire to take the wind right out of your sails. Sat Aug 28 15:11:19 +0000 2010 It's such a small thing, but imagine: a key project of yours makes the front page of a major national publication! Your institution \(1/2\) Sat Aug 28 16:09:14 +0000 2010 proudly posts a story about the story! And gets your name wrong in the headline! After working there for 12 years! \(2/2\) Sat Aug 28 16:10:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @RepoRat True. Have written for correction, and I'm sure it'll be fine. But it's a good ego deflater, in case I needed one. Sat Aug 28 16:23:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Yup. How'd you guess? Sat Aug 28 16:23:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Front page of the college website, baby! \(Have written for correction; all will be well\. But a nice ego deflater if I needed one\.\) Sat Aug 28 16:24:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @pronetolaughter Fitzgerald. Sigh. Sat Aug 28 16:25:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I love it. Sat Aug 28 16:27:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Or at least for a descendant of his illegitimate son. Sat Aug 28 16:35:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Thus far today the main thing I have done is systematically getting out of bed at 7am and then falling back asleep on my sofa. Sun Aug 29 14:24:52 +0000 2010 Compelling thinking from @JenHoward on continued reasons for hope in academic publishing: http://bit.ly/aHN2Cu. Mon Aug 30 11:11:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @Vaguery In brief: experimental failures are individual, not general. If you send me an address I can email the article. Mon Aug 30 11:23:11 +0000 2010 Letters to NYTimes about open review article, including one from A.R. Gurney! http://tinyurl.com/2bulu3l //via @mlaconvention Mon Aug 30 12:52:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Pretty awesome, huh? Particularly coupled with the "rough justice" of academic publishing. But several thoughtful ones, too. Mon Aug 30 15:24:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @marcruppel Wow: that's absolutely amazing. Mon Aug 30 16:53:38 +0000 2010 RT @marcruppel: Absolutely incredible use of Google Chrome + Arcade Fire: http://www.thewildernessdowntown.com/ We need more of this. Mon Aug 30 16:53:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @ProfHacker "Pleads for help in" might be more accurate than "suggests" in this case! :) http://bit.ly/avKf2L Mon Aug 30 20:09:54 +0000 2010 I have had "Hey Ya" stuck on repeat in my head all day. Not the whole song, mind you; just the "hey ya" part. Mon Aug 30 20:14:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Indeed! Mon Aug 30 20:23:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @PhDeviate True. It was only getting it stuck in my head for days & not being bothered by it that uncovered my love for "Sister Christian." Mon Aug 30 22:30:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @billwolff You go! Mon Aug 30 23:33:06 +0000 2010 iPad reading crisis: both Amazon and the iBooks store offer the next book I was planning on reading, at the same price. Must... decide... Mon Aug 30 23:56:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Hmmm. That's a good thought! Tue Aug 31 00:04:42 +0000 2010 Decision on book went to iBooks, mostly for the jailbreakability @briancroxall reminded me of & future data portability of epub standard. Tue Aug 31 00:57:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @billwolff Ha - this had me thinking about Spalding Gray & Swimming to Cambodia... Tue Aug 31 01:06:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @betajames You know, in the long term, I bet that more or less equals out. Tue Aug 31 01:09:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate I was remembering the ad campaign and thinking whatisthe. Tue Aug 31 01:55:18 +0000 2010 Reading @NealStephenson's Anathem on the iPad and wishing iBooks would let me redirect word lookups to the book's glossary. Tue Aug 31 02:23:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell The non-fetishist in me says yes: the book is the content, not the container. But there is something particular about such a tome. Tue Aug 31 02:33:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality and @jmittell I'd agree with that; HOL is largely about its own materiality in a way that most novels \(even tomes\) are not. Tue Aug 31 03:16:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras This is the approach I've been working on. Went up for full last year w/all dig portfolio \(& 2nd book published online\) ... Tue Aug 31 12:47:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras ...and now hope to be someone others can point to as precedent. & to direct their chairs/deans/cmtes to: http://bit.ly/zjXhV Tue Aug 31 12:48:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras Thanks! It took a bit of persuasion but I had some senior colleagues willing to argue against their peers' print fetish. Tue Aug 31 13:02:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras And I had lots of data about how the online text has been used and the responses it's generated, which they couldn't dispute. Tue Aug 31 13:03:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @johnmjones Not without a proper security clearance, you wouldn't. Tue Aug 31 13:07:17 +0000 2010 RT @FrostDavis: #NITLE launches digital humanities initiative for small liberal arts colleges: http://bit.ly/aFh90E #digitalhumanities /Yay! Tue Aug 31 13:19:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @FrostDavis My pleasure! I'm really excited about it. Tue Aug 31 13:23:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Agreed. http://bit.ly/aK1F0v \(The chunk from PO in which I focus on that issue; there's a bit more earlier in the chapter\.\.\) Tue Aug 31 13:52:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward UC and UMich are both returning to this mission by offering their faculties a range of publishing services. \(1/2\) Tue Aug 31 13:58:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Catherine Mitchell is brilliant on this. http://bit.ly/9ICvT8 and http://bit.ly/dlzLjb \(2/2\) Tue Aug 31 14:01:21 +0000 2010 New post: Past and Future http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/past-and-future/ Tue Aug 31 14:46:42 +0000 2010 It's a blogging kinda day! New post: To Read: How Not to Run a University Press. http://bit.ly/bfBUnW Tue Aug 31 17:45:44 +0000 2010 Have a bad feeling about this disturbance in my visual field... Tue Aug 31 20:41:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar Tis what I'm doing. Just trying to wrap up last details before this thing hits, if it's going to... Tue Aug 31 21:25:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @tmcphers It's only a migraine, but thanks for the concern! \(And I'll add, not for the first time, yay Maxalt\!\) Tue Aug 31 22:24:01 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 08 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @theorymeg You'll be great! Wed Sep 01 16:53:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Ugh, so sorry to hear this! I've had it happen a couple of times and found the waiting part to be the hardest. Sending good vibes. Wed Sep 01 16:54:21 +0000 2010 Hey, @thatcamp: did I see a tweet go by in the last day or so about THATCamp NYC? Looking for dates... Wed Sep 01 17:47:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @thatcamp Aw, dude. It was WordCamp NYC. Rats. Wed Sep 01 17:53:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Yup; that was it exactly. Wed Sep 01 17:53:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @thatcamp Undoubtedly! Alas, October would be the ideal moment for me to talk with a bunch of DH nerds about a *Drupal* based project... Wed Sep 01 17:58:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Who, me? \(Uh, yeah, actually\.\) Wed Sep 01 18:21:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Your feet are going to be on the ground. Wed Sep 01 20:25:24 +0000 2010 Okay, what gives? The Weather app on my iPhone keeps changing my New York entry to Hoboken. Wed Sep 01 20:31:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @nowviskie Wasn't really trying; just wondering who else here is as suggestible as I am. Wed Sep 01 20:33:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I carry a compass. It helps me along. Wed Sep 01 20:40:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Forward. Wed Sep 01 20:42:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @utmediaresearch I doubt that's it; there is definitely a weather station in NYC \(in Central Park\) , and this is a new problem... Wed Sep 01 20:54:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @utmediaresearch The Apple discussion pages have other folks pointing to the same issue; may be a problem in Yahoo's feed, or in iOS 4.02. Wed Sep 01 20:54:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention This just started today; for the last three weeks, it's said "New York." My Paris entry changed, too. Might be a Yahoo prob. Wed Sep 01 21:03:28 +0000 2010 I do not feel at all ready to start writing this article yet, but I must force myself. Tomorrow. There Will Be Sentences. Wed Sep 01 21:23:51 +0000 2010 By the by, the 1st goal of my sabbatical has been met: I ran 3mi today without feeling like I was going to die. It's the first time in 5yrs! Wed Sep 01 21:25:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @nmhouston Calling me a marathoner would seriously over dignify it, but I have run 2. Blew an arch in training for the 2nd; hence the 5yrs! Wed Sep 01 21:47:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde I'd say that's a good question, except that I've been breathing August Manhattan air for the last month, and that can't be better. Thu Sep 02 02:08:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall You. Go. Thu Sep 02 02:09:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Eek! Even if you mean minutes per mile, you sorely overestimate me. I'm running just under 11 right now, aiming for 10. Thu Sep 02 02:10:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @nmhouston Not just the arch that's healing, but the desire to run. So nice to have it back. No more marathons for me; I'm happy with my 3. Thu Sep 02 02:15:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention That's great, though! We'll definitely do it. My secondary goal this year will be motivating others. http://bit.ly/dc0aVw Thu Sep 02 02:28:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench What I find myself astonished by are the conferences that use campus facilities and still charge hundreds. Thu Sep 02 13:23:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Yes, but surely campuses don't charge hotel conv center rates? Or perhaps not *don't* but *shouldn't*. Thu Sep 02 13:34:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek No, really. I love the DH confs, don't get me wrong, but they're crazy expensive in comparison with most humanities confs. Thu Sep 02 13:35:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @fraistat Don't misunderstand: I'm not operating under the illusion that ADHO is making $$ off the conf. But they're expensive enough \(1/2\) Thu Sep 02 13:48:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @fraistat in comparison with other confs that I wonder. If it's the campus facilities/services that are the cause, that's upsetting. \(2/3\) Thu Sep 02 13:51:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @fraistat One would like to think campus facilities/services were there to, say, facilitate and serve academic endeavors. \(3/3\) Thu Sep 02 13:52:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @sshreeves Yeeks. That's awful. Thu Sep 02 14:04:21 +0000 2010 The entire university system has become a demented revenue generation machine, with one branch being extorted by another \(1/2\) Thu Sep 02 14:05:23 +0000 2010 and looking for ways to pass those charges on. \(2/2\) Thu Sep 02 14:05:48 +0000 2010 Speaking of DH conferences, is anyone from #DH2010 still reading that email account? I have an issue I inquired about a week or so ago... Thu Sep 02 15:55:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras Thanks, Melissa! I'll do so. Thu Sep 02 16:03:13 +0000 2010 Walked into the office... having left the laptop at home. It's me and the iPhone and a... whaddaya call those things? Oh, yeah: a book. Thu Sep 02 17:22:33 +0000 2010 The book? Ironically enough, Ted Striphas's brilliant The Late Age of Print. Thu Sep 02 17:25:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @nkelber This is an even older model. Paper-based, if you can believe it. A little static, but very stable. Thu Sep 02 17:30:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj My sense is that it starts with stuff you bought from the iTunes store, because you must like that stuff the best! Thu Sep 02 18:03:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj Mine was off bc I've bought so much more from Amazon than iTunes, but yeah, the iTunes purchases it grabbed were weird. Thu Sep 02 18:15:20 +0000 2010 I don't know if it's the heat or what, but I've had a lousy headache and a total lack of focus for three days now. Enoughs, already. Thu Sep 02 21:15:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley It's possible. But it also feels like it's the weight of my glasses on my nose. Could they be heavier in NY than in CA? Thu Sep 02 21:26:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Maybe it's the higher humidity interacting with the antireflective coating or something. Thu Sep 02 21:36:17 +0000 2010 I have literally just put in my contact lenses because my glasses were giving me a bridge of the nose ache. Thu Sep 02 21:37:27 +0000 2010 Given that I don't see as well with contacts anymore, and have been forbidden from wearing them full time anymore, this does not bode well. Thu Sep 02 21:38:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp No, mine are plastic. I'm not sure why they're suddenly bothering me so much; they never have before... Thu Sep 02 21:43:59 +0000 2010 Just got an email from Facebook saying they were sorry I was having trouble logging into my account. I wasn't. It's a password reset msg... Fri Sep 03 02:47:02 +0000 2010 ...and all the links appear to actually go to Facebook. Does this mean someone was trying to login to my account? Fri Sep 03 02:47:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp Hmmm. Raw email message is a mass of code; no real text. Headers look realistic, though. Fri Sep 03 03:30:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @mikejolk Definitely did not follow any link. Headers of the message look realistic but the raw message itself is a mass of code. A puzzle. Fri Sep 03 03:31:43 +0000 2010 After an inexplicably lousy day yesterday, today is thus far 100% rocking. Fri Sep 03 14:31:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward I'm just trying to imagine a scenario in which a 2-yr-old needs to pronounce "macchiato." Fri Sep 03 14:39:19 +0000 2010 I'm not usually one for the #FF, but if you're not following @mlaconvention, you should. \(1/2\) Fri Sep 03 15:31:04 +0000 2010 When before has the membership of a massive disciplinary organization had such great access to its executive director? \(2/2\) Fri Sep 03 15:31:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm Yes! I love that I can dump messages that I have to do something about but not exactly respond to into "starred." Fri Sep 03 15:33:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm So they're still there & I don't forget about them, but they're not mixed in with the ones that need answering. Fri Sep 03 15:34:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention My pleasure! Fri Sep 03 15:35:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde As well it should. Fri Sep 03 15:54:18 +0000 2010 A 2-inch palmetto bug just came blithely strolling across my floor. I miss the desert. Fri Sep 03 15:57:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @AnitaBlanchard Oh, trust me: I grew up in Louisiana. I know, all too well. Fri Sep 03 16:00:15 +0000 2010 So now I have shoes on. Fri Sep 03 16:02:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Yes. I shall now tweet the cup of tea I am making. \(Actually, it's a response to the 2\-INCH FLYING COCKROACH in my apartment\.\) Fri Sep 03 16:12:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I had a cat years ago in Louisiana that loved to hunt these things. I really miss him right now. I swear I can hear it... Fri Sep 03 16:18:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton No, but I missed him with the legal pad I threw, and now have NO IDEA WHERE HE IS. Fri Sep 03 16:24:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm not sure Meiga would really like being borrowed, alas. Fri Sep 03 16:26:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @digipub Thank *you*! Fri Sep 03 16:53:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ I miss many things about southern Louisiana. These things are not one of them. Fri Sep 03 18:51:57 +0000 2010 Haven't seen the water bug since I flung a legal pad at him. Hoping the threat of flying office supplies has him looking for other housing. Fri Sep 03 20:18:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara Say you cannot discuss today, period. Do not talk with law firm AT ALL until you've talked with former chair or college counsel. Fri Sep 03 20:49:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Nasty things. The day I realized they could fly was one of the most traumatic days of my childhood - & that's saying something. Fri Sep 03 20:59:55 +0000 2010 A tiny shout-out to the NYTimes article on open peer review embedded in this Paris Review post: http://bit.ly/9YjTHc Mon Sep 06 18:33:46 +0000 2010 My latest is up at @ProfHacker: "Traveler's Aid: Delayed Flight Edition" http://bit.ly/aw9o4s Tue Sep 07 19:34:29 +0000 2010 I've been here a month today. A month! Already! The good news is that exciting things are happening, which I'll get to post about soon. Tue Sep 07 19:51:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy YAY!!! So glad to hear the good news. Tue Sep 07 21:42:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui Yay, congratulations! Tue Sep 07 23:26:54 +0000 2010 Uh-oh: Gourmet Garage curry cashews. Wed Sep 08 00:16:26 +0000 2010 High today: 82º. Current temperature: 84º. Before 11 am. Hmmm... Wed Sep 08 14:54:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench I thought it was hotter than it should have been! Wed Sep 08 15:02:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Quite possibly! I'll keep you posted. Wed Sep 08 15:29:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Not clickable in Chrome; Safari gives an error msg saying the url is invalid; Firefox tries to go to a url w/in the MLA site. Wed Sep 08 16:09:27 +0000 2010 I just bought a pair of reading glasses, which I now need if I'm wearing my contact lenses. This makes me less happy than you might think. Wed Sep 08 17:03:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @AlxJrvs Hey there, from my temporary quarters at NYU! Wed Sep 08 18:24:27 +0000 2010 You go, Dr. @alicetiara! RT @BiellaColeman: Another doctor in the house; just got back from @alicetiara 's defense. congrats. job well done! Wed Sep 08 19:56:13 +0000 2010 I am once again missing my laundry machines. Awesome delivery fluff-n-fold service, but with two \(pretty minor\) problems: [more] Wed Sep 08 23:01:41 +0000 2010 Problem 1: I am just anal enough that I wind up refolding everything. Really, I'd almost rather just fold it right the first time. [more] Wed Sep 08 23:02:29 +0000 2010 Problem 2: The running bra of mystery. A more serious problem. Where did it come from? Where has it been? Will try not to ponder. [fin] Wed Sep 08 23:03:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I have heard tell of such troping. Wed Sep 08 23:07:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @billiehara OMG. If you haven't already, get your university counsel on this. They have no right to corner you w/o your representation! Wed Sep 08 23:11:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges Hey, happy birthday! Thu Sep 09 11:49:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek That's hysterical. And dismaying, when you think about it. And then hysterical again. Thu Sep 09 12:04:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @cpatell Hey, welcome back! Looking forward to saying hi sometime soon. Thu Sep 09 13:03:26 +0000 2010 New post: Talk at the Hemispheric Institute http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/talk-at-the-hemispheric-institute/ Thu Sep 09 13:14:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yikes! Now stop twittering - both hands on the wheel! Thu Sep 09 14:23:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Phew. In both cases. Thu Sep 09 14:50:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @eetempleton and @samplereality I stayed there during SCMS last year and have to say that the place just made me feel really really old. Thu Sep 09 15:26:02 +0000 2010 For #mla11: don't miss 12 ACH-sponsored demos of New \(& Renewed\) Work in Digital Literary Studies: http://is.gd/f2ExJ //via @nowviskie Thu Sep 09 16:08:18 +0000 2010 Announcing the relaunch of The New Everyday at MediaCommons: http://bit.ly/cJJxUM See The New Everyday at http://bit.ly/9tS6PY Thu Sep 09 17:31:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum The author function? Thu Sep 09 18:08:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @whitneytrettien This is the beauty of Instapaper; I can placate myself with the idea that I'll read it someday. Thu Sep 09 18:14:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @jasonrhody I think this is the first time I've ever heard Foucault associated with flowchartiness. Thu Sep 09 18:39:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @lblanken I used it for a conf call this wk over an 800 # service. All I have is my cell & I didn't want to use minutes. Worked brilliantly. Thu Sep 09 20:07:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Oh, Vika, I'm so sorry. Sending you all my good wishes. Fri Sep 10 03:23:13 +0000 2010 Reasons to "pre-publish" your book online: reviews that appear before the print edition. \(Online, of course\.\) http://bit.ly/al7vcU Fri Sep 10 12:32:16 +0000 2010 Working at home today, and the internets have gone down. This is not cool. Fri Sep 10 14:41:18 +0000 2010 Okay, by my last tweet I meant to say COME BACK, INTERNETS. No fooling. \(Are you listening, RoadRunner NYC?\) Fri Sep 10 15:14:28 +0000 2010 I'd be worried about the relationship between the fact that the Internet is down and the fact that I've just made a breakthrough on [more] Fri Sep 10 15:56:53 +0000 2010 this article except that I've been frantically checking my Internet connection the whole time. Also, iPhone. So no correlation, right? [fin] Fri Sep 10 15:58:34 +0000 2010 Best birthday present ever: the massive playlist @mmwwah and Steph gave me for my 40th, composed of top songs from all 40 years. [more] Fri Sep 10 17:34:51 +0000 2010 I put it in my running playlist & am always thrilled when something I forgot I had comes up in rotation. Today post-run, "My Sharona." [fin] Fri Sep 10 17:36:28 +0000 2010 RunKeeper just sent me a FitnessAlert! email to say that I'd set a new personal record. That's the only kind of Alert! email I want to get. Fri Sep 10 17:47:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Heh. I get a lot from the mix and am almost always surprised, mostly in a good way. But there's always a second of "I own this?" Fri Sep 10 18:56:14 +0000 2010 Fabulous dinner with my sister, but am happily home. Windows open, fan going. There shall be valerian tea, and dammit, I'm sleeping tonight. Sat Sep 11 01:50:20 +0000 2010 You know what's awful? When people you really respect are totally gushing about the new thing from someone you hate. I mean, hate hate. [+] Sat Sep 11 01:55:46 +0000 2010 And they gush such that you think you might like it too if you gave it a chance, but you hate the person involved too much to do it. [+] Sat Sep 11 01:57:44 +0000 2010 So, to cut the cryptitude, everybody needs to STOP GOING ON about the new Franzen already. Not for cash dollars already. So just stop. [-] Sat Sep 11 02:00:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde There's definitely that. But I have hate hate deep in my heart, and even if this book were made of GENIUS, I don't think I'd do it. Sat Sep 11 02:11:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar In sooooo many ways. And they just keep growing. Seriously, Time Magazine? Sat Sep 11 02:12:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar That's what pushed me over the edge from "he's an ass" to "he's an unforgivable ass." & I'm less convinced about the gifted part. Sat Sep 11 02:27:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley I always knew I liked you. Sat Sep 11 02:28:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley and @billiehara White sausage, indeed. I now have an image I can carry with me that will keep me happy in these dark times. Sat Sep 11 02:29:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @kdettmar That was beneath comment. Suffice it to say: NO. Sat Sep 11 02:59:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattbucher Someday when we're in the same room I'll tell you about his performances at Pomona. Assholism of all kinds of varieties. Sat Sep 11 03:09:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattbucher And yes, Twilight, hands down. Sat Sep 11 03:10:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @naypinya Nice comparison! Similar smug self-congratulation. I never met Updike, though, so I can't do any personal comparison. Sat Sep 11 03:13:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Beyond awful. I so wish I could write about it. Sat Sep 11 03:13:56 +0000 2010 Okay, this bit of Franzen-bashing has been fun & all, but my friend the water bug has reappeared. Coincidence? So much for sleeping tonight. Sat Sep 11 03:15:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke That's... I... Sat Sep 11 03:19:37 +0000 2010 And with one mighty blow from her legal pad of doom, JF the water bug was no more. Thank god. http://yfrog.com/mxhqjlj Sat Sep 11 03:26:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Please don't. Sat Sep 11 03:30:35 +0000 2010 Conniving bastard was only playing dead! This time he's much more discouraged, I assure you, and also in a watery grave. Sat Sep 11 03:32:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley This is very, very true. Must figure out how to account for this in the second edition of Planned Obsolescence. Sat Sep 11 03:32:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @ambrose_mensch In the interest of someday once again sleeping in this apartment, I'll probably pass on the video... Sat Sep 11 03:34:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench They were mostly separate efforts. The CP developer is for the moment unable to push forward; not sure about digress.it... Sat Sep 11 03:37:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench I'm hoping we can find a way to get CP re-jumpstarted. Anyhow, I'd love to see your footnotes in action! Sat Sep 11 03:38:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @ambrose_mensch Oh, yes, I do remember that one! Sat Sep 11 03:39:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Ooh, wait! Are you running CP in WP3? I was under the impression that the upgrade broke the plugin! Sat Sep 11 03:43:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench That's what I was afraid of. MCPress is hanging in with 2.9 for now, but I worry about unpatched security holes... Sat Sep 11 03:49:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I got a bit carried away. He posed nicely before jumping up and running off again, though. Sat Sep 11 04:18:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Yeah, you can never be too redundant with a water bug. Sat Sep 11 04:21:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @PhDeviate It's a bit misleading; the project forked. http://bit.ly/cvLHkf MediaCommons is running in CommentPress. Sat Sep 11 14:28:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @PhDeviate CP runs in MU quite well, but make sure you're running 2.9 -- the upgrade to WP 3.0 broke the plugin. Updates TK soon, I hope. Sat Sep 11 14:35:59 +0000 2010 Having one of those dithery moments of difficulty figuring out how to get started today. Work on cutting the talk or on writing the article? Mon Sep 27 15:23:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Silly me. It's enhanced with simplicity! Mon Sep 27 15:27:21 +0000 2010 Left the apt with umbrella still in my bag. Got half a block down & had a 70ish man offer me his. Love this place. Mon Sep 27 16:47:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattbucher I'm a little behind today, but many congrats and all best thoughts to you and your family! Mon Sep 27 19:22:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Congratulations! Mon Sep 27 19:36:11 +0000 2010 Congrats to @jeromemcdonough, @karikraus, @Liebenwalde, @sheepeeh, @DigiArchivist and @mkirschenbaum on IMLS Nat'l Leadership Grant! Mon Sep 27 19:37:40 +0000 2010 RT @omeka: Happy to announce that @US_IMLS is funding the @omeka Commons project http://bit.ly/c9xbka! Congrats to all NLG award winners. Mon Sep 27 19:38:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @mckenziewark Hey, congrats! \(It's good to be full of something, and "it" is useful ballast\.\) Mon Sep 27 19:56:56 +0000 2010 Evernote, you've let me down. \(Made extensive notes at home toward the PH post I need to write; now finding they apparently did not sync\.\) Mon Sep 27 19:58:21 +0000 2010 The question is whether it's better to recreate from memory or to go home and see if the notes are there. Mon Sep 27 19:59:07 +0000 2010 Okay, home it is. Brain too foggy to reproduce yesterday's ideas. Notes, you better still be there when I get home. Mon Sep 27 20:11:45 +0000 2010 The good news: notes are indeed present on home machine. The bad news: have discovered MobileMe account has been hacked. Gah. Mon Sep 27 20:49:55 +0000 2010 Hmmm. Notes for blog post didn't sync because Evernote says the content isn't valid. Used TextExpander snippets; is that the problem? Mon Sep 27 20:53:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic Yeah, found that out after a long wait for support. An error msg other than "your password has been reset" would be better. Mon Sep 27 21:27:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Is all okay; was apparently a server crash. Suggested that some error msg other than "your password has been reset!" might be good. Tue Sep 28 01:42:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I can't say that I figured it out, but things seem to be syncing now. I reconstructed one problematic note & all appears ok. Tue Sep 28 01:53:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @captain_primate If it's the same one I got last week, start powering the zinc; it's no fun but it should pass quickly. \(Sorry you're sick\!\) Tue Sep 28 01:57:15 +0000 2010 RT @shanakimball: The future of the publishing industry may be bleak, but the future of publishing is full of possibility. #heckyeah Tue Sep 28 02:12:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @shanakimball Awe. Some. Tue Sep 28 03:11:23 +0000 2010 My latest at @ProfHacker: "Five Nifty Tricks in Google Chrome": http://bit.ly/9KwDTM Tue Sep 28 12:06:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Woo-hoo! \(I have to say, Pin Tabs totally made my day, too\.\) Tue Sep 28 12:10:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @annehelen and @jmittell Pin tabs is the entire reason I wanted to write that post. Tue Sep 28 12:58:14 +0000 2010 Following my own advice and restarting my 30min morning writing sessions. Today was day 2; so far, so good. Further worksprints later today. Tue Sep 28 13:03:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Ha! I sort of did it here: http://bit.ly/azREpi and here: http://bit.ly/aWKABS and here: http://bit.ly/bPO5Fa Tue Sep 28 13:26:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @nkelber Chrome made me loosen my death grip on Zotero a bit. Being between research projects helped. But I can't wait for Zotero Anywhere! Tue Sep 28 13:28:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie I was simultaneously inspired and massively envious, being trapped in a cold-induced haze at the time. But this week! I'm on it! Tue Sep 28 13:47:49 +0000 2010 Excellent! RT @nowviskie: SCI8 \(Scholarly Communication Institute\) report released! Emerging genres, experimental models http://is.gd/fxxs2 Tue Sep 28 13:49:11 +0000 2010 RT @jondresner: "Where Are People Stoned to Death?" line 1: "In Iran, sure, but those are mostly women." http://bit.ly/bVBZFK \(via @mmwwah\) Tue Sep 28 14:26:50 +0000 2010 This day started out fantastically, and has ended up not so much. Got more done than I expected, but the old morale is drooping. Tue Sep 28 20:28:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @nancybaym See you there! #ir11 Wed Sep 29 00:38:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley Almost makes me wish I had a kid to put one on! Wed Sep 29 00:43:58 +0000 2010 Still trying to decide how I feel about the ads for the new season of InTreatment using an utterly unironic cover of Gnarls Barkley's Crazy. Wed Sep 29 00:57:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @2xlp That's a VERY funny idea, indeed. Wed Sep 29 02:02:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @billwolff Fantastic news - congratulations on this first step! Now onward! Wed Sep 29 04:08:08 +0000 2010 RT @DHanswers: Got digital humanities questions? Experience to share? http://digitalhumanities.org/answers/ Wed Sep 29 12:13:04 +0000 2010 The draft of the article I'm working on now is really bad. But it's a rickety structure around which I can see something good building. Wed Sep 29 13:29:19 +0000 2010 \(I hope\.\) Wed Sep 29 13:29:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality It's one of my favorite Voyager projects, alongside Roy Rosenzweig's Who Built America. Wed Sep 29 14:38:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality \(FYI, rumor has it that if:book did a proof\-of\-concept port of Maus into Sophie\. So the texts are in theory recoverable\.\) Wed Sep 29 14:39:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory That would be absolutely brilliant. And you're right about the "in theory" part; Sophie may wind up in the same boat. Wed Sep 29 15:05:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Will. Be. There! Wed Sep 29 15:24:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention My favorite is the W4th station: ABCDEF, um, M. Wed Sep 29 15:33:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @footage and @samplereality Fantastic news! Thanks for letting me know. Wed Sep 29 21:01:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory No. Wed Sep 29 23:25:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Sorry, I was being "funny." The negative in your question made it ambiguous and seemed to call out for the negative response. Wed Sep 29 23:49:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Honestly, I think it depends on the issue: there are probably some provable negatives, and some that can't be proven. Wed Sep 29 23:49:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Hey, excellent; that date I can actually make! Thu Sep 30 00:40:16 +0000 2010 Just got a really nice set of speakers for my computer as a late birthday gift; amazing how much more I want to listen to music now. Thu Sep 30 00:42:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid and @amandafrench Much appreciated! Thu Sep 30 00:43:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Bose Music Monitor. Non-cheap, but really good. Quite rich sound for small speakers with no subwoofer. Thu Sep 30 01:12:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Welcome back! Thu Sep 30 02:38:20 +0000 2010 Tired is getting the better of me. Am crashing with the iPad, and will back for DC in the morning. See some of you tomorrow. Thu Sep 30 02:38:53 +0000 2010 Knocking some stuff off the old to-do list before hitting the road - um, the rails - for Archiving Social Media. http://bit.ly/9JXhOI Thu Sep 30 13:36:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Conveniently, I'll need one on this end to make it to the subway without drowning. Thu Sep 30 13:52:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Not when it's 110 degrees there, no. I'll take the rain. But ask me that question again in February. Thu Sep 30 14:04:47 +0000 2010 Standing in Penn Station waiting for the train, having massive grad school flashbacks to the two years R. was in DC. Weird to do this again. Thu Sep 30 15:20:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Happily, I stopped being busted flat in Baton Rouge in 1991. :) Thu Sep 30 15:28:54 +0000 2010 Uh-oh. Had a brilliant idea a couple of days ago for my next big project. Except I think I'm reading it right now, and it's not by me. Thu Sep 30 16:02:52 +0000 2010 There's likely still room for me to do some work. But it'll require a little rethinking. Which is probably not a bad thing at all. Thu Sep 30 16:05:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell Yes, absolutely; I have a few blog posts in the works \(or at least on the to\-do list\) . Thu Sep 30 16:11:47 +0000 2010 New post: Upcoming Dates http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/upcoming-dates/ Thu Sep 30 16:34:23 +0000 2010 Whoa. Flooding on tracks just north of Baltimore Penn Station. Thu Sep 30 18:45:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @nkelber Yes, just very slowly. But I'm headed south - I think one set of tracks is shut down, and it may be affecting northbound trains. Thu Sep 30 18:52:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie I have been having that fantasy, too. It may be supplanted by whisky. Thu Sep 30 18:53:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Hi, from a southbound train heading to Washington DC! Thu Sep 30 19:14:00 +0000 2010 Okay, I think this book I'm reading is not my project after all; it bears retain things in common with it, but there's lots to push against. Thu Sep 30 19:24:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @nkelber Looks like it should be clear by then, but I'll keep my fingers crossed for you too! Thu Sep 30 19:28:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Welcome! I'm waiting for the metro to Vienna. See you soon. Thu Sep 30 19:49:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench See you there shortly! Thu Sep 30 20:08:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde It should be cleared up by then; it wasn't deep but was enough to slow things down a lot. Thu Sep 30 20:32:34 +0000 2010 Okay, am at the Mason Inn, which is pretty swell. Starting to think about food... Thu Sep 30 21:30:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Good to know; thanks! Thu Sep 30 21:40:39 +0000 2010 In the Well, behind the bar. Looking forward to seeing any other #asome folks who might be around. Thu Sep 30 22:05:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Fab. @jcmeloni should be arriving not long after. Thu Sep 30 22:16:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Dude. You are in TRINIDAD. There is no being jealous when you're in Trinidad. \(But it would be lovely if you were here\.\) Thu Sep 30 22:17:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Great - we're behind the bar. Fri Oct 01 00:04:19 +0000 2010 Mine is called Sugar Dragon, but it's alternately been described as Monday in the sacristy and Wiccan bookshop. #asome Fri Oct 01 01:44:01 +0000 2010 Also, @mbtimney is rocking the Baby Gorgon Birthday Party - I can almost describe it, but then my brain totally freezes. #asome Fri Oct 01 01:46:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Report has it that they've still got @boonebgorges Star Lucy. Fri Oct 01 02:14:07 +0000 2010 @sramsay Report has it that they've still got @boonebgorges Star Lucy. \(Second time, now with \#asome goodness\!\) Fri Oct 01 02:15:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay I'm working on a bottle of Jameson instead. Maybe tomorrow. #asome Fri Oct 01 02:16:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmcclurken Don't worry! We're all ready to go, and looking forward to it. #asome Fri Oct 01 02:23:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmcclurken We wish you were, too. See you in the morning. #asome Fri Oct 01 02:28:19 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 09 01 tweets

docs.text: Whatever the noises coming from the wall behind my head are, they Must Stop. Srsly. Fri Oct 01 04:46:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall More Gorgon than unicorn, I think. Fri Oct 01 04:53:49 +0000 2010 #asome getting started. @foundhistory introducing. Fri Oct 01 13:33:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I swear I read about a service that did that. But now I can't find it. Maybe it was somebody's wish list. #asome Fri Oct 01 15:18:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @briancroxall Yes: the death switch variant of ThinkUp. #asome Fri Oct 01 15:32:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Yes, absolutely! Fri Oct 01 15:35:21 +0000 2010 Google Docs notes from copyright breakout session at #asome: http://bit.ly/bxhH6S Fri Oct 01 15:41:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Aw, shucks. You're *much* too kind. #asome Fri Oct 01 18:51:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Lots of issues, but for one, dark archives would defuse IRB concerns, making clear that archive = texts, not "people." #asome Fri Oct 01 19:06:51 +0000 2010 RT @JenHoward: Will some eager UK reader plow through the recalled text of "Freedom" & pick out the differences btw it and the approved one? Fri Oct 01 21:01:59 +0000 2010 RT @jwolman: @kfitz Let's let our silicon organs handle that one: OCR + compare documents in a word processing program. Fri Oct 01 22:02:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman I just need an unpulped copy. Oh, and a copy of the corrected version. And then something to get the taste out of my mouth. Fri Oct 01 22:03:44 +0000 2010 RT @nowviskie @foundhistory @jmcclurken Thanks to you both for the invitation! Really, top-notch conversations. #asome // hear, hear! Sat Oct 02 01:24:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @barbarahui I'm so sorry, Barbara. Sat Oct 02 02:40:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Thanks for the ride, and see you soon! Sat Oct 02 13:43:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie and @samplereality I was just happy to be there to witness it all. Also, that *someone* smelled like baby unicorns. Sat Oct 02 16:52:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum My eyebrows were merely arched in recognition and agreement. #asome Sun Oct 03 02:26:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Nice! Mon Oct 04 01:23:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley It's going to be a whirlwind, that's for sure! Tue Oct 05 02:16:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @joguldi Oh, Jo! How awful - I'm so sorry to hear this. I hope you're okay. Tue Oct 05 11:36:39 +0000 2010 It has been long enough since I've had a day to work that I've completely forgotten what I'm doing here. Tue Oct 05 20:33:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges Every time I travel I wonder about that. It would be a good Photosynth extension, I think, to be able to search for yourself. Tue Oct 05 22:12:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges Ugh. I don't even want to think about that. Tue Oct 05 22:15:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality The degree to which this is true is pretty much the degree to which I'm actually in DH, I think. Wed Oct 06 15:59:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I thought my ears were burning. Would love to hear about it! Wed Oct 06 16:44:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Nice! What was the response to your argument like? Wed Oct 06 16:52:55 +0000 2010 Final sentences are really hard. And they get harder as a project gets longer; it's hard to balance an entire book on such a little point. Wed Oct 06 19:16:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ooh, no. I just looked: it was "blues." \(Sort of\. I ended with a quotation, which was followed by a citation\. Bad dissertator\!\) Wed Oct 06 19:27:14 +0000 2010 What I really want to end with is "do it, dammit." Wed Oct 06 19:28:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I wish I could take credit. A favorite quotation: "One need not have suffered to sing the postmodern blues." Wed Oct 06 19:30:03 +0000 2010 I think I managed to avoid ending this thing with the phrase "into the future." Whew. Wed Oct 06 19:58:47 +0000 2010 That final sentence? "Change is coming, with or without us, and the choice is ours: we can work to create a scholarly communication [+] Wed Oct 06 20:25:09 +0000 2010 environment that will help new ideas thrive, or we can watch our current publishing system die, allowing the profession to become not [+] Wed Oct 06 20:25:54 +0000 2010 just obsolete, but irrelevant, silenced." [-] Wed Oct 06 20:26:19 +0000 2010 My feeling: the rhythm is still off. It's got too many phrases, and yet the final one is one item too short. Wed Oct 06 20:27:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Hmmm. Good idea! Wed Oct 06 20:37:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke Perhaps. I'm striving for exhortation but it makes me nervous, esp in the last sentence of the book. Wed Oct 06 20:39:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yeah, I think I'm attached to ending on the negative, too. It's the "or else" that feels most motivating, for better or for worse. Wed Oct 06 20:43:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke Except that in this case the future I'm projecting doesn't exist yet. What I'm basically arguing is "build it or die." Wed Oct 06 20:44:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke I hope that's what the whole book has done! And the thrust of the last few paragraphs has been not change-in-this-way [+] Wed Oct 06 20:48:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke but instead change-is-the-only-constant. Embrace change! Wed Oct 06 20:50:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke Perhaps it's that analogy I need. I'll ponder. Wed Oct 06 20:51:03 +0000 2010 I love that I left Twitter frustrated with my sentence, off to meet a friend for drinks, & return to many awesome stabs at a better one. Thu Oct 07 02:57:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @plragde The funny thing is that before I left I did break it into three sentences, first the part before the colon, and last: Silenced. Thu Oct 07 03:00:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @BGCDML Ha! I totally understand the issues of 140ch. Thanks for the ideas; I think they're bang-on, so to speak. Thu Oct 07 03:02:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @mariabustillos You rock. I mean, we rock. I mean, thanks! Thu Oct 07 03:02:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @mariabustillos Where @plragde is absolutely right, and where I felt the rhythm off, is in the need for the sentence to end in a three. Thu Oct 07 03:04:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @shanakimball Thanks! Thu Oct 07 03:04:48 +0000 2010 All of my Google calendars except my primary one are gone. This is not a happy thing. Thu Oct 07 12:32:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @mlaconvention The dept'l access also doesn't help the un- and under-employed. & many depts cut off former grad students. Thu Oct 07 14:40:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Is that access no longer controlled by the dept chair? Or is it now automatically available? Thu Oct 07 14:47:05 +0000 2010 My landlord just came in and pulled the a/c out and closed the window. Good grief but it's quiet in here now! Thu Oct 07 15:31:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I haven't heard it yet, though I've certainly felt it. Thu Oct 07 17:36:25 +0000 2010 The generic project-overview talk I've been giving is being published in the ADE Bulletin later this fall \(OA, thx to @mkirschenbaum\!\) [+] Thu Oct 07 17:43:27 +0000 2010 which means I'm going to need a new standard talk soon. You win some, you lose some. [-] Thu Oct 07 17:44:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Now *that* would be worth the price of admission. Thu Oct 07 17:49:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenServenti I suppose I had better spend some time with that dancing master, indeed! Thu Oct 07 18:01:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @patrickgmj and @dancohen Well, there's why he moderated you out, I suppose. \(How irritating\.\) Fri Oct 08 00:49:40 +0000 2010 Dear Google: It's awesome that you've solved the disappearing calendar problem and are rolling out the fix. Could you pls roll it out to ME? Fri Oct 08 15:20:34 +0000 2010 Have been without full calendarage for 36 hrs. Cannot schedule anything. Not good. Am now backing up via Backupify, but not a full solution. Fri Oct 08 15:21:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold @amandafrench @academicdave Yes, CP definitely works with WP3. Be sure to get the most recent version here: http://is.gd/fRtFL Fri Oct 08 15:34:49 +0000 2010 Hey, so, is there any kind of automatic link shortening available in #newtwitter? If so, it's not working for me. Fri Oct 08 15:36:03 +0000 2010 GCal: "we expect a resolution for all users in the near future. Please note this time frame is an estimate and may change." To what exactly? Fri Oct 08 16:08:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Which I guess is honest, at least. "We really hope to fix this, you know. Someday." Fri Oct 08 16:19:32 +0000 2010 My super-genius pal @mariabustillos on the for-profit college racket, in The Awl: http://is.gd/fRyIS Fri Oct 08 16:31:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @PhDeviate and @veek I'm pretty sure it's CYA GMT -7. :) Fri Oct 08 17:08:29 +0000 2010 One of my six missing calendars is back. Crossing my fingers that the other 5 will return shortly. Fri Oct 08 18:30:03 +0000 2010 New post: Ouch. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/ouch-2/ Fri Oct 08 19:06:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I'd say no, but that infighting sometimes looks pretty irrelevant to those on the outside. Fri Oct 08 20:13:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Or, to put it better, what we know to be serious critical debates sometimes look to the outside like irrelevant infighting. Fri Oct 08 20:19:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave Yes, exactly. Fri Oct 08 20:51:00 +0000 2010 While I wasn't paying attention, BackType Connect for WP got retired. http://is.gd/fTaci Need to find a replacement. Sat Oct 09 14:16:38 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Will do -- and backatcha. Sat Oct 09 14:31:07 +0000 2010 3 of 6 missing calendars are back, at last. Come on, GCal! Sat Oct 09 18:43:52 +0000 2010 All of my calendars are back, at last. Thank goodness. Now to figure out a better backup strategy. Sun Oct 10 15:48:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I told you that adulteration would come to no good. \(Or was it the fact that you were drinking Irish?\) Mon Oct 11 21:24:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni That figures; more Brit abuse of the Irish. :) For the record, I oppose @jbj's method of adulteration as well. Neat, only. Mon Oct 11 21:29:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I figured! The funny part is that I'm actually more Italian than I am Irish. Even with this name. Mon Oct 11 21:36:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Whoa. My brain just exploded. Mon Oct 11 21:42:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj Fair enough, and sorry for the false attribution! I took your "ice only" to be your own preference rather than a limit of toleration. Tue Oct 12 03:15:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges I was totally out in it, trying to walk down Dekalb in a microburst \(insane wind, rain, hail, rushing water over the sidewalk\) Tue Oct 12 03:16:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges Happily I made it to the wine bar of safety. Tue Oct 12 03:17:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @boonebgorges In retrospect, that would not have been a bad idea. It was mighty dramatic. Tue Oct 12 12:04:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @nancybaym I'll be there! #ir11 Tue Oct 12 18:58:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @captain_primate Me too. Tue Oct 12 19:11:28 +0000 2010 Need more hours in the day. Also need more focus in the brain cells. Tue Oct 12 19:41:37 +0000 2010 Related: drank my first cup of coffee in over two months yesterday. \(In homage to @jcmeloni, it was an Americano\.\) Drank my second today. Tue Oct 12 19:42:25 +0000 2010 Written on postcard in presentation I'm watching: "All fine and hope you are the same." Looked to me like "hope you are the sauce." Tue Oct 12 21:50:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Exactly. Tue Oct 12 21:52:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I love it. Tue Oct 12 21:54:45 +0000 2010 Presenter just used the word "relatable." Presenter is not a student. Tweeting so I don't scream. Tue Oct 12 21:55:47 +0000 2010 Can feel a big bout of writing coming on - perhaps even leading to the next big project... Tue Oct 12 22:43:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @ncecire And from a prof. Wanted to gnaw my own ears off. Tue Oct 12 22:44:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jasonrhody and @briancroxall Oh, great; now he's instigating infighting. Tue Oct 12 23:55:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @cully_hamner Ah, LA cats. I have a little anorexic one who had to be put on antidepressants to convince her to eat. Wed Oct 13 02:11:18 +0000 2010 @fordgilmore Ah, LA cats. I have a little anorexic one who had to be put on antidepressants to convince her to eat. Wed Oct 13 02:11:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Not sure. I'm headed to Sweden for #ir11 later that day. Might try to make it. Wed Oct 13 02:18:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Indeed. The madness begins day after tomorrow. Wed Oct 13 02:30:48 +0000 2010 Tonight, there was broccoli rabe. And good grief, but it was good. Wed Oct 13 03:04:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Nice. \(Also nice new avatar\.\) Wed Oct 13 03:04:49 +0000 2010 Like @veek, excited and impatient! // RT @stakats New post: Zotero Everywhere First Look \(with smokin' hot screenshots\) http://is.gd/g0neC Wed Oct 13 15:44:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @kfitz Originally posted on In Media Res! // RT @annehelen: Great @henryjenkins post on Harry Shum Jr. and transmedia success: http://is.gd/g0ur4 Wed Oct 13 16:38:50 +0000 2010 Have been experimenting w/ #newtwitter as my primary interface for a week now. I like it overall, like having it in a pinned Chrome tab. [+] Wed Oct 13 16:47:13 +0000 2010 But a few annoyances: want full convo in right column when I click on an @-reply. Want Mentions to highlight when something new appears. [+] Wed Oct 13 16:48:35 +0000 2010 Want a Quote option in addition to Retweet. And want option to maintain place when new tweets load, rather than having to scroll down. Wed Oct 13 16:49:34 +0000 2010 Interestingly, these are all in the Twitter iPhone/iPad app. Wed Oct 13 16:50:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yeah, I'm with you; the irritations are beginning to take over my attention here. #newtwitter Wed Oct 13 16:57:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I'd tried a bunch of 3rd party clients but had settled into Tweetie, pretty exclusively. Wed Oct 13 17:03:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall If you're asking if I'll leave the browser client set to #newtwitter rather than reverting, yes. But may go back to Tweetie. Wed Oct 13 17:05:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @annehelen No worries - just wanted to keep us in the mix! Wed Oct 13 17:13:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @footnotesrising See, but, two tabs? Way too clunky. \(Also, pinned tabs don't show the number\.\) Wed Oct 13 19:08:27 +0000 2010 @samplereality I do not know why this JUST NOW occurred to me, but \(a\) I love your blog audit post on PH, and \(b\) an AWESOME way of [+] Wed Oct 13 19:20:26 +0000 2010 @samplereality producing such an audit might be to use @Anthologize. Have to run to a meeting but would love to ponder. Wed Oct 13 19:21:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality @patrickgmj Should have known you were all over it. I'll look forward to seeing how that develops! Wed Oct 13 20:57:50 +0000 2010 Rats. New running shoes arrived - a size too small. So another ten days in the old ones. Wed Oct 13 23:28:33 +0000 2010 Oh god yes please. // Stop the Signature File Insanity. http://nyti.ms/d6H3Dq Thu Oct 14 11:43:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I'm doing the same thing.
