Sun Oct 24 23:15:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @sleonchnm I'm just hoping that I'm breaking them in rather than vice versa. Sun Oct 24 23:16:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Is there ginger ale in that? Sun Oct 24 23:16:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I had one neat \(i\.e\., the proper way\) at the end of dinner, and am going to have another now. Sun Oct 24 23:20:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Unfortunately, I'm not back up to racing yet, in any sense - still just trying to build strength & endurance. Maybe this spring! Sun Oct 24 23:24:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Maybe not heathen, per se, but certainly no Maloney. Sun Oct 24 23:25:25 +0000 2010 So there's reading I should really do tonight. But I think the iPad is mostly going to be a viewing device tonight. I'll read tomorrow. Sun Oct 24 23:50:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave and @mlaconvention LA's the perfect place for it! Mon Oct 25 00:10:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave and @marcparry The minimalist shoes thing \(less the V5Fs than the Frees\) has me rethinking how my arch issues have been treated. Mon Oct 25 00:39:45 +0000 2010 I just want to point out that I made it to 9pm. After having woken up 24 hours ago & nearly requiring an ODH extraction shortly thereafter. Mon Oct 25 01:01:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell My fear was crashing at 8 and waking up at 3, which is all too likely. I'm giving it another 15 minutes or so and then I'm down. Mon Oct 25 01:09:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @jasonrhody, @JenServenti and @brettbobley You guys rock. Certainly made what could have been some un-fun moments this morning much more amusing! Mon Oct 25 01:15:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave and @triproftri You know the conv center is on the LA marathon route; might be useful for a long run. Mon Oct 25 01:17:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave It's all roads, of course, but it should be manageable, esp. if early in the morning. Mon Oct 25 01:20:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @kfitz @marcparry: @academicdave is dedicated. I am not. I am really just trying to get myself running again & not being entirely successful at it. Mon Oct 25 01:21:51 +0000 2010 Okay, now it's 20 minutes later than I really needed to stay up until. Good night, US twitter folk, and see you tomorrow. Mon Oct 25 01:23:09 +0000 2010 Wow. Went to bed at 10, and woke up at 2.30, thinking, oh no. And then woke up again at 4, at 5.30, and at 8.30. Am actually up now. Mon Oct 25 13:06:01 +0000 2010 Now waiting for tea to steep so that I can be awake as well. Mon Oct 25 13:08:02 +0000 2010 New post: Peer-to-Peer Review and Its Aporias http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/peer-to-peer-review-and-its-aporias/ Mon Oct 25 14:25:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings I find it terrifying. And realize that I need to make much more religious use of my VPN. Mon Oct 25 15:56:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Congratulations! So sorry not to be there to raise a glass with you. Mon Oct 25 16:36:10 +0000 2010 Am right on the cusp of making the decision to attend DH2011 without presenting. It's not as though I'd be able to get it paid for, anyhow. Mon Oct 25 16:56:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @drbrake Thanks! I'll look forward to reading. Mon Oct 25 17:14:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality I'm still not 100% convinced that they're no longer meaningful, but I do think they've become banal, which is worth noting. Mon Oct 25 17:56:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality If the zombie is a figure for the narcotized subject of late capitalism, its banality reflects that we are all that subject. Mon Oct 25 17:56:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Which is in its own way a really important thing to note. Mon Oct 25 17:57:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality http://www.lacan.com/zizdazedandconfused.html Mon Oct 25 18:00:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Ha. Zizek has always already written an essay on the zombie. Mon Oct 25 18:01:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @zota Does it work if you're using a VPN? Mon Oct 25 22:08:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @zota I will hope. It's the kind of thing that really makes me rethink how casual I've become about wireless network use... Mon Oct 25 22:17:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @striphas Thanks, Ted! Tue Oct 26 13:39:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @mcalamelli Works perfectly; thanks! Tue Oct 26 13:45:54 +0000 2010 For those of you who used BackType Connect to pull Twitter posts into your blog comments, there's an excellent new fork: http://is.gd/gkfXc Tue Oct 26 13:49:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @shanakimball Yay! Congratulations to all! Tue Oct 26 14:02:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Yay! Looking forward to it. Tue Oct 26 14:06:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Safe travels! Tue Oct 26 19:37:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Thank you! So glad to hear it. Now MAKE IT HAPPEN, Goog. Tue Oct 26 19:39:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Damn. The pressure's on. Wait! Now more pressure! Tue Oct 26 19:41:06 +0000 2010 I will use my 5000th tweet to say thanks to all of you: This community of scholars and smarty-pants folks continues to inspire me every day. Tue Oct 26 19:48:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Oh, crap. So sorry! Tue Oct 26 20:22:47 +0000 2010 I may or may not have installed firesheep today in order to see whether anything was visible over NYU's closed wifi. \(It wasn't\.\) Tue Oct 26 20:48:22 +0000 2010 Just discovered that \(a\) the pay network in this tea shop is totally firesheepable, and [+] Tue Oct 26 22:20:40 +0000 2010 \(b\) I didn't have it configured properly before, so need to test the NYU network again. \(PS: this thing is scary\.\) Tue Oct 26 22:22:41 +0000 2010 Interesting fact: used Boingo to log onto tea shop pay network; fired up VPN; lost connection to wifi. When I reconnected, my VPN [+] Tue Oct 26 22:23:59 +0000 2010 automatically, and though I'm now logged off of Boingo, I'm still online.[-] Tue Oct 26 22:24:29 +0000 2010 Wait! I was using VPN & watching firesheep, & my own Google id \(running in Chrome\) came up! Was it captured in the machine or over the air? Tue Oct 26 22:32:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @xina Yeah, not that, unfortunately. It's the Firefox plugin announced yesterday that basically lets you grab anyone's info off open wifi. Tue Oct 26 22:49:02 +0000 2010 Firesheep somehow just captured my Google id even though \(a\) I'm on VPN, and \(b\) I have nothing Google open. Freaked out; going offline. Tue Oct 26 22:51:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @moncia Basically it sniffs your login info as you use FB, Twitter, etc. Lets them log in as you. \(Am now on iPhone 3G, not wifi\.\) Tue Oct 26 22:55:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Hmmm. I wonder if Ff has my Google acct saved somewhere. I'll delete cookies... Tue Oct 26 23:00:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni How's your knee? You holding up? Tue Oct 26 23:05:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella Must have been the cookies; deleted & no sign of me. Still unnerved that my sessions are captured when I'm logged into VPN, tho. Tue Oct 26 23:14:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella I think. What I need is two machines side by side, one logged into VPN, to see what the other can capture. Tue Oct 26 23:25:06 +0000 2010 Ugh. Also need to test out using iPad while monitoring Firesheep. Tue Oct 26 23:27:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I'm so sorry. I'd send Vicodin if it would get there in time. Or, you know, if I had any. Sending good thoughts instead... Tue Oct 26 23:28:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @cscannella I want to say that I may have looked at it at some point, but I could sure use a refresher! Tue Oct 26 23:36:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @zota Exactly what I was afraid of. Is there a VPN client for iPad? Wed Oct 27 01:36:46 +0000 2010 Where are you now, @jcmeloni? Are you at least partaking of adulterated whisky to get you through? Wed Oct 27 02:14:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Ach. Airborne. I forget that airlines other than mine have that fancy in-air wifi. Will you be able to get comfortable in SEA-TAC? Wed Oct 27 02:22:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Figured out that it was google.com that was in http rather than https; if yr logged in & google sthg yr acct cd be open. Wed Oct 27 03:47:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek You said it; it's sticky down here, too. I did request actual fall crispness for Friday's trip, so... Wed Oct 27 14:22:57 +0000 2010 Two nights of stress dreams. Feeling overwhelmed by my to-do list. And I'm supposed to be on sabbatical! How did I get myself into this? Wed Oct 27 16:25:34 +0000 2010 In happier news, the 1Password Chrome plugin has made a huge advance as of today. Wed Oct 27 16:27:52 +0000 2010 Also, one of those good-news/bad-news situations: the good news is that my property taxes have dropped almost $1K over the last two years. Wed Oct 27 16:44:21 +0000 2010 The bad news is that there's a reason why my property taxes have dropped almost $1K over the last two years. Wed Oct 27 16:44:44 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Oh, good lord! I'm glad to know you're fine, but how terrifying! Wed Oct 27 17:19:24 +0000 2010 Okay, figured out a crucial part of how to manage a project with a very impending due date. Now to do the actual managing. Wed Oct 27 17:41:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @dougreside Alas, I think "manage" was misleading. What I figured out is specific to *this* project. Oh, if it were only transferable! Wed Oct 27 17:58:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Must figure out how to do that. But YES, absolutely. Wed Oct 27 19:50:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention There's always a catch, isn't there? Wed Oct 27 20:05:23 +0000 2010 RT @mlaconvention: NYU grads get another chance to vote to unionize: http://chronicle.com/article/Labor-Board-Gives-NYU-Graduate/125135/ Thu Oct 28 20:51:24 +0000 2010 @mkgold Just catching up here -- but wow! Congratulations! Thu Oct 28 20:57:48 +0000 2010 Home from all-day meeting at MLA. Need to go run. Need to finish slides for tomorrow. Need to take nap. Guess which one is winning? Thu Oct 28 21:14:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Sadly, it did not work out that way. Decided it was too late to nap, so goofed around for an hour instead. Now to work! Thu Oct 28 22:43:08 +0000 2010 Futzing with slides goes better with a nice bit of Jameson. Fri Oct 29 01:37:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @loganpoppy Boy, I should have clarified. Yes, the whisky, thank goodness. Fri Oct 29 01:43:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ I wonder how the Chron would feel about that? Fri Oct 29 02:57:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj and @midwinter_ I think you meant to say Irish. Fri Oct 29 02:58:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj We can certainly meet up where bourbon is concerned. And possibly over a Lagavulin. Fri Oct 29 03:07:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj Ooh nice. Fri Oct 29 03:22:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @sramsay Whoa. Fri Oct 29 03:22:51 +0000 2010 Have finished reworking tomorrow's talk. Now, packing and sleep. Fri Oct 29 03:24:39 +0000 2010 And with that, I'm down to three overdue items on my to-do list, and eleventy-billion upcoming deadlines. Fri Oct 29 03:25:51 +0000 2010 Why is Gowalla convinced that I'm at CLT instead of LGA? Fri Oct 29 13:58:58 +0000 2010 On the shuttle to Boston. See some of you there! Fri Oct 29 14:44:08 +0000 2010 The view from my hotel room in Boston. No kidding. http://yfrog.com/emmmndj Fri Oct 29 16:52:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic So sorry you weren't able to make it! I hope you \(metaphorically\) caught that truck. Sat Oct 30 00:22:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek @jwacooks A wonderful dinner, indeed. Thanks so much for it & for your hospitality today. I'll look forward to the next opportunity! Sat Oct 30 01:18:13 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Funny thing is I just went downstairs & grabbed a Jameson, whereupon a waiter nearly ran into me, causing me to spill part of it. Sat Oct 30 01:30:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni For a packed bar in a fancy hotel, I think it's the closest outcome. Sat Oct 30 01:31:53 +0000 2010 Thought of both @mlaconvention and @samplereality when I added this image to the slides for today's talk: http://is.gd/gtcYi Sat Oct 30 01:37:53 +0000 2010 Wow, another huge interface leap for @1Password for Chrome. Nice! Sat Oct 30 01:39:49 +0000 2010 In contrast with last week's room in Sweden, the hotel room I'm currently in is bigger than my NYC apartment. Alas, only one night here. Sat Oct 30 01:42:04 +0000 2010 Pondering costume for tomorrow night. Torn between "academic on sabbatical," "woman four weeks overdue for a haircut," and [+] Sat Oct 30 01:54:09 +0000 2010 "hey, it's finally cool enough to wear my black leather jacket!" [-] Sat Oct 30 01:54:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @CapitolClio That's kinda what I'm thinking. Sat Oct 30 01:56:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley It's actually a bit nicer now. They've implemented most of the FF/Safari features. A few issues yet, but it's pretty cool. Sat Oct 30 03:41:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality and @brettbobley Oh, drag. Sorry about that! I'm sure it'll be coming, though. Sat Oct 30 03:43:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @cliotropic Thanks for the offer; I have breakfast included with my room, & then am off. Next time, though! Sat Oct 30 03:44:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost I was thinking about the other day, sadly enough. Also, that the post-pushback video shows you how to store your carryon bags. Sat Oct 30 13:37:09 +0000 2010 Goals for today: read. Sleep. Maybe run. That is pretty much all. Sat Oct 30 14:12:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Yes! My flight's at 2; I'll probably show up there about 12.30. Sat Oct 30 14:18:48 +0000 2010 Just had my first chance to make the choice between the intrusive body scan & the intrusive pat down. Went for the scan. Enjoy the pics, TSA Sat Oct 30 16:22:19 +0000 2010 I have run. I have obtained a serious haircut. That is all I intend to accomplish today; now back to my scheduled lolling about. Sun Oct 31 18:36:27 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 10 01 tweets

docs.text: RT @mediacommons: Call for participation in a forthcoming cluster on The New Everyday focused on the Tea Party: http://bit.ly/c8EUpA Mon Nov 01 14:27:21 +0000 2010 FYI: a call from the subletters/catsitters beginning "I hate to be the bearer of bad news" is not an optimal turn of events. Mon Nov 01 19:42:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Small cat was allowed to escape last night. She was found but may be injured. Theyre taking her to the vet now. I'm trying to breathe. Mon Nov 01 20:18:23 +0000 2010 Small cat apparently got in a fight while she was out; being held overnight for surgery tomorrow. Mon Nov 01 22:27:29 +0000 2010 If I wind up being arrested for assault on my renters, I hope you'll consider testifying on my behalf. Mon Nov 01 22:28:53 +0000 2010 There is a category of justified assault, right? Mon Nov 01 22:29:07 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Appears to be just cleanup, though the description sounds serious enough. Grrr. Mon Nov 01 23:29:07 +0000 2010 Thanks for the good thoughts, all. Small cat will certainly be fine, though I'm devastated not to be there with her. Mon Nov 01 23:30:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Thanks. I may be giving you a call. Tue Nov 02 01:55:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Aww. Thanks! Tue Nov 02 01:56:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Thanks! That helps a lot. I'm sure she'll be better in short order. Tue Nov 02 01:58:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @nowviskie Whoa. Tue Nov 02 03:19:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek True. But a compelling enough image that I was overcome by the spirit of Keanu. Tue Nov 02 03:41:10 +0000 2010 I #voted by mail three weeks ago - happily in a state in which the choices, if not necessarily the outcomes, are clear. Tue Nov 02 13:43:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek As I am fond of saying, we perfectly well have seasons; they're just more subtle, and require more careful attention. Tue Nov 02 14:30:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention No news yet; vet won't open for another hour or so. Surgery today; I'll call for updates in a bit. Tue Nov 02 14:36:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Thanks so much, Kari; I'll definitely update as soon as I have more info. Tue Nov 02 15:22:08 +0000 2010 Small cat just headed into surgery. Doc can't tell what got her; if it was another cat, it was a big one, he says. [+] Tue Nov 02 16:41:16 +0000 2010 Signs of infection which did not clear up with antibiotics overnight. And they suspect there may be other wounds they haven't found yet. [+] Tue Nov 02 16:41:59 +0000 2010 Grrr. [-] Tue Nov 02 16:42:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Me too, trust me. Plus, major tenant throttling. Tue Nov 02 16:45:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni You're the best. I'll let you know if that becomes necessary. Am employing mode "Fear of God" first. Tue Nov 02 17:28:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah I'm pretty sure but no longer as positive as I was. 4 deep lacerations+2 punctures on side; serious infection on leg. I'm just sick. Tue Nov 02 17:57:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Honestly? Makes me wonder if they lied to me about how long she was missing. Tue Nov 02 19:12:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention It's pure negligence - they left the door open while taking out the trash. & said "it was just for a minute!" My cats [+] Tue Nov 02 19:19:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention have never tried to get out, so I guess they thought they would never try? But how many cats can resist a wide-open door? [-] Tue Nov 02 19:19:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I completely missed that your cat was sick. I'm glad s/he's doing better! Tue Nov 02 19:30:35 +0000 2010 Small cat is out of surgery & doing ok. Several lacerations, a couple of drains, many sutures. Wounds fit the timeline, so I wasn't lied to. Tue Nov 02 20:13:58 +0000 2010 They're keeping her again tonight for observation, & she'll probably go home tomorrow. Which means my tenants may survive. Tue Nov 02 20:14:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Poor kitteh! My thoughts \(and small cat's meows\) are with her. Tue Nov 02 23:01:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Side and front, I think. I suspect it attacked and then clawed her as she ran past. Tue Nov 02 23:04:16 +0000 2010 Just got another call from the vet. Small cat's labs had gotten lost last night, and they just now came in. She's in early kidney failure. Wed Nov 03 01:21:56 +0000 2010 This is apparently the cause of the weight loss issues from earlier this year. They're keeping her until Friday. Wed Nov 03 01:22:44 +0000 2010 I am way less happy about leaving her with the tenants than you might think. Wed Nov 03 01:23:14 +0000 2010 The vet will walk the tenants through the new regime - drugs, new food, possible subcutaneous fluids. But... am thinking about heading back. Wed Nov 03 01:24:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I'm going to hang tight for a day or so and hear how she's doing before I make a plan here. Will keep you posted. Wed Nov 03 01:25:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward I would feel a lot better about that if I were there with her. I'm not at all sure how I'm going to handle this. Wed Nov 03 01:45:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Possibly the latter; definitely not the former. Ugh. Wed Nov 03 01:59:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward The surgery seems to have gone quite well. They put in a couple of drains and a bunch of sutures, but she seems to be doing fine. Wed Nov 03 02:06:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @morganmundum @sshreeves That's really good to know; thanks so much! Wed Nov 03 02:21:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention She's coming up on 11. You are incredibly kind to offer! Wed Nov 03 02:34:11 +0000 2010 This is shaping up to be a thoroughly depressing night. Wed Nov 03 02:36:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Pretty much, yeah. It's like watching a train wreck you've seen coming for six months... Wed Nov 03 02:47:50 +0000 2010 Breaking down and making coffee for the first time in months. \(A depressing night has given way to a pancake\-flat morning\.\) Wed Nov 03 13:22:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention No updates yet. Will send as I have them! Wed Nov 03 14:37:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @foundhistory Yes, and not a moment too soon. \(In fact, at least a week too late\.\) Wed Nov 03 14:49:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Aw, man. That would be tomorrow, as in Thursday, right? I made plans with my sister. Will see what I can do. Wed Nov 03 20:05:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Yes, definitely, another time! Wed Nov 03 20:34:05 +0000 2010 Was seriously regretting being out of cookies. Right up until I found the hidden bar of dark chocolate with a touch of sea salt... Thu Nov 04 01:04:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Woo-hoo! Thu Nov 04 01:45:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @BGCDML Hey, thanks - and back atcha! Thu Nov 04 04:13:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention All I got out of the vet assistant yesterday was "she's fine." So I'm hoping the same thing. Thu Nov 04 12:22:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen and @JenHoward I think "free rider" = unis that require faculty to publish but don't have a press. Riding on labor of few uni presses. Thu Nov 04 13:47:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen @JenHoward Perhaps so; I obvs don't have context. But key to the problem is uneven distribution of presses. If every uni had [+] Thu Nov 04 13:50:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen and @JenHoward its own publishing system, there would be no "free riding" involved. Everyone would be contributing. [-] Thu Nov 04 13:50:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward and @dancohen Exactly: unis w/o presses are tenuring faculty based on investments of few unis with presses. Thu Nov 04 13:51:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave It's not an atom/bit problem: not about how the object of publishing gets distributed. It's about how it gets produced [+] Thu Nov 04 13:57:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave and what it counts for. Unis w/o presses that require books for tenure are relying on investment/labor of unis w/presses. [+] Thu Nov 04 13:58:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave And that's where the "free riding" comes in: tenuring your own faculty at the expense of another institution. [-] Thu Nov 04 14:00:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave I agree, but that's not how they see it; they see it as other unis free-riding on their investment. Thu Nov 04 14:03:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave Again, agreed, but original comment was about presses' sense that other unis are freeriding. I am explaining, not endorsing. Thu Nov 04 14:04:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave I will say, however, that every uni that requires its faculty to publish must develop its own scholarly communication plan. Thu Nov 04 14:06:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @academicdave If for no other reason than that the presses aren't going to be there \(or be available\) for much longer. Thu Nov 04 14:07:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Not a problem I identified; I'm only explicating the Esposito comment tweeted by @JenHoward. Thu Nov 04 14:16:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid That said, it is a problem: every institution has got to think about its scholarly comm program. Might include consortial [+] Thu Nov 04 14:18:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid arrangements with unis that do have presses. Should def include digital publishing. But every institution needs a plan. Thu Nov 04 14:19:39 +0000 2010 Without solid schol comm plans on the part of ALL unis that require their faculty to publish, commercial pubs will continue to gain ground. Thu Nov 04 14:23:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @aasher Yes, precisely. But unis won't innovate in pub forms until they're required to take responsibility for it. Thu Nov 04 14:25:45 +0000 2010 Giving serious thought to bagging today's brown-bag lunch meeting, and instead staying home and reading all day. #sabbatical #dammit Thu Nov 04 14:43:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri Not sure what "constricting" would mean in this context. Such a plan should be flexible, but unis must take responsibility [+] Thu Nov 04 15:41:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri for the dissemination as well as the production of knowledge. [-] Thu Nov 04 15:42:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @BGCDML and @briancroxall Agree w/you w/r/t design. The port could be on the bottom w/o interfering with lines... Thu Nov 04 16:27:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall I always have to look, too. Super annoying. And many cords aren't *quite* long enough for free movement when plugged in. Thu Nov 04 16:30:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri I think I missed a verb there. It'll *leave* someone out? Or something else? Thu Nov 04 16:39:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @tanyaclement and @triproftri But there are discussion groups - Media & Lit and Computer Studies in Lang & Lit. It's just that divisions are [+] Thu Nov 04 17:47:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @tanyaclement and @triproftri still nationality/century-bound. An outdated model for many scholars; there are plans to reconsider soon. [-] Thu Nov 04 17:49:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @triproftri I sure hope so! Thu Nov 04 20:51:56 +0000 2010 I know there are several DailyMile users out there among my tweeps; are there any RunKeeper users? Thu Nov 04 20:52:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Excellent! Thu Nov 04 20:58:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I've heard this talk; I wouldn't hold your breath. Thu Nov 04 21:39:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum o. m. g. Thu Nov 04 21:48:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus and @mkirschenbaum Never mind abusing its workforce and lying to its students while driving them into debt. For profit. Thu Nov 04 21:52:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @karikraus Just terrifying. Thu Nov 04 21:59:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings I almost added "other than Liz" to the tweet! :) Fri Nov 05 12:59:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @wynkenhimself Libraries are increasingly negotiating persistent access for materials released during a digital subscription period. Fri Nov 05 13:01:54 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni The kitty is "fine," according to the vet assistant. I'm fine, too, if a little battered by this week. And you? And yours? Fri Nov 05 13:39:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum There's a little cognitive dissonance for you, right there. Fri Nov 05 15:24:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Indeed. Choose your ship wisely. \(And watch out for fog\.\) Fri Nov 05 15:39:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Dude, I'm two hours late on the twitters, but I'm totally with you on the September thing. Sat Nov 06 00:15:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I'm in for one. After that, I'm probably out for the day. Sat Nov 06 17:40:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Perfect. Sat Nov 06 17:41:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Finished revising one letter of rec; got a good way into revising a second. Sat Nov 06 18:31:57 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni @voncookie @literarychica @newkidonthehall I'm in. Sat Nov 06 18:40:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Read part of an article, responded to several bits of email backlog. Which feels better. Sat Nov 06 19:18:41 +0000 2010 Un-freaking-believable: Small cat is FINE. As in, they rechecked her renal panel, and it's within normal limits. Thu Nov 18 13:52:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I dunno. I have bus-related motion/exhaust issues that don't bother me on trains. So the extra $ is way more than worth it. Thu Nov 18 17:40:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention and @amndw2 Oh, no. The bus is sounding better and better, I bet. Thu Nov 18 20:38:43 +0000 2010 Having a drink with @amandafrench \(whose phone is out juice; she says hi\) prior to the @sivavaid/@anya1anya smackdown at Baruch. Thu Nov 18 21:54:39 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid-@anya1anya smackdown starts... NOW. Thu Nov 18 23:16:04 +0000 2010 Hashtag for @sivavaid-@anya1anya is #blsci. Thu Nov 18 23:16:57 +0000 2010 .@anya1anya's book was required reading for CUNY provosts' retreat. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:21:46 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid: Events like this are part of the value added that universities provide. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:25:27 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid: universities are a national treasure at a moment when all our other national institutions have let us down. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:27:41 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid listing the key aspects of contemporary culture that originated in universities: sendmail, stents, open-source, Google. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:29:30 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid defines education as exploration and transformation, when done right. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:30:51 +0000 2010 .@anya1anya: I don't really care about the future of the university; I care about the future of learners. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:32:21 +0000 2010 .@anya1anya: universities don't have a lock on innovation, and the university as an institution is becoming obsolete. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:34:10 +0000 2010 .@anya1anya: issue with institution is partly debt, partly that teaching methods haven't changed. How to foster informal learning? #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:36:27 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid taking issue with @anya1anya's assertion that teaching methods haven't changed. not fair to blame the secondary victim [+] #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:42:03 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid primary victims are definitely students, but faculty suffer along with them, esp in consumerist mentality in education. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:43:32 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid: Many ways to gain knowledge and skills; doesn't necessarily equate to a liberal education, which is a specialized thing. #blsci Thu Nov 18 23:47:11 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid: my job is not to give a set of skills or quantity of information, but to help you see what you dont know; unquantifiable. #blsci Fri Nov 19 00:01:50 +0000 2010 .@sivavaid: @anya1anya is the best evidence for the success of my argument \(about the importance of a liberal education\) . #blsci Fri Nov 19 00:03:32 +0000 2010 Very good question from audience about whether the benevolent dictator model of education might lead to more egalitarian outcomes #blsci [+] Fri Nov 19 00:10:41 +0000 2010 than the market model of peer-to-peer education, which can subtlely exclude. #blsci Fri Nov 19 00:11:32 +0000 2010 Great comment from the audience from a "College Unbound" student: the future university is a synthesis of these ideas. #blsci Fri Nov 19 00:24:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @mamamusings Am all but done at this point. Fri Nov 19 00:24:54 +0000 2010 Sorry for the twitter-flood, folks. Am done. Fri Nov 19 00:35:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @mattthomas Thanks, Matt. Fri Nov 19 00:48:15 +0000 2010 Simultaneously brilliant and terrifying: the Whip My Hair cover to end all covers. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SabKz3jdX_o Fri Nov 19 03:41:56 +0000 2010 RT @mkgold: RT @melissaterras We just launched http://bit.ly/ds8ixK - 4Humanities, advocating for why the humanities matter. Fri Nov 19 12:56:44 +0000 2010 I'm headed to Ambience in the Humanities this morning, a conference sponsored by the NYU Humanities Initiative. http://bit.ly/a2LA4I Fri Nov 19 13:47:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni You. Go. Sat Nov 20 00:30:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I'm about to heat up some leftovers and try to solve a weird javascript problem. And then watch a bunch of episodes of House. Sat Nov 20 00:31:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Oh, forgot to say: #wildfridaynight. Sat Nov 20 00:31:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni So sorry about the lonely cat. :( Sat Nov 20 00:34:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni No, I guess not. It's very sad, but at least you have each other. Sat Nov 20 01:15:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni It appears I'll be leaving on the 15th, but not before. Sat Nov 20 01:47:22 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Hmmm. We'll have to plot... Sat Nov 20 02:03:43 +0000 2010 I've had a song in my head for about two weeks now. I will decline to name it out of concern for the public wellbeing. [+] Sat Nov 20 02:07:58 +0000 2010 It's not really "stuck" there, as it comes and goes. But everytime it pops up, I'm convinced it's popped up for a reason. Like I just [+] Sat Nov 20 02:08:47 +0000 2010 thought something close to the lyrics. Or something. BUT I HAVE NO IDEA WHAT. [+] Sat Nov 20 02:09:19 +0000 2010 Make it stop. [-] Sat Nov 20 02:09:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni That I absolutely believe. Sat Nov 20 02:10:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @annettemarkham and @jmittell It's quite possible a cure will be worse. It's a little crazy-making, but mostly I want to know what's causing it. Sat Nov 20 02:16:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @kakennedy Also remember how much awesome is on the other side of it. You can do this. \(And if I can help\.\.\.\) Sat Nov 20 13:59:25 +0000 2010 20 Things I Learned About Browsers & the Web, a pretty nifty HTML5-driven "book" by the Google Chrome team. http://www.20thingsilearned.com/ Sat Nov 20 16:10:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @ddchamberlain Absolutely! They've been doing a whole series of such projects designed to show off Chrome's capabilities. Sat Nov 20 17:44:49 +0000 2010 Dear magazine publishers: The Economist has figured out how to give subscribers iPad app access w/o add'l charges. Catch up, or lose out. Sat Nov 20 18:54:59 +0000 2010 In fact, you can subscribe to the Economist directly within the iPad app, & the rate is less than the print edition. So it *can* be done. Sat Nov 20 18:59:07 +0000 2010 \(I'm looking at you, New Yorker\.\) Sat Nov 20 19:01:14 +0000 2010 This game is clearly trying to kill me. #nosrsly #geauxalready Sat Nov 20 23:06:02 +0000 2010 Wow. Ill-timed tweet. Sat Nov 20 23:08:36 +0000 2010 I swear LSU had defensive players on the field. I'm not sure where they went. Sat Nov 20 23:38:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @hollykruse You are Not Kidding. Sat Nov 20 23:46:38 +0000 2010 Completely worn out this morning. I blame the LSU-Ole Miss game, which tried to put me in an early grave. Sun Nov 21 13:17:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @KelliMarshall I totally understand. My attachment to LSU has significantly shortened my lifespan, I fear. Sun Nov 21 14:28:41 +0000 2010 RT @trevormunoz: Orwant: "I now want to formally and publicly recant my earlier statements"—Google going to crowdsource GBooks metadata ... Sun Nov 21 22:55:24 +0000 2010 New post: The Future of the University Press http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/the-future-of-the-university-press/ Mon Nov 22 14:26:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Wow, cool. You're right; that's what it ought to look like: aggregating the best of what's being done & fostering discussion. Mon Nov 22 16:07:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Hey, I've got a trunk of costumes! Mon Nov 22 16:10:30 +0000 2010 Skype 5 Mac beta just paused my iTunes when I got a call and restarted it when I was done. Probably everyone knew that, but I was impressed. Mon Nov 22 21:14:01 +0000 2010 Humanities Initiative panel, The Fate of Trans-, in the not so "Great" Room in 19UP. Ridiculous overflow crowd. Surely there's a better rm? Mon Nov 22 22:49:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Woo-hoo! Mon Nov 22 23:11:45 +0000 2010 Very interesting piece on the place of peer review by Bonnie Wheeler \(to which I offer a slight comment\-correction\) : http://bit.ly/cJto0Y Tue Nov 23 00:10:12 +0000 2010 Had to flee the hot crowded room where the panel I tweeted earlier was held. It's a shame; it was fascinating. But passing out seemed bad. Tue Nov 23 00:29:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @sivavaid Fewer than it sounds! This panel was an NYU Humanities Initiative thing on campus. Trying to take advantage of this sabbatical! Tue Nov 23 00:40:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah No kidding. I could use that martini my own self. Tue Nov 23 01:26:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Also, plan for MLA! Tue Nov 23 02:17:16 +0000 2010 New post: Relaunching The Anxiety of Obsolescence http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/relaunching-the-anxiety-of-obsolescence/ Tue Nov 23 14:00:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Rotation lock: double-click home button to show open apps; swipe app tray left-to-right. Rotation lock along w/iPod controls. Tue Nov 23 15:28:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @briancroxall Yeah, I hear you. I wish the hardware button were assignable. Tue Nov 23 15:32:11 +0000 2010 In case you missed it earlier: the full text of my first book is now available in CommentPress: http://bit.ly/9LcV3G Tue Nov 23 18:24:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum You mean I need to pay someone to give it away for me? Tue Nov 23 18:38:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Twitter's misbehaving today; this fell in my replies but not my main timeline. Annoying. Tue Nov 23 18:51:59 +0000 2010 About 5 hours into Thanksgiving. There has been gravlax, devilled eggs, charcuterie, lots of bubbles. Goose, mash, Brussels sprouts TK. Thu Nov 25 21:38:29 +0000 2010 Happy Thanksgiving, all. Thu Nov 25 21:39:19 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni and @veek Happy Thanksgiving to you two! Thu Nov 25 21:54:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras Congratulations, and all best to you and the rest of your family! Fri Nov 26 13:21:50 +0000 2010 NYU is so closed today that I couldn't go to the office if I tried. \(Yes, I tried\.\) Fri Nov 26 15:48:19 +0000 2010 Sitting in a coffee bar instead. Green tea, laptop, iPhone. Trying to finish cutting an article down to size. Fri Nov 26 15:49:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @antoniomarcos Yes - probably late summer. \(The ironies of the slowness of print, etc\.\) Fri Nov 26 16:01:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @BiellaColeman Hey, thanks! I'm actually pretty happy in this coffee bar, so all's well, but it's good to know how to get in if need be. Fri Nov 26 16:32:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @antoniomarcos Hey, that's fantastic - thanks! Fri Nov 26 17:40:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention and @triproftri There was some talk with @academicdave of putting together a group run... Fri Nov 26 18:41:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Oh, no! I'm glad they caught it, and hope you start feeling much better soon. Fri Nov 26 22:21:00 +0000 2010 @academicdave Sign me up for the #mla11 fun run. Looking forward to some LA running! Fri Nov 26 23:46:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell I'm so sorry, Jason. Sat Nov 27 03:02:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention and @mkirschenbaum But MLA regulations require that 15 minutes be preserved for discussion at the end of the session! Sat Nov 27 18:03:35 +0000 2010 Extremely amusing dream this morning about hanging out with my pal Barack. He offered me a ride; I took him to breakfast with my parents. Sat Nov 27 18:05:03 +0000 2010 Replying to @amandafrench Hahahahahahahaha! Sat Nov 27 19:57:33 +0000 2010 Just remembered that my dream about hanging with my bud Barack involved buying cigarettes & a long chat about being photographed smoking. Sat Nov 27 20:09:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Good grief, Jen, so sorry you're still feeling so bad. I hope they figure the antibiotics out soon! Sun Nov 28 00:41:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @melissaterras Too adorable! I hope you're doing well. Sun Nov 28 14:49:38 +0000 2010 The article I'm working on has taken a turn for the interesting. Of course, it now threatens to be much too long. Never fails. Sun Nov 28 18:34:43 +0000 2010 Started reading Zero History this weekend. Got 30 or so pages in and decided to back up. Pattern Recognition and Spook Country again first. Mon Nov 29 01:06:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkirschenbaum I completely believe it. But I found myself with so much half-remembered data that I figured full recall was better. Mon Nov 29 01:48:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @kimonizer On first read, not at all as compelling as Pattern Recognition. I'll let you know if I feel the same way this time. Mon Nov 29 02:12:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman Exactly. It would probably be fine with no familiarity, but the half-remembering is the worst. Mon Nov 29 02:37:18 +0000 2010 My new worst writing habits: this/such. As in "this mode of reference reveals my lack of imagination" and "such writing tics are annoying." Mon Nov 29 13:37:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @newkidonthehall I periodically find myself similarly profligate with "thus," I think as a substitute for actual logic. Mon Nov 29 15:11:24 +0000 2010 Productive day so far: drafted the section of the article I was most afraid of and began my Christmas shopping. Now for a bit more of each! Mon Nov 29 16:24:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @hollykruse and @jmittell Made me feel better, too; I'm a mere two months late at this point. But it's the latest I've ever been! Mon Nov 29 19:10:51 +0000 2010 In theory, one might have found a way to convert one's \(legally paid for\) Kindle books to ePub so as to have one's library all in one place. Mon Nov 29 22:54:05 +0000 2010 It is a known fact that of all the categories of my life activities, at least one such category must be sucking wind at all times. Mon Nov 29 23:46:23 +0000 2010 At least I must feel that way. Because what would I do with myself if I felt like I was actually accomplishing everything I'm supposed to? Mon Nov 29 23:47:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @budtheteacher One might have used a combination of an AppleScript http://bit.ly/a5s78x and some Calibre magic http://bit.ly/gr78Ih Mon Nov 29 23:52:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @audreywatters Awesome - congratulations! Tue Nov 30 02:28:25 +0000 2010 RT @anildash: Person who is adequately represented by software defaults feels software defaults are adequate: http://j.mp/el1en1 #Softwa ... Tue Nov 30 13:58:02 +0000 2010 I am approx two paragraphs away from having a working draft of the vastly overdue article. Of course, those two paragraphs require research. Tue Nov 30 14:14:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @tanyaclement and @jcmeloni Probably less funny as it's happening. Tue Nov 30 14:43:09 +0000 2010 Have re-read almost-draft, and aside from the two small holes that need filling, it's not bad. I think. About ready for another reader. Tue Nov 30 14:47:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @jmittell I hope to post publicly; just need to check with my editors to be sure they're okay with that before I do it. Tue Nov 30 14:52:52 +0000 2010 Editors of volume for which the article I'm working on is destined have ok'd public posting of the draft for comment. Expect requests soon! Tue Nov 30 15:35:35 +0000 2010 The first essay hole has been patched. That was easy. Alas, the second does not seem quite so promising. Tue Nov 30 16:30:24 +0000 2010 Hey, lazyweb: is there anybody out there writing about online community through Blanchot/Nancy/Levinas/Bataille? Tue Nov 30 21:12:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @mirk79 Thx. I'm specifically trying to uncover any work that thinks about "online community" through those theorists. Search results thin. Tue Nov 30 21:31:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @mirk79 Excellent; thank you! I'd only come up with one other possibility, so this is a huge help. Tue Nov 30 21:42:45 +0000 2010 I have just crossed a line: I am now annoyed when I can't get a book electronically. I remember crossing this line with the web yrs ago [+] Tue Nov 30 22:05:12 +0000 2010 the first time I got irritated that a business didn't have a web site with its hours & I realized I would have to call them to find out. [+] Tue Nov 30 22:05:55 +0000 2010 Feels much the same: under the irritation, shock that the assumption it will be there has come so quickly. [-] Tue Nov 30 22:06:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @brettbobley I'm still a bit stunned by how quickly that change set in for me, once I got the right device. Tue Nov 30 22:21:23 +0000 2010 And who is it that's holding up my fantasies of full digital textuality, my whole library on my computer? You'll never guess. Tue Nov 30 22:22:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @CapitolClio So. Annoying. Tue Nov 30 22:27:04 +0000 2010 Why, thanks for asking, @jcmeloni! University presses, that's who. \(Ba\-dum\-bum\.\) Tue Nov 30 22:41:25 +0000 2010 Okay, not all university presses; some are with the program. But the rest of you: ePub editions. I will pay for them. Tue Nov 30 22:45:08 +0000 2010 Oh, and I'm looking at you, too, Chicago. The Adobe Digital Editions thing does not cut it. I want to read your books on my iPad. Tue Nov 30 22:50:39 +0000 2010 Ugh, I forgot not to say the i-Word on the Twitters. Goodbye, spambots. Tue Nov 30 22:53:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @RepoRat Indeed. Tue Nov 30 22:55:26 +0000 2010 Replying to @shanakimball Hey, both are open standards! And both can be read on my now-nameless i-Device. So absolutely. Tue Nov 30 23:01:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @JasonParkhill I'll check it out! Of course, I'd rather an open standard. Tue Nov 30 23:05:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @JasonParkhill just pointed that out, too; I'll check Bluefire out. Thanks! Tue Nov 30 23:11:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais You know, I've heard these wacky tales of people actually TAKING THEIR BOOKS APART. Just urban legend, I'm sure. Tue Nov 30 23:12:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @KarlSteel Alas, the ADEPTs in question are pdf-based, not ePub. Also, I'm a Mac. The obstacles! Tue Nov 30 23:14:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @gkiely What I'm after is Liu's The Laws of Cool, which seems available in ADE only. I'll get Bluefire, but I'd still rather ePub. Tue Nov 30 23:17:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @gkiely Great to know; thanks! Wed Dec 01 01:09:56 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwissoker My funding offer was to purchase! Conversion of older titles is of course a problem, but there are good tools developing. Wed Dec 01 01:12:20 +0000 2010 RT @jafurtado: iPad Still Not Bigger Than Kindle As E-Reader, But It Will Be Soon, Says ChangeWave Survey http://is.gd/i12G7 /via @naypinya Wed Dec 01 01:14:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @kwissoker Fair enough & something I should definitely keep in mind. I am, it will not shock you to hear, impatient with change's slowness. Wed Dec 01 01:22:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @jwolman As am I, given ongoing selection issues! Wed Dec 01 01:23:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @JenHoward Missed this before, but Yay, welcome home! I know it's got to be a relief. Wed Dec 01 03:09:07 +0000 2010 A first: I am chilly in my apartment. I hope it lasts \(but suspect the radiators will kick in any minute\) . Wed Dec 01 03:35:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @halavais How old is he now? That's genius. Wed Dec 01 03:36:27 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 11 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @jcmeloni WHAT?!? I... am very glad I did not see this before going to sleep last night. Wed Dec 01 13:24:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @sherah1918 and @jcmeloni Oh, that's just the icing on the cake. Hmph. Wed Dec 01 13:25:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni We were so in sync, on UConn women, on USC football. How could things have gone so wrong? Wed Dec 01 13:41:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni You do, indeed. I think I can forgive them; I have my own complications. Wed Dec 01 13:48:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni And at least it's not USC football. That I honestly don't think I could forgive. Wed Dec 01 13:53:07 +0000 2010 I'm participating in #reverb10 this year, hoping to use the reflection to sort out some of my thinking for the year ahead. [+] Wed Dec 01 15:39:31 +0000 2010 I promise to try not to deluge you with posts, but if you're interested, the first is here: One Word http://bit.ly/ff80BP #reverb10 [-] Wed Dec 01 15:40:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @mkgold Hey, how long do you think you'll be there tmw? I've got a 5.30 book release party I have to go to, but wd love to come by after... Wed Dec 01 15:51:45 +0000 2010 Today is one of those days when I'm extremely grateful for a sabbatical that lets me work at home in my pajamas when it's nasty out. Wed Dec 01 16:44:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @lwaltzer I've been a dept chair the entire time I've had tenure, so no. But oh I look forward to someday having that fatcat tenured life. Wed Dec 01 16:49:40 +0000 2010 Happily eating at least some of my words from yesterday; this month's free ebook from UChicagoP is available in Kindle/BN formats [+] Wed Dec 01 17:19:36 +0000 2010 as well as ADE. A fantastic step - thanks, @gkiely. Wed Dec 01 17:20:42 +0000 2010 Hey, WP geniuses: just updated a site from 2.9 to 3.0.2 and now the media uploader opens in a new page instead of the popup/lightbox. Halp? Wed Dec 01 17:42:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Hmmm. You know, no. I think I can pull myself together sometime in the next six hours. Wed Dec 01 17:47:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah and @wynkenhimself I haven't read it before, and am totally looking forward to it. Wed Dec 01 18:19:35 +0000 2010 Replying to @janaremy Take care, Jana; sending you all good thoughts. Wed Dec 01 19:25:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Wait, what? I'm confused. Wed Dec 01 21:33:11 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ Oh, I see. I was getting the pronouns & referents confused. \(It was that he said she didn't grade them that threw me\.\) Nervy! Wed Dec 01 21:53:44 +0000 2010 Sitting in amazing Italian restaurant with @mlaconvention, plotting world domination. Wed Dec 01 23:53:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Nah, you're not off the team, Duke aside. You're just on the wrong coast! Thu Dec 02 01:25:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni That will be better. \(Though it hits me: at some point in the future I will cease to be on this coast\. Hey\.\) Thu Dec 02 02:20:50 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek I know! Thu Dec 02 03:12:03 +0000 2010 Quick poll: If someone were to post an article draft online for comment, would you be more likely to read and comment: [+] Thu Dec 02 15:34:55 +0000 2010 if that draft were a standard \(if very, very long\) blog post, or if it were in CommentPress? [-] Thu Dec 02 15:35:05 +0000 2010 \(The above assumes both pre\-existing interest and available time, of course\.\) Thu Dec 02 15:35:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @swarthmoreburke Good point. Thu Dec 02 15:39:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I do take your point about the apparent complexity of CP. But I worry about the tl;dr factor of a very long column of text. [+] Thu Dec 02 15:48:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I'm also not sure that paragraph-level permalinks are less alienating/more helpful. But the standard blog post is certainly so.[-] Thu Dec 02 15:49:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @BGCDML The newest CP actually has a nice "general comments" feature; hasn't yet been fully tested to see if it gets that thorough response. Thu Dec 02 15:50:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen No, I suppose it's not. I mostly was curious what the state of thinking about platform and discussion is right now. [+] Thu Dec 02 15:58:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen But I think @swarthmoreburke is right: the key factor \(as always\) is social rather than technological - community not platform.[-] Thu Dec 02 16:00:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen and @swarthmoreburke I could not possibly agree more. It's the key to the future of scholarly communication. Thu Dec 02 16:06:03 +0000 2010 Beginning to think I need to rearrange my books. This is starting to freak me out. http://yfrog.com/064x2xj Thu Dec 02 16:10:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @midwinter_ You should see it from here. Thu Dec 02 16:25:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander Publishers have the potential to be highly influential as aggregators, but they haven't yet figured out [+] Thu Dec 02 16:39:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander that that's where their future lies. Most influential now are the communities that form within social media, I think. [+] Thu Dec 02 16:40:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander But we need better ways of uncovering and filtering the overflow of content, which is where publishers would be helpful. [-] Thu Dec 02 16:41:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander Not entirely sure I'm following the question, but my immediate sense is that the vast majority is non-campus-based. [+] Thu Dec 02 17:24:20 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander and @dancohen has pointed to http://foodpress.com/ as being emblematic of the kind of aggregation we're talking about. Thu Dec 02 17:25:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj I am. \(Today's post: http://bit\.ly/gjOF2F\.\) #reverb10 Thu Dec 02 17:29:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @jbj Oh, whoops. "Crazy." Not "curious." Eyes not with the working today. Thu Dec 02 17:30:12 +0000 2010 Replying to @richardhross Worse: it's that he knows it. Thu Dec 02 17:40:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen and @BryanAlexander I'd like to know more about that, too. Thu Dec 02 19:02:09 +0000 2010 Replying to @BryanAlexander My sense? Not far at all. Some \(mostly centered in DH\) get it, but on average, non-print publishing still doesn't "count." Thu Dec 02 19:11:05 +0000 2010 I really did mean to go work at the office today. But I just looked up and saw it's almost 3, and realized all I've eaten today is a banana. Thu Dec 02 19:49:59 +0000 2010 It has been an intense day of work. And it's not done. The fruits of which will be available soon. Thu Dec 02 19:50:47 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Just got them - many thanks! They'll definitely come in handy. Thu Dec 02 20:53:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Yes, absolutely! Have been considering my reply as I work on the thing I'm about to post... Thu Dec 02 21:03:46 +0000 2010 I'll re-announce this tomorrow, but the draft of my article on Infinite Summer is now up and open for comment: http://bit.ly/eXdvWZ #infsum Thu Dec 02 21:04:01 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Both of those things are absolutely true, and serious issues. Was thinking abt the Instapaper issue just today, in fact. Thu Dec 02 21:42:18 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Wondering if a plugin like Anthologize might help mitigate the single-page issue? Thu Dec 02 21:44:05 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen This is much like what I was complaining about w/r/t books that are unavailable for iPad the other day. What did I do without it? Thu Dec 02 21:46:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen There isn't, alas. Something that I think needs to get built into CP. I'll pass it on to the developer, who's been very receptive. Thu Dec 02 21:47:41 +0000 2010 Replying to @ernestopriego Ooh, nice. I'll definitely pass that idea on, too. Thu Dec 02 21:55:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @ernestopriego Thanks so much. I do hope you'll pass on comments, either on the site \(once others have jumped in\) or directly to me... Thu Dec 02 22:05:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @wynkenhimself Thanks for letting me know! I'll see if we can find a solution for SQv2. Fri Dec 03 00:18:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen Thanks, Dan! [-] Wed Dec 22 09:36:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Yeah, the iPhone is nicely global but AT&T... well, 'nuff said. Wed Dec 22 10:05:18 +0000 2010 This has been a ludicrously productive morning. I've more than earned this evening's Pilsner Urquells. Wed Dec 22 10:06:40 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 20: Beyond Avoidance http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-20-beyond-avoidance/ Wed Dec 22 10:32:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Unthwarting ourselves required purchasing a last-second ticket from DUB to PRG and cancelling the DUB-FRA-PRG flight we already had. Wed Dec 22 15:36:51 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah But we were Determined, not least because the hotel in PRG was prepaid... Wed Dec 22 15:37:32 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Whoa. Wed Dec 22 15:37:47 +0000 2010 In case you missed it yesterday... Wed Dec 22 15:43:03 +0000 2010 We're happy to announce an online discussion of Ch6 of Tom Streeter's brilliant The Net Effect at MediaCommons: http://bit.ly/fc4428 Wed Dec 22 15:44:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Children, schmildren. Lazy little buggers should get a job. Wed Dec 22 15:48:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Have I told you lately that you rock? Especially for a suit. :) Wed Dec 22 15:50:06 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention That is a major issue, indeed. It's a lot easier for lightweight conferences to break even. Wed Dec 22 15:54:30 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost and @mlaconvention You joke, but the last couple of MLAs have been among my favorite conference experiences. Wed Dec 22 16:02:24 +0000 2010 Replying to @ibogost and @mlaconvention A fantastic argument for separating the convention from the job market, that. Wed Dec 22 16:06:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Good lord. Get to work on that ark already! Wed Dec 22 16:34:37 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Here's hoping you get to stay in there all day! Wed Dec 22 16:41:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Killer day. Ludicrously productive, with a good run to boot. Now there shall be beer. Wed Dec 22 16:51:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Last I heard, doing swell. Honestly, I'd like to know more. Wed Dec 22 16:52:16 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah No, won't be vehicular or have time enough, alas. Plus I don't really want to see what the tenants have done to the condo. Wed Dec 22 16:59:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Yeah, he's writing up a storm! Wed Dec 22 17:00:10 +0000 2010 This, I will say, has been a seriously kick-ass day. Wed Dec 22 20:50:37 +0000 2010 Morning, folks. Thu Dec 23 06:42:37 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 21: Future Self http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-21-future-self/ Thu Dec 23 07:02:47 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 22: Travel http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-22-travel/ Thu Dec 23 07:46:27 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 23: New Name http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-23-new-name/ Thu Dec 23 08:31:29 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I hope you got some sleep - I was worried when I saw that you'd hit the wall last night! Thu Dec 23 08:42:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Sounds good. Sending you good rest vibes. Thu Dec 23 08:50:01 +0000 2010 Today did not kick quite as much ass as yesterday did, but it was still pretty awesome, on the whole. Thu Dec 23 15:39:21 +0000 2010 Replying to @noradio Dude, where have you been? Clearly working too hard. This news has long since come and gone. \(And the trolling was mutual, I fear\.\) Thu Dec 23 23:40:14 +0000 2010 Morning, folks. Fri Dec 24 06:49:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Hey, welcome back stateside! I hope the journey goes smoothly from here. Fri Dec 24 07:09:15 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 24: Everything's OK http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-24-everythings-ok/ Fri Dec 24 07:33:02 +0000 2010 Shot a lovely bit of video of the mobile carillon last night - which transferred to my laptop in portrait rather than landscape. Fri Dec 24 07:56:10 +0000 2010 Need QTPro to rotate; only have that on my desktop computers. Grrr. Fri Dec 24 07:56:42 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Safe flying, Vika - and then safe driving after that! Fri Dec 24 10:13:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention It's a heck of a suit, though! #litfolksarehip Fri Dec 24 15:02:32 +0000 2010 Among the things we saw last night: http://bit.ly/eRreib Fri Dec 24 15:33:41 +0000 2010 Took me until now to get that posted because my iPhone insisted I'd shot it in portrait rather than landscape. Fri Dec 24 15:34:05 +0000 2010 New post: Among the Things We Saw Last Night http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/among-the-things-we-saw-last-night/ Fri Dec 24 16:36:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Hey, merry merry there. Nice to see you're having a productive Christmas Eve! Sat Dec 25 07:27:15 +0000 2010 Morning, folks. And Merry Christmas, for those of you who roll that way! It's a gorgeous, cold one here in Prague. Sat Dec 25 07:28:14 +0000 2010 Replying to @mmwwah Ugh, so sorry to hear about Kev's Christmas - but happy you're home. I hope the rain has stopped! Sat Dec 25 07:38:45 +0000 2010 New post: Christmas Eve in Prague http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/christmas-eve-in-prague/ Sat Dec 25 08:39:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Good night! And merry merry, to the two of you and the cat. Sat Dec 25 08:40:00 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Hey, you're allowed to tweet again. Enjoy the downtime, and safe driving tomorrow. Um, today? You know. Sat Dec 25 08:47:45 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek That's awfully nice. I hope the drive is going well this morning; I've just woken up from a most lovely Christmas nap! Sat Dec 25 15:09:43 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 25: Photo http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-25-photo/ Sat Dec 25 15:27:45 +0000 2010 You know what's awesome? When you gripe on Twitter about a book's lack of Kindleness, get a response from the ed, and poof: Kindle edition. Sat Dec 25 17:14:59 +0000 2010 Which is to say: thanks, @gkiely! \(Alan Liu's The Laws of Cool, now available in a Kindle edition\.\) Sat Dec 25 17:16:59 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Oh, no! Go get checked out! Sat Dec 25 17:19:58 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Ugh, I get that. But promise me you'll go on Monday if you're not better... Sat Dec 25 18:58:34 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni I take it @veek is driving. :) Sat Dec 25 22:49:17 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek *sigh* I'm on your side one this one. \(Though I'm happy to see the wasabi pea win ascendancy\.\) Sat Dec 25 22:50:54 +0000 2010 Alright, folks. It was a fantastic Christmas; I hope you had a great one, too. Sat Dec 25 23:11:15 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Yay! I hope things continue so fabulously. \(And that you win the no\-tweeting\-while\-driving battle\.\) Sun Dec 26 07:49:03 +0000 2010 Morning, folks. A good hour-plus later than usual here; we actually SLEPT IN. It's a Boxing Day miracle! Sun Dec 26 07:49:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @newkidonthehall My niece was reading those when I saw her last week, and just raving about them. Glad to have confirmation! Sun Dec 26 09:27:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @newkidonthehall I've stayed far from the Twilight series, so this is another good bit of endorsement. :) Sun Dec 26 09:32:56 +0000 2010 New post: Soundtrack http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/soundtrack/ Sun Dec 26 13:28:02 +0000 2010 Replying to @ayjay Actually, Echofon does better than Tweetie for Mac - shows convs backward & forward in time. Sun Dec 26 17:44:40 +0000 2010 Replying to @ayjay Yeah, I miss some of Tweetie's polish, though I actually like the drawer. What I really want is a desktop version of Tweetie 4 iPad. Sun Dec 26 17:51:33 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention How are you feeling today? Any better? Sun Dec 26 18:41:49 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Ugh - I'm so sorry to hear that, on all counts. I hope you're getting lots of fluids and rest... Sun Dec 26 19:30:39 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention Indeed. Just make sure you're making with the fluids and Tylenol too Sun Dec 26 19:33:46 +0000 2010 Replying to @ayjay I find I have to tell my Python scripts to "open with..." Python Launcher; otherwise, the Mac seems to assume I want to edit them. Sun Dec 26 20:04:08 +0000 2010 Replying to @ayjay Heh. The same Python scripts I was tinkering with. Except I wanted mine in ePub. Sun Dec 26 20:04:43 +0000 2010 Morning, folks. Mon Dec 27 05:30:43 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Good night! Glad you two are doing well - safe driving tomorrow. Mon Dec 27 05:39:17 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 26: Soul Food http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-26-soul-food/ Mon Dec 27 05:56:31 +0000 2010 Replying to @iantrevor I haven't taught it in a while, alas. I've thought about possible switches, but am still fairly attracted to those four. Mon Dec 27 20:22:48 +0000 2010 Replying to @iantrevor My pleasure! Mon Dec 27 21:12:52 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Woo-hoo! Tue Dec 28 05:59:10 +0000 2010 Replying to @veek Yay! Tue Dec 28 06:07:09 +0000 2010 Oh, yeah: morning, folks. Tue Dec 28 06:07:20 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 27: Ordinary Joy http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-27-ordinary-joy/ Tue Dec 28 06:26:39 +0000 2010 There's an irony of sorts in publishing this one in the middle of the night. I'll have to reannounce when folks are awake. But... [+] Tue Dec 28 07:59:04 +0000 2010 New post: On the Scholarly Press, the Manual of Style, and Intellectual Property. http://bit.ly/gulBRU [-] Tue Dec 28 07:59:50 +0000 2010 A quick self-RT for those of you who were \(sensibly\) asleep six hours ago... [+] Tue Dec 28 14:22:03 +0000 2010 New post: On the Scholarly Press, the Manual of Style, and Intellectual Property. http://bit.ly/gulBRU Tue Dec 28 14:22:36 +0000 2010 Replying to @dancohen I like that. "Hey, here's what you missed while you were out, Australia!" Tue Dec 28 14:25:27 +0000 2010 Replying to @sshreeves That's very interesting, esp since use of publisher PDF is one of the things the style manual seems worried about. Tue Dec 28 14:32:21 +0000 2010 @jcmeloni Hey, congrats. Good luck with all of it! I'll look forward to catching up in LA. Tue Dec 28 21:26:11 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 28: Achieve http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-28-achieve/ Wed Dec 29 08:24:28 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 29: Defining Moment http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-29-defining-moment/ Wed Dec 29 08:59:01 +0000 2010 Hey, @mlaconvention - how are you feeling? Wed Dec 29 21:00:28 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention That's great, I guess! And the fever? Gone, I hope? Wed Dec 29 21:44:11 +0000 2010 Boy, the stuff you people get into while I'm sleeping. Thu Dec 30 09:04:40 +0000 2010 Oh yeah: morning, folks. Thu Dec 30 09:04:53 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Are you still on the east coast? Thu Dec 30 09:10:25 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Oh, hey; I see you merged my DUB checking. Thu Dec 30 09:11:34 +0000 2010 @jcmeloni check-in, that is. Thu Dec 30 09:11:46 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 30: Gift http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-30-gift/ Thu Dec 30 10:29:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @jcmeloni Sad indeed. By the time I got a signal we were in the cab & the real site wdn't come up. I'll get the note when I'm outbound, tho! Thu Dec 30 12:13:55 +0000 2010 Replying to @samplereality Happily, I paid $10 for it two months ago. \(Actually, I'm not upset about that; I've really enjoyed it\.\) Fri Dec 31 08:13:39 +0000 2010 Morning, folks. Fri Dec 31 08:13:53 +0000 2010 New post: #reverb10, day 31: Core Story http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/reverb10-day-31-core-story/ Fri Dec 31 09:38:23 +0000 2010 Replying to @HASTAC Thanks - I'm very excited about it! Fri Dec 31 13:30:15 +0000 2010 Late NYE lunch, with a pint of Harp. I love this place. Fri Dec 31 14:12:32 +0000 2010 The definition of old: sitting at the bar at 9.30 on NYE and struggling to stay awake. Fri Dec 31 21:26:04 +0000 2010 Replying to @mlaconvention I tried a nap this afternoon but whoever was pacing over my head had other plans. :( Fri Dec 31 22:06:41 +0000 2010
docs.title: 2010 12 01 tweets

docs.text: The one day that everyone was still awake when I'd normally have gotten up, I slept until 9. Ah well - happy new year, all Sat Jan 01 09:31:36 +0000 2011 I have just spent a very pleasant hour reorganizing my Chrome bookmarks. Now I think I'll read a novel. Ah, holidays. Sat Jan 01 13:04:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Hey, happy new year. I hope it's starting well for you! Sat Jan 01 13:37:34 +0000 2011 New post: Twenty-Eleven http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/twenty-eleven/ Sat Jan 01 13:41:05 +0000 2011 2011 ate my blog archives. Not really, but it apparently hosed the WP Collapsing Archives plugin; all previous years are now inside 2011. Sat Jan 01 13:48:13 +0000 2011 I take it back; it's not the Collapsible Archives plugin's fault. It looks like WP. I've switched plugins but have the same problem. Sat Jan 01 13:57:16 +0000 2011 Looks like there's yet another WP release that'll hit soon; plugin pages describe compatibility with WP3.1. Sat Jan 01 13:58:03 +0000 2011 Never mind; was a collapsing archives problem after all, coupled with a cache that wouldn't clear. All's well with new plugin. Sat Jan 01 14:07:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni I love that this is all stuff that falls into the category of "important enough to fit into the car for the crosscountry drive." Sat Jan 01 15:51:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni That sounds about right to me. Sat Jan 01 15:54:37 +0000 2011 Hey, @goodreads doesn't block non-US IP addresses, does it? I can't get the site to load, but http://bit.ly/e1OaB6 says it's just me... Sun Jan 02 11:25:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @finnarne Hmm. Maybe it's the ISP here in Dublin. Thanks! Sun Jan 02 11:32:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @lddurbin Hmm. I'm in Dublin. Maybe it's an ISP thing? Sun Jan 02 11:45:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @lddurbin Interesting. I never get a response, in any browser. Sun Jan 02 11:48:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Yikes! Please feel better soon! Sun Jan 02 14:06:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie There's also Friday morning, of course. The good news is that the time zone should work in your favor. Sun Jan 02 14:08:25 +0000 2011 Morning, folks. Mon Jan 03 06:40:14 +0000 2011 New post: Homeward Bound, At Least Sorta http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/homeward-bound-at-least-sorta/ Mon Jan 03 10:01:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention NO! And I'm bummed about it. I have seen pictures though, and they both look good. Mon Jan 03 12:06:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention \(The pictures, though\.\.\. I'm trying not to focus too much on the questions they raise\. Like "where's my dining room table?"\) Mon Jan 03 12:07:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm not all that worried about it. Mostly just curious about its whereabouts. It's a pretty big table & wd usu be in the pix. Mon Jan 03 12:17:54 +0000 2011 Hey, @jcmeloni: you would not BELIEVE what I'm about to drink. Mon Jan 03 15:58:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni There was adulteration. It's pretty good! Mon Jan 03 16:29:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Oh no! Feel better, Bethany! Mon Jan 03 17:24:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Woo-hoo! Congrats, at last! Mon Jan 03 21:23:43 +0000 2011 Morning, folks. About to start the long journey: DUB-EWR-LAX-#MLA11. See some of you there. Tue Jan 04 06:10:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @triproftri Likewise! Tue Jan 04 06:11:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Thanks & will do! Tue Jan 04 06:27:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Wow, you're awake early. Regular insomnia or pneumomnia? Tue Jan 04 09:29:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Ugh - I'm so sorry. I hope the meds kick in quickly, and that all of you are well soonest! Tue Jan 04 10:01:46 +0000 2011 New post: Nothing to Be Done http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/nothing-to-be-done/ Tue Jan 04 10:02:26 +0000 2011 EWR. Tue Jan 04 17:40:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlmcgill No, 1055 in about 3 hrs. Safe travels! Tue Jan 04 18:04:40 +0000 2011 Will be arriving at LAX for #mla11 at 7.30pm; anybody else showing up around then and want to share a cab? Tue Jan 04 18:24:49 +0000 2011 On plane for LAX and #mla11. Sneaking suspicion that there's a nap in my future. Tue Jan 04 20:47:05 +0000 2011 Attempting to make a correction to a blog post via the WP mobile app \(bc the iPhone is my only connection\) hosed all the formatting. Thanks! Tue Jan 04 21:03:38 +0000 2011 Well, that post will be borked for six hours, as I can't fix it until I can connect with the laptop. Tue Jan 04 21:16:43 +0000 2011 LAX. Wed Jan 05 03:27:29 +0000 2011 Srsly annoyed to discover that while Marriott hotels are Boingo hotspots, the JW Marriott is not. [+] Wed Jan 05 04:20:13 +0000 2011 So now the dance of the ridiculously expensive hotel wifi begins. [-] Wed Jan 05 04:20:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @jsench Yup. There will be wifi at the conf, but in room it's $$. Wed Jan 05 04:22:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah $12.95/day. Wed Jan 05 04:37:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni You are SO RIGHT. Wed Jan 05 04:37:39 +0000 2011 Time to commit some alterations to the phone, I think. Wed Jan 05 04:37:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie I'm so sorry, Bethany! It's the right choice, though. I hope you start feeling better soon. Wed Jan 05 13:30:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie That would be fabulous; it would be great to have your voice present. Will get someone to keep an eye on the Twitter stream, too. Wed Jan 05 13:33:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Have had several friends whose email has been hacked "send" such messages. Almost certainly not true, but I can see why it works! Wed Jan 05 13:47:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander Actually, the connectivity has been pretty good so far, but then the MLA masses have not yet arrived... Wed Jan 05 14:17:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander Both of the hotels I stayed at in Europe, however, were a disaster of exactly that variety. Insane prices and awful connex. Wed Jan 05 14:18:23 +0000 2011 Yo, #MLA11 folks: this conference uses both the Marriott and the JW Marriott hotels. If you want people to meet in the JW, please be clear! Wed Jan 05 15:30:13 +0000 2011 New post: <rant> http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/rant/ Wed Jan 05 16:04:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni I hear you. I had exactly the same not-wanting-to-come this year: I just want to go home and be still. Wed Jan 05 16:07:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself Amen. I was just thinking exactly this. Wed Jan 05 16:29:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself Excellent! Wed Jan 05 16:41:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @swarthmoreburke Because they don't want to? Wed Jan 05 18:36:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Hey. Thu Jan 06 01:27:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @tanyaclement That ain't right. Thu Jan 06 01:30:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie When is 309? Would be happy to oblige if I'm not elsewhere! Thu Jan 06 06:03:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Maybe they were just trying to save you from adulteration. Thu Jan 06 07:41:29 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Sure, but that's the thing about being Irish Catholic. Confess your sins & pledge to sin no more, and it's like it never happened. Thu Jan 06 07:58:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni So you can do it again next weekend with a clear conscience. Thu Jan 06 07:58:31 +0000 2011 Jet lag has hit: woke up after 2.5hrs sleep; gave up on trying to get back to sleep after another 1.5hrs. This may be an interesting day. Thu Jan 06 13:19:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Not. At. All. Woke up before 3 am like it was a normal thing to do. Thu Jan 06 14:27:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Ooh! Thanks for the alert. Was just wondering when there might be an update! Thu Jan 06 14:28:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Guess this means I need to update to 10.6.6, though. Thu Jan 06 14:34:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender The old Window > Open in New Window isn't there? Thu Jan 06 14:34:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Fantastic - I've been hoping for a good update! Thu Jan 06 14:39:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Wow, you're right. It's gorgeous. Thu Jan 06 15:10:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Can't find it. I begin to fear they've done away with it. Thu Jan 06 15:32:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Oh yeah. And I practice every day, so I'm really good at it! Thu Jan 06 15:35:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Ha. If only. Thu Jan 06 15:36:42 +0000 2011 Running on 2.5hrs sleep. Not sure when I'll get to eat lunch. Combo entitled me to big cholesterol breakfast. Yum. Thu Jan 06 15:42:49 +0000 2011 One thing I'm discovering, though: Ireland has completely ruined butter for me. Thu Jan 06 15:44:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @annettemarkham Yes. I'll be dreaming about Irish butter for weeks. Thu Jan 06 16:12:02 +0000 2011 Speaking of Irish butter, the waistband of my skirt was suspiciously hard to fasten this morning. Someone must have shrunk it. Thu Jan 06 16:17:29 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay Works for me! Thu Jan 06 16:24:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @sherah1918 Happily, I just finished eating breakfast, or I'd be drooling. Thu Jan 06 16:24:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay Kind of figured, but heck, add salt anyhow. Thu Jan 06 16:27:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay If it doesn't, it should. Thu Jan 06 16:29:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay Alternate ending: I think that's the recipe for Bailey's Irish Cream. Thu Jan 06 16:30:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings Super cool, eh? Thu Jan 06 18:22:26 +0000 2011 #MLA11 folks: super-secret passcode for wifi at the JW Marriott is mla2011. Thu Jan 06 19:18:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @halavais I swear this kid is preternaturally grown up. Thu Jan 06 19:19:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball and @courant48 Hi, and welcome! Thu Jan 06 19:19:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @courant48 Thank you! Thu Jan 06 19:28:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Was temporarily. Back on now. Fri Jan 07 00:42:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention You see me when I'm sleeping? You know when I'm awake? Fri Jan 07 00:50:07 +0000 2011 Have been off the twitters for hours. Anything happen at #mla11 while I was gone? Fri Jan 07 08:53:28 +0000 2011 RT @jcmeloni: First up tomorrow at #mla11 : New \(& Renewed\) Work in Digital Lit Studies: An Electronic Roundtable ... Plaza I, JW Marrio ... Fri Jan 07 08:53:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni No way. Fri Jan 07 08:54:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Clearly I am still 6 hours behind. Fri Jan 07 08:55:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @jbj Young, adaptable, *and* experienced. That's... both flattering and alarming. Fri Jan 07 09:10:24 +0000 2011 Managed three hours of sleep before finding myself Awake. I sense a major crash coming - but it must wait until after 3. \(Preferably 5\!\) Fri Jan 07 14:00:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Dinner plans. Sigh. But I hope to collapse early. Just not too early! Fri Jan 07 14:43:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Clearly! Fri Jan 07 14:43:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Yes, absolutely! And a huge part of why we lobbied so hard for wifi. Thanks again; it's getting good use! #mla11 #newtools Fri Jan 07 15:46:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Need stickers! Fri Jan 07 16:41:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Haven't seen any yet; I'll have to pester him! Fri Jan 07 16:47:21 +0000 2011 Great crowd at DH demo session! #mla11 http://yfrog.com/h63r8hj Fri Jan 07 17:02:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie and @ryancordell I had asked an #alt-ac question, which was followed by the sound of crickets. Fri Jan 07 23:28:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie and @sramsay rocked, though you were ENORMOUSLY missed. Fri Jan 07 23:29:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni You're not downstairs in the JW again, are you? What is with that bar? Fri Jan 07 23:39:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Boo indeed! Fri Jan 07 23:43:24 +0000 2011 Realizing belatedly \(reading the stream\) how much I missed in the #309 discussion; wish I could have read along in real time. #mla11 Sat Jan 08 00:05:10 +0000 2011 Interesting \(and sad\) how moderating a discussion took my head out of that discussion. I plead ongoing jet lag. #mla11 Sat Jan 08 00:06:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat and @briancroxall Thanks, you guys! Sat Jan 08 00:09:18 +0000 2011 Survived until 5. Now the big question: nap before 7pm dinner? Or a drink? #mla11 #dilemmas Sat Jan 08 00:47:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @mariabustillos Thank you for coming and being so awesome. Sat Jan 08 00:59:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Good to know! Sat Jan 08 05:46:32 +0000 2011 RT @kathiiberens: Fun runners ready to rock! #mla11 http://twitpic.com/3o5at0 // Sorry not to make it; slept last nt for first time in 3! Sat Jan 08 15:20:07 +0000 2011 Have questions for a member of the Program Cte? I'll be in the MLA book exh booth in the LACC 9-11am. Other cte mbrs at other times. #mla11 Sat Jan 08 16:17:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @GeorgeOnline You & me both, man. was ecstatic to wake up at 7.15. #mla11 #nofunrun Sat Jan 08 16:19:12 +0000 2011 Rats. Arrived at conv ctr too early. Need coffee but coffee is trapped in exhibit hall, where I am not permitted until 9. :( #mla11 Sat Jan 08 16:36:38 +0000 2011 Weird. Got a slew of messages telling me about spam comments in my moderation queue last night, but there's nothing there. Unnerving.
