Thu Jan 20 18:44:45 +0000 2011 New post: Does Tenure Undermine Academic Freedom? http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/does-tenure-undermine-academic-freedom/ Thu Jan 20 18:46:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @samplereality Really, I think everyone should teach undergrads. Perhaps a shifting research-teaching balance, though. Thu Jan 20 19:34:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @samplereality Yes, absolutely, and academic freedom itself is about more than just risk-taking. Still an interesting thought exp. Thu Jan 20 19:38:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench So untenuring the tenured? Under what kinds of circumstances? Thu Jan 20 19:49:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench Yeah, that's different, but interesting nonetheless. Thu Jan 20 19:55:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @joguldi Good grief; that's some heady company! Thu Jan 20 20:17:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @joguldi Ditto both of those. Thu Jan 20 20:19:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @mkirschenbaum and @joguldi Indeed. Just re-read it for a seminar here, and boy, it's even more relevant now. Thu Jan 20 20:21:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @swarthmoreburke I'm trying to think of what the Fail Whale equivalent here would be. \(Also: sheesh\.\) Thu Jan 20 20:40:21 +0000 2011 The always brilliant Barbara Fister on the Chicago kerfuffle \(and she's not just brilliant because she's quoting me\!\) . http://www.libraryjournal.com/lj/newslettersnewsletterbucketacademicnewswire/888894-440/the_chicago_way__peer.html.csp Thu Jan 20 23:31:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni That's funny, because I may or may not have just poured myself a Jameson & ginger ale in your honor. Fri Jan 21 00:53:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm It's sad but true: @jcmeloni has led me down the primrose path. I am now an adulterator. Fri Jan 21 00:56:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni I live to please. Fri Jan 21 01:00:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm and @jcmeloni It is so, I fear. Fri Jan 21 02:13:48 +0000 2011 Sadly, remembering why I dropped my pre-streaming Netflix subscription: first disc into renewed membership is unwatchably damaged. Fri Jan 21 02:46:51 +0000 2011 Have requested replacement. If I have another damaged disc this month, will back off to streaming-only. Fri Jan 21 02:47:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj Hmph. Fri Jan 21 02:51:51 +0000 2011 Okay, two tasks accomplished this morning! Alas, one of them wasn't actually on the to do list. Fri Jan 21 17:26:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @triproftri I've offered feedback already, but a second reader might be good, too! #mla12 Fri Jan 21 17:46:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @triproftri Nope, I meant on finished proposal. I'm all over it. #mla12 Fri Jan 21 17:49:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken We draw a somewhat arcane distinction between cross-listing \(which involves a course \# in each dept\) and cross-referencing [+] Fri Jan 21 19:37:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken \(in which the home dept course \# is listed under the other dept as well\) , but the latter is quite easy. The former, not so much. Fri Jan 21 19:38:04 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken The course reg system is a big reason for the distinction we draw; separate course # = big headache. Fri Jan 21 19:39:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken So mostly technical, but partly philosophical: the course remains an ENGL course, but counts toward MS, for instance. Fri Jan 21 19:40:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken Very few courses really require true cross-listing, that way. Fri Jan 21 19:40:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken Yes, and it will appear in the semester's course bulletin under both ENGL and MS, though only with an ENGL course number. Fri Jan 21 19:41:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken Let me know if you need more detail at any point! Fri Jan 21 19:43:41 +0000 2011 RT @dougreside: Off the Tracks: Bill of Rights for Collaborators http://mith.umd.edu/offthetracks/recommendations/ //#trx4hx folks, you rock. Fri Jan 21 22:05:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @wpwend42 Congratulations! That's a great turn of events, however surreal! Fri Jan 21 22:19:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @maximolly Ooh, nice! Fri Jan 21 22:37:13 +0000 2011 I think the time has come to acknowledge that I'm no longer accomplishing anything, and collapse on the sofa with a novel. Fri Jan 21 23:05:58 +0000 2011 Well shoot. Was just settling in for a nice night of reading, but I seem to be having a migraine aura. This does not bode well for my night. Sat Jan 22 02:25:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton Thanks! What are your heading-off strategies? My drugs are specifically do-not-take-before-onset. Sat Jan 22 02:28:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton \(of actual headache; not aura\) Sat Jan 22 02:28:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton That said, they work. Sat Jan 22 02:29:13 +0000 2011 First sign of a migraine: visual disturbances. In my case, a burned-in spot like from a flash, except it grows & develops technicolor cilia. Sat Jan 22 02:42:30 +0000 2011 Second sign of an incipient migraine: disordered thinking. Like why am I still on the twitters when I know what's coming? Sat Jan 22 02:43:47 +0000 2011 And every siren in NYC needs to roll down my street NOW. Sat Jan 22 02:48:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @cmleitch I think this one may have turned out to be an ocular migraine; I've never had one of those before! Sat Jan 22 12:49:09 +0000 2011 I think all is well this morning, though it's hard to say. Slept 9.5 hours. Was aware of having a headache during the night, but [+] Sat Jan 22 12:53:27 +0000 2011 it felt like just an all-on-one-side headache, not my usual railroad spike. Feel a bit of a migraine hangover this morning, though. [-] Sat Jan 22 12:55:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj That 7yo sounds like a troublemaker. Sat Jan 22 19:20:16 +0000 2011 Slept 9.5 hours last night, and 2 more this afternoon, and yet I'm wondering how soon I can go back to bed. Sun Jan 23 01:21:42 +0000 2011 My eyes seem to be behaving better now, but the left half of my skull still doesn't seem like it fits properly. Sun Jan 23 01:22:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @CapitolClio I'm hoping so. I'll probably give in in half an hour or so. 9pm seems respectable. Sun Jan 23 01:33:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Thanks! It's not my favorite, I'll say. Sun Jan 23 01:33:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Thanks. Sleep certainly saved me last night! Sun Jan 23 01:46:02 +0000 2011 What I ought to be doing: building slides for a talk on Tuesday. What I want to be doing: collapsing on the sofa with a novel. Sun Jan 23 17:38:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Yes, quite! Thanks for asking. Sun Jan 23 17:49:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Better, thanks! Sun Jan 23 17:49:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde I take it that's a vote in favor of collapse? Sun Jan 23 17:52:47 +0000 2011 I feel the need to congratulate myself for finishing the bunch of bananas I bought this week before they turned. Sun Jan 23 22:05:48 +0000 2011 \(Of course doing so required eating three of them today\.\) Sun Jan 23 22:06:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym The big one or the little one? I saw a little one in action at #mla11, and was TOTALLY JEALOUS. Mon Jan 24 15:05:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym I will hope you have it by the time I visit so that I can experience that envy again! \(My 1G MBAir is due for replacement soon\.\) Mon Jan 24 15:09:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym I'm excited, too! Very much looking forward to seeing you and KU. Mon Jan 24 15:13:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @sivavaid Congrats, Siva! Mon Jan 24 16:57:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave Boy, they're awfully confident about on-time mail delivery, huh? A week's cushion would make me nervous esp this time of year. Mon Jan 24 18:19:23 +0000 2011 Slides built for 4.5 pages of 11 page talk, to be given tomorrow at noon. This is taking way longer than I'd like. Mon Jan 24 20:27:03 +0000 2011 Just got a big handful of Christmas cards. Ah, the joys of having your mail forwarded. Tue Jan 25 16:25:31 +0000 2011 Hey, my super-genius friend Maria makes me look not like an imbecile \(and gets the brilliance of \#mla11\) in The Awl: http://www.theawl.com/2011/01/the-126th-mla-convention-invite-us-all-in Tue Jan 25 21:05:04 +0000 2011 MT @mlaconvention: @mariabustillos, I <3 u for this: "After all, the MLA could be TED, just a century and a couple decades older." #mla11 Tue Jan 25 21:53:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Uh, hm. That better be an awesome proposal, @georgeonline! \(As I'm sure it will be\.\) #mla12 #notreallythreateningamI Tue Jan 25 21:56:08 +0000 2011 RT @alnthomas: Best piece ever on the MLA convention? @mariabustillos even loves the paper titles. #mla11 @Awl - http://goo.gl/GgoH1 Wed Jan 26 02:14:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @janaremy Oh, Jana - I'm so sorry. Wed Jan 26 02:21:31 +0000 2011 RT @mediacommons: Call for Submissions for a forthcoming cluster at The New Everyday: "Distraction Span." http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/content/tne-distraction-span-call-contributions Wed Jan 26 04:45:55 +0000 2011 RT @mediacommons: Call for contributions for a forthcoming cluster at The New Everyday: "Distraction Span." Please submit! http://t.co/A ... Wed Jan 26 14:05:36 +0000 2011 Can I just skip today? Or, like, pre-emptively call for a do-over to be deployed when I scrape myself off the couch? Wed Jan 26 15:03:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @rachaelsullivan Rats. Wed Jan 26 15:25:23 +0000 2011 The view from my window. This will suck soon, but you gotta admit it's pretty now. http://yfrog.com/gy4iqdvj Wed Jan 26 16:02:17 +0000 2011 MT @mlaconvention: 989 ppl gave to grad student travel fund for #mla11. 200 applicants got grants. Help us for #mla12? http://www.mla.org/contribute Wed Jan 26 16:06:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde It's less slackerdom than it is just not wanting to deal with today. Blankets over head, it's not happening, la la la. Wed Jan 26 16:21:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @boone wp-includes/kses.php does some heavy-duty stripping. I've had to edit it to get @clioweb's tooltip footnote script to work. Thu Jan 27 00:51:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I thought I heard thunder. Thu Jan 27 02:59:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I've seen a LOT of snow today. And I even heard it. And I heard the thunder. But I did not see the #thundersnow. Sad. Thu Jan 27 03:02:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention If #thundersnow fell in the city but no one was outside to see it, would it make a noise? Thu Jan 27 03:12:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention It's gone eerily silent over here, which I can only take to mean it must be coming down hard right now. Thu Jan 27 03:17:25 +0000 2011 NYU just closed for the day. The up side is that meeting is now cancelled. The down side is another week-long process of finding a time. Thu Jan 27 12:54:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @PhDeviate Yeah, no kidding. I think the closure was driven by transit shutdowns more than anything. Thu Jan 27 15:54:53 +0000 2011 @ernestopriego Yay, congrats! Thu Jan 27 17:46:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @drnels Indeed. Honestly, in the five years I was in grad school here, I don't remember a single snow day. Thu Jan 27 18:06:00 +0000 2011 Wow. Somebody just turned on the Pomona email spigot. \(I was apparently being migrated overnight\. Migration complete\!\) Thu Jan 27 18:07:06 +0000 2011 Today's view. Would have been more dramatic a few hours ago, of course. http://yfrog.com/h4buqlxj Thu Jan 27 18:18:37 +0000 2011 Will be exciting walking under those trees for a while. Thu Jan 27 18:18:51 +0000 2011 Thank you, EWR, for reopening as expected. R's flight in is on track to be 10 minutes early! Thu Jan 27 18:29:37 +0000 2011 RT @mediacommons: New post: Updates to "In Syndication" http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/content/updates-syndication Thu Jan 27 19:29:57 +0000 2011 Hey, it's 2.30. Maybe I should eat something sometime today. Thu Jan 27 19:32:55 +0000 2011 RT @mediacommons: If you have a media studies blog \(or know of one\) you'd like to see syndicated on MediaCommons, let us know! http://t. ... Thu Jan 27 19:41:12 +0000 2011 After a couple of trying days, this has been a good one. And R's plane actually did land at EWR 10 minutes early! Thanks, snow day. Thu Jan 27 20:15:54 +0000 2011 Remember when I was all excited about R's plane landing 10 minutes early? Well, he's been sitting on the tarmac for an hour and 10 minutes. Thu Jan 27 21:11:25 +0000 2011 Come on, EWR: plow out the gates, not just the runways! Thu Jan 27 21:11:55 +0000 2011 Gah! CO106, on the ground at EWR for an hour and 40 mins: still 100 feet from the gate. Thu Jan 27 21:44:35 +0000 2011 After 2 hours trapped on the tarmac, CO106 is finally at the gate and deplaning. Not cool, EWR. Thu Jan 27 22:04:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay Hi, class! Fri Jan 28 17:29:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave Go, Dave! Sun Jan 30 01:31:01 +0000 2011 Dear winter: I have to travel Wednesday. On an ERJ. Please knock it off already. Love, kfitz. Sun Jan 30 14:53:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Yay indeed! I shall face down the wintry mix with sheer force of excitement. Sun Jan 30 14:55:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Cold I can deal with! I'm mostly worried about the LGA end of things; next storm hits here Tues/Weds. :( Sun Jan 30 14:58:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Crossing my fingers that it will be wimpy by the time it gets here, and cleared out by noon Weds. Sun Jan 30 15:02:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Nice! Mine would want their paws on the paper I'm working on, though. Sun Jan 30 17:19:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah and @mlaconvention Mmmm... mouse. Sun Jan 30 17:50:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave Fabulous news, Dave. Sorry the sub-3:15 didn't happen, but a 5min PR at that pace is amazing! Sun Jan 30 18:22:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni It is a VERY useful term. Mon Jan 31 13:37:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Yeah, often in a kind of friendly way. Frequently preceded by "complete." Mon Jan 31 13:53:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Yes, definitely. Mon Jan 31 13:57:03 +0000 2011 One keeps hoping against hope that the existing curbside snowbanks will disappear before the next onslaught. Winter's response: fat chance. Mon Jan 31 16:23:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Whoa, indeed! Mon Jan 31 20:20:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay If you guys wouldn't mind passing it on sooner rather than later, it would be awesome. I'm flying from here to there Weds am. Thx! Mon Jan 31 20:24:07 +0000 2011 Continental website says my Weds afternoon LGA > CLE flight is cancelled. Agent on phone says only CLE > LGA is cancelled. Hmm. Tue Feb 01 01:55:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Absolutely. I'm keeping an eye on it. The good news is we have a little wiggle room... Tue Feb 01 01:57:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @adarel Yup. I'm dubious, but she was adamant that only CLE > LGA was cancelled, and that my segment would go. On what plane, I don't know. Tue Feb 01 01:58:18 +0000 2011 Second phone call: flight is indeed cancelled. Rebooked on flight 2hrs later, connecting to same second leg as before. Crossing my fingers. Tue Feb 01 02:12:17 +0000 2011
docs.title: 2011 01 01 tweets

docs.text: Okay, this storm is officially annoying me. And it's not even storming here. I might be able to forgive it if it were. Tue Feb 01 21:53:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @grammarsnark and @mlaconvention French at times uses objective pronouns for emphasis. \(Moi, je pense que\.\.\. C'est moi\.\.\. etc\) Tue Feb 01 23:48:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention, @mbtimney, @cmleitch and @rays6 Happy to advise when you're ready! #mla12 Tue Feb 01 23:57:09 +0000 2011 RT @noradio: Twitter on the Mac Tip: Right click any highlighted text in all native OS X apps and "Services > Tweet" \(or just Tweet\) ... Tue Feb 01 23:58:51 +0000 2011 Slowly catching up on everything that happened while I was speaking up at the Bard Grad Ctr. Including tomorrow's flights getting canceled. Wed Feb 02 00:00:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @footnotesrising, @mlaconvention, @mbtimney, @cmleitch and @rays6 We do in fact look for panel diversity \(incl multi\-institution\) . #mla12 Wed Feb 02 00:05:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @RayS6 Please don't hesitate to let me know if I can be of help as you're putting the CFP/proposal together... Wed Feb 02 02:31:06 +0000 2011 I am having something of an impromptu snow day due to cancelled travel, but it's raining and the high today is going to be 40. This is odd. Wed Feb 02 14:19:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I obviously haven't gotten near a sidewalk & can't see them from here. Good to know that my stay-inside plan is correct! Wed Feb 02 14:23:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @veek Yes, it's going to be nasty. I guess I'm not having a snow day but a slush day, which is all for the best. Wed Feb 02 14:24:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @veek It's what I always wanted in grad school: keep it in the 20s, haul as much snow away as possible, and then two good days in the 60s. Wed Feb 02 14:25:35 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: Extended til Monday: deadline to suggest yourself or others for #MLA committees. Change begins w YOU! http://www.mla. ... Wed Feb 02 15:23:37 +0000 2011 Okay, hacky folk: If one were thinking about beginning the process of migrating from MacOS to Linux, which flavor would you choose? Wed Feb 02 16:26:56 +0000 2011 If you're curious, it's this that's put me over the edge. Long-time Apple fangirl, now thinking of walking away. http://arstechnica.com/apple/news/2011/02/apple-responds-to-app-store-furor-says-it-wants-a-cut-of-e-book-sales.ars Wed Feb 02 16:28:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni That was what I was thinking, but wanted to be sure. Wed Feb 02 16:30:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Indeed. Wed Feb 02 16:31:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj It's either insanely stupid or part of some larger strategy that I can't yet figure out. Either way, as you say, sub-optimal. Wed Feb 02 16:34:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I'll look into that; stuck in my contract for a while, though. Wed Feb 02 16:35:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @brendabethman Assuming my rescheduled flight takes off tomorrow morning, yes! \(And I'll gripe on Twitter if things go otherwise\.\) Wed Feb 02 16:37:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @2xlp The point is not the hardware; I'm outfitted. It's the OS. Wed Feb 02 16:38:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm contemplating. I need to look at options more carefully before leaping, but am seriously considering the jump. Wed Feb 02 16:41:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention To android, esp if I wind up moving life to Linux more generally. This will all take time to sort out, though. Wed Feb 02 16:45:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Will do! Wed Feb 02 16:50:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Hmm. MBAir is coming up on 3yrs; iMc is a year-plus. Does that qualify as oldish? Wed Feb 02 17:07:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML I do see that complexity. But 30% is unreasonable as a tax on that resource use. Wed Feb 02 17:09:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Thanks. And ugh. It's kinda what I'm afraid of. Wed Feb 02 17:10:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML The issue for me is whether iOS devices are open or closed platforms. This decision makes that more clear than I'd like. [+] Wed Feb 02 17:12:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML I've got little sympathy for Amazon \(and zero for Sony\) , but taking 100% of the profit margin on book sales from them makes [+] Wed Feb 02 17:13:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML the device way more locked-down than I'm willing to live with. [-] Wed Feb 02 17:14:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML Except that if the only apps you can run must come from the App Store, the device is effectively closed. Wed Feb 02 17:20:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML Agreed on ePub. Happily, there are ways of forcibly opening those formats. Not that I would know anything about them. Wed Feb 02 17:20:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML Trust me, am a long time user. Wed Feb 02 17:21:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML There are plugins and droplets that can handle it. Wed Feb 02 17:21:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @BGCDML Hey, thanks! I had a great time, and look forward to further conversations. Wed Feb 02 17:28:22 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: CFP for #mla12: “Why Comics Are and Are Not Picture Books” http://tinyurl.com/65ul3x7 Wed Feb 02 23:12:59 +0000 2011 Okay, Ubuntu geniuses: have downloaded & burned installation CD. When I try to boot from it, I get a flash of the ISOLINUX version info, [+] Thu Feb 03 01:30:19 +0000 2011 a funky little graphic at the bottom of the screen, and then black screen & blinking cursor. Drive spins down. Nothing happens. Um, help? Thu Feb 03 01:32:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave I'm hoping to set the Mac up to dual boot, but it's Mac-only right now. Tried the USB approach & couldnt even get this far. Thu Feb 03 01:48:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @sheepeeh Nope! Don't get a menu at all, just the text line w/ version number, odd little graphic \(keyboard \= little person?\) and then blink Thu Feb 03 01:49:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni and @sheepeeh Yay! Though feeling like Team Noob right now. Thu Feb 03 01:50:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @wayne_graham I'm 98% sure it was the liveCD version; will reboot in Mac & check. Thu Feb 03 01:51:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ No, starting with the iMac, as I couldn't get the bootable USB to work. Thu Feb 03 01:52:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @sheepeeh and @wayne_graham yes, it was definitely the desktop edition. Thu Feb 03 01:52:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ It's a 9,1. \(Circa early 2009, I think \) Thu Feb 03 01:56:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @sheepeeh Yeah, those are the instructions I'm working off of. Got the right iso file, did a checksum, burned to DVD as slowly as possible. Thu Feb 03 01:58:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @wayne_graham Yup! *sigh* Thu Feb 03 01:59:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @sheepeeh Yeah. Puzzled. Found some folks in forums having trouble booting from CD, but I tried their workarounds & got nada. Thu Feb 03 02:02:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ DVD. Can't get the iMac to recognize the bootable USB at all. Thu Feb 03 02:04:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @sheepeeh Will show you what I've got; it's not much. Thu Feb 03 02:04:32 +0000 2011 @sheepeeh Whoa. This reboot actually brought up the language/boot menus! Now blinking cursor. But I'll wait a little longer... Thu Feb 03 02:10:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Yup, that's how I did it. Thu Feb 03 02:12:10 +0000 2011 Starting over clean. Probably more than I should have gotten myself into tonight, but what the heck. Thu Feb 03 02:33:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman Ha! Um, wait. Yikes! Thu Feb 03 02:43:11 +0000 2011 No dice. It's all blinking cursor, all the time. At least I've gotten a nice hard disk reformat out of the deal. Thu Feb 03 04:17:40 +0000 2011 Here's the weird little graphic I get right before I get the blinking cursor of doom. What's it trying to tell me? http://yfrog.com/gzo1hrj Thu Feb 03 04:29:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @nkelber Possible, yes. I'll test that out in the next go-round. Thanks! Thu Feb 03 05:08:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley Oh, you. \(Actually it would be saying "dude, I'm a Mac\. What are you trying to do?"\) Thu Feb 03 05:09:27 +0000 2011 LGA. Way too early. But flights are going, and mine appears to be on time. Thu Feb 03 10:16:18 +0000 2011 Ugh. What getting up this early will do: not only forgot the noise-reducing headphones, but also the earbuds. Flights will be audio-free. :( Thu Feb 03 10:48:42 +0000 2011 LGA > CLE > MCI > University of Kansas. See you there. Thu Feb 03 11:09:13 +0000 2011 Cleveland. Boy, there's a whole lot of snow out there. Thu Feb 03 12:54:45 +0000 2011 I mean, it's not deep, but everything between here and New York is covered. And at 10 degrees, it's not going anywhere soon. Thu Feb 03 12:56:43 +0000 2011 Whoa. Glad I brought my hat. RT @nancybaym: The current temperature outside is 13 below. That's -25 for you Celsius people. Thu Feb 03 12:57:47 +0000 2011 For an airport that was shut down yesterday, CLE is surprisingly non-mobbed. Thu Feb 03 13:15:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @urbanhumanist Again, not depth but breadth, all the way here... Thu Feb 03 13:17:29 +0000 2011 Okay, next stop, MCI. More from the frozen center of the country. Thu Feb 03 13:35:13 +0000 2011 Greetings from KC, MO, where it is Cold as Shit. \(That's the technical term, I believe\.\) Thu Feb 03 15:47:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @philnel Thanks! Nice to be here. Thu Feb 03 16:01:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat and @rays6 Nice! Would definitely come in handy. \(Plus allow me to go incognito when desired\!\) Thu Feb 03 17:53:36 +0000 2011 RT @HASTAC: Kathleen Fitzpatrick, "Future of Authorship" Feb 9, Duke lunchtalk, open to public http://j.mp/fQ3cDE Thu Feb 03 17:56:23 +0000 2011 Ahhhh. A good night's sleep, a cup of coffee, and my head is at last starting to clear... Fri Feb 04 14:19:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @blros and @nancybaym I need that time-management answer, too! I'll get to work on it. Fri Feb 04 14:20:58 +0000 2011 Fantastic morning of getting a little stuff done. Later on, a discussion of digital networked texts at the KU IDRH: http://idrh.ku.edu/events/fitzpatrick.shtml Fri Feb 04 15:51:18 +0000 2011 Fantastic day with the folks at the KU Institute for Digital Research in the Humanities! But boy, thinking so hard can make a person tired. Sat Feb 05 00:03:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @blros Thanks much! I'll look forward to future conversations. Sat Feb 05 03:57:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenServenti Alas, it is too late, as I'm flying out first thing tomorrow. But the window of my hotel overlooks the store! Sat Feb 05 04:03:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenServenti Absolutely! It's like the ur-American downtown. But with good restaurants and things. Sat Feb 05 04:09:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @blros and @jenserventi *That* is *awesome*. Sat Feb 05 04:10:14 +0000 2011 Up, dressed, packed, and now coffee has been achieved. MCI > CLE > LGA. With a presentation to polish and an article to revise on the way. Sat Feb 05 12:24:41 +0000 2011 Huge thanks to @nancybaym and @blros, as well as the folks at the Hall Center for the Humanities for a fantastic visit to KU! Thu Feb 24 14:06:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @boone In fact, now that I think about it, that's a very useful multipurpose t-shirt design template. Thu Feb 24 14:07:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @eetempleton On mine, it's Settings > General > Network > Enable/Disable 3G. Thu Feb 24 15:48:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention You should find out what the deal is from Verizon in terms of roaming charges abroad, but the answer is likely yes. Thu Feb 24 15:49:09 +0000 2011 Have been off the Twitters since around 2 yesterday afternoon. Missed the congratulate-@dancohen window by rather a lot. \(Congrats, Dan\!\) Fri Feb 25 14:53:56 +0000 2011 Well hi, twitters. Funny how far out of the loop I now feel if I spend a day offline. Sat Feb 26 16:29:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Hi back! Go make something! Sat Feb 26 16:36:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality It's always the way, man. I'll be forever out of the loop. They'll say, "where were you when..." and I'll say, "wait, what?" Sat Feb 26 17:03:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench and @matthewbaldwin Very nice! Though I think I'm a couple of those things that weren't going to get done. Story of my life. :) Sat Feb 26 17:36:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @mkirschenbaum That is a very good question. Sat Feb 26 20:26:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @matthewbaldwin So not only am I at the top of the list, but I've got staying power! That's definitely something. Sat Feb 26 20:49:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Sadly, I have. "I wrote a blog the other day..." Sun Feb 27 01:31:23 +0000 2011 @tcarmody Also, I am living 45 minutes in the past. Sun Feb 27 01:34:43 +0000 2011 Really? Whoa. "When used the right way in a job hunt, a personal Web site can help showcase your talents." http://nyti.ms/g5sU9X Sun Feb 27 02:21:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni There was snark. I confess. Sun Feb 27 04:03:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Excellent! Looking forward to it. Sun Feb 27 04:05:31 +0000 2011 Spent part of yesterday upgrading blogs to latest WP. Spending part of today upgrading wikis that broke due to some server update. Enough! Sun Feb 27 14:49:40 +0000 2011 After the upgrades, there will be writing! And then perhaps some preparations for this week's travel. Sun Feb 27 14:53:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave There's a little trickiness in the upgrade with CP: if you disable & reactivate the plugin, CP forgets your special pages. [+] Sun Feb 27 14:54:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave And you have to disable the WP3.1 admin bar, which covers the CP nav bar. But other than that, it works. [-] Sun Feb 27 14:55:18 +0000 2011 Wednesday, I'm giving a talk at the Illinois Program for Research in the Humanities: http://www.lis.illinois.edu/events/2011/03/02/future-authorship-scholarly-writing-digital-age Sun Feb 27 14:57:08 +0000 2011 Replying to @unsworth I'm very much looking forward to it, too! Sun Feb 27 14:57:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @shermandorn I've read about half; the jury's out. My sense is that she's right about the problems in peerreview but can't see a way around. Sun Feb 27 15:03:42 +0000 2011 Sometimes when the update script tells you "this may take a long time," it's really not kidding. Sun Feb 27 16:05:11 +0000 2011 Okay, I caved: this is another major televised event about which I Do Not Care. And yet I've turned on my tv for the first time in months. Mon Feb 28 01:12:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender I'm absolutely following #wiunion! Part of the schizophrenia of the tv/internet experience. Mon Feb 28 01:22:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Multitask? I am eating dinner, editing a document, following #wiunion, and watching tv. I win. Mon Feb 28 01:27:40 +0000 2011 RT @audreywatters: F BOMB FTW Mon Feb 28 02:01:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Best supporting actress. I have absolutely loved her since Homicide. Mon Feb 28 02:01:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni Very good taste. Mon Feb 28 02:03:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @roxieblog and @jcmeloni Indeed. I showed a TV class the first two seasons last year, and was amazed how well it's held up. \(They loved it\.\) Mon Feb 28 02:05:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @jcmeloni and @roxieblog La la la la la, I didn't hear that. Mon Feb 28 02:12:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention You are apologizing to me because...? I'm fond of some of Sorkin's stuff, but I don't think it was the best of those scripts. Mon Feb 28 02:16:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Yes, true! But \(a few isolated episodes of West Wing aside\) none of them by Sorkin. Mon Feb 28 02:22:29 +0000 2011 I have briefly achieved inbox zero. Of course, I've still got starred messages \(i\.e\., stuff in progress\) four. But hey! I'll take it! Mon Feb 28 02:51:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley Heh. Mon Feb 28 02:54:51 +0000 2011 RT @CapitolClio: Anne is going to be upset when she learns that hosting is a group project and she's being graded on her partner Mon Feb 28 04:18:17 +0000 2011 I begin to wonder if we'll find out tomorrow that James Franco was actually played by Joaquin Phoenix this evening. Mon Feb 28 05:00:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @mattbucher and @amandafrench Sounds like a plan! Mon Feb 28 19:08:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @samplereality LOL, most literally. Mon Feb 28 21:29:25 +0000 2011 RT @wynkenhimself: RT @FolgerResearch: 18 comments so far on Shakespeare Quarterly's open review. Are you part of the conversation? http ... Mon Feb 28 21:39:50 +0000 2011
docs.title: 2011 02 01 tweets

docs.text: Some weeks back somebody wrote a great post about returning to blogging from Twitter as a means of creating a more permanent archive [+] Tue Mar 01 13:06:47 +0000 2011 but now I can't track that post down. \(Irony? Perhaps\.\) Anyhow, if you remember that post, would you let me know? Thanks! [-] Tue Mar 01 13:07:17 +0000 2011 New post: The Disappearing Month http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/the-disappearing-month/ Tue Mar 01 13:23:03 +0000 2011 LGA > ORD > CMI. Tomorrow, speaking at IPRH/I3, and hanging out a bit with @unsworth! Tue Mar 01 20:42:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Sounds good. Will look forward to hearing more. Tue Mar 01 23:59:04 +0000 2011 Um, HELL no? // RT @wpmuorg: WPMU News: Should we move to Facebook Comments? http://wpmu.org/should-we-move-to-facebook-comments/ Wed Mar 02 02:28:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @boone Anytime. Or anytime I can loudly oppose a stupid idea. Wed Mar 02 02:38:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj Why thank you! Wed Mar 02 12:32:30 +0000 2011 RT @HASTACscholars: Awesome interview by H-Scholar Lisa Klarr with @kfitz on authorship, hidden labor, the university and so much more: ... Wed Mar 02 13:33:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Skype and Google Voice both allow you to dial landlines for cheap, but you need to pay $ into your account first... Wed Mar 02 17:43:25 +0000 2011 About to begin my talk at Illinois's IPRH/I3! Wed Mar 02 22:02:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @CCdiglib Yes, definitely. I think there'll be audio, and I can send slides w/notes. There's also video from an earlier version at Duke. Thu Mar 03 04:08:43 +0000 2011 CMI > ORD > LGA. Thanks so much to Dianne Harris, Chris Catanzarite, @unsworth, and everyone else for a great visit to IPRH/I3! Thu Mar 03 13:55:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Thanks so much - will look forward to talking again soon! Thu Mar 03 14:46:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @sshreeves Fantastic meeting you as well - thanks so much for coming! Thu Mar 03 14:47:12 +0000 2011 There are several folks out there with my name who think that one of my email addresses is theirs. One just sent me a test message from [+] Thu Mar 03 15:10:01 +0000 2011 another address. I hope she'll be amused when she gets a response. [-] Thu Mar 03 15:10:27 +0000 2011 Free wifi, empty middle seat. Win! \(But dude on the other side of the middle seat passive\-aggressively taking up most of the space between\.\) Thu Mar 03 16:39:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @bnreviewer Perhaps. But also has a banker look about him, and thus no doubt believes that it's his for the taking. Thu Mar 03 17:16:41 +0000 2011 Flying time, LGA to ORD: just shy of 3 hours. Flying time, ORD to LGA: 1 hour 50 minutes. That's some tailwind! Thu Mar 03 17:51:43 +0000 2011 Worst. Cab ride. Ever. All I could do not to puke in the back seat. Not to mention that he had no clue how to get to the west village. Thu Mar 03 18:50:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Only got away with the medallion number, not his license. But have complained nonetheless. Blech. Thu Mar 03 19:15:07 +0000 2011 Now there's a name I never thought I'd hear again, especially in reference to contemporary politics: Buddy Roemer. http://motherjones.com/mojo/2011/03/who-buddy-roemer-republican-presidential-candidate Thu Mar 03 22:49:08 +0000 2011 And by the by, that whole new-age-midlife-crisis thing? 100% true. But I didn't know about the medicinal marijuana! Thu Mar 03 22:52:03 +0000 2011 RT @audreywatters: How Recent Changes to Twitter's Terms of Service Might Hurt Academic Research http://rww.to/eIahi3 // Not cool. Fri Mar 04 02:23:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @tcarmody I'm in there now too. Twice. Probably should not have left the second comment but could not stop myself. Fri Mar 04 03:13:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @wynkenhimself The purpose of fences is having a lawn to defend. You'll be sorry when you have nothing to shoo those kids off of. Fri Mar 04 03:52:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @wynkenhimself Sounds about right. I've got a 3'x3' balcony facing the street in CA, and, well, in NY I have the street. Fri Mar 04 04:01:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention No, sadly. Super irritating. I may have to switch apps if they don't provide a disable mechanism. Sat Mar 05 02:42:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave and @mlaconvention Even worse: it's the latest "upgrade" of the official Twitter app. And it's AWFUL. Sat Mar 05 02:45:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @cscannella, @academicdave and @mlaconvention I liked echofon before the last Twitter update; will probably go back to that. Sat Mar 05 03:02:08 +0000 2011 Replying to @MissLadyB Hey, congratulations! A really exciting step! Sat Mar 05 13:47:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @nirak It's been a couple of years since I tried that, as the results last time were demoralizing. But now I'm on the first page twice! Sat Mar 05 13:56:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie, @samplereality and @nirak Just figured that out myself, but thought I'd let it slide. Sigh. Sat Mar 05 14:16:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @cscannella Other things I'm remembering I liked about Echofon: syncing read tweets across platforms; tracking convs both back & forward. Sat Mar 05 16:46:06 +0000 2011 Just splurged on a package of incredibly overpriced raw vegan kale chips. They are insanely good. This may be a problem. Sat Mar 05 21:25:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @rebeccaonion Nice! These are very fancy \(w/ nuts, seeds, spices, nutritional yeast, etc\) , but I can't figure out how they qualify as raw. Sat Mar 05 21:35:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @rebeccaonion They're delish \(if even more pricy here\) . Once I have an oven again, I'm definitely going to make the epicurious ones! Sat Mar 05 21:39:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde Yeah, I am currently ovenless. But not for long! Sat Mar 05 22:13:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah A fancy schmancy New York State something or other brand. Anyhow, making them's super easy. If one has an oven. Which I do not. Sat Mar 05 22:17:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings So *everyone* has told me! It's easy if you have an oven, which I currently do not. :( Sat Mar 05 23:07:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings I'm prohibited from getting one by my OCD landlord. But in a few months, I'll be ovenfull again! Until then, I can splurge. :) Sun Mar 06 00:04:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde Excellent! If you try the dehydrator route at some point, let me know how it goes... Sun Mar 06 00:04:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @bighandsome Hey, congrats, first of all! I've got a fair recipe \(thanks, epicurious\) but alas no oven. Will have to wait for summer. Sun Mar 06 00:21:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde Hilarious. I've now said this 6 times, but to ppl who don't follow each other. I'm sadly ovenless this year. This summer, though... Sun Mar 06 00:41:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde The other thing I miss? Not having a place to put a head of kale that big. Sun Mar 06 00:42:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality By that standard, my iPad is moving toward success at a precipitous rate. Sun Mar 06 15:22:49 +0000 2011 This has not been the most productive weekend in the history of weekends. Here's hoping for a more focused few days before SXSW... Sun Mar 06 23:34:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen Hmmm. Perhaps we can find a way. You're going to SCMS, tho, right? Sun Mar 06 23:36:29 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen No; I was going to do both, but it got to be too much $ and travel-wise. The workshop I was on at SCMS will be fine without me. Sun Mar 06 23:37:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen But let me know when you're back in Austin! And... my understanding is that I could get *my* partner into my panel, IYKWIM. Sun Mar 06 23:39:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen Yeah, my panel's on Sunday, which would be hard. Sun Mar 06 23:39:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen Well that's annoying. If there's anything I can do to get him in, I'd be happy to... Sun Mar 06 23:43:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen I get in lateish Thursday, and leave on the afternoon of the 16th \(Weds I think\) . Sun Mar 06 23:44:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @mirk79 I'll let you know! Sun Mar 06 23:50:17 +0000 2011 Home from lovely dinner with @nycdlq. Of course we'd finally manage to get together in a monsoon. Mon Mar 07 03:23:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention And filled with enormous puddles instead. And sideways-blowing rain. Mon Mar 07 03:33:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @alicetiara and @annehelen Hey, excellent! I'm there that entire time; let me know what works for you! Mon Mar 07 03:45:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmaxsfu I'm using BusyCal, which is way more Google Calendar fluent. If you find another good option, though, let me know! Mon Mar 07 12:07:43 +0000 2011 Hoping to write a bit about the AAUP's report on Sustaining Scholarly Publishing a bit later today: http://bit.ly/f0g5CK Mon Mar 07 15:24:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat As will I. I want to draw a breath and be sure I'm reading as carefully as possible before I jump in, though. Mon Mar 07 15:27:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj Always a problem, that ambiguous acronym. And indicative of something, I'm sure, though I can't quite put my finger on what. Mon Mar 07 15:28:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench Woo-hoo! Mon Mar 07 15:29:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Actually, that's not entirely true; the org has been arguing for such change for years. It's the members that haven't picked it up. Mon Mar 07 17:04:04 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Leaning on accreditors isn't what needs to happen. It's leaning on senior faculty, esp. on T&P committees, to take a stand. Mon Mar 07 17:07:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat There's a lot of work to be done there. And it's going WAY too slowly. But it is coming, honestly. Mon Mar 07 17:07:25 +0000 2011 RT @kylebeachy: Extremely pleased to be joining the full time faculty at Roosevelt University. // Woo-hoo! \(And congrats\!\) Mon Mar 07 18:01:27 +0000 2011 RT @jmittell: You shd read @weinmanj's take on watching TV from the beginning, but start at the midpt of article: http://bit.ly/hV4Sxu //Ha. Mon Mar 07 18:09:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell What part of "time-based media" has gotten obscured here? Mon Mar 07 18:09:44 +0000 2011 Am on day 3 of Plan "Eliminate Pointless Scary Chemicals" \(i\.e\., diet Coke\) . Resolve is definitely slipping right now. More green tea, stat! Mon Mar 07 18:52:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @moncia and @shanakimball Precisely! We've got to find a way to free presses from that bottom-line orientation for them to be able to innovate. Tue Mar 08 00:45:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @msbonn, @moncia and @shanakimball There's certainly always a bottom line in the sense of a budget that must be adhered to, but my hope is that + Tue Mar 08 02:00:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @msbonn, @moncia and @shanakimball the MPubs model might be the beginning of thinking about that budget differently, as part of a larger + Tue Mar 08 02:01:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @msbonn, @moncia and @shanakimball scholarly infrastructure. Some of it might bring in $, but cost recovery can't continue to be as focal. Tue Mar 08 02:02:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF Actually, the first US UPs were founded to publish the work of their own faculties, which was given away to other institutions. + Tue Mar 08 02:42:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF UPs later professionalized, and detached from their institutions to greater & lesser extents. So much of what I at least have been + Tue Mar 08 02:43:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF arguing for is a return to an earlier relationship between institution & press. - Tue Mar 08 02:43:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF There must be following! :) Tue Mar 08 02:50:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @foundhistory Me as well. I'm still getting the monthly thank you notes, which are super sweet, but seem wasteful! Tue Mar 08 16:33:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I would want to know what percentage of those "personnel" costs are administrative versus faculty. #11ACE Tue Mar 08 16:34:19 +0000 2011 My Gmail formatting has gone wonky on my laptop; in message view, each label now lives on its own line beneath the subject line. + Tue Mar 08 16:40:46 +0000 2011 This does not occur on my desktop, and no amount of disabling Chrome extensions has made a difference. Tue Mar 08 16:41:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall I don't think so; there seems to be plenty of room. http://plixi.com/p/82539387 Tue Mar 08 16:44:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall Is all I'm saying. Tue Mar 08 16:44:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall It is nice, in fact, to have someone else agree that it's just wrong. Tue Mar 08 16:45:50 +0000 2011 I helped do something pretty cool today. I'll look forward to showing it to all of you tomorrow. Tue Mar 08 23:11:23 +0000 2011 Alan Simpson utterly reasonable tone in saying "Snoopy Snoopy Poop Dogg" may be the funniest thing I've ever heard. http://politi.co/g4rFFT Wed Mar 09 01:17:53 +0000 2011 I start giggling again every time I think of it. I'm really, really glad there's no one in the apartment to whom I have to explain why. Wed Mar 09 01:18:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Are we about to find ourselves in a new version of the east-coast/west-coast wars? Because I don't think you mess with Alan. Wed Mar 09 01:48:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Dude, he was street before there were streets. Wed Mar 09 01:51:42 +0000 2011 For some reason, having missed Mardi Gras is hitting me harder today than it has in years. Maybe it's having time to realize what day it is. Wed Mar 09 02:10:11 +0000 2011 In any case, I think it's time to celebrate with a beverage. At least something I can atone for tomorrow. Wed Mar 09 02:10:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell Maybe that's it! I'm sorry not to be joining you all there. But I'll raise a breakfast taco to the east. Wed Mar 09 02:16:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention God, what an irritating article. Wed Mar 09 03:01:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Not least the claim that freaking Franzen is writing another essay about him. Wed Mar 09 03:02:08 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I was on that #mla09 panel. And am in the Iowa collection & on the SXSW panel mentioned. Apparently I'm part of the problem. Wed Mar 09 03:06:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah You didn't say it, sister. Wed Mar 09 03:07:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention There definitely seems a fair bit of ressentiment there... Wed Mar 09 03:12:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj, @mirk79 and @mlaconvention I'm archiving this exchange in anticipation of the 2vol history I'll write on the founding of cashing-in studies Wed Mar 09 03:31:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @mlaconvention Absolutely. @mattbucher was badly de-contextualized, as you can see from his feed. Wed Mar 09 03:33:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @mlaconvention Happily, @mattbucher will be on the panel with me at SXSW, so he can cash in on his irritation. Wed Mar 09 03:33:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention, @tcarmody and @mattbucher I think there's room for an article about whether cashing in is dead, though. Wed Mar 09 04:06:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody, @mlaconvention and @mattbucher You kids and your "location-based media" and your Snoopy Snoopy Poop Dogg. Wed Mar 09 04:14:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody, @mlaconvention and @mattbucher You need to write a thank-you note after you cash in. Wed Mar 09 04:15:18 +0000 2011 In which I discover the depth of my feeling about my fleece jacket: More Than Mere Polyester Would Suggest. http://bit.ly/hZiHQS Wed Mar 09 14:37:11 +0000 2011 MediaCommons is hosting an open discussion of the AAUP report, Sustaining Scholarly Publishing. Come join us! http://bit.ly/gPWGMo Wed Mar 09 15:00:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @mkgold Thanks for helping spread the word! Wed Mar 09 15:05:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen We'd love to have you dig in further there! Wed Mar 09 15:25:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself I knew I was running that risk when I posted, but I wanted to get it out of the way before starting to announce the report! Wed Mar 09 15:34:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball and @wynkenhimself It's a seriously awesome jacket. Wed Mar 09 15:35:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen Thanks! Wed Mar 09 16:10:11 +0000 2011 I believe this is day 5 of the new no-diet-Coke regime. And my head hates me today. May have to go out and get a good coffee to compensate. Wed Mar 09 17:59:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @moncia Ooh, good to know! I'm working from home today, though, so it'll likely be a Clover from the Starbucks on the corner. Wed Mar 09 18:24:43 +0000 2011 My answer on Quora to: Will scholarly communication go through a die-off? http://qr.ae/9Ids Wed Mar 09 19:03:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Thanks! Wed Mar 09 19:42:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton So glad to hear that. Did you replace the soda with something in particular? Was it a full caffeine elimination, or just soda? Wed Mar 09 19:45:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton That's pretty much why I'm trying to cut it out, too. Plus there's a specific headache I associate with diet Coke - both + Wed Mar 09 19:51:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton a craving headache and an overdose headache. Exactly the same. Which can't be good. So tons of green tea & a little coffee. - Wed Mar 09 19:52:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton That's really good to hear. I'm determined to make this stick. Wed Mar 09 19:55:23 +0000 2011 A watched post office box never fills up with the checks you're expecting. Wed Mar 09 20:15:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @lnakamur The problem for me is quantity; when I drink it at all, I drink way too much & it gives me wicked headaches, general feeling bad. Wed Mar 09 20:30:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @lnakamur Also recent study suggests link between diet soda consumption & heart issues. Correlation not causality, of course, but still. Wed Mar 09 20:31:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench and @captain_primate That post seems to have been removed from the AAA blog. Wed Mar 09 21:02:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench No. Way. These are the folks behind the "50 best academic blogs!" lists that are nothing more than link farms. Wed Mar 09 21:09:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench How on earth... surely they didn't... I'm just gobsmacked. Wed Mar 09 21:12:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate and @amandafrench Particularly those that build the most innovative scholarly portal in conjunction with a uni press... + Wed Mar 09 21:16:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate and @amandafrench And then sell it to a most problematic corporate publisher. Which triples institutional subscription costs! - Wed Mar 09 21:19:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @KelliMarshall Very likely, yes. :( Wed Mar 09 21:38:56 +0000 2011 Thanks to everybody starting the discussion of the AAUP report, "Sustaining Scholarly Publishing," at @MediaCommons. http://bit.ly/hRqMRi + Wed Mar 09 21:46:35 +0000 2011 Come join us, and help set for future innovation at university presses and scholarly societies! http://bit.ly/hRqMRi - Wed Mar 09 21:49:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @KelliMarshall If I could keep my diet Coke consumption anything like moderate, I'd worry less. But that seems not to be in the cards. Wed Mar 09 21:51:28 +0000 2011 \(That was set \*the agenda\*, obvs\. Sigh\.\) Wed Mar 09 21:52:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton It was exactly that thought that made me decide to go cold turkey. The craving is miserable, and I'm clearly better off w/o it. Wed Mar 09 21:53:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton Cannot. Wait. Wed Mar 09 21:55:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen Yes: double-bubble icon is all comments by page; little people icon is all comments by commenter. Wed Mar 09 22:28:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen I'm feeling a bit bad, too. That the task force was willing to do this is something; I hope more AAUP folks will enter the disc. Wed Mar 09 22:31:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward and @gmbritton emailed the AAUP lists this morning, yes. I'm hoping they jump in this evening! Wed Mar 09 22:32:27 +0000 2011 Okay, Echofon has a ton of gaps in its timeline today. What gives? Thu Mar 10 03:52:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley, @RepoRat and @dancohen Oh, why even bother? As soon as we get the peas archived, they're going to start going bad. Legume rot. Thu Mar 10 13:40:06 +0000 2011 Ostensibly flying out at 3pm for #SXSW, right in the midst of another monsoon. Weather, do not interfere with my quest for breakfast tacos. Mon Mar 28 15:15:07 +0000 2011 Text message 11:12 am EDT: The emergency is over! It's safe to go back to work! More information soon! Mon Mar 28 15:15:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave Claremont University Consortium. Mon Mar 28 15:16:23 +0000 2011 You know, here I am in New York, and it completely freaked me out. I cannot imagine what that minute would have been like on campus. Mon Mar 28 15:21:20 +0000 2011 I await reports of at least one head, rolling. Mon Mar 28 15:21:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman Not a freaking clue. Last text said info would be posted on CUC site. Nothing so far. Mon Mar 28 15:22:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @grammarsnark Nice. I did get both the texts and the email, and I'm sure my office phone will save the voice mail for me. Mon Mar 28 15:23:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave Students, sure, but offices open at 8am, so the campus is populated. Mon Mar 28 15:23:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @sunsiare Right! You're off campus, but not south of it, and not a mile away! What to do? Mon Mar 28 15:25:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @grammarsnark No kidding. Or Alexander Hall \(PO's admin bldg\) . Mon Mar 28 15:27:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @dr_morel Yes, we're taking advantage of the current crisis to practice for our crisis management drills. Mon Mar 28 15:29:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @swarthmoreburke Indeed. At least without a really big "HEY WE'RE JUST TESTING HERE." Mon Mar 28 15:30:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @sunsiare Oopsie. Mon Mar 28 15:34:04 +0000 2011 Oopsie. // RT @sunsiare: @kfitz: "we inadvertently sent an evacuation notice". Hum... :( Mon Mar 28 15:35:39 +0000 2011 Via email: "This morning during one of our routine emergency exercises we inadvertently sent an evacuation notice..." + Mon Mar 28 15:37:38 +0000 2011 "Please note that there was no emergency and we apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused." Mon Mar 28 15:38:08 +0000 2011 Because *inconvenience* is I'm sure the most appropriate descriptor for what happened on campus during that minute. Mon Mar 28 15:39:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @jdrouin Oh, good grief. Mon Mar 28 15:41:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman No kidding. I eagerly await a euphemistic message telling us that someone at CUC is leaving to seek new opportunities. Mon Mar 28 15:45:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself Yes, she should have, tho @mmwwah lives in the safe zone & so likely just had the same WTF response I did. Mon Mar 28 15:49:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell God, I love that scene. Mon Mar 28 15:59:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @mkirschenbaum What is the difference, after all, between an alarm and a false alarm, but one word? Mon Mar 28 16:00:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah I have been! But was hoping you might be having a lingering morning at home. Ah, well. Mon Mar 28 16:06:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen Yay, congratulations! Mon Mar 28 22:40:04 +0000 2011 How much did I need to accomplish today? 10! How much did I accomplish today? 2. Shoot. Tue Mar 29 00:05:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @amitorit I wish I could. Tue Mar 29 00:14:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @patrick_mj Heh. It's bad, but it's not that bad. Merely 10 exclamation. Tue Mar 29 00:26:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen Hey, excellent! One of my favorite students ever was a Putney graduate, and had fabulous things to say about it. Congrats 2x! Tue Mar 29 00:28:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @profwernimont I can only imagine! It threw my morning off, and I'm on the other coast! Tue Mar 29 01:42:17 +0000 2011 Am playing a bit of hooky today in order to polish up tomorrow night's talk at @cunydhi \(6\.30pm, Grad Center rm 6417\) . http://cunydhi.commons.gc.cuny.edu/2011/03/25/march-30-kathleen-fitzpatrick-on-%E2%80%9Cpeer-review-open-scholarship-and-the-digital-humanities%E2%80%9D/ Tue Mar 29 15:30:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @boone and @rebagorges Hey, congratulations! Tue Mar 29 16:15:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat I am curious to meet some of these "humanists" of which Gioia speaks! #CIChum #sigh Tue Mar 29 18:37:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat I am clearly hanging out with the wrong crowd. From one perspective, at least. #CIChum Tue Mar 29 18:39:13 +0000 2011 Good grief that's depressing. // RT @mlaconvention: Gioia: turns @fraistat q on the future into resistence to technology. Wow. #CIChum Tue Mar 29 19:00:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat Phew. #CIChum Tue Mar 29 19:24:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat Excellent! #CIChum Tue Mar 29 19:38:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention You can mute particular hashtags in some twitter clients, but not on Twitter per se. Tue Mar 29 20:16:48 +0000 2011 Note to self: "I'll just merge those two pieces of writing into one talk" should not have a "just" in it. Tue Mar 29 20:17:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah I've been bouncing back and forth between Twitter & Echofon. Neither is exactly right. Tue Mar 29 20:19:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @joshhonn Hey, thanks! \(I'm a little excited about it, too\.\) Tue Mar 29 20:55:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @themba Hey, me too! Tue Mar 29 21:10:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @themba Really? I'm... surprised to hear that! My memory must be failing me. Tue Mar 29 21:16:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @themba Perhaps it was a conflict with tomorrow's talk at CUNY. I'll have to look back in my email and see! Tue Mar 29 21:17:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings I'm sorry to hear about Stanford, but RIT honors will be great! Wed Mar 30 00:15:17 +0000 2011 New post: The Never-Appeared http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/the-never-appeared/ Wed Mar 30 14:50:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender and @captain_primate I've done it, too, and also pick up my edu email through Gmail so it's all in one place. Wed Mar 30 15:12:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @fraistat That's hilarious. I wonder if his assistant carries his books to class. Wed Mar 30 15:35:35 +0000 2011 "I'm tired of this shit." A hilariously rude and dead-on grammar lesson. http://learnyourdamnhomophones.com Wed Mar 30 16:22:36 +0000 2011 The talk I'm giving tonight now draws upon two prior talks and three pieces of writing. Let's be kind and call it a pastiche. Wed Mar 30 16:38:40 +0000 2011 \(Srsly, how do I get myself into a spot like this? More importantly, how do I get myself out? Must find way to end talk soon\.\.\.\) Wed Mar 30 16:39:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @veek The problem is less the newness \(or not\) of the material than creating something coherent out of so many pieces... Wed Mar 30 16:40:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @mbtimney Congratulations! Fantastic news! Wed Mar 30 16:46:49 +0000 2011 Getting that unsettling feeling that I took a wrong turn about 10 slides ago, and need to back up and start again. Wed Mar 30 17:25:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde That's part of the problem: it's part overview of the larger issue, and part attempting to do some specific work with an example. Wed Mar 30 18:17:20 +0000 2011 Well, it's a talk. Fortunately, I have a couple of hours left in which to polish up some rough edges. Wed Mar 30 19:18:21 +0000 2011 Just as I finish the last touches on the slides for tonight, my copy of The Pale King arrives. Must. Not. Read. Now. Wed Mar 30 19:57:37 +0000 2011 MT @mkgold: Tonight at 6:30: @kfitz at #cunydhi on “Peer Review, Open Scholarship, & the Digital Humanities” http://bit.ly/fL6TS3 Wed Mar 30 20:58:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @subsublibrary Yes, absolutely - lots to catch up on. Let me know when works for you! Wed Mar 30 22:01:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @redwards7 It is, in fact, though I understand theres a little backlog & it may be a while before it's up. Wed Mar 30 22:28:49 +0000 2011 Will be starting my talk at #cunydhi shortly! Wed Mar 30 22:36:57 +0000 2011 \(Will not be livetweeting it\.\) #cunydhi Wed Mar 30 22:37:33 +0000 2011 Fabulous time at #cunydhi tonight! Thanks so much to @mkgold for inviting me. \(Also for the cookies\.\) Thu Mar 31 01:47:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @wynkenhimself Yup. "The papers will all be really good. And they'll work together brilliantly. Take my word for it." Thu Mar 31 02:06:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @ncecire, @mlaconvention and @sselisker Love it. I would go to that panel. Thu Mar 31 02:17:29 +0000 2011 Headed to Louisiana in the morning, where it's supposed to be sunny and mid-80s all weekend. It'll be 37 & raining here when I leave. Thu Mar 31 02:42:33 +0000 2011 On the one hand, I'm ecstatic. On the other, I have no idea how to pack. Thu Mar 31 02:42:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Well, sure. But I don't think the layers go down far enough for that! Thu Mar 31 02:47:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Actually, I'm appalled to say that I probably do need to bring one. Going to my parents' camp on the river. Thu Mar 31 02:51:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandalicastro and @mkgold It was fabulous getting to talk with you tonight; I'll look forward to another opportunity! Thu Mar 31 02:59:45 +0000 2011 LGA > IAH > BTR. Ready for some sunny & 80 degrees. Thu Mar 31 11:03:12 +0000 2011 Showing Twitter to my mom. Fri Apr 01 01:58:29 +0000 2011
docs.title: 2011 03 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @jcmeloni Thanks much, very belatedly! Fri Apr 01 04:43:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @philnel Most awesome; thanks! Fri Apr 01 04:44:14 +0000 2011 Thanks for all the hellos to my mom. She was suitably impressed \(but wants to know how I know each of you and why you would want to say hi\) . Fri Apr 01 04:46:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention That's hilarious. "I know them from conferences" got eyebrows raised only slightly less than "I know them from the Internet." Fri Apr 01 13:06:02 +0000 2011 RT @FolgerResearch: 164 comments, 25 reviewers, 1 week left to join Shakespeare Quarterly's open review. Great performance scholarship t ... Sat Apr 02 19:14:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar Bought the hardback, partly as collectible object. But will likely read it in ebook form. Sun Apr 03 03:32:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar No, I didn't hear. Glad to know about Adam's review! Sun Apr 03 04:50:33 +0000 2011 Fabulous trip to Louisiana, but very happy to be home. Have declared Twitter bankruptcy; if there's anything I need to know about, tell me! Mon Apr 04 21:55:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde There's a great blog post out there somewhere on declaring email bankruptcy. Lifehacker, maybe? Mon Apr 04 21:59:10 +0000 2011 Dear @AnthemBlueCross: your website user registration process is completely borked. Help, please. Wed Apr 06 20:26:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender The third time through, it finally worked, but not without making me re-enter data again just for fun. Wed Apr 06 20:42:17 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: MLA Executive Council Deplores Open-Records Requests That Infringe Academic Freedom: http://www.mla.org/ec_records @A ... Wed Apr 06 21:28:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @foodblogga Ha. @kfitz has the most -ette of kitchenettes right now & would find it too painful to contemplate real cooking. Wed Apr 06 21:50:57 +0000 2011 RT @nowviskie: 2 of 33 speakers at the \(very cool\!\) Digging into Data event are women. Casting no aspersions on NEH here! but I wonder: ... Wed Apr 06 22:01:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie I was wondering the very same thing. Wed Apr 06 22:02:23 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: The #MLA condemns the politicized intrusion on faculty and staff members: http://www.mla.org/ec_records Wed Apr 06 22:04:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie and @reporat Sad but true. Wed Apr 06 22:06:42 +0000 2011 Two big things and a couple of small things crossed off the to-do list today. Two more deadlines ahead. Wed Apr 06 22:38:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar and @mlaconvention The learning curve is utterly horizontal. You have an ayfone; you already know how the bigger one works. Wed Apr 06 22:42:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar Yeah, typing takes a little getting used to. And I find the autocorrect factor annoying. But it comes pretty quickly. Wed Apr 06 22:44:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar Back atcha! PS: I find typing in landscape mode easier than horizontal. Wed Apr 06 22:46:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave My $ is on that yutz who said "it would be silly to claim that new means of comm can affect a protest" a month ago. Gladwell? Thu Apr 07 00:15:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @academicdave Rats. It would have been awesome. Thu Apr 07 00:17:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar and @eetempleton I'm not disappointed in the least. Maybe in part because I accidentally wound up with a 3G iPad this weekend. Thu Apr 07 01:01:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton and @kdettmar You can activate & deactivate month-to-month. Thu Apr 07 01:05:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton Yes, I did. I maxed out the first one, too; I like traveling with a lot of video. 32GB wd probably be plenty, but just in case. Thu Apr 07 01:09:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality More or less, yeah. The internet is exciting! Thu Apr 07 01:35:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Yup! Thu Apr 07 14:01:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Wow! No, I'm covered, ha ha. I'm sure somebody out there could use one. Thu Apr 07 14:04:29 +0000 2011 I am now at the point of lack of motivation at which I am resorting to frank bribery. If I go to the gym, I can have a fancy coffee after. Thu Apr 07 14:11:03 +0000 2011 It's pathetic, but I did make it to the gym today. \(And this coffee is really good\.\) Thu Apr 07 14:11:17 +0000 2011 Attempted a return to Firefox in order to have full Zotero support. Cannot take it, not one more minute. Chrome, I'll never leave you again. Thu Apr 07 14:43:01 +0000 2011 \(At least not until you do something that really annoys me and I abandon you for Opera\. So don't get too comfortable\.\) Thu Apr 07 14:43:29 +0000 2011 In case you're interested, what's killing me with FF? That you can't ⌘➔ to get to the end of a line in Gmail. Cannot take it. Thu Apr 07 14:50:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @savasavasava You are so right. Thu Apr 07 14:51:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall I just can't make the adjustment. And that ⌘➔ works everywhere else in FF except Gmail makes it worse. Thu Apr 07 14:52:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @ricksva Yes, FF4. And it's a collection of small things, but the no ⌘➔ to the end of the line in Gmail is the dealbreaker, weirdly enough. Thu Apr 07 14:53:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall What I really want is a browser that's smart enough to absorb the stuff I like from other browsers without getting kludgy. Thu Apr 07 14:54:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @jvinopal Hmmm. I haven't been having the Zotero/PythonExt problem. But I'm running Python 2.6; wonder if that makes a difference? Thu Apr 07 14:56:36 +0000 2011 Congrats, @mkirschenbaum and @johnmjones - a good day for good news! Thu Apr 07 15:00:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde Control-e? I'll have to check that out. But I'm not sure it'll be able to overcome my ⌘➔ habit. Thu Apr 07 15:05:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @joshhonn Wait, really? I'm... boggled. I'd ask how they did that, but I'm not sure I want to know. Does it appear to come from me? Thu Apr 07 15:07:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @joshhonn Yeah, I'd love to see that! kfitz47 at gmail. Thu Apr 07 15:11:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde Oy. I was never an emacs user; I'd have to work slowly on building this fluency... Thu Apr 07 15:14:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde This is true. Thu Apr 07 15:16:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @joshhonn Wow. Just amazing. Thu Apr 07 15:16:33 +0000 2011 .@joshhonn and I are imagining #spamcamp, a means by which we harness the programming ingenuity of spammers for productive use. Thu Apr 07 15:39:51 +0000 2011 This got started when a tweet of mine showed up within minutes as the text of a spam comment on his blog. Imagine that put to work for good! Thu Apr 07 15:40:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention You. Go. Thu Apr 07 15:45:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @jamesjbrownjr Ooh, excellent! I'll look forward to hearing/reading more! Thu Apr 07 15:49:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Excellent. Let me know. Thu Apr 07 15:54:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @subsublibrary Hey, excellent - I'll see you there! Thu Apr 07 16:18:42 +0000 2011 Finished a pass thru the copyedits on my book. Fun but exhausting. Hope to do one more read \(with view changes off\) before handing it back. Thu Apr 07 18:59:06 +0000 2011 Oh! And! I just got the catalog copy from the press, which is great. With two most awesome blurbs, which I look forward to sharing soon. Thu Apr 07 19:13:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention So sorry to hear you're not feeling well! @dancohen and I will soldier on without you. Thu Apr 07 21:18:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Ugh. Do take care of yourself! Thu Apr 07 21:25:10 +0000 2011 At the NYU Humanities Initiative to hear @dancohen & Alexander Provan on digital scholarly communications. Thu Apr 07 22:02:36 +0000 2011 .@dancohen beginning "Scholarly Communication the Web Way" by discussing Paleofuture. Thu Apr 07 22:09:25 +0000 2011 .@dancohen interested in tracking folks who are unsatisfied with conventional ways of doing scholarly work, and how they're working around. Thu Apr 07 22:12:08 +0000 2011 Layer tennis, digital journals as examples of work that can't be done in print. Desire for direct communication, interactivity. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:13:36 +0000 2011 What might we do if we had this kind of DIY work take place in the academy? What would academic layer tennis look like? @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:14:49 +0000 2011 Part of problem: AAUP survey a year ago about digital publishing strategies stuck in conventional forms. Online work as "companion" sites. Thu Apr 07 22:17:04 +0000 2011 Need to think about new, unconventional forms as actual scholarly work, not companions to "real" work. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:17:56 +0000 2011 Examples of scholarly blogs driving conversation within a field. Shout out to @MediaCommons and The New Everyday. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:18:54 +0000 2011 First question that gets raised: what about review? It's the wild west! @dancohen says this is a misunderstanding of how review works online Thu Apr 07 22:19:48 +0000 2011 Online review is "recursive review" - cycles of commenting that are often more critical than traditional blind peer review. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:20:36 +0000 2011 When you're out there on the open web, different but equally critical form of reviewing takes place recursively. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:22:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings I was actually asked to tweet. You may want to unfollow or mute for the next hour. Sorry about that! :) Thu Apr 07 22:23:25 +0000 2011 Aggregation and curation as key aspect of sorting out most important material for a community at any moment. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:25:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings I'm sorry to overwhelm your stream! I'll be as judicious as possible. Thu Apr 07 22:26:25 +0000 2011 Examples of blog aggregation and curation: PLoS Blogs, The Browser, Digital Humanities Now. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:27:49 +0000 2011 Thoughts don't come in either 8K or 100K words; need to experiment with the full range of sizes of scholarship. See Tyler Cowen. @dancohen Thu Apr 07 22:30:20 +0000 2011 .@dancohen showing CIBER UCL survey of importance attached to modes of scholarly communication; users of social media have radically + Thu Apr 07 22:33:31 +0000 2011 different senses of the significance of those forms. This is with 7% of scholars using \(or 1991 email usage levels\) . What will happen when + Thu Apr 07 22:34:52 +0000 2011 we reach 50% usage? What experimentation will become possible? - Thu Apr 07 22:35:30 +0000 2011 CIBER survey mentioned by @dancohen: http://www.ucl.ac.uk/infostudies/research/ciber/social-media-report.pdf, see esp. Figure 31. Thu Apr 07 22:43:19 +0000 2011 Alexander Provan of Triple Canopy discussing the publication's use of digital affordances and genealogy in zines & arts magazines. Thu Apr 07 22:49:37 +0000 2011 Provan asks about the problem of proliferating closed publishing platforms - the web may be open, but can we share programming resources? Thu Apr 07 22:56:03 +0000 2011 Anna McCarthy opens discussion by asking Provan about choice not to have comments on Triple Canopy. Provan sees value in controlled space. Thu Apr 07 23:00:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody, @paleofuture and @dancohen Yup - lead texts and images in his talk. Excitement in the audience, too. Thu Apr 07 23:08:09 +0000 2011 .@dancohen acknowledges degree to which great editors can surface important new kinds of work, but that's not universal & can't get around. Thu Apr 07 23:10:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings and @amitorit I completely agree. The only clients that I know allow hashtag muting I find otherwise unusable. :( Thu Apr 07 23:11:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings and @amitorit Okay, okay, I'll cop to being a grownup. :) Thu Apr 07 23:17:24 +0000 2011 Concern from artist in audience that digital humanities has left out artists - designers, perhaps, but no spaces to mix artists and writers. Thu Apr 07 23:22:04 +0000 2011 Replying to @amitorit and @dancohen interesting comment from former press editor who talked about bizarre physical constraints; pre-Amazon, her press tried+ Thu Apr 07 23:23:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @amitorit and @dancohen to publish print "singles" but bookstores wouldn't take them bc their spines were too skinny to be seen on the shelves. Thu Apr 07 23:24:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @tezcatlipoca A panel at the NYU Humanities Initiative on digital humanities and new modes of scholarly communication. Thu Apr 07 23:26:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @tezcatlipoca Absolutely! Let me know if I can be of help. Thu Apr 07 23:28:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @tezcatlipoca I'll ponder names & pass them on. Thu Apr 07 23:31:11 +0000 2011 Plug for #thatcamp from @dancohen as site where multiple digital communities - humanists, artists, and programmers can come together. Thu Apr 07 23:40:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @amitorit and @dancohen Absolutely! Half the humanists I know are anti- or a-. Fri Apr 08 01:25:27 +0000 2011 So this whole self-bribery thing is going well; the promise of a good cup of coffee afterward has gotten me to the gym two days in a row. Fri Apr 08 15:14:22 +0000 2011 Still, I'm hoping that some more internally motivated spirit might take the place of such bald manipulation. Fri Apr 08 15:15:04 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench It would be fantastic to open up that conversation beyond designer-as-project-collaborator & think about digital art per se. Fri Apr 08 15:44:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell I've run up against that same problem; it was less about citing me than about awareness that there was existing work in the area! Fri Apr 08 15:57:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell What I did was name a number of other texts, acknowledging the awkwardness of including my own. Fri Apr 08 15:58:08 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I hope your voice holds out, and that you start feeling better ASAP! Fri Apr 08 17:09:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @drnels No, I'm not in NYU housing; I've got a small furnished studio in the w. village. Pricy but fabulous. Fri Apr 08 17:46:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @drnels Getting on the NYU housing list is hard; you need a dean to intervene for you. But there are sublets to be found... Fri Apr 08 17:47:06 +0000 2011 Yay, another item off the to-do list. Nobody send me anything new; I'm making progress. Fri Apr 08 17:53:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @drnels Nice! WSV gets referred to as the "faculty ghetto"; the buildings are ugly as hell outside but the apts are often nice. If possible+ Fri Apr 08 18:22:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @drnels go see what they're offering you \(and don't feel you have to accept the first thing they show\) . Advice from an NYU fac friend. Fri Apr 08 18:22:34 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: Time to stop thinking of diss as larval monograph, as the MLA put it yrs ago!! @JenHoward @chronicle // Indeed. Fri Apr 08 18:58:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench and @mlaconvention No, wrong direction! The diss should serve its own purposes. Not all should be expected to become a book. Fri Apr 08 19:02:50 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: in other words, challenge assumpt that diss = bk = tenure. Ask what is impt scholarship instead. // This. Fri Apr 08 19:05:26 +0000 2011 MT @nowviskie @mkimarnold: Shutdown solidarity.//To dear colleagues @NEHgov. Thinking of you. You're wonderful-thx for grt svcs you provide. Fri Apr 08 19:15:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself, @amandafrench and @mlaconvention This is a huge part of what past MLA pres Sid Smith has been working on w/ad hoc wk group. Fri Apr 08 19:35:29 +0000 2011 Undermining net neutrality while we're all paying attention to the threat of shutdown? http://nyti.ms/fYAVGD Fri Apr 08 21:22:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself That's awful! My first publisher didn't go to MLA the year my book was out & sent it to lie ignored on an association table. Fri Apr 08 22:18:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself But they're a small press & times were hard, so I got it. Palgrave not bringing your book the first year is not forgivable. Fri Apr 08 22:18:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention, @samplereality, @wynkenhimself, @tcarmody and @mkirschenbaum It really boggles the mind. Fri Apr 08 23:01:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself, @tcarmody, @mlaconvention and @mkirschenbaum Increasingly so, as more & more libraries turn to patron-driven acquisition models. Fri Apr 08 23:07:50 +0000 2011 It's Friday evening, I'm tired and grouchy, and so I've come to the most important decision of the day: wine or whisky? Fri Apr 08 23:21:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @bighandsome An inexpensive Malbec vs. Jameson. Fri Apr 08 23:25:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenServenti If only DC were closer. I'd bring them both! Fri Apr 08 23:26:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @bighandsome Good point. I should know I can count on you in this arena. Fri Apr 08 23:27:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @bighandsome Your BR friends and family would be proud. Fri Apr 08 23:37:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @jasonrhody and @jenserventi I hear that. Fri Apr 08 23:45:35 +0000 2011 RT @RepWeiner: Maybe I'm just dense, but how does defunding the biggest provider of contraceptives reduce abortions? Fri Apr 08 23:50:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @brendabethman A woman after my own heart! I did start in on the whisky, happily. Sat Apr 09 00:09:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @brendabethman Oh, yes, the grouchiness is fading admirably. And yes, let's plan on a drink in Seattle! Sat Apr 09 00:16:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @joshhonn Done and done. Sat Apr 09 00:21:31 +0000 2011 Everybody knows that facts have a clear left-wing bias. http://thinkprogress.org/2011/04/08/kyl-walks-back-claim-about-planned-parenthoo/ Sat Apr 09 00:31:40 +0000 2011 RT @CapitolClio/@jaketapper: Dem & GOP sources: Deal is done and signed off on. $38.5b in cuts, no elimination of Planned Parenthood funding Sat Apr 09 03:13:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj A surprising number of people in my totally bougie suburban SoCal town keep chickens, but roosters are specifically poultry non grata.
