Will make a pilgrimage to hunt it down. And perhaps sometime we might actually meet up down your way for one? Fri Apr 29 23:57:57 +0000 2011 I have Weird Al's "White and Nerdy" stuck in my head. This does not say much good about my #wildfridaynight. Sat Apr 30 01:42:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Just FYI, if you edit custom.css in the theme's main folder instead, it'll override the defaults without future updates undoing + Sat Apr 30 14:48:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender your customizations. - Sat Apr 30 14:48:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @HASTAC Thanks so much! Looking forward to many exciting new collaborative possibilities! Sat Apr 30 14:49:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @CatinStack Thanks so much, Cathy! I'm thrilled to have the opportunity. Sat Apr 30 15:08:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench Do you mean something like MITH's TILE \(text\-image linking environment\) ? #thatcamp Sat Apr 30 15:42:36 +0000 2011 One last thank-you to everyone for the kind wishes yesterday. I tried to catch everybody individually but I'm sure I missed some of you. Sat Apr 30 16:06:23 +0000 2011 I'm very excited about getting some of the new #MLA experiments going; I'll look forward to talking with all of you about them! Sat Apr 30 16:08:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken My inst's email was down for 1/2 the faculty a while back, and the rest of us kept getting email updates about the problem. #hmm Sat Apr 30 17:01:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken That is a very generous interpretation of the situation! But yeah, a plan B would be a good thing, especially at crunch times. Sat Apr 30 17:05:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken That is precisely my sense. If there is a problem, send an email. If there is an email problem, send an email. Sat Apr 30 17:08:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @alanpharvey Thank you - it should, indeed! Sat Apr 30 17:20:24 +0000 2011 This has been the slowest and most painful 1500 words I've ever produced. And I have 1000 to go. Must take a walk to try to clear my head. Sat Apr 30 17:21:54 +0000 2011 Archiving ancient teaching websites \(i\.e\., 2006\-2008\) by exporting from dynamic site to static HTML. A very weird exercise. Sat Apr 30 21:09:41 +0000 2011 Why does the last 10% of bean doneness take longer than the first 90%? Sun May 01 00:04:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender That must be it! Sun May 01 00:18:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @mlaconvention Oh, but there's a huge difference between failure not being possible and not being an option! Sun May 01 01:18:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @mlaconvention Oh, okay. I didn't see it go by, but it's got the ring of #thatcamp. Sun May 01 01:19:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @roxieblog Can you dig it? \(Yes, I can\!\) Sun May 01 01:21:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward That was quick! :) Sun May 01 01:48:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward I figured as much. Frankly, it was nice having only about 20 secs of "oh, how sad!" before the resolution. Sun May 01 01:51:20 +0000 2011
docs.title: 2011 04 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @wgthomas3 Thank you! I'm quite excited about it. Sun May 01 19:50:06 +0000 2011 Just back from fantastic brunch at the Brooklyn home of some old friends. I could once eat brunch without needing a nap after, but no more. Sun May 01 19:52:12 +0000 2011 Folks, do you remember which study it was that ranked the reasons scholars choose their publication venues? \(Audience, prestige, etc\.\) Sun May 01 22:43:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF Yes, that's it; thanks! Sun May 01 22:46:09 +0000 2011 Thanks, @surlyF: it's the Ithaka S+R Faculty Survey 2009. Sun May 01 22:46:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF I could see it clearly as well; I just couldn't remember where it was! Sun May 01 22:49:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball **brain explodes** Mon May 02 00:13:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball $8385 for a departmental license \(up to 10 users in one office\) ! Mon May 02 00:14:53 +0000 2011 Boy, do I stink at final sentences. Mon May 02 02:18:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso Maybe that is how I should end the article! Mon May 02 02:22:26 +0000 2011 Any bets on what we're not going to be allowed to carry on airplanes tomorrow? #nationalsecurityannouncement Mon May 02 02:23:41 +0000 2011 Okay, I have now turned on my television for the first time since the Oscars. Please don't make me listed to Wolf Blitzer for long. Mon May 02 02:29:06 +0000 2011 Listen, that is. But you get the point. Mon May 02 02:29:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF No kidding. Mon May 02 02:30:32 +0000 2011 Oy, with the speculating. I wouldn't want to speculate, but there's been a lot of speculation. As has been widely speculated. Mon May 02 02:31:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell I know, right. The set in my apt this year is awful enough I'd rather download & watch on my computer. Mon May 02 02:32:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I still have very fond memories of Wolf from Gulf War I. But he's a shadow of that rugged former self, alas. Mon May 02 02:33:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Oh, man, you and me both. Mon May 02 02:34:54 +0000 2011 The White House is saying that I have behaved SO responsibly by not speculating. Other Twitterers are wildly speculating, but not me. Mon May 02 02:37:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF As long as I have multiple sources to go with this. The world will know very soon. Mon May 02 02:39:03 +0000 2011 John King, on the other hand, not so much afraid of the speculating. Poor Wolf! Mon May 02 02:44:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF So curious about the choice to let John King say it and keep Wolf Blitzer pure... Mon May 02 02:45:01 +0000 2011 And now Wolf gets to say it out loud? What's that all about? Mon May 02 02:45:52 +0000 2011 Yeah, I'm not sure I'm going to remember that I was sitting in front of my computer when John King announced that Osama bin Laden was dead. Mon May 02 02:47:44 +0000 2011 Wolf apparently pretty upset about having his evening interrupted. Mon May 02 02:50:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @budtheteacher You are not kidding. Mon May 02 02:50:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @jeanburgess I know, right. A little spoiler alert might have been nice. Mon May 02 02:51:08 +0000 2011 Poor Barack. He's now going to come in and say "look, forget it. It was gonna be a surprise, but it's ruined now." Mon May 02 02:53:24 +0000 2011 Ah, Wolf. They must already be plotting retaliation -- because CNN must have something to freak us out about. Mon May 02 02:55:14 +0000 2011 So is this a lot of Blackberry product placement on CNN, or what? Mon May 02 03:00:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @jeanburgess Indeed. Mon May 02 03:02:17 +0000 2011 Actionable: John King, I do not think that word means exactly what you think it means. Mon May 02 03:07:27 +0000 2011 CNN may be infuriating, but at least they're surreal. Mon May 02 03:08:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse This is true. Mon May 02 03:09:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse True. And I should probably switch. But it's like watching a slow-motion car crash. I'm horrified, but I can't quite turn away. Mon May 02 03:12:19 +0000 2011 RT @McLemee: It sure seems like Obama's job as secret Muslim operative imposing Sharia law on the US just got a whole lot harder. Mon May 02 03:12:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Indeed. He needs a much better stage manager. Mon May 02 03:21:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @jasonrhody and @mlaconvention Absolutely - but those contacts should have happened before the announcement. Really badly mistimed. Mon May 02 03:24:46 +0000 2011 RT @maddow: A huge night for the country and the world, but particularly for post-9/11 US military vets and intelligence personnel. Mon May 02 03:25:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @jasonrhody and @mlaconvention Wolf had some weird story about Obama being out somewhere this morning and getting called back to the WH... Mon May 02 03:26:29 +0000 2011 Dude, if the New York Times has already published it, this announcement has taken too long. http://nyti.ms/ldiAZg Mon May 02 03:28:06 +0000 2011 Apparently the Fox News crawl says that "Obama Bin Laden" is dead. Nice. Mon May 02 03:33:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Friend posted a shot of it on FB. Mon May 02 03:34:15 +0000 2011 RT @acavender: I can't say I'm sorry to see him gone. But celebration doesn't sit right either. A necessary evil may be the best descrip ... Mon May 02 03:39:27 +0000 2011 RT @CapitolClio: Thank you Mr. President for not turning announcement into a xenophobic pep rally. Mon May 02 03:46:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @jeanburgess and @katecrawford NYT's "how it leaked" story: http://mediadecoder.blogs.nytimes.com/2011/05/01/how-the-osama-announcement-leaked-out/ Mon May 02 03:49:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @jeanburgess Me too. Mon May 02 03:51:05 +0000 2011 Okay, horrible television turned off again. Feeling better already. Mon May 02 03:52:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @cjprender Oy. I had to shut it off about 5 mins ago. Mon May 02 03:59:00 +0000 2011 You know, I never did finish writing that last sentence. Dammit. Mon May 02 04:06:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @roxieblog Friend of mine standing in an airport not long after 9/11, next to a guy staring at news reports showing ground zero. Guy shook + Mon May 02 04:20:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @roxieblog his head and said "First Dale [Earnhardt], and now this." Mon May 02 04:21:14 +0000 2011 RT @NYCAviation: RT @NBCNewYork: Added checkpoints and patrols tomorrow in NYC by FBI and NYPD, per @Jonathan4NY. Mon May 02 04:22:36 +0000 2011 Alright folks. I do have the MLA Program Committee starting tomorrow at 9, so I'm off to force myself to sleep. Mon May 02 04:29:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @jasonaboyd Thanks! I'm looking forward to getting started! Mon May 02 13:42:47 +0000 2011 RT @FordhamDH: Thrilled that @kfitz will be keynoting for Fac Tech Day ... http://fordhamdh.blogspot.com/2011/05/noted-digital-scholar-kathleen.html?spref=tw Mon May 02 15:31:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender I have a longstanding argument that Irish + Italian is as close to Jewish as one can get. Mon May 02 16:04:17 +0000 2011 MLA Program Committee meeting, day 1, now done. A long and exhausting day, but it'll pay off wonderfully in January! Tue May 03 01:48:29 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention Um. Wow. Closer than I thought, but still no cigar. Tue May 03 02:23:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @CaseyBrienza Thanks for the link; I'll look forward to pondering the possibilities! Tue May 03 19:50:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @BendProf Will look forward to seeing you on the committee next year! Tue May 03 21:04:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @BendProf We will make a point of it. Tue May 03 21:08:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @CaseyBrienza Oh, excellent! Tue May 03 21:08:39 +0000 2011 RT @mlaconvention: Att #MLA members: go to mla.org, take 5 mins to read proposed amenmnt + resolution, then vote. It's impt that a great ... Tue May 03 21:39:02 +0000 2011 Unbelievable. Lufthansa just refunded the full purchase price on our Dublin-Prague tickets \(which were undone by the European blizzard\) . Tue May 03 22:26:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @moncia Ooh, I hadn't thought of it that way! Tue May 03 22:33:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention I'm amazed. I assumed they'd come up with some cancellation fee that would eliminate any refund... Tue May 03 22:33:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself No kidding. Tue May 03 22:33:51 +0000 2011 Hey, folks! So, totally hypothetically: say you've been tweeting both professionally and personally for several years now. + Wed May 04 00:06:58 +0000 2011 & say you take a job working for a very public organization & you *know* that your tweets will be taken by some as the Voice of the Org. + Wed May 04 00:09:47 +0000 2011 Do you start a new "org" account, or a new private account? Do you eliminate the personal from your tweeting? Do you handle some other way? Wed May 04 00:10:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso Ha! Wed May 04 00:13:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself That's more or less what I'm thinking. Though there are a number of tweeps who might be around due to the new position... Wed May 04 00:19:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself Anyhow, yes, it's obvious that everything is public, so needs to be cautious. Wed May 04 00:19:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym That's the direction I'm leaning, I think... Wed May 04 00:20:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @clioweb, @chnm and @scholarslab Fair enough - caution seems in order there! Wed May 04 00:20:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @caseyboyle Oh, you know. One is popular on one's own account. :) Wed May 04 00:23:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso Hmmm. Good point. Wed May 04 00:23:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen and @sheepeeh Oh, this is my own scruples, not the hypothetical org's. Wed May 04 00:25:34 +0000 2011 FYI, this is really my own worry & not the org's. And I don't worry about much, except perhaps the occasional rant about a certain novelist. Wed May 04 00:27:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen Oy. I can only imagine. Wed May 04 00:28:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen That is a very good point. Wed May 04 00:29:21 +0000 2011 A social media policy that, to quote @nowviskie, boils down to "We're all grown-ups here. Just don't be stupid" sounds like a good thing. Wed May 04 00:34:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Nice! Though I'm way less worried about the org claiming I'm representing them than a publication thinking I am. Wed May 04 00:36:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @pmhswe That's a huge part of why I went to Twitter with the question. Research! Wed May 04 00:42:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @roxieblog Thanks, on both counts! Wed May 04 00:43:06 +0000 2011 Thanks for the insight, all. I was quite curious what the thinking out there was. Seems a fair division between maintaining one acct & + Wed May 04 00:56:16 +0000 2011 starting a new org account, while keeping the current account as "me." Wed May 04 00:57:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @karikraus You are too kind! Wed May 04 00:59:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen Don't tempt me! Wed May 04 00:59:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @dancohen Will start swearing like a sailor posthaste. But do you read the consensus as sticking solely with @kfitz, or + Wed May 04 01:04:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @dancohen adding an org account but keeping @kfitz alive? Seems split to me. Wed May 04 01:04:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell Oh, do I have a line I want to use in response! Wed May 04 01:05:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @kfitz lives on regardless. I'm thinking that I'll just do all my tweeting, official and un-, until it seems prudent to change. Wed May 04 01:10:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen This is a most persuasive point. Wed May 04 01:12:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @mlaconvention and @jmittell Al? I don't remember him having opinions on students. But then he's pretty down on everyone. Wed May 04 01:13:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Probably there should be a generic org acct anyhow, one not associated with, say, one's orgconvention. Wed May 04 01:14:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @swarthmoreburke and @publishdifferentyouidiots is the PERFECT handle. Wed May 04 01:15:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley You are totally my model in this, btw. Personable, friendly & fun, and yet professional & discreet. Plus separate org acct. Wed May 04 01:22:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley I certainly hope so! Wed May 04 01:23:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward HAHAHAHAHAHAHA. Um. I mean. Hmm. Wed May 04 01:26:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley Absolutely. But given the intimate \(some might say fishbowl\) environment of a SLAC, work/life blend has been long on my mind. Wed May 04 01:42:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenServenti, @brettbobley and @jasonrhody Now I'm really intrigued. Wed May 04 01:42:27 +0000 2011 Thanks, all. A most interesting conversation. Like @pmhswe, I hope someone is doing research on personal/professional identity on Twitter! I've seen uses of it in which I want to say "no you did not. I'm quite sure of it." Fri May 20 03:36:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @MLAconvention That's nuts. Fri May 20 03:38:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @alicedaer Congratulations! What a doll. I knew the weather was too good to last. #gettingsoggy Sat May 21 20:04:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @emarsh Enjoy it! It was gorgeous here earlier today, but we're now back to the rain we've been living with for about ten days. Sat May 21 20:16:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings I will certainly hope so - we could use it! Sat May 21 20:21:18 +0000 2011 It wasn't exactly the rapture, but it was a really really good sushi dinner. Sat May 21 23:32:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @emarsh Behold the power of suggestion! Sat May 21 23:40:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal I went to Aki on your suggestion! Sun May 22 00:20:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Went for the early prix fixe. Quite nice! And definitely better than the end of the world. Sun May 22 00:32:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Fair enough! Sun May 22 00:47:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality No kidding. I'm glad he's all over the tweaks and fixes, but I kinda wish he'd save them up a bit. Sun May 22 17:05:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality PS: Nice use of "egads." Sun May 22 17:06:02 +0000 2011 New post: Complexity http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/complexity/ Sun May 22 19:58:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @MoodyFred The draft is up at the link in the blog post; not sure about the revised version yet. Mon May 23 01:07:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Oh, good grief. Well, all's well and all that, but sheesh. Mon May 23 12:23:20 +0000 2011 New post: Enough http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/enough/ Mon May 23 14:17:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @jenterysayers Congratulations! Mon May 23 15:03:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @pmhswe It's really hard to let go of the idea of running! Mon May 23 16:50:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Thanks, to you and @loriemerson! Mon May 23 19:38:08 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar Little Rock? That's some itinerary. Mon May 23 23:05:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @kdettmar Gah! I'm so sorry. Hope you make it to an appropriate destination soon. Mon May 23 23:14:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @nmhouston and @rgfeal Oh, that would be nice... Mon May 23 23:15:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @nmhouston and @rgfeal Only problem I have with defining "enough" in advance is the "more than I can chew" phenomenon. Tend to set ridic goals. Mon May 23 23:32:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @bukuwawa While you're at it, could you make everyone stop using "relatable"? Tue May 24 00:24:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality This question is what laid the groundwork for MediaCommons; the change came when we decided we needed a network, not a press. Tue May 24 15:44:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Well, that's sort of good news, I guess. Take care of yourself! Tue May 24 15:46:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality Exactly. Tue May 24 15:46:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @eymand and @samplereality The issue for us was also valuing process and community over product. Tue May 24 15:51:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @eymand What I like to think of as maintaining reverse compatibility with an out-of-date but still in-use system \(i\.e\. T&P\) . Tue May 24 15:53:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie and @samplereality Indeed. It's much the same in fields in the US in which journal impact factor is the arbiter of publication value. Tue May 24 15:58:17 +0000 2011 Revising is hard. Tue May 24 16:02:54 +0000 2011 There is a very odd yellow radiance emanating from the sky. I do not know what to make of it. Tue May 24 17:03:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @kimonizer That *must* be it. Tue May 24 17:56:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah and @wynkenhimself Sheesh, I was thinking I was immune. You mean to say that it just hasn't hit me yet? Tue May 24 22:34:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @mmwwah No, lots of @ today, but no emails. I suspect they're rolling it out gradually. Awesome. Tue May 24 22:36:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @mmwwah The call... it's going to be coming from upstairs! At some really annoying moment in the near future! Tue May 24 22:40:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah and @wynkenhimself Nope! There's no setting for it. Trust me, I tried. Tue May 24 22:41:02 +0000 2011 Revision update: have marked all the spots where changes need to be made, with annotations describing the changes. Now the actual changing. Wed May 25 13:27:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @cscannella Hey, with that many wives and mistresses to buy for, credit is crucial! Wed May 25 13:29:04 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall Happy birthday, Brian! Have a great one. \(But ditto @GeorgeOnline & @wynkenhimself: not sure where the last decade went\!\) Wed May 25 14:19:33 +0000 2011 First 25% of article now sucks 31% less. Not quite enough, but getting there! Wed May 25 16:47:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality Cool! \(But it's the Office of Scholarly Communication\.\) Wed May 25 17:20:05 +0000 2011 I am hugely flattered \(& not a little humbled\) by @samplereality's hopes for the MLA Office of Scholarly Communication. http://www.samplereality.com/2011/05/25/the-digital-humanities-is-not-about-building-its-about-sharing/ Wed May 25 17:23:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @leoba Nice, thanks! Wed May 25 17:48:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @dougreside Interesting. Though of course it's really the @mkirschenbaum etymology, which in turn is the @unsworth etymology... Wed May 25 17:53:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @parezcoydigo It's a new "feature"! It's been fun watching the ripple effect as it hits people's accounts - mostly bc it hasn't hit me yet. Wed May 25 18:05:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @parezcoydigo 100% agreed. Except for the cranky. But that's only because I remain spared; cranky will follow shortly, I suspect. Wed May 25 18:25:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings Safe trip! It was great getting to see you here. Wed May 25 18:59:01 +0000 2011 Have now revised 45% of article to be 63% better. \(There was a really bad patch in there\.\) Wed May 25 19:21:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @kathiiberens and @samplereality You people are going to make me blush. AGAIN. Wed May 25 19:33:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @karikraus I very much look forward to facilitating the peculiar! Wed May 25 19:34:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @brettbobley It's all about the metrics, man. Wed May 25 19:41:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @johnmjones And one really hopes that one goes the way of the "book in the bathtub" soon; it seems a pointless bit of safety theater. Wed May 25 19:55:08 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Absolutely! Word count: 8463. Words that don't suck: 6491. Wed May 25 20:55:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Love it. Go, engineer, go! Wed May 25 21:17:33 +0000 2011 In a NISO/@internetarchive planning meeting on ebook bookmark and annotation standards at NYU today. #bkmk11 Thu May 26 14:16:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @cscannella Is it a private tweet? It might be a good thing! Thu May 26 15:53:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @cscannella \(By which I mean from someone's private stream, not a DM\.\) Thu May 26 15:54:23 +0000 2011 RT @moncia: Enjoying some of the lingo in a tech convo that's way over my head: "graceful degradation," "fail robustly." Concepts I can use. Thu May 26 17:04:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward I know; bummer. At least I'll see you in Baltimore. Thu May 26 19:07:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward Me too. But somehow appropriate that the two are happening at once on opposite sides of the DC corridor. Thu May 26 19:09:29 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself, @jenhoward and @amandafrench Alas, I'll be on my way to California Sunday afternoon... Thu May 26 19:20:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @triproftri Congratulations! Fri May 27 22:11:24 +0000 2011 Revision complete. Article now sucks 47% less. On to the next project! Sat May 28 18:36:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @chutry One of the most brilliant things about Chrome: opening a new window gives you links to recently closed items. Sat May 28 18:40:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Once a Sagehen... Sat May 28 18:50:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @chutry Bummer. That's one of the reasons I rely so heavily on tabs; I've accidentally closed windows w/15 tabs & have been able to recover. Sat May 28 20:14:02 +0000 2011 Two! Two! Two major tasks crossed off the to-do list today! Sat May 28 21:56:21 +0000 2011 OK, one last productivity brag for the day. Two major things crossed off the to-do list, and: In! Box! Zero! No one send me anything to do. Sat May 28 23:35:36 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Is sad, but true. Alas, if you could see what my next three weeks look like... Sat May 28 23:43:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Now, however, goofing off begins. Plus beer. Sat May 28 23:44:21 +0000 2011 RT @ftrain: Oh Franzen oh Franzen oh no oh why. OH GOD FRANZEN NO STOP IT FRANZEN PLEASE FRANZEN...oh. Oh, god. http://www.nytimes.com/2011/05/29/opinion/29franzen.html Sun May 29 13:23:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @msbonn Maria!!! So amazingly glad to hear from you here! Sending you all my best wishes! Sun May 29 13:25:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Oh, dear. Not me. No, no. I would never. Sun May 29 13:34:26 +0000 2011 I am attempting to take a day "off." I believe that's what people call it, at least. Sun May 29 13:38:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman As my grandfather used to say, even a broken clock is right twice a day. Sun May 29 13:47:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate I know, right? I've heard people talking about it, but I'm not quite sure how it works. Will experiment and advise. Sun May 29 14:13:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal I can only imagine that I would. But I think I'm going to lie around and read today. Have... fun? Sun May 29 14:14:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal There should be all of the above! Enjoy! Sun May 29 14:17:12 +0000 2011 Amen. //MT @stevenbjohnson: Nothing acclimates the mind more quickly to the idea of an all e-book future than packing your house for a move. Mon May 30 19:32:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @mariabustillos Okay wow. Chills. Mon May 30 20:18:31 +0000 2011 Oh no. Aura. Mon May 30 20:49:09 +0000 2011 And it appears to be another ocular migraine. Bizarro aura, no real headache, super loopy. Excuse to spend the evening in bed, I guess. Mon May 30 22:37:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ This is only the second one I've gotten; all my previous auras were followed immediately by the headache. Weird. Mon May 30 22:43:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Good grief! Are you sure that's coming in from outside & not a busted pipe? \(Stupid question; I'm sure you're sure\.\) Mon May 30 23:26:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Oh, man. I hope it stops soon! Mon May 30 23:28:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings Thanks for the link! And I'm definitely better today, despite a bit of a migraine hangover... Tue May 31 10:54:45 +0000 2011 My MBAir has suddenly become unable to mount any .dmg file it downloads. This is becoming seriously inconvenient. Tue May 31 13:27:51 +0000 2011 Have narrowed down the problem, but not in a good way. Downloaded the .dmg with the iMac, transferred to the MBAir, still can't open. Tue May 31 13:32:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ I do, on CD/DVD. Which of course the MBAir does not possess. Will ponder a workaround. Tue May 31 13:41:35 +0000 2011 Ugh, and software update has now failed. Begin to fear that the MBAir's SSD may be corrupted or worse. Tue May 31 13:43:27 +0000 2011 SSD is indeed corrupted. This is the moment when the no-optical-drive thing becomes a liability. Tue May 31 14:01:12 +0000 2011 I'm all for lifting the veil of secrecy on double-blind peer revew. But I believe this is a step in the wrong direction. http://www.insidehighered.com/news/2011/05/31/american_economic_association_abandons_double_blind_journal_reviewing Tue May 31 14:16:54 +0000 2011 That last tweet brought to you by my need to step back for a moment from my rapidly degenerating laptop situation. Tue May 31 14:17:25 +0000 2011 WHY, oh why, do critical laptop problems always only creep up two days before a three week stint of total laptop dependency begins? Tue May 31 14:42:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander It will certainly have a greater chance of boosting fairness than will giving one set of participants full knowledge [+] Tue May 31 14:50:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander while keeping the other in the dark. The potential abuses of one-sided secrecy are far higher than double-blindedness. Tue May 31 14:51:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander Interesting question. I'd be surprised if that were a viable argument. How would single-blind create savings? Tue May 31 14:58:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @sgsinclair This one's on about 3.2 years, so I've probably worn out its welcome. Tue May 31 15:03:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @sgsinclair \(Congrats on the fabulous new gig, btw\!\) Tue May 31 15:04:23 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Yeah, but the machine is 3.2 years old, so even the extended care of the apple will be of no use. Tue May 31 15:05:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah And the most frustrating thing is that I only need it to live until July, when it will be supplanted by an employer-owned machine. Tue May 31 15:05:47 +0000 2011 Updates: attempt to restart MBAir w/OS X Remote Install failed. Now attempting to restart from clone of desktop drive so I can repair SSD. Tue May 31 15:08:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander I would not be terribly surprised to hear that as well. Tue May 31 15:11:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Yeah, me too. iPad will not suffice for three weeks, I fear. Tue May 31 15:11:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @CatinStack Huge news, and well-deserved congratulations! Tue May 31 15:12:01 +0000 2011 Booting from clone worked. Running Disk Utility worked. Disk repairs claim to have worked. Now repairing permissions before trying reboot. Tue May 31 15:14:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Thank you! Tue May 31 15:15:50 +0000 2011 PS: If this works, I encourage everyone to install Carbon Copy Cloner and make a donation to its developer, who will have saved my bacon. Tue May 31 15:18:54 +0000 2011 Reboot successful. Now attempting the software updates that uncovered the problem in the first place. Tue May 31 15:21:53 +0000 2011 Success. Not only is everything working like it's supposed to, but I seem to have recovered some speed that I'd lost of late. Tue May 31 15:29:57 +0000 2011 Still somewhat afraid to breathe, though. Tue May 31 15:30:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody No kidding. And all my backup paranoia now seems utterly justified. Tue May 31 15:32:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander and @jtheibault I think the assumption is the other way - that reviewers will Google author identity. Less sure about the other. Tue May 31 15:43:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @BryanAlexander and @jtheibault Nonetheless, you know which way I vote on this. Tue May 31 15:43:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah No, everything's working. I'm just in a bit of disbelief. Tue May 31 15:43:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Good lord. Tue May 31 16:26:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @sivavaid You could show up and say ANYTHING. Wed Jun 01 01:40:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Apparently there's a setting. But I'm mostly fascinated by the fact that that still hasn't happened to me! Wed Jun 01 01:41:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym Okay, that's infuriating. Wed Jun 01 01:42:23 +0000 2011 Sony pulled the trailer for Fincher's Girl w/Dragon Tattoo off YouTube based on a copyright claim. They do realize trailers are ads, right? Wed Jun 01 01:50:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @mattthomas It's down now, with the big "copyright claim" notice. Maybe they did both, in which case, bravo. Wed Jun 01 01:53:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF How the university can and should take responsibility for ensuring that scholarly communication has a \(non\-extortive\) future. Wed Jun 01 01:58:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF That's fabulous. I did mean the response seriously, though. If current sch comm practices are bankrupting uni libraries, unis must + Wed Jun 01 02:05:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF think seriously about new models, but those new models won't come without uni investment. I'm thinking of sch comm as a part of + Wed Jun 01 02:06:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF the university infrastructure as necessary as the library, the faculty, the buildings. How can universities take ownership? Wed Jun 01 02:07:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF Those are absolutely the *slightly* more than $64,000 questions, indeed. Wed Jun 01 02:11:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF Gives one chills. Wed Jun 01 02:11:23 +0000 2011
docs.title: 2011 05 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @ddchamberlain Doubtless. Wed Jun 01 17:09:30 +0000 2011 RT @SHAQ: im retiring Video: http://bit.ly/kvLtE3 #ShaqRetires Wed Jun 01 18:50:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @amyeetx and @triproftri Yes. "But what have you done lately?" is endemic. Wed Jun 01 18:54:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Have I not told you about my deep tie to Shaq? He defended my sister from her raging asshole bf. I am honor-bound to root for him. Wed Jun 01 19:07:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah \(Thus the announcement comes as a bit of a relief\.\) Wed Jun 01 19:07:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah To be honest, it would be impossible to have been at LSU during 1989-91 and not be something of a fan. Wed Jun 01 19:10:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Yeah, that would make it a lot harder. Wed Jun 01 19:13:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Personally, I'd start with the ADs, but the coaches \(of men's football and basketball\) are next in line, fer sure. Wed Jun 01 19:15:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @barbarahui Is prednisone driving you to talk to yourself? :) Wed Jun 01 20:24:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @barbarahui I can only imagine. I've had to take prednisone before and I can say that I'm not a huge fan. Wed Jun 01 20:27:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @barbarahui \(Actually, it was the reply to yourself that had me wondering\.\) Wed Jun 01 20:27:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat If only they would follow it off with a little lemon sorbet. Thu Jun 02 00:47:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate It would be great, if you could figure out how to type a really good raspberry. Thu Jun 02 01:19:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate Ugh, crossed pop cultural references. I was imagining a GIlda Radneresque "and that's the truth phbbbbbt." Thu Jun 02 01:24:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate God, another crossed reference. I was thinking Emily Litella. But that's the "never mind" conclusion. Thu Jun 02 01:41:14 +0000 2011 Taking care of some last business before hitting the road \(or the rails, more precisely\) for Baltimore and #aaup11. Thu Jun 02 12:39:50 +0000 2011 Very sorry to be missing #thatcamp this year, but I'll be there in spirit! Thu Jun 02 12:40:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @sramsay There may be a mole on site willing to help. Thu Jun 02 12:45:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @mamamusings I'll be thinking about you today. Take care of yourself! Thu Jun 02 12:54:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender, @nowviskie and @academicdave Absolutely! I'll be reading along from afar. Thu Jun 02 12:55:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @amyeetx The future of the diss is being explored by an #MLA working group led by past president Sidonie Smith. Hugely impt. #9sinst11 Thu Jun 02 13:44:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @amyeetx Cool! There will be a panel discussing the working group's efforts at #mla12. More TK! #9sinst11 Thu Jun 02 13:49:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen ORCID promises to be enormously important for scholarly networks. Thu Jun 02 15:16:42 +0000 2011 @NewYorker: Having trouble downloading the June 6 issue to my aypad - getting a "file is missing or invalid" error. Any ideas? Thu Jun 02 15:37:48 +0000 2011 @NewYorker: And just like that, it's working. Thanks, or never mind - whichever applies! Thu Jun 02 15:41:07 +0000 2011 On the Acela to Baltimore for #aaup11. Hooray for public wifi and a working VPN. Thu Jun 02 16:01:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @mkirschenbaum Many thanks! I appreciate what sounds like some awfully heavy lifting. Thu Jun 02 16:17:42 +0000 2011 RT @lexinyt: Our new executive editor and I have set a Twitter date. I predict she will be a champion tweeter. #twittermakesyousmart Thu Jun 02 17:51:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball Indeed. Sending nothing but good vibes Michigan-ward. Thu Jun 02 18:43:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball, @moncia and @ericzinner I was thinking about that amazing dinner we had just today. I think I'm the only one of us here this year! Thu Jun 02 18:57:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @ericzinner, @shanakimball and @moncia Indeed. In future years, though. And -- hey, in New York! Thu Jun 02 19:26:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @gmbritton Nitpicking: hasn't NAP been completely OA for a bit? They're making downloadable PDFs \(instead of online images\) free. #aaup11 Thu Jun 02 19:41:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @parezcoydigo Word. Thu Jun 02 20:01:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @moncia and @gmbritton Yes, that's a huge move! Just clarifying that they were technically OA all along, if only in less desirable formats. Thu Jun 02 20:17:02 +0000 2011 Really, really hoping that that odd rattling hum coming through my ceiling is a vacuum cleaner. #ahhotels Thu Jun 02 20:36:34 +0000 2011 Tonight's keynote at #aaup11 from David Simon about to begin. Simon has donated his fee to the Ella Thompson Fund. http://parksandpeople.org Fri Jun 03 00:30:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell Nice! Fri Jun 03 00:56:39 +0000 2011 Simon is fascinating: at once a defense of noncommercial values in journalistic/cultural production & an impassioned defense of IP. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 01:04:34 +0000 2011 First plenary at #aaup11 beginning: Grant McCracken, "The 'shapeless problem' problem." Fri Jun 03 13:05:44 +0000 2011 McCracken: Degree of change in contemporary culture is resulting in our culture's signature phrase: "Wait, what?" #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:12:17 +0000 2011 Wait, what? Malcolm Gladwell is the answer? Steve Jobs is the answer? #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:29:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward Ditto. Fri Jun 03 13:30:08 +0000 2011 Aha. It's the liberal arts that's McCracken's answer: the ability to give shape to a seemingly shapeless problem. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:32:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward That is indeed what it begins to sound like! #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:33:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward Steve Jobs can solve that problem. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:33:48 +0000 2011 Okay, massively reductive rendering of what postmodernism is. One might have noted that Jameson was *critical* of the postmodern. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:35:18 +0000 2011 So what we need is a business school embedded within the liberal arts. \(What could possibly go wrong?\) #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:46:59 +0000 2011 Wait, did he say that we need to reform the university by ending tenure and bringing managers in and kicking scholars out? #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:48:27 +0000 2011 Is that meant to re-liberal-arts-ize the managers and let scholars loose upon the business world? #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 13:49:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @lwaltzer I guess it's a good thing I missed it the first time! Fri Jun 03 13:50:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell Ire. Dismay. Exhaustion. Whatever. Fri Jun 03 13:51:14 +0000 2011 I am interested in the fact that he's told the attendees at #aaup11 to disintermediate the publishing process between ideas and practice... Fri Jun 03 13:54:16 +0000 2011 ...though I don't think that's going to get a lot of uptake here. Fri Jun 03 13:54:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @synch101 What you said. Fri Jun 03 13:55:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @medeamalmo Thank you! I'd love to hear how your journal develops. Fri Jun 03 13:57:16 +0000 2011 Interesting response to an earlier version of this McCracken keynote: http://cac.ophony.org/2011/05/20/the-qidz-are-alright/ #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 14:00:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @synch101 I am mildly surprised to find myself on the publishers' side in most of this. Fri Jun 03 14:05:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward You know, I'm all about disintermediation in publishing, and even I don't love it. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 14:06:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward There's a slippage from "get ideas out more quickly" to "get rid of publishing" that's really problematic. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 14:07:17 +0000 2011 Okay, here I'm on his side. "Most blogs are not good" is true, but no more true than "most books are not good." Has ever been thus. #aaup11 Fri Jun 03 14:08:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Exactly. Had he gone for "disrupt" it would have been a far more nuanced and arguable point. Fri Jun 03 14:10:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat A bullet point suggested we "move from ideas to practice w/o going through the finding, reviewing, editing, publishing process." Fri Jun 03 14:27:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Rethink that process, yes. Disrupt that process, yes. Eliminate that process? Dunno. Mon Jun 27 13:41:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality Otherwise, yes, the cross-town move does provide a false sense of security. "How hard could it be?" Mon Jun 27 13:41:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @publichistorian Why yes! I will hope to hold onto it for a while, and remember how small most problems really are. Mon Jun 27 13:43:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @cjprender Wow. I can completely imagine. Having spent so many days carefully wrapping and taping, though, it's nice just [+] Mon Jun 27 13:52:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @cjprender to throw things in boxes in no particular order, knowing I'll be pulling them out again in five hours. Mon Jun 27 13:52:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality and @cjprender Also, this part of the move is way more akin to changing hotel rooms than it is a move per se. Mon Jun 27 13:53:31 +0000 2011 I accumulated an untoward number of boots this year. Mon Jun 27 13:58:27 +0000 2011 Packed. Have truck. Upon arrival of volunteer moving assistants, will be underway! Mon Jun 27 16:13:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @CCdiglib I'm not sure at this point. I hope so - but the college has given me a two year leave to decide. Mon Jun 27 16:14:13 +0000 2011 First, the good news: I'm in, the move was super easy thanks to my pals, and the apartment is even more awesome than I'd remembered. [+] Mon Jun 27 20:30:27 +0000 2011 Now, the bad news: the CA shipment is not arriving until next week sometime. So no furniture \(or pots, pans, dishes, etc\) for a bit. [+] Mon Jun 27 20:32:22 +0000 2011 On the other hand, they're charging me less rather than more for the service. So I'm not complaining too much. [-] Mon Jun 27 20:33:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @PacificPet Hey awesome! Thanks so much. I hope Henry calmed down. Mon Jun 27 20:34:36 +0000 2011 In other news, hooray for having mailed the air mattress. Also, hooray cheap lightheadedness from blowing up the air mattress. Mon Jun 27 20:54:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @savasavasava They may now. Back in 1998, the cheap ones from Walmart apparently did not. Mon Jun 27 20:59:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality Truly. I will at least eat very well. Mon Jun 27 21:06:01 +0000 2011 Ahhh. Open wifi, finally. Thank you to the owner of the open belkin 54g out there. Mon Jun 27 21:19:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @samplereality I actually love a good working vacation, when R is working, too. If only one of us is working, it blows. Mon Jun 27 21:36:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality My Race, Gender, & SF syllabus. Some things on it are more fun than others, but it's all pretty great. http://machines.pomona.edu/55-2008/syllabus/index.html Mon Jun 27 22:01:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality It takes a bit to work past the simplistic species = race assumption, but Butler complicates it quickly. It's really great. Mon Jun 27 22:13:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality In that case, Lilith's Brood would be perfect, absolutely. Mon Jun 27 22:29:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @RayS6 and @uvichumanities Congrats, Ray - fabulous news! Mon Jun 27 23:07:54 +0000 2011 Replying to @moncia Oh, you are so nice! I think I'm okay for the moment. Will let you know if the air mattress & delivery get old! Mon Jun 27 23:38:10 +0000 2011 Not for nothing, but the view from my terrace. \(Did I mention I have a terrace?\) http://yfrog.com/kiw44nj Tue Jun 28 00:03:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @bighandsome I will confess: this place pretty much rocks. Let me know if you pass through the city; a terracy beverage is in the offing. Tue Jun 28 00:12:54 +0000 2011 The kitties just arrived! Both seem a little agitated but otherwise good! Tue Jun 28 02:16:04 +0000 2011 Can tell I'm going to have a hard time keeping big kitty in the adjustment room. Tue Jun 28 02:16:22 +0000 2011 Little kitty, on the other hand, has found a safe spot from which she is giving me the "I know what you did" look. Tue Jun 28 02:19:26 +0000 2011 By special request: big kitty. http://yfrog.com/kjcg5foj Tue Jun 28 02:21:27 +0000 2011 And little kitty: http://yfrog.com/kio1sggj Tue Jun 28 02:22:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @savasavasava He is, isn't he? He'd tell you so himself. Tue Jun 28 02:24:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm Me too. After Thursday's adventure, I was worried how they'd take the trip. Tue Jun 28 02:26:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal They did get delivered to the bldg, though I met them in the lobby. Both seem happy with the view from here. Tue Jun 28 02:33:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal and @briancroxall Exactly. She's NYC apt ready. He's more of a sprawler. Tue Jun 28 02:34:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @patrick_mj So far, so good! Tue Jun 28 02:34:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball Thank you! The vibes all feel good now. Tue Jun 28 02:42:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @acavender Yeah, she gets a cross little look when she stops to consider it. :) Tue Jun 28 02:43:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal By keeping the door to the terrace closed? Tue Jun 28 02:44:04 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Don't know about Mouser, but big kitty has a healthy strain of Russian Blue in him. Makes him ornery. Tue Jun 28 02:45:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Worry not; they can't get anywhere. It's totally latticed in. And I managed to keep them off my SoCal balcony for six years, so... Tue Jun 28 02:46:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @AlecHosterman No, they're litter mates. Both 11 years old. She's increasingly tiny, while he's just increasing. Tue Jun 28 02:48:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball Murray Hill. Tue Jun 28 02:48:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Trust me, I got it covered. Tue Jun 28 02:49:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Ha! Alice climbed up on top of my china cabinet the other day and faced the same puzzle. But she'd gotten herself up! Tue Jun 28 02:53:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @RepoRat Aw, sweet. I love white patches! Tue Jun 28 03:13:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @newkidonthehall Why thank you! Tue Jun 28 03:49:20 +0000 2011 Breakfast on the terrace. Will be even more awesome once I have a chair. Tue Jun 28 13:29:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @boone Aw, little peanut! Congratulations to all of you! Tue Jun 28 15:56:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell Have a great trip! I hope settling in there goes quickly and well. Tue Jun 28 20:15:17 +0000 2011 It looks like my stuff is not going to show up until a week from Friday. Far more importantly, the internet should arrive day after tmw. Tue Jun 28 21:51:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Big pipe-laying truck, yeah. Tue Jun 28 22:48:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmittell Augh! I'm so sorry. How utterly frustrating. Wed Jun 29 00:37:14 +0000 2011 Last night's sleep option - air mattress topped with memory foam - was too soft for comfort. Tonight, I test the memory foam on the floor. Wed Jun 29 00:39:04 +0000 2011 \(If it works, this means I can use the air mattress as a temporary sofa\. Two comfortable spots\!\) Wed Jun 29 00:40:03 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench I find it a little too... springy. But that's the next stage of the experiment. Tonight, it's a fat hunk of memory foam. Wed Jun 29 01:18:01 +0000 2011 I love my apartment. I will love it more with furniture. Wed Jun 29 14:01:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman This is a problem Google's had for a while - Wave & Buzz were both flawed & a startup might have been given time to sort them out. Wed Jun 29 19:27:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @sivavaid and @briancroxall The evals that haunted me all the way through my tenure review were in my 1st year & were all about tech failure. Wed Jun 29 19:37:21 +0000 2011 Replying to @sivavaid and @briancroxall One failed video conference \(in 1999\!\) and all of my review letters said "she should scale back on the technology." Wed Jun 29 19:37:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall and @sivavaid They stuck so long bc of the "we have never done it that way before" factor. SLACs are lovely but slow to change. Wed Jun 29 19:39:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @johnmjones and @briancroxall "I said no pencils in this room!" Wed Jun 29 19:52:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @johnmjones and @briancroxall You kids and your "automatic ball-point ink pens." Sheesh. Wed Jun 29 19:54:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward I'm with you. I like my hyphens whether the compound modifier precedes or follows the noun! Wed Jun 29 20:29:49 +0000 2011 It appears that Google+ invites have gone wide. I've got them, too, if you want one. Thu Jun 30 01:33:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @adarel Send me your email? Thu Jun 30 01:34:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @westcenter Done! You should have it shortly. Thu Jun 30 01:39:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @shanakimball Invites appear to be unlimited, as opposed to the Gmail/Wave rollout. Interesting. Thu Jun 30 01:50:32 +0000 2011 Replying to @heathermwhitney Have walked away from the computer for a sec, but will send ASAP. Thu Jun 30 01:52:47 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @shanakimball Yes, and particularly a social network that didn't have anyone you wanted to be social with. Thu Jun 30 01:53:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @jvinopal They must have shut down invites for the night; everyone's reporting that theirs are gone. I'll send one when they come back, tho! Thu Jun 30 04:08:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @jvinopal Got invites back, apparently. On its way. Thu Jun 30 11:34:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Yes! Sending. Thu Jun 30 11:34:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @kakennedy DM me your email and I'll send! Thu Jun 30 11:35:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @seth_denbo, @briancroxall and @johnmjones I had invites earlier this morning, but now they're gone again. Will let you know if they reappear. Thu Jun 30 13:49:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @midwinter_ Excellent! Thu Jun 30 15:51:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman shift-refresh... shift-refresh... shift-refresh... Fri Jul 01 00:07:59 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody and @jwolman It's... not bad. Way less ugly than the old layout. But things feel really spread out, even in the dense version. Fri Jul 01 00:11:01 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Not unlike the revamp of OS X Lion to be consistent with iOS. Fri Jul 01 00:19:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen EVERYWHERE. Fri Jul 01 00:42:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @juliandibbell You can definitely add people to more than one circle! Just check multiple boxes. Fri Jul 01 00:45:37 +0000 2011 Hey, I just realized! I'm actually not employed from tomorrow through Monday. Weird. Fri Jul 01 00:51:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde Good point: I have a job; I just won't be doing it. Fri Jul 01 01:07:12 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself Woo! I'm in if you are! Fri Jul 01 01:10:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate Coincidentally, also my favorite Muppet Show bit! http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ltlPINPn8UU Fri Jul 01 01:28:35 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate Indeed! Fri Jul 01 01:32:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender I suppose I'll survive the four days... Fri Jul 01 01:39:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Hmmm. Probably not. I think I'll just store up a few days' worth of sleeping in, as that's about to be history. Fri Jul 01 01:43:58 +0000 2011
docs.title: 2011 06 01 tweets

docs.text: Hey, happy birthday @amandafrench and Canada! One of you is 144 and the other really really is not! Fri Jul 01 11:45:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @amandafrench Thanks! Seriously, I hope you have a fantastic day. Fri Jul 01 11:53:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Ha. Somebody might have a generous future employer who allowed her insurance to become effective a couple of days early. Fri Jul 01 12:11:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Hey, if we can have it at eomega.org, I'm in! Fri Jul 01 12:14:46 +0000 2011 I have never been more ready to have some damn furniture in my house. And yet there are Six. Days. To go. Sat Jul 02 15:33:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj, @tcarmody and @paleofuture It does need a certain breadth. The part that grates with me, though, is the security stuff, which basically + Sat Jul 02 16:43:27 +0000 2011 Replying to @jbj, @tcarmody and @paleofuture says that if things go wrong, it's your fault, and don't come crying to them. Not a good PR move. - Sat Jul 02 16:43:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @paleofuture, @jbj and @tcarmody I'm fine with the "publicly display" in theory, as there is a Public folder to be accounted for. But the last + Sat Jul 02 16:50:19 +0000 2011 Replying to @paleofuture, @jbj and @tcarmody couple of weeks have made me profoundly unsettled by "to the extent we think it necessary for the Service." + Sat Jul 02 16:50:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @paleofuture, @jbj and @tcarmody I do not trust their judgment about what's necessary at this point. - Sat Jul 02 16:50:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken and @academicdave In fact, I'd suggest that the new TOS/Privacy/Security policies make things worse. The default position is that + Sat Jul 02 16:56:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmcclurken and @academicdave security is your responsibility, and that they have the right to do whatever they think best with your stuff. Sat Jul 02 16:56:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball Have a fabulous trip! If you've never read David Mitchell's Cloud Atlas, I'd highly recommend it. Excellent jetlag reading. Sat Jul 02 17:07:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball and @sleonchnm CLOUD ATLAS. I liked JdZ a lot but... CLOUD ATLAS. Sat Jul 02 19:34:35 +0000 2011 All this Googlemania inspired by G+ has me testing out Tasks again. So far, I'm liking it; stripped-down, non-fussy, multiple to-do lists. Sat Jul 02 20:07:01 +0000 2011 I will also confess to buying a few new-apartment/new-job presents for myself. Fancy new pots to replace the ones my tenants destroyed. + Sat Jul 02 20:08:25 +0000 2011 A soda machine I've had my eye on. And a Roku box, which I'll hook to my TV if it ever actually shows up here... Sat Jul 02 20:09:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse One could, perhaps, wash them very badly and then repeatedly cook with them in the unwashed state, leaving them uncleanable. Sat Jul 02 20:10:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse Though I will confess to being very fussy about my pots, these were really disgusting, and no amount of scouring helped. Sat Jul 02 20:11:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @annehelen I have been DYING for one. So excited. Sat Jul 02 20:11:34 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse Go, go, go! Sat Jul 02 20:12:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @LyonCommaRachel Yes, it will be! I'm very fired up about it. Sat Jul 02 20:12:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse Perhaps you could fantasize about having ice cream and hot fudge WITH IMPUNITY post-gym. Sat Jul 02 20:14:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse I can see that. I often find myself thinking about things like that "oh, I could do that anytime" and so never doing it. Sat Jul 02 20:16:51 +0000 2011 Replying to @samplereality Ooh, thanks - I'll do that! Sat Jul 02 20:22:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Yes, Soda Stream. And All-Clad stainless, which I'm beside myself with excitement about. Sat Jul 02 21:45:13 +0000 2011 You know, I thought my email had gotten awfully light. Turns out Gmail hadn't picked up messages for one of my accounts in a week. Sat Jul 02 22:01:16 +0000 2011 And now I have 100+ messages to wade through. 30+ of them categorized as "important." Most awesome. Sat Jul 02 22:02:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @CapitolClio SpiderOak! It's a little more complex to set up, but because it's far more flexible and powerful. https://spideroak.com/download/referral/525c5656d1c56eccf12b5a466f7553ca Sat Jul 02 22:17:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself Clearly! Sat Jul 02 22:50:17 +0000 2011 Replying to @CapitolClio Everyone says great stuff about it. Very much looking forward to testing mine out! Sat Jul 02 23:02:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @audreywatters Am working on doing the same. Sat Jul 02 23:24:13 +0000 2011 Among the casualties of my email fiasco: just now getting to read Matthew Reisz on alternatives to the monograph in THE: http://www.timeshighereducation.co.uk/story.asp?sectioncode=26&storycode=416572&c=2 Sat Jul 02 23:46:24 +0000 2011 Replying to @KatharineKLiu It actually works pretty well for me but it takes a little training. Make sure you mark as unimportant stuff that's not, etc. Sun Jul 03 02:28:21 +0000 2011 Little kitty does not like New York thunderstorms. Found her wedged in between the wall and the refrigerator. Sun Jul 03 13:28:37 +0000 2011 Replying to @jaltreuter Boy, wouldn't that be something! Sun Jul 03 13:31:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @GeorgeOnline She seems to be better now that the lightning has stopped. And since she figured out how to get out from next to the fridge. Sun Jul 03 13:34:49 +0000 2011 Replying to @jaltreuter Catnip for everyone! Sun Jul 03 13:39:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @msbonn Happy 4th, Maria! Mon Jul 04 19:09:02 +0000 2011 Reading your completed book manuscript can be exciting! Reading it the twentieth time as you review the proofs... less so. Mon Jul 04 22:22:30 +0000 2011 I've made it to page 121 of 231, and am about to take the rest of the evening off. Thoughts of tomorrow are becoming too distracting. Mon Jul 04 22:23:26 +0000 2011 I'm at work! In a new job! With a new office! It's possible I'm a little excited! Tue Jul 05 12:42:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Blue sauce?! Wow. Tue Jul 05 15:22:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie and @backtype Ooh, that sucks. Wed Jul 06 12:59:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @soleysomma and @rgfeal Thank you! I'm very excited about what's ahead. I'll hope to meet before long as well. Wed Jul 06 17:29:26 +0000 2011 Thanks for all the good wishes yesterday, folks. Surprisingly enough, I'm back in my office again today! Wed Jul 06 17:30:06 +0000 2011 \(And have been since 8am, and this is the first chance I've had to look at the twitters\. Tons of fun here so far\.\) Wed Jul 06 17:30:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall The MLA offices. I'm sure folks on the twitters are having loads of fun, but I've missed it, alas. Wed Jul 06 17:35:19 +0000 2011 Day 2 at the MLA, successfully navigated. Tomorrow? The movers come! There. Will. Be. Furniture. Thu Jul 07 00:32:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself You know, I told someone this afternoon that I can imagine feeling nostalgic for the empty apartment. Someday. Thu Jul 07 00:34:48 +0000 2011 Anyhow, that's the up side. The down side is that I still have 100 pgs of proofs to get through, and am too exhausted to contemplate it now. Thu Jul 07 00:38:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF "I did a bunch of really awesome stuff over the last few years. I've got more awesome stuff to do. Promote me!" Done. Thu Jul 07 00:41:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself I'll say to myself, "ah, those were the salad days." Something like that? Thu Jul 07 00:42:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @wynkenhimself \(I was mostly thinking that the place is about to feel a lot smaller, but I like your version better\.\) Thu Jul 07 00:42:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @surlyF Mwa-ha-ha. Thu Jul 07 00:43:42 +0000 2011 I'm happy I have a little Irish whisky left. But it's reacting unpleasantly with my paper cup. Would it be bad to drink it from the bottle? Thu Jul 07 00:49:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm I always did like you. Thu Jul 07 00:49:50 +0000 2011 Replying to @kikidotca Smart. And thrifty! Thu Jul 07 00:50:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm Precisely what I'm enjoying here. Will be even better when I have glass glasses. Thu Jul 07 00:51:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm I thought I was safe with my paper cup, but it turns out the bottom of the cup is wax-coated. Blech. Thu Jul 07 00:55:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @JenHoward Are you sure it's not because you're in her seat? Thu Jul 07 00:55:53 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm And so it is now being done. Very happy there's no one with a camera here. Thu Jul 07 00:56:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @patrick_mj Ha! I would never have attempted a Dixie cup. This was a higher-end cup that I thought was all paper - but I was so wrong. Thu Jul 07 00:57:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm Oh, they are! I'm sure the honeymoon part will fade, but I'm thrilled so far. Thu Jul 07 00:58:33 +0000 2011 Replying to @patrick_mj You're right. 100%. Thu Jul 07 00:59:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody My family got some lace curtains a couple of generations back, but you can't take the Irish out of the Irish, that's for sure. Thu Jul 07 01:01:33 +0000 2011 PS, for those who might be concerned: we're talking about less than 1/2 inch of whiskey left in the bottle. Neither I nor it will be harmed. Thu Jul 07 01:03:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate You are not kidding. It's lovely on the iPhone, but with a freaking full-size browser, why default to the mobile version? Thu Jul 07 01:04:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @captain_primate No, exactly. The desktop version on my iPad refuses to scroll. Srsly, scrolling! How hard is that? Thu Jul 07 01:07:39 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm and @alienweedman I can think of several worse things. Thu Jul 07 01:08:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @halavais Given that we are now post hoc, I cannot help but agree with you. Thu Jul 07 01:21:09 +0000 2011 I defy one of you to explain to me why Hall & Oates's "Private Eyes" is stuck in my head. Thu Jul 07 01:28:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse You may be on to something there. Thu Jul 07 01:29:11 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse PS: I am hoping we did not just trade. I'll stick with mine, thanks. Thu Jul 07 01:29:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @hollykruse I would say that I could only imagine, but I got curious. That was worth the potential earworm. Thu Jul 07 01:34:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @Cosmosis Aw, man! That's exactly what it was - I don't follow him, but somebody RT'd him. Thu Jul 07 01:34:57 +0000 2011 If you were curious? It's Wil Wheaton's fault: he's been tweeting the lyrics, and somebody RT'd him. DIE, WESLEY CRUSHER, DIE! Thu Jul 07 01:36:05 +0000 2011 Ah, aerobed of my friend Stephanie, I will remember fondly how you rescued me from the floor, but it's time for us to go our separate ways. Thu Jul 07 02:09:40 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Yeah, I have to say, Steph's is nothing like the pool float I bought in 1998. Thu Jul 07 02:19:46 +0000 2011 Replying to @stevebrier Oh, awful. I have a sure-fire cure: one rousing chorus of "Lonesome Loser." Strong enough to drive anything else out, but + Thu Jul 07 13:18:02 +0000 2011 Replying to @stevebrier weirdly, not strong enough to take up residence itself. Thu Jul 07 13:18:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @stevebrier Pretty close. I keep expecting it not to work, and yet it keeps working. Thu Jul 07 13:21:30 +0000 2011 At home this morning, waiting for the movers. Please please let them show up on time. And let them put my tables back together properly. Thu Jul 07 13:22:27 +0000 2011 Yay, movers! RIght on the dot! Thu Jul 07 14:01:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah You said it! Thu Jul 07 14:16:14 +0000 2011 Not exactly a false alarm: the movers are here but are now wrangling with parking. Sigh. Thu Jul 07 14:16:43 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Nice! Thu Jul 07 14:27:10 +0000 2011 Okay, guys. Don't tease me like this. Thu Jul 07 14:34:59 +0000 2011 Phew, that did it. Have seen a mover. Stuff is about to appear. Thu Jul 07 14:38:16 +0000 2011 Replying to @cjprender Thanks! Thu Jul 07 14:41:42 +0000 2011 Awesome to see that my living room rug arrived with all the cat hair still intact. Thu Jul 07 14:45:16 +0000 2011 31 of 114 items, now in the apartment. Mostly boxes, but at least the rugs got down first. Thu Jul 07 15:08:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah Many thanks. The parts box has been found, so I'm hopeful about the rest. Thu Jul 07 15:13:46 +0000 2011 Like a cat, I find myself sitting in each new chair as it arrives. Chairs! Amazing! Thu Jul 07 15:18:15 +0000 2011 Replying to @micahvandegrift Hi! Not yet, alas, but soon. Email me? kfitzpatrick at mla. Thu Jul 07 15:21:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @buridan It's both territoriality and amazement at the invention, after ten days on the floor. Thu Jul 07 15:22:31 +0000 2011 One hour into actual delivery, close to two-thirds done. The tables issue is yet to be faced. Thu Jul 07 15:33:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal They're closed up in the room of adjustment. Would freak them out to much to be in the middle of this. Thu Jul 07 15:34:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Alas, that is not to be. But the room of adjustment is pretty awesome in its own right, if a bit small. Thu Jul 07 15:37:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmaxsfu Heh. I wish that were solvable in one hour. Thu Jul 07 15:47:06 +0000 2011 The putting-together of the tables has begun. Thu Jul 07 17:12:03 +0000 2011 Movers gone. Just enough time to shower and get myself downtown for a 4pm meeting. Whew. Thu Jul 07 18:28:22 +0000 2011 \(PS, for those following at home: the tables mostly came back together fine\. The coffee table isn't right, though, and will take work yet\.\) Thu Jul 07 18:28:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso and @darenchapin Hey thanks! Would love to come but my pal Megan Abbott is having her book release party at the Strand at 7. Next time? Thu Jul 07 20:40:44 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso and @darenchapin \(On Megan: http://www\.meganabbott\.com/\) Thu Jul 07 20:40:55 +0000 2011 Everywhere I turn it looks like this. Don't even know where to begin. http://yfrog.com/kehnesgj Fri Jul 08 00:40:48 +0000 2011 I decided to start with the kitchen. Just grabbed the first box I saw. I *swear* it did not have "wine" written on the outside of it. Fri Jul 08 00:54:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @pgilbert142001 It's called Office2HD - it works well with straightforward formatting, but beware: it eats footnotes, track changes, etc. Sat Jul 09 18:14:25 +0000 2011 Replying to @pgilbert142001 PS: Hi! Sat Jul 09 18:14:34 +0000 2011 Unpacked yesterday from 8am to 10pm nonstop. Crashed at midnight and slept until 10am. Could use another day like that but everything hurts. Sun Jul 10 14:39:09 +0000 2011 Replying to @jwolman Interns! Why didn't I think of that? Unpaid labor operating on the vague promise of future employment... it's just the thing! Sun Jul 10 15:16:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Yes, major progress. And am gradually making more today. One room at a time! Sun Jul 10 16:40:27 +0000 2011 Okay! Project Splice Together Functioning Speaker Wires from Bits Hacked Out of Living Room Shelves, accomplished! Surround sound achieved. Sun Jul 10 20:02:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @alanbenson Point well taken. Operation Sound Future is pretty good, but perhaps too subtle ideologically. Operation Audio Freedom? Sun Jul 10 20:11:37 +0000 2011 Despite the fact that I only got out of bed 11.5 hours ago, I am completely crashing. Another successful day of unpacking. Mon Jul 11 01:22:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @shanakimball Thanks, Shana! Looking forward to seeing you in a few days. Mon Jul 11 02:08:10 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Everybody's a critic. Wed Jul 13 02:12:59 +0000 2011 RT @tmcphers: #sci9 lively + far-ranging bunch o' folks in the room: should be an interesting 2 days Wed Jul 13 18:31:39 +0000 2011 One hope of #sci9 is to establish the necessary conditions, incl infrastructure, for conducting humanities scholarship in the digital realm. Wed Jul 13 18:39:18 +0000 2011 Abby: imagine we are not attached to the institutions we work for, but rather to the *ends* of those institutions. [+] #sci9 Wed Jul 13 18:45:12 +0000 2011 How might we reimagine paths to a goal if we focus on the goals rather than the paths? [-} #sci9 Wed Jul 13 18:45:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @foundhistory Certainly meetings and conferences should be included, in that vein; those will change in the same ways publishing has. #sci9 Wed Jul 13 18:51:16 +0000 2011 How do we re-engineer systems of credit in scholarly production? \(NB: \*not\* just about tenure and promotion\!\) #sci9 Wed Jul 13 19:00:30 +0000 2011 @rgfeal @dancohen His was a direct quote; mine was a paraphrase of a longer thought. Wed Jul 13 20:02:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal and @dancohen I'm less sure of that. Abby's point was that we get so focused on institutions - "the way things have been done" [+] Wed Jul 13 20:19:52 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal and @dancohen that we can't imagine change. If we detach our thinking from those institutions and focus on their ends, what might we do? Wed Jul 13 20:20:31 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal and @dancohen Exactly. "Institutions" understood very broadly. Wed Jul 13 20:27:06 +0000 2011 RT @dancohen: My answer to how to get an audience for new academic pubs/networks: highlight high-quality niche content for scholarly com ... Wed Jul 13 20:37:29 +0000 2011 Replying to @nowviskie Interestingly, that divide isn't always tied to $$, but sometimes to institutional size and type. #sci9 Wed Jul 13 21:13:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @kfitz Absolutely! // RT @nowviskie @kfitz Or even just to priority-setting \(and policy\-setting\) within a given institution. #sci9 Wed Jul 13 21:14:48 +0000 2011 RT @rgfeal: What if you printed books and nobody read them? @RepoRat @JenHoward #sci9 Wed Jul 13 22:01:59 +0000 2011 Fantastic afternoon and evening with the #sci9 folks, leaving me really enthusiastic for tomorrow's unconference sessions! Thu Jul 14 03:09:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody I'm a little behind the times, but this is great news - congrats! But passwordwalled, yes. Do make an account when you have your copy on hand. Fri Jul 15 17:09:05 +0000 2011 Happily seated on the Terrace of Awesomeness. Glad to be home, if only briefly. Sat Jul 16 00:17:41 +0000 2011 Processing the amazing conversations at #sci9 and looking enormously forward to what's next. Thanks for everything, @nowviskie & UVa! Sat Jul 16 00:19:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @miriamkp Oh, no! I hope you're on your way home now... Sat Jul 16 00:22:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @miriamkp Absolutely! Beyond "Dead to Me." Even more awesome would have been not getting the time wrong by an hour. xo, @kfitz Sun Jul 17 20:43:27 +0000 2011 Sooooo not happy with how it looks like this flight is about to go. #economyminusismorelikeit Sun Jul 17 21:31:42 +0000 2011 Okay, so it could be worse; it appears we're going to get in to LA an hour early. So only five hours in the very back of the plane. Sun Jul 17 22:28:07 +0000 2011 Replying to @veek I think it was a time zone thing; the msg listed the flight as departing an hour before sched, but the time on the msg was wrong too. Sun Jul 17 22:39:48 +0000 2011 Replying to @sleonchnm I'm so sorry, Sharon. I'll cross my fingers for her... They're window units but they're not in the windows; they're in the wall below. Wed Jul 20 14:12:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall and @samplereality Hey, where is this DH playlist? I'm only seeing two public playlists for you, neither of which is that one... Thu Jul 21 13:49:45 +0000 2011 Replying to @briancroxall and @samplereality Ah, got it. Thanks! docs.title: 2011 07 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @jmittell Yay! Enjoy! Mon Aug 01 14:23:25 +0000 2011 Packed myself a fantastic lunch last night, including leftovers from last night's fab dinner- and proceeded to leave it in the fridge. Rats. Mon Aug 01 14:39:36 +0000 2011 More information about this November's Berlin9 Open Access Conference is now available: http://www.berlin9.org/news/11-0801.shtml Mon Aug 01 20:45:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal Ah, just wait until you see the awesome Chemex carafe I'll be bringing to the office. I'll be unstoppable. Mon Aug 01 22:01:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal It's a 3-cup, so I suppose I could be persuaded to share. Just more frequent brewing! Mon Aug 01 23:31:31 +0000 2011 Fantastic blog post on obsolescence and nostalgia, jumping off from my first book, The Anxiety of Obsolescence: http://digiauthors.wordpress.com/2011/07/26/obsolescence-and-nostalgia/ Tue Aug 02 12:01:55 +0000 2011 Replying to @mmwwah I've requested @Profhacker update that, but it's taking some time. Tue Aug 02 16:02:42 +0000 2011 Replying to @nancybaym It is very hard for me to imagine that this is the same place that was in single digits at the start of my visit. Tue Aug 02 20:11:28 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen If you're looking for more early humanities bloggers, you might look at my blogroll from June 2003: http://web.archive.org/web/20030802101442/http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/po/ Wed Aug 03 15:47:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen Some familiar folks there... Wed Aug 03 15:48:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @dancohen Blogging completely transformed my sense of scholarly community, esp when I met the Wordherders! HI-LARIOUS. Wed Aug 03 18:27:57 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Eh, it was just wishful thinking on my part. Wed Aug 03 19:26:30 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody I get that, I guess. But it serves TWC, not HBO. I'd pay more for HBO Go by itself than I would for HBO as a cable add-on. Wed Aug 03 19:33:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @tcarmody Heh. Hard to separate them. On the previous convo: TW might in the long run be okay with a no-cable HBO; TWC would certainly not. Wed Aug 03 20:17:32 +0000 2011 Am enjoying my first cup of made-in-the-office Chemex coffee. Holy moly, I may never drink anything else. Thu Aug 04 14:04:39 +0000 2011 Oh no: Just saw a report saying Etta James has passed away. An enormous loss. Thu Aug 04 15:22:26 +0000 2011 Just finished revising a short piece for a certain higher ed publication. Prepare to have your swashes buckled - and by a girl, no less! Thu Aug 04 15:30:06 +0000 2011 Replying to @kathiiberens What a heartbreaking version. I got to see her play Jazz Fest in 2006; she was just amazing. Thu Aug 04 15:34:38 +0000 2011 Replying to @escapegrace Oh, thank goodness! Thu Aug 04 15:40:51 +0000 2011 A hoax like this is enormously depressing. Particularly given the more than sufficient sadness of the actual situation. http://www.nme.com/news/etta-james/58450 Thu Aug 04 15:43:26 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmaxsfu True. Though one does feel scientific when making coffee with it! If only I had a Bunsen burner... Thu Aug 04 15:55:13 +0000 2011 Replying to @jmaxsfu Oh, no. Surely not. Just gadgetyness. Thu Aug 04 16:00:15 +0000 2011 I had to get a tetanus/whooping cough booster shot yesterday, and today it feels like my shoulder has been punched from the inside. Thu Aug 04 16:05:20 +0000 2011 Replying to @eetempleton It *was* vaguely obscene; she was raunchy in the best possible way. An amazing performance. Thu Aug 04 17:51:14 +0000 2011 Replying to @mattthomas As I already noted. But thanks. Thu Aug 04 17:52:06 +0000 2011 I am suddenly NOT HAVING A GOOD DAY AT ALL. Cooperate with me, world. #sheesh Thu Aug 04 18:22:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @tmcphers Thank you! PS: if you know someone looking for an awesome rental about 35 miles east of you, let me know, okay? Thu Aug 04 18:25:00 +0000 2011 Replying to @plragde INDEED. Thu Aug 04 18:35:22 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso That is not the worst idea anyone has suggested to me. Any book in particular this evening? Thu Aug 04 19:37:05 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso Excellent! What time will you be there? Thu Aug 04 19:46:41 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso and @amandafrench Hey, now there's an agenda I can get behind! Thu Aug 04 19:50:18 +0000 2011 Replying to @Ed_Raso Great. I'll try to make it by unless I've already pulled the covers over my head by that point. Thu Aug 04 19:55:56 +0000 2011 I really, really hate the degree to which one craptastic event can utterly undo an otherwise rockin' day. Thu Aug 04 22:08:58 +0000 2011 Replying to @rgfeal imwtty :) Thu Aug 04 22:12:09 +0000 2011 Perfect weather for a drink on the terrace, though. Mood improving already! Thu Aug 04 22:20:56 +0000 2011 Replying to @patrick_mj Srsly. Exactly the right kind of overcast. Low humidity. 80 degrees. What could be better?
