docs.title: 2012 08 01 tweets

docs.text: A bleg that’s way too long for Twitter; help me, lazy web! New post: Multiple Calendar Query. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/multiple-calendar-query/ Sat Sep 01 14:37:16 +0000 2012 Replying to @tcarmody I think the switch happened this year. I haven’t heard anyone say “two thousand twelve” lately. Twenty-twelve, twenty-thirteen. Sat Sep 01 20:57:42 +0000 2012 Well, shoot. I finished the talk, which is meant to be 18 minutes. It is now 13.5 minutes. At least I can revise longer instead of cutting. Sun Sep 02 14:00:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @dandyroll Pause. Sip water. Boy, that’s a nice image, isn’t it? Sip water. As I was saying... Sun Sep 02 16:16:20 +0000 2012 Okay, it’s now somewhere in the 16.5 minute vicinity. I think I can stick a fork in this talk. On to the rest of the to-do list. Sun Sep 02 16:48:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Hmm. Would you leave feedback or a query in the alpha testing forum? Thx. Mon Sep 03 18:45:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Ok. If the query went to our developer, he should respond tomorrow. \(We're closed today\.\) Mon Sep 03 19:03:35 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline No, I think it's a setting issue. Should be a fairly easy fix. Mon Sep 03 19:16:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline This is on our agenda but we haven’t gotten far with it yet. I’d love it if you’d start a forum discussion about it there. Mon Sep 03 19:35:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @jwolman Sadly, I will not be there. Next time, I hope! \(Also, I have a DC trip or two coming up\.\) Tue Sep 04 14:15:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @jwolman Will do! Tue Sep 04 14:22:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @nancybaym That sounds like the pharma hack. It's at the database level, and affects metadata but not direct content. It's super pernicious. Tue Sep 04 14:48:47 +0000 2012 Replying to @nancybaym And very hard to get rid of. Google pharmahack - I would up having to rebuild from the ground up to get rid of it. Tue Sep 04 14:49:27 +0000 2012 Replying to @uheise Congratulations, Ursula! I'm sorry I'm not in SoCal to welcome you. Tue Sep 04 15:39:13 +0000 2012 A spectacular day to arrive back in NYC, after a spectacular trip \(which I hope to be able to write more about very soon\) . Mon Sep 10 21:37:04 +0000 2012 PS: If you sent me a message in the last week, I'll try to answer it tomorrow. Mon Sep 10 21:38:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott Thanks! I hope we can get together soon - I want to hear about your tour! Mon Sep 10 21:40:14 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal Many thanks; I will need done intervention to get over the jet lag fast! More tales soon. Mon Sep 10 21:42:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal That's divine intervention. Sigh. Mon Sep 10 21:43:16 +0000 2012 Dear beloved cats: As there seems to be some confusion: peeing *on* the litter box is not the same thing as peeing *in* the litter box. xo k Mon Sep 10 23:41:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @Ed_Raso I'm a "close the lid" person - equal inconvenience for all! Mon Sep 10 23:47:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @amandafrench and @rgfeal We don’t yet have a timetable, but are working as quickly as we can. More as soon as possible! Tue Sep 11 17:23:42 +0000 2012 There was something I meant to do today… What was it… \(snap\) Oh, right! Eat! *sigh* Tue Sep 11 20:07:08 +0000 2012 New post, for a new academic year: Making Room. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/making-room/ Wed Sep 12 13:57:46 +0000 2012 RT @THATCampMLA: We are now accepting applications for #THATCAMP MLA. Apply here http://mla2013.thatcamp.org/apply/ Wed Sep 12 15:32:36 +0000 2012 Me too! // RT @CameronNeylon: Very proud to have been part of process that led to BOAI10 recommendations on #openaccess http://www.soros.org/press-releases/scientists-foundations-libraries-universities-and-advocates-unite-and-issue-new Wed Sep 12 15:33:17 +0000 2012 RT @hjoseph: OA Advocates set ambitious new goal with the BOAI10: #openaccess in 10 years. http://tinyurl.com/9gtblbo Wed Sep 12 15:33:27 +0000 2012 Replying to @nowviskie Safe travels, Bethany! Wed Sep 12 15:34:06 +0000 2012 I’ll consider it a big update if the new iTunes stops automatically playing every time I plug in an iOS device connected to my wifi network. Wed Sep 12 18:08:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @academicdave Hey, good luck with that. \(sigh\) Wed Sep 12 18:10:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella There is a switch to prevent iTunes from launching when you plug in a device. But mine doesn’t just launch - it starts playing.+ Wed Sep 12 18:21:13 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella Which it’s not supposed to do at all. Makes me crazy. There’s something goofed up in my network, I think. Wed Sep 12 18:21:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella Truly. I plug my iPhone into a bedside dock at night \(just plugged into the wall\) & this morning got up to find that + Wed Sep 12 18:30:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella my comp had been playing music all night. It was far enough into my library that it had to be caused by plugging in the iPhone. Wed Sep 12 18:32:32 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella I know, right? I’ve looked everywhere and have seen no evidence that anyone but me has this issue. I’m taking it personally. Wed Sep 12 18:39:29 +0000 2012 Okay, Twitters, I need opinions: Flickr vs Picasa. Go. Thu Sep 13 13:03:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @boone When I moved to NY for grad school I was horrified to discover that “regular” meant milk & sugar, rather than ALL THE CAFFEINE, THX. Thu Sep 13 13:07:44 +0000 2012 Replying to @jwolman I let my Flickr Pro account go a while back, but have a set of photos I want to share with a few folks. Hey, maybe G+ will do. Thu Sep 13 13:12:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @jwolman Makes sense. I’ll give it a try and see how it feels. Thu Sep 13 13:14:13 +0000 2012 Replying to @academicdave and @boone Srsly. The other is hot coffee ice cream. A totally different thing. Thu Sep 13 13:22:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava Ah, I need a little more controlled access, at least at first. Still pondering the best balance! Thu Sep 13 13:26:03 +0000 2012 Replying to @sherah1918 So far, that’s three votes for Flickr, but the tide is running heavily against coffee with milk. :) Thu Sep 13 13:27:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Reasons? Thu Sep 13 13:28:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Okay, that’s helpful, thanks. Votes thus far are running 4-to-1 in Flickr’s favor, but my sample may be a bit skewed. Thu Sep 13 13:33:16 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Yeah, & G+ has pretty finely-tuned privacy/sharing settings. Not sure it’s as CC-friendly as Flickr, tho. Will keep pondering. Thu Sep 13 13:36:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni I’m not surprised you’re not a Picasa user, except for its Google integration... Thu Sep 13 13:37:40 +0000 2012 Replying to @JenHoward Tricky indeed. I haven’t been good at striking a balance between the cool stuff I’m asked to do and the big picture things. Thu Sep 13 13:38:59 +0000 2012 Replying to @JenHoward Also, thanks for the link! Thu Sep 13 13:39:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @samplereality That’s interesting. Last time I looked at the Mac Picasa client it rubbed me totally the wrong way. Can’t remember why. Thu Sep 13 13:40:18 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni Heh. Thu Sep 13 13:40:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @john_overholt Good to know - thanks! Thu Sep 13 13:41:25 +0000 2012 Replying to @JenHoward Absolutely. Especially when the group effort is not just urgent but important, too. Thu Sep 13 13:45:20 +0000 2012 Go, @unsworth! http://www.whitehouse.gov/the-press-office/2012/09/12/president-obama-announces-more-key-administration-posts Thu Sep 13 13:46:17 +0000 2012 Replying to @Exhaust_Fumes That’s a very good point. Thanks! Thu Sep 13 13:51:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @timlockridge Ooh, interesting. I’ll have to poke around there. Thu Sep 13 13:53:08 +0000 2012 New blog post: The Shape of Thought. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/the-shape-of-thought/ Thu Sep 13 14:00:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @readywriting It is indeed. We’re progressively trained to write articles & books from the youngest age; it’s hard to imagine other shapes. Thu Sep 13 14:03:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @literarychica Thank you so much! Thu Sep 13 15:54:13 +0000 2012 ROCK. ON. RT @boone: Currently testing the first really-working version of Commons In A Box, and it Kicks. Ass. #cbox /cc @ray_i_am Thu Sep 13 16:28:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 Yay, welcome! Fri Sep 14 00:07:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside and @LLCjournal Doug, you can only make this decision for yourself, of course, but there are two key things to know: + Fri Sep 14 16:20:35 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside and @LLCjournal \(1\) as Edward notes, LLC is what provides the income that keeps all of ADHO and its constituent orbs running, and + Fri Sep 14 16:21:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside and @LLCjournal \(2\) the ADHO pubs cte will be working on initiatives related to open access over the course of this year. Fri Sep 14 16:21:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside and @LLCjournal \(Constituent orbs, that is\. Sigh\.\) Fri Sep 14 16:22:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside and @LLCjournal \(ORGs\! Stupid autocorrect\.\) Fri Sep 14 16:22:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside I hear you; the pubs committee will certainly be talking about that. Fri Sep 14 16:32:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @dougreside It’s a tough decision, but I hope that you will support the orgs, and help us figure out how to improve our policies. Fri Sep 14 16:33:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @micahvandegrift A great list! I would add http://www.insidehighered.com/blogs/library-babel-fish from @bfister, certainly. I’ll keep pondering. Fri Sep 14 17:09:26 +0000 2012 The first crowd-funded ebook release from unglue.it, Oral Literature in Africa, is now up at Open Book Publishers! http://www.openbookpublishers.com/product/97/oral-literature-in-africa/d44bfd60764217b43c6a31d769e0a6a9 Sat Sep 15 14:27:58 +0000 2012 After two nights in a row of next to no sleep, having gotten 9 hours last night feels positively DREAMY. They were the right 9 hours, too! Sat Sep 15 14:30:00 +0000 2012 Finally beginning to feel as though I’m on something approaching the right time zone. Sat Sep 15 14:30:18 +0000 2012 Replying to @phdaisy Good for you, right backatcha. I hope you get caught up! Sat Sep 15 14:34:31 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal Ooh, nice. Sat Sep 15 16:03:18 +0000 2012 RT @studentactivism: You've got to read this. CUNY is dismantling an English department in retaliation for a faculty vote. http://t.co/R ... Sat Sep 15 18:37:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @LizClausen Good god -- be safe! Mon Sep 17 17:01:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @LizClausen Quite well! Thanks for the update; I hope all continues as safely. Mon Sep 17 17:36:15 +0000 2012 New post: Public Responsibility, Public Access. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/public-responsibility-public-access/ Mon Sep 17 18:55:06 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself I hope your new year is very peaceful and joyous, indeed! Mon Sep 17 19:16:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @foundhistory Yay! Congratulations! Tue Sep 18 13:51:41 +0000 2012 At the Institute for Computer Policy and Law for the next couple of days. Getting started with introductions. http://www.sce.cornell.edu/exec/programs.php?v=CPL&s=Overview Wed Sep 19 16:35:06 +0000 2012 Rumor has it that my #CUicpl keynote at 1.30 today is going to be live streamed. http://cit.webcast.video.cornell.edu/Mediasite/Play/dc09a9fb8739459bb75d44ba6a2a1b6c1d Wed Sep 19 16:48:14 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat The Humanities in and for the Digital Age. Will you be at DLF in Nov? Wed Sep 19 16:51:38 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat I am, indeed. You may hear a very similar talk then. :) Wed Sep 19 16:56:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat Of course, that we will finally be in the same room may bring on the singularity. But I guess that’s a risk we’ll have to take. Wed Sep 19 16:57:12 +0000 2012 Absolutely thrilled to announce that I’ll be keynoting the DLF Forum in November! http://www.diglib.org/archives/3642/ Thu Sep 20 15:27:14 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself Hey, thanks! That’s a whole lot of MLA Bibliographies behind me, which I just love. Thu Sep 20 15:51:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @shanakimball I’m sorry you won’t be there, too, but thanks! Thu Sep 20 15:51:37 +0000 2012 Replying to @barbarahui YAY!!! Thu Sep 20 17:17:58 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 Thank you! I hope you’re settling in well... Thu Sep 20 17:27:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @amandafrench Excellent! I’ll look forward to actual in-person catching up. Thu Sep 20 17:33:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @cgbrooke Rock. On! I will very much look forward to hearing more. Thu Sep 20 20:52:24 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself, @sleonchnm, @sherah1918 and @jcmeloni If you can make it a Wednesday, I'll be in DC Oct 10... Fri Sep 21 23:06:07 +0000 2012 Replying to @sleonchnm, @wynkenhimself, @sherah1918 and @jcmeloni Excelllent! Fri Sep 21 23:14:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni, @sleonchnm, @wynkenhimself and @sherah1918 I'm on a panel that goes til 5.30 \(at Renaissance Mayflower\) but will rush over right after. Fri Sep 21 23:19:44 +0000 2012 Replying to @briancroxall Thanks, Brian! I'm pretty excited about it. Sat Sep 22 19:27:25 +0000 2012 Replying to @BillTorg I hope you can make it! Mon Sep 24 12:41:25 +0000 2012 Replying to @s2ceball Actually, Interfolio is waiving many of those charges for JIL users. http://www.mla.org/interfolio_seekers_faq Mon Sep 24 14:49:47 +0000 2012 Spent a fantastic afternoon talking about the future of authorship out at St. John’s today. Thanks for the hospitality, @aegeller! Mon Sep 24 22:29:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @aegeller Ha! Keeping up with your conferences will be well worth it. Thank you for today - and more soon. Mon Sep 24 22:30:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @aegeller You are way more than welcome. I thoroughly enjoyed it, and will look forward to getting together again at a less pressured time. Mon Sep 24 22:33:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @charlottefrost Yep! What's the passage? Tue Sep 25 11:18:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @charlottefrost That's all on page 2. Tue Sep 25 11:28:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @charlottefrost You're welcome! I'm thinking this convo may need to be part of my next talk on the future of authorship... Tue Sep 25 11:31:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal and @brendabethman Indeed! Let’s make a point of it. Tue Sep 25 19:05:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @brendabethman and @rgfeal Hooray! Tue Sep 25 19:07:11 +0000 2012 Replying to @literarychica, @brendabethman and @rgfeal MLA hearts you back! Tue Sep 25 19:14:49 +0000 2012 RT @kepitcher: Starting to read @kfitz #plannedobsolescence book and already impressed, pleased at how many librarians involved, consul ... Tue Sep 25 19:14:56 +0000 2012 Replying to @kepitcher Thank you so much for that tweet. I’m so glad to hear you’re enjoying the book thus far! Tue Sep 25 19:15:20 +0000 2012 RT @MLAconvention: Hey, #MLA members who know Boston food scene: can you do for #mla13 what @jenifer_ward did for #mla12? It was awesome ... Tue Sep 25 22:27:22 +0000 2012 Replying to @karikraus Yay! I hope it goes well. Wed Sep 26 00:41:45 +0000 2012 New blog post \(and a plea to myself\) : Slow. Down. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/slow-down/ Wed Sep 26 13:31:09 +0000 2012 Replying to @plragde Sigh. I think it’s the mixed blessing of living on an academic calendar; three months seems like a long time, until it’s not. Wed Sep 26 13:37:58 +0000 2012 Replying to @atrubek As a prof in grad school advised a friend of mine whose first baby had just been born, "don't buy into the culture of need." Fri Sep 28 18:36:17 +0000 2012 Replying to @atrubek Ah, crud. Blew the quote. It was "economy of need." Fri Sep 28 18:37:00 +0000 2012 60-ish year old African American man on the subway reading Rick Springfield's memoir. I would love to know the back story. Sat Sep 29 16:00:21 +0000 2012 @wynkenhimself It made it all the worse when they renamed the Bullets, because *that* was apparently presenting a bad image of the city. Sun Sep 30 16:53:44 +0000 2012
docs.title: 2012 09 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @adelinekoh This is a bit too complex for 140, I think. I’m going to ponder and post something longer... Mon Oct 01 12:06:53 +0000 2012 At @adelinekoh’s request: Advice on Academic Blogging, Tweeting, Whatever. New post: http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/advice-on-academic-blogging-tweeting-whatever/ Mon Oct 01 14:39:25 +0000 2012 RT @MLAnews: MLA Commons to Launch at #mla13 in Boston! http://www.mla.org/news_from_mla/news_topic&topic=671 Mon Oct 01 14:51:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @surlyF Hey, excellent. I’m only at ARL briefly, but I’ll be at DLF for the duration. I’ll look forward to seeing you! Tue Oct 02 01:33:26 +0000 2012 I’ll retweet this tomorrow for the morning crowd, but in the meantime: come work with me! http://www.mediabistro.com/joblistings/jobview.asp?joid=140583&page=1 or https://secure.interfolio.com/apply/16924 Tue Oct 02 01:42:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @finnarne, @patriksv and @UmeaUniversity I’m sorry I missed this before -- congratulations, Patrik! Tue Oct 02 02:17:46 +0000 2012 Hey, everybody who has posted great things about our awesome community facilitator job at the #MLA: apply, apply, apply! Tue Oct 02 02:26:00 +0000 2012 Had a fabulous time last night in @mkgold’s #dhdebates12 class! Thanks for the hospitality, all. Tue Oct 02 13:18:47 +0000 2012 Okay, srsly? Where in this post do I suggest that I want to “control what people write on the internet”? http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/advice-on-academic-blogging-tweeting-whatever/ Tue Oct 02 13:46:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat This dude: http://scientopia.org/blogs/proflikesubstance/2012/10/02/english-profs-want-to-control-the-internet/ Tue Oct 02 13:48:11 +0000 2012 Replying to @noelrk Mwah-ha-ha. Tue Oct 02 13:58:32 +0000 2012 Replying to @wpwend42 Why, thank you! Tue Oct 02 13:58:41 +0000 2012 Looking for an energetic, engaging community facilitator for the #MLA. Come work with me! http://www.mediabistro.com/joblistings/jobview.asp?joid=140583&page=1 or https://secure.interfolio.com/apply/16924 Tue Oct 02 17:23:33 +0000 2012 Heading to Vancouver tmw to speak at UBC on Thursday. Then on to UVic on Friday. If you’re in the area come say hi! http://blogs.ubc.ca/bcrlglectures/2012/09/17/planned-obsolescence-publishing-technology-and-the-future-of-the-academy/ Wed Oct 03 03:34:41 +0000 2012 RT @TAC_NISO: Modern Language Assn to launch MLA Commons, new scholarly comm platform, at 2013 MLA conf @kfitz in @LibraryJournal http:/ ... Wed Oct 03 18:06:04 +0000 2012 The good news about the crazy long immigration line I've been in for the last half hour is that the free wifi has let me catch up on email. Wed Oct 03 18:30:27 +0000 2012 The bad news is that I still have three lengths to go. Wed Oct 03 18:30:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @briancroxall I think this crowd could bust out in a solid "Thriller" any time. Wed Oct 03 18:34:08 +0000 2012 I swear I'm not exaggerating: THOUSANDS of people in the non-CA residents line. ONE agent. ONE. Wed Oct 03 18:40:17 +0000 2012 Replying to @ayjay This wont be that bad, but in definitely looking at a solid 1.5hrs. Wed Oct 03 18:45:12 +0000 2012 Hey! A second agent! Wed Oct 03 18:45:33 +0000 2012 Replying to @ayjay Woo-hoo! Wed Oct 03 18:49:11 +0000 2012 Replying to @ayjay Ooh, excellent. I'm on my own for the rest of the day today, so ill check it out! Wed Oct 03 18:50:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @cryptopix I know! Not only was everyone using mobile devices in an unmolested fashion, but there was free wifi. Made it bearable. Wed Oct 03 20:06:04 +0000 2012 ICYMI: Library Journal reports on the forthcoming launch of MLA Commons. http://lj.libraryjournal.com/2012/10/organizations/mla-to-launch-scholarly-communications-platform/ #MLA13 Wed Oct 03 20:07:07 +0000 2012 RT @JennyHolzerMom: POLITE DEBATE IS A GOOD WAY TO KEEP YOURSELF BUSY WHILE THE WORLD CRUMBLES; ALSO, FACEBOOK Wed Oct 03 20:17:32 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal I’m even in the hotel. But for real, it was literally 1.5 hours in that line. Craziness. Wed Oct 03 20:29:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal That, I cannot say. Our flight was an hour late and got in after \(instead of before\) two huge flights from Asia. So I dunno. Wed Oct 03 20:30:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal Weather is crazy beautiful. Apparently something of a drought right now; 8mm rain since August, instead of the usual 200+. Wed Oct 03 20:31:31 +0000 2012 Apparently I am having notability issues \(snif\) . http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kathleen_Fitzpatrick_ \(academic\) Wed Oct 03 21:35:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat Well, no wonder then. Wed Oct 03 21:36:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat I suppose there is such a thing as too much notability. Wed Oct 03 21:37:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Yeah, it’s at least 3 years out of date -- pre-promotion, apparently even before my move wholly into media studies. Wed Oct 03 21:50:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @jmittell I apparently rely on primary sources. I’m quite sure there’s a pharmaceutical in development for that, though. Wed Oct 03 21:57:38 +0000 2012 Replying to @TAC_NISO I’m afraid I’m in Vancouver/Victoria thru Saturday. Can we chat later in the weekend, or early next week? Thu Oct 04 00:56:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @TAC_NISO Sounds great. Let me know what works for you. Thu Oct 04 01:03:26 +0000 2012 Very much looking forward to participating in this joint MLA/LSA session on open access! http://idiom.ucsd.edu/~ebakovic/LSA-OA/ #mla13 Thu Oct 04 12:51:07 +0000 2012 RT @MLAconvention: Spread the word on your campus: #MLA13 travel grants for grads, un-/underemployed, and residing outside US/Canada: ht ... Thu Oct 04 14:17:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat Eduroam! I have been so jealous so often of folks from Eduroam institutions! Fri Oct 05 01:20:25 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni Yay! I didn’t know you were going to be there! Fri Oct 05 01:27:09 +0000 2012 Replying to @sherah1918 This sounds just like my life, except that the thing due on the 15th isn’t done either. Fri Oct 05 03:36:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @joguldi Backblaze for cloud backups, plus Carbon Copy Cloner for local bootable backups. Plus Dropbox. Lots of copies, you know. Fri Oct 05 15:03:37 +0000 2012 Nearing the end of a fabulous trip to UBC and UVic. Thanks to everybody here for their hospitality! Sat Oct 06 01:56:51 +0000 2012 I bought myself a little present in Victoria today. Sat Oct 06 02:47:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava I couldn’t help myself! Sat Oct 06 02:50:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @samplereality and @adelinekoh It reminds me of the researcher’s headgear from Vannevar Bush’s “As We May Think.” Sat Oct 06 16:51:47 +0000 2012 Replying to @jmaxsfu No, the BC Libraries group brought me in; I gave a talk Thursday at UBC and Friday at UVic. So sorry to have missed you, too! Sat Oct 06 19:21:37 +0000 2012 Escalator out. Missed the train by 10 seconds. Next train is running 30 minutes late. SUPER HAPPY. Sun Oct 07 03:06:48 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal and @brettbobley It's a nice night to sit on a deserted train platform. Especially knowing there's still a bus to take after the train. Sun Oct 07 03:18:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @brettbobley Thank you; I could use it. This will have been a long day, should it ever manage to end. Sun Oct 07 03:24:22 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal and @brettbobley The Newark Liberty Airport rail station. Really really happy I didn't spring for the cab right now. Sun Oct 07 03:35:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal and @bendprof Thanks for concern! The train should be here in 3 mins now & trying to get a cab here would be worse in wait & scariness. Sun Oct 07 03:44:28 +0000 2012 Replying to @BendProf and @rgfeal Will do, definitely. Oh, also: I'm on a train! Sun Oct 07 03:52:15 +0000 2012 Home. Sun Oct 07 04:39:27 +0000 2012 Replying to @brettbobley Very happy that you guys did not have to bust out the helicopter, or the laser-equipped sharks. Sun Oct 07 04:42:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @literarychica I’m sorry I missed you, but I hope the visit was great! I’m sure we’ll cross paths in Boston. Sun Oct 07 14:21:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @literarychica Excellent! Sun Oct 07 14:23:13 +0000 2012 I’d be far prouder of myself for finishing the big PITA task I just checked off my list if it hadn’t actually been due several weeks ago. Mon Oct 08 18:04:11 +0000 2012 \(And I’ll just say, so much for a day off\. Yes, I am feeling aggrieved\. Why do you ask?\) Mon Oct 08 18:04:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah Eh, had a bunch of errands to go run. Am celebrating the last fifteen minutes of this day "off," tho, on my slightly chilly terrace. Mon Oct 08 21:09:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @folgerresearch Also? Ew. Mon Oct 08 21:30:04 +0000 2012 I have spent much of the evening being slobbered on by one of my cats. It's a good thing he's pretty. Tue Oct 09 01:08:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @tcarmody He's a good cat except for the drool. And the predictable plot twists. Tue Oct 09 01:10:24 +0000 2012 Replying to @kathleenreed Thanks! It was great getting to talk with you; thanks for making the drive down! Tue Oct 09 10:32:10 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah it's a relatively recent thing, and it's totally love slobber. I love that cat, but sheesh. Tue Oct 09 10:33:12 +0000 2012 New post, at Planned Obsolescence: Out of the Habit. http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/out-of-the-habit/ Tue Oct 09 13:30:15 +0000 2012 RT @NYUpress: PLANNED OBSOLESCENCE "highly recommended" in portal: Libraries and the Academy. See full review here: http://bit.ly/PlTFNa ... Tue Oct 09 18:55:59 +0000 2012 Replying to @sherah1918, @wynkenhimself, @jcmeloni, @sleonchnm and @sheepeeh 2 hrs late, but heck yeah I'm there. Done with my session at 5.30 & 2 blocks away. Wed Oct 10 00:06:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal Sorry it took me so long to get your comment out of moderation! It’s live now -- and thanks! Wed Oct 10 00:26:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal Yeah, I resemble that remark. I hope all’s well on your end of the world! Wed Oct 10 00:37:34 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal Wow, I can’t imagine what that must feel like. \(Kidding\!\) Wed Oct 10 00:40:15 +0000 2012 Oh, Amtrak wifi, why must you so taunt me with the illusion of connectivity? Wed Oct 10 14:52:29 +0000 2012 It's a gorgeous day in DC - a good one for a little conversation about scholarly communication \(and then a meetup with some local pals\) ! Wed Oct 10 17:06:26 +0000 2012 Replying to @BendProf and @rgfeal Will join the call as soon as I get to my hotel! Wed Oct 10 17:07:27 +0000 2012 Replying to @ernestopriego Burritos are the pizza of the 21st century. \(Which is to say, ubiquitous, and way harder to do right than people think\.\) Wed Oct 10 18:32:30 +0000 2012 Replying to @sleonchnm, @JenServenti, @jcmeloni, @sheepeeh and @sherah1918 A very peaceful domination, yes. You’ll love it. Thu Oct 11 03:59:55 +0000 2012 Crud. Teeny tiny little migraine aura. So completely not okay with an overnight migraine, even if its a teeny tiny one. Fri Oct 12 03:05:51 +0000 2012 Thanks for the good wishes last night, all. The headache never materialized, happily enough, but the hangover this morning did. Fri Oct 12 12:42:56 +0000 2012 I’ve never had a purely ocular migraine before; my visual disturbances have always been followed by the headache. So this is weird. Fri Oct 12 12:43:37 +0000 2012 I’m particularly surprised at how rattled I feel this morning despite the absence of the headache. Very weird. Fri Oct 12 12:45:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @CapitolClio Thank you! I find myself sitting here wondering if my computer screen has always been this glaringly bright... Fri Oct 12 12:47:46 +0000 2012 Replying to @parezcoydigo Indeed. Fri Oct 12 12:57:32 +0000 2012 Oh god I just got an email in which “webinar” is used as a verb. THIS IS NOT HELPING MY HEAD. Fri Oct 12 12:58:46 +0000 2012 I kid you not. “If you would like to webinar in…” LEFT SIDE OF BRAIN EXPLODES. Fri Oct 12 13:00:35 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself I knew what you meant -- and thanks! Fri Oct 12 13:23:38 +0000 2012 Replying to @lynnegoldstein They’re very freaky, not least because I associate that visual disturbance with the pounding to come. Fri Oct 12 13:24:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah Yeah, I’ll take it, definitely. Fri Oct 12 13:40:05 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal Bizarre, yeah? I still feel today as though I had the headache, even though I didn’t. Fri Oct 12 15:49:21 +0000 2012 After 9 hours of sleep, I'm finally feeling a bit more human. Here's hoping it lasts! Sat Oct 13 12:42:15 +0000 2012 RT @zunguzungu: New blog post. "Creepshots and the Self-Fulfilling Prophecy of Free Speech": http://thenewinquiry.com/blogs/zunguzungu/creepshots-and-the-self-fulfilling-prophecy-of-free-speech/ Sat Oct 13 19:35:14 +0000 2012 Exercising my powers of NO, if somewhat sadly and reluctantly. Sun Oct 14 21:58:13 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat The laws of time and space often do not permit anything otherwise. Sun Oct 14 22:04:46 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat More or less what I’ve done! Sun Oct 14 22:06:36 +0000 2012 Oh, the satisfaction of *both* being able to check two items off the to-do-list *and* get three messages out of the inbox in one fell swoop. Sun Oct 14 22:40:45 +0000 2012 Replying to @sleonchnm Every once in a while it works! Sun Oct 14 22:58:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Yes. Mon Oct 15 01:43:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Precisely. I'm sorry for the aggravation, but happy for the lack of aggravation! Mon Oct 15 02:28:38 +0000 2012 Getting my inboxes & to-do lists under control no longer produces relief but instead the mildly panicky sense that I’m forgetting something. Mon Oct 15 13:48:47 +0000 2012 Just leaving the ITHAKA Sustainable Scholarship meeting. Right next door, same hotel space, a big Romney fundraiser. It's... I'm... Mon Oct 15 22:48:29 +0000 2012 And it's pouring and I am umbrellaless. Maybe I won't leave after all. Mon Oct 15 22:52:14 +0000 2012 Have $5 street umbrella, will get the heck away from the crazy rich people. Mon Oct 15 22:56:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @gluejar They were! That was the creepiest lobby scene I’ve ever seen. Hundreds of identical androids waiting for coat check. Mon Oct 15 23:43:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @katinalynn So far, so good! I’m up in the morning; hopefully the crazy will have moved on. Mon Oct 15 23:44:18 +0000 2012 Rats rats rats. Finished tomorrow’s talk this morning and was very happy with it. Just read through it: 7 mins, or 25%, too short. Tue Oct 16 00:20:02 +0000 2012 Finding it a little difficult to admit how much less unpleasant the walk to Grand Central is at 7.15 rather than 7.50. Tue Oct 16 11:35:55 +0000 2012 Two small overdue pieces of writing are left on my list - and then it's all new projects, I hereby promise myself. Tue Oct 16 11:43:37 +0000 2012 Me too. // RT @rschon: I am attending ITHAKA's Sustainable Scholarship conference today and may be tweeting occasionally from it. #iss2012 Tue Oct 16 13:14:45 +0000 2012 Replying to @gluejar One of the core reasons why we advocate for non-anonymous processes, but we acknowledge that isn’t a universal value. Tue Oct 16 14:16:47 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself That right there is the line for me between sanity and madness. I cannot handle off-the-screen spillover. Tue Oct 16 16:17:27 +0000 2012 The temperature in this room has gone right past cold-enough-to-keep-me-alert to cold-enough-to-induce-hibernation. #iss2012 Tue Oct 16 16:22:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @IthakaSR Um, thanks? Tue Oct 16 16:25:22 +0000 2012 Okay, the cold is now well past hibernation-inducing and into making-me-want-to-cry territory. Fingers going numb. Brain function slowing. Tue Oct 16 17:02:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @Ted_Underwood Grrr. Arrgh. Tue Oct 16 18:08:34 +0000 2012 Tried to watch. Managed about 45 seconds before blood pressure went through the roof. Let me know how it all turns out. Wed Oct 17 01:23:11 +0000 2012 Not incidentally, I voted on Sunday. Wed Oct 17 01:24:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @catherinemitch Phew. Wed Oct 17 01:47:43 +0000 2012 The annual list of DH sessions at #mla13, created by @samplereality: http://bit.ly/U3EclZ [+] Wed Oct 17 19:03:15 +0000 2012 Last year, we made a list of such lists: http://www.mla.org/yourconvention. Create a pathway through the program for your field, and send us the link! Wed Oct 17 19:04:09 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself Excellent; thanks! Wed Oct 17 19:07:27 +0000 2012 Hey #mla13: We want convention guides \(like @samplereality’s http://www\.samplereality\.com/2012/10/17/digital\-humanities\-at\-mla\-2013/\) . How will you navigate the program? http://www.mla.org/yourconvention Thu Oct 18 14:10:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @CUNYRomantics Fantastic - thanks! Thu Oct 18 15:00:56 +0000 2012 RT @CUNYRomantics: #MLA13 for Romanticists http://romanticism.commons.gc.cuny.edu/2012/10/17/romantics-at-the-2013-mla-convention/ Thu Oct 18 15:01:29 +0000 2012 Are you participating in an amazing session at #mla13? Want to drum up interest? We’re gathering pre-convention blog posts; send links! Thu Oct 18 15:20:37 +0000 2012 RT @ChrisParrTHE: Were you involved in #Twittergate? @kfitz's blog on the ethics of live tweeting is this week's scholarly web: http://t ... Thu Oct 18 15:55:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @Sarah_M_Ford Obligatory tweet about deep sadness resulting from not being at #ir13. Thu Oct 18 15:57:06 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni Missiles missiles missiles. Thu Oct 18 18:45:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @pbenzon, @loriemerson, @zachwhalen and @samplereality Excellent; thank you! Thu Oct 18 20:39:05 +0000 2012 The rain is not making it easier to get moving this morning. Fri Oct 19 10:25:27 +0000 2012 Replying to @pbenzon Thanks! We’ll post a link once we’ve gotten the collection built. Fri Oct 19 13:21:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @jsanofranchini Fantastic; thanks! Fri Oct 19 17:28:56 +0000 2012 Replying to @nowviskie and @trouts Ditto what @rgfeal said. It was great having you here for a couple of very productive and fun days! Fri Oct 19 18:10:14 +0000 2012 RT @BarackObama: “I don’t know if he knows how many lives he’s changing by just saying that simple thing. He's saving lives.” http://t.c ... Sat Oct 20 13:06:17 +0000 2012 Replying to @melissaterras I saw the @timeshighered piece online but didn’t know that it was in print too. I’d love a copy, thanks! Will DM address. Sat Oct 20 13:08:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @melissaterras Thank you! Sat Oct 20 14:13:43 +0000 2012 Okay, folks. I’ve got an article to write that MUST be done by the end of the day tomorrow. Anybody up for some work sprints this weekend? Sat Oct 20 14:18:18 +0000 2012 Replying to @academicdave Heh. That’s a little more multitasking than I can really handle. Sat Oct 20 14:20:48 +0000 2012 Replying to @charlottefrost I was just reading that! My November is NUTS, but I might just try to work it in. But first, this article, this weekend... Sat Oct 20 14:21:31 +0000 2012 Okay. Have set up Concentrate. Am a few minutes behind the half-hour, but am starting… now. See you back here in 30. Sat Oct 20 14:34:09 +0000 2012 Sprint 1 went well, but coming back turned into a software update process. Anyhow, next sprint starting imminently. Sat Oct 20 15:29:44 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni Fab. Let me know how you’re doing. Sat Oct 20 15:30:37 +0000 2012 And I’m off. #worksprint Sat Oct 20 15:30:50 +0000 2012 Okay, #worksprint 2, done. Article is progressing! If you’re working today, how’s it going? Sat Oct 20 16:01:45 +0000 2012 My next #worksprint is going to involve focused research rather than network-disabled writing. Last one uncovered need for information! Sat Oct 20 16:03:35 +0000 2012 First, however, must make lunch. Then onward! Sat Oct 20 16:07:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @joshhonn *high five* Sat Oct 20 16:08:14 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat If you happen to have a new revenue model for scholarly societies handy, that would be awesome. Sat Oct 20 16:11:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @dancohen Wow, that’s nice. Sat Oct 20 17:49:28 +0000 2012 Replying to @dancohen My theme’s nicely responsive, but I’m tempted by this one anyhow. Sat Oct 20 17:51:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @dancohen It’s a modification of Ari: http://www.elmastudio.de/wordpress-themes/ari/ Sat Oct 20 17:52:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal The coffee at Think? Or the venue as a space for writing? Sat Oct 20 18:05:34 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal By the by, I’m about to start a research sprint, and then after a break, back into writing. I hope you’re doing well! Sat Oct 20 18:06:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal I find most of the coffee places around NYU impossible venues. But there’s definitely better coffee out there. Sat Oct 20 18:09:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal and @moncia Excellent! Sat Oct 20 18:09:16 +0000 2012 Okay, after a nice lunch and some goofing around, I’m going back in. 30-minute research sprint starts now. Sat Oct 20 18:09:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal Exactly. Sat Oct 20 18:09:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @jvinopal and @amndw2 Exactly. The only place I’ve had luck with is VBar, and then only if I show up early. Sat Oct 20 18:32:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @SashaCA2 My pal @jcmeloni got me started on these: pick 1 task, turn off distractions, set a timer for 30 \(or 15\) mins, and go! Then rest. Sat Oct 20 18:33:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @SashaCA2 And repeat. Sat Oct 20 18:33:22 +0000 2012 Replying to @kidolopez Excellent! I’m about 2/3 of the way done with this weekend’s writing. Hoping to wrap it up tonight! Sat Oct 20 21:38:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @kidolopez I know, right? I’ve been using Concentrate, which forces me to focus by shutting off certain apps & sites. Sat Oct 20 21:39:57 +0000 2012 Just a quick YAY to say that I have a draft of my article! I may press onward today; I’ve got another piece I could stand to get written. Sun Oct 21 12:57:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 and @wynkenhimself Indeed. It’s so nice to feel things moving forward. And that’s not just the coffee speaking! Sun Oct 21 13:30:26 +0000 2012 Replying to @shanakimball Thanks! I hope your travels have gone well so far... Sun Oct 21 15:30:30 +0000 2012 Completely fired up about the MacHeist 4 bundle, including Scrivener and an Evernote Premium subscription. http://macheist.com/bundle/t/619262 Sun Oct 21 15:36:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @acavender I already have Scrivener, but not Evernote Premium. And there are a few games that might be fun. Sun Oct 21 16:08:59 +0000 2012 Placed an Amazon Prime order yesterday. Order was delivered to my building today. SUNDAY. This is how Amazon will kill other retailers. Sun Oct 21 17:02:30 +0000 2012 Replying to @ricksva Good reminder; thanks. Sun Oct 21 17:17:52 +0000 2012 RT @ricksva: @kfitz Well,yes,and also how it'll kill its warehouse employees :-( http://finance.yahoo.com/blogs/daily-ticker/inside-world-online-retail-warehouse-worker-130235671.html Sun Oct 21 17:18:31 +0000 2012 Thanks to the cast-iron dutch oven my mom got me for my birthday, I’ve just succeeded in making the darkest roux I’ve ever made. #gumbotime Sun Oct 21 17:38:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @DanielPaulOD I’ll post what I’ve done this time; it’s usually a make-it-up-as-you-go affair for me. Sun Oct 21 18:01:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves I’m hoping. There is a tiny chance that I may have overdone the darkness of the roux. But hey - I could try again next Sunday! Sun Oct 21 19:52:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Ha! I was indeed thinking of our writing date. But you’re right - I should actually focus on, I don’t know, writing maybe. Sun Oct 21 20:17:51 +0000 2012 Okay, Mountain Lion: I have had it up to HERE with your popping up windows that steal focus while I’m in the middle of typing. KNOCK IT OFF. Mon Oct 22 13:08:03 +0000 2012 Replying to @briancroxall Other stuff. Double-click to open a doc; click back into the window you were in; new doc still steals focus on open. Mon Oct 22 13:10:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @surlyF Woo hoo! Looking forward to seeing you there. Tue Oct 23 01:37:53 +0000 2012 Two! Two! Two major tasks accomplished today! Tue Oct 23 16:22:51 +0000 2012 RT @bukuwawa: @kfitz Ah! Hah! Hah! http://img268.imageshack.us/img268/5703/countvoncount.jpg Tue Oct 23 16:28:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @foundhistory Back at LSU, there was a point when he drove a smallish SUV with the driver’s seat removed. He sat in the back seat. Wed Oct 24 17:53:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @elotroalex Hi back to the three of you! It was great meeting them; I’ll look forward to talking more. Wed Oct 24 18:48:03 +0000 2012 Oh, yes. This was definitely all about “the integrity of the vital record-keeping system.” http://bostonglobe.com/news/politics/2012/10/24/mitt-romney-overruled-state-agency-and-rejected-new-birth-certificates-for-children-born-gay-parents/TqOHBb99V98H6nGQqUQrjO/story.html // via @jbj Thu Oct 25 14:29:06 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @jbj I think you’re right. I misread that line as coming from a counsel in the Romney admin re their policing of changes. Thu Oct 25 14:51:22 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @jbj But it now appears to me to be about the need for manual changes since the form itself wasn’t revised. Thu Oct 25 14:52:05 +0000 2012 Replying to @ayjay Had to go back in time to catch the announcement I’d missed, but well worth doing. Congratulations! Thu Oct 25 14:56:54 +0000 2012 Gee, I really wish I could, but no. Thanks for the tempting offer, but no. That sounds fantastic, but no. \(Just practicing\.\) Thu Oct 25 15:15:26 +0000 2012 Replying to @sleonchnm Well said! Thu Oct 25 15:24:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @magpie I am a little fonder than I should be of the “no sandwich”: Wow, what a tempting invitation. I just can’t. Perhaps another time! Thu Oct 25 15:25:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @moncia and @jmittell Oh, that’s priceless. Thu Oct 25 15:40:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Ack! When are you supposed to fly out? Thu Oct 25 23:44:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves You should be good. Looks like Monday & Tuesday will be rough, but if gets crossed that all is well thereafter. Fri Oct 26 01:29:35 +0000 2012 Replying to @melissaterras That sounds not so vaguely obstetric. Fri Oct 26 11:37:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @chutry and @jmittell “Since the dawn of time, man has…” Fri Oct 26 19:52:51 +0000 2012 Dear NYC: the Midtown tunnel is closed. \(For repairs, not the storm\.\) Don't bother trying 36th - it's going nowhere. Sat Oct 27 22:52:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @ucsdBECS I have my moments of temptation. But I'll take hurricanes over earthquakes, any day. I hope all's well in SD! Sat Oct 27 23:55:42 +0000 2012 RT @NYTMetro: BREAKING: MTA suspending subway, bus, rail service starting 7 p.m. tonight. #Sandy Sun Oct 28 14:18:11 +0000 2012 RT @debcha: Mandatory Zone A evacuation. Be safe, New York friends. Sun Oct 28 15:31:44 +0000 2012 “I give a 50% chance that Sandy's storm surge will end up flooding a portion of the New York City subway system.” http://www.wunderground.com/blog/JeffMasters/show.html Sun Oct 28 15:52:15 +0000 2012 My goofball little girl cat likes to sleep on the back of the sofa, and has fallen off of it twice in the last hour. #lowbarometricpressure? Sun Oct 28 17:16:24 +0000 2012 Fortunately, she fell seat-ward both times, and mostly just looks a little embarrassed. Sun Oct 28 17:16:48 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal The only way I could have video would be *if* I pushed her. Sun Oct 28 17:23:06 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat Alice meowed at me and came over to be petted, as if to say “what did you do?” Sun Oct 28 17:26:15 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 Exactly. Sun Oct 28 17:38:07 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava Phew. Glad you made it home in plenty of time for the transit shutdown. Be safe! Sun Oct 28 21:36:30 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava Yup! Safe and dry and waiting for the rain to start. Sun Oct 28 22:15:27 +0000 2012 Spent the day today with @ayjay’s The Pleasures of Reading in an Age of Distraction - a perfect book for a day alone waiting on a storm. Mon Oct 29 00:20:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @ayjay I thoroughly enjoyed it, and I hope we’ll get the chance to talk about it in person some time! Mon Oct 29 00:40:28 +0000 2012 And now for an evening with season 1 of Parenthood and nature's perfect food, the tater tot. #stormconditions Mon Oct 29 00:51:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @antinats I never watched Brothers and Sisters, but I've heard the connection elsewhere. Mon Oct 29 00:56:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @literarychica Happily, I've got seasons and seasons still ahead of me. Mon Oct 29 01:00:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @shanakimball Thanks! Likely the worst danger is a case of cabin fever... Mon Oct 29 01:01:37 +0000 2012 I have just discovered a crucial miscalculation in my storm preparations. I only have about two days’ worth of coffee left. Mon Oct 29 12:52:32 +0000 2012 Replying to @elotroalex And the two of us from hurricane central! Maybe it’s just hard to believe this kind of thing can happen in NYC. Mon Oct 29 12:59:24 +0000 2012 Replying to @hedgielib Phew! There’s plenty of tea. It’s green tea, though, so I’ll have to find the IV infuser. Mon Oct 29 13:06:00 +0000 2012 The folks at http://weather.com seem to be freaking out. Mon Oct 29 13:07:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott No kidding. I’m trying to figure out how to get myself to focus on some reading today. Mon Oct 29 13:09:38 +0000 2012 Just finished a Google hangout. Thru my earbuds, I thought I was hearing the wind pick up. Turns out it was the fan on my laptop going nuts. Mon Oct 29 15:06:37 +0000 2012 Pure awesomeness. // RT @boone: Introducing Participad, realtime collaboration for WordPress http://blo.so/ka http://participad.org Mon Oct 29 16:45:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @ComicsGrid and @boone The main difference is that it’s LIVE multi-author collaboration. Simultaneous co-authoring. Mon Oct 29 16:54:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat, @dancohen and @boone Srsly. I can imagine that, say, if one were running a large WP-based platform for a large scholarly org + Mon Oct 29 17:54:23 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat, @dancohen and @boone something like this might come in handy at its annual meeting. Just for instance. Mon Oct 29 17:54:45 +0000 2012 Whoa. Major wind gust here just made a LOT of noise. If there’s a full evening of that to come, I’m going to need to steady my nerves. Mon Oct 29 18:05:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @cjprender I thank you. I’m hoping to wait another couple of hours before busting out the non-virtual ones. Mon Oct 29 18:12:23 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Oh, no! Seriously not good. Is anybody on duty in the bldg? Mon Oct 29 18:51:33 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Well, at least they’re there. Keep me posted! Mon Oct 29 18:55:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Indeed! Mon Oct 29 19:07:04 +0000 2012 RT @BendProf: Friendly reminder to #mla members: Please vote! There are 4 #comm_college members + 3 tweeps running for Executive Council ... Mon Oct 29 19:17:14 +0000 2012 Little power flickers. Mon Oct 29 19:31:31 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves Thank goodness! I’m so glad he did; things have barely started, and that was going to get worse. Mon Oct 29 20:12:13 +0000 2012 Yesterday was a productive stay-at-home-and-wait-for-the-storm day. Today has been the opposite. Time to give up on getting things done. Mon Oct 29 20:27:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @CapitolClio TOTALLY. Mon Oct 29 20:53:16 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott I have Irish whiskey. Ergo, am doing fine. \(Now\. The howling wind was getting to me\.\) How are you? Mon Oct 29 21:02:25 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott Oh, if I had any xanax in the house, I’d be right there with you, trust me. Mon Oct 29 21:05:03 +0000 2012 Cop car driving up 3rd Ave telling pedestrians over the loudspeaker to get off the street, go back home or to your hotel… Mon Oct 29 21:06:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @mamamusings Heh. I did have a moment of thinking “seriously? You better be out after something like diapers.” Mon Oct 29 21:11:06 +0000 2012 Replying to @parezcoydigo Good grief. Mon Oct 29 21:19:25 +0000 2012 Replying to @parezcoydigo Indeed. Mon Oct 29 21:20:12 +0000 2012 RT @MLAconvention: The 1 Nov deadline to apply for travel grants for #MLA13 has been extended to 5 Nov, courtesy of #Sandy. Mon Oct 29 22:09:56 +0000 2012 Either my building is swaying or I’m more nervous than I thought. Mon Oct 29 22:16:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @ernestopriego Thanks, Ernesto! Mon Oct 29 22:19:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @drkassorla Truly. Just a bit unnerving, despite the good design. Mon Oct 29 22:24:24 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava Backatcha! Henry & Alice seem unperturbed, though curious. Mon Oct 29 22:25:44 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava On and off, yes. They’re pacing around a little, but about to settle back in, I think. Mon Oct 29 22:40:03 +0000 2012 Replying to @katinalynn 16. It’s exciting up here. Mon Oct 29 23:04:56 +0000 2012 Replying to @katinalynn Ugh. I hope you guys are safely away from the sludge! Mon Oct 29 23:47:34 +0000 2012 Power out in Murray Hill. Tue Oct 30 00:44:42 +0000 2012 A couple of years ago my mother gave me a rechargeable battery phone charger and an LED camping lantern. I am sooooo grateful. Tue Oct 30 13:26:27 +0000 2012 Which is to say: no power, erratic cell. Water ok for now. Safe on the 16th floor but not going anywhere for a while. Hope you are all well. Tue Oct 30 13:27:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself FORGOT TO GRIND BEANS STOP. SEND RESCUE STOP. Tue Oct 30 13:46:44 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself Srsly, am drinking tea. All is well. Tue Oct 30 13:47:07 +0000 2012 Replying to @rgfeal I'm buying them for everyone for the holidays this year. Tue Oct 30 13:48:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava Yup. Went down in a blinding flash a little before 9pm. Nothing since. Hoping I can hold out here until elevators are back. :) Tue Oct 30 14:31:14 +0000 2012 Sigh. @ConEdison saying power back in BK & Manhattan w/in 4 days. Better than it could be, but not ideal. Tue Oct 30 15:45:48 +0000 2012 RT @MTAInsider: The MTA will restore limited bus service today at 5pm, with more service coming tomorrow. Fares will be waived for the t ... Tue Oct 30 15:46:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself Temp is ok, thankfully. We'll see how it goes, I guess! Tue Oct 30 16:11:10 +0000 2012 Water now out. Packing up to head to my sister's. Happy to have local family with power willing to receive my cranky unshowered self! Tue Oct 30 16:45:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @savasavasava Am going to leave them lots of food & water; I'll come back & brave the giant StairMaster as needed :) Tue Oct 30 16:57:38 +0000 2012 Okay, am settled in at my wonderful sister’s, with hot and cold running internet. Sending hopes that the rest of you are safe & comfortable. Tue Oct 30 19:04:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott Me too! I take it you came through okay? Tue Oct 30 19:05:28 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah Nope. There are few cabs, all occupied. I walked, carrying 3 bags, 40+ blocks. My exercise for the day! Tue Oct 30 19:13:52 +0000 2012 So happy that my calendar app has continued to notify me all day of meetings it is very concerned are just about to start! Tue Oct 30 20:25:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves I had a little bit of very intermittent reception at home. Better reception here. Lots of towers w/o power downtown, I think. Tue Oct 30 20:39:34 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves How are you guys? I’m glad you’ve been able to charge devices, but the no water thing is no fun. Tue Oct 30 20:40:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @stephharves No kidding. Might be worth a trip to an NYSC north of 42nd? Tue Oct 30 20:51:45 +0000 2012 Replying to @brendabethman, @katinalynn and @rgfeal Alas, it’s looking like 4 days before power returns. I’m safe at my sister’s, though. Thanks for asking! Tue Oct 30 21:29:13 +0000 2012 Replying to @mkgold Sending you guys good power-returning vibes. Take care! Wed Oct 31 02:10:56 +0000 2012 Got an extremely good night’s sleep at my sister’s. The lights are on, the coffee is made, the kids upstairs are pounding on the ceiling…+ Wed Oct 31 11:47:58 +0000 2012 One could almost begin to believe that nothing has happened. Except that I’m at my sister’s, with a growing pile of meetings to reschedule. Wed Oct 31 11:49:01 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah This is a bigger worry than it must sound to you. Without those meetings, a massive project with an impending deadline stalls. Wed Oct 31 12:09:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah And looking at my Nov. travel itinerary, rescheduling becomes very hard. So it’s not just missing meetings, it’s the ripple effect. Wed Oct 31 12:09:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah Ah, got it. Yes, it is a very different order of worry from yesterday! We’ll get moving forward again, I’m sure. But looking at + Wed Oct 31 12:15:31 +0000 2012 Replying to @mmwwah the pix of the Bowling Green subway station \(across the street from our office, and the station I come into\) has me mildly freaked. Wed Oct 31 12:16:22 +0000 2012 Ok, here I am, with power, internet, and time. Let’s get some stuff done! I need to… no, that server is down. How about… no, that one too. Wed Oct 31 13:11:30 +0000 2012 Replying to @sleonchnm Woo hoo! Congratulations! Wed Oct 31 14:40:22 +0000 2012 Replying to @annehelen I’m kinda digging it myself. So much better than having to do the window pop-out thing to see your inbox. Wed Oct 31 17:12:33 +0000 2012 Replying to @jmittell and @annehelen Try hard-refreshing your Gmail tab. That made it pop up for me. Wed Oct 31 17:15:22 +0000 2012 Having a very hard time not thinking Beavis & Butthead thoughts every time I look at the ConEd URL. Probably I’m just punchy. Wed Oct 31 17:17:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @annehelen and @jmittell My word. Working on two email messages at once! What will they think of next? Wed Oct 31 17:18:05 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself W00t! Celebrate all you like; I will when mine is back! Wed Oct 31 18:20:58 +0000 2012
docs.title: 2012 10 01 tweets

docs.text: I can bear many of iOS’s skeuomorphs \(the pageturns & bookshelves\) . But I despise the reel-to-reel player in Podcasts. http://nyti.ms/QVp6vc Thu Nov 01 11:29:17 +0000 2012 Replying to @literarychica It’s not the old-school thing I hate; it’s that it’s all form over function. It’s very hard to control with any precision. Thu Nov 01 11:39:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @plragde I forgot the leather apps. Yes, those really irritate me. Thu Nov 01 12:04:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat You have no idea how much I look forward to getting on that plane to Denver. Looking forward to DLF, too, of course! Thu Nov 01 14:45:33 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat I migrated to my sister’s place on the UES Tues; have to reclimb the stairs today to check on cats, and again tmw/Sat to pack… Thu Nov 01 14:47:30 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat I keep telling myself that I need the exercise. Thu Nov 01 14:48:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @boone This is true of all buses. I walked down 2nd Ave from 74th to 45th; passed 3 select buses, none of which ever caught back up to me. Thu Nov 01 14:53:55 +0000 2012 RT @MLAnews: the application deadline for travel grants to #MLA13 has been re-extended to *Friday, 9 November*, as we recover from #Sandy. Thu Nov 01 16:00:58 +0000 2012 Replying to @noctambulate Ten-plus years of blogging at that URL and I still mis-type it the first time, every time. Thu Nov 01 16:02:46 +0000 2012 Replying to @noctambulate Would love to hear about your presentation, by the by. Thu Nov 01 16:04:41 +0000 2012 Replying to @noctambulate I’d love to see it! And thanks for the kind words... Thu Nov 01 16:24:22 +0000 2012 Just made the climb to the 16th floor. Cats are fine. Fridge now cleaned out. Door guys think we'll have power tomorrow. May they be right! Thu Nov 01 21:10:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @ernestopriego Thanks, Ernesto! Thu Nov 01 21:15:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella By Sat 11pm for all of Manhattan, they're saying, but I leave at noon, so I'm really going for tomorrow! Thu Nov 01 22:04:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella UGH. I'm so sorry! I'll hope it's sooner. Thu Nov 01 22:31:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella That's awful, and must be incredibly frustrating. I'm 3 blocks south of the line in Manhattan and it makes me crazy. Thu Nov 01 22:44:31 +0000 2012 Replying to @eetempleton Thanks! I'm hopeful, but trying not to be over-optimistic. Thu Nov 01 22:46:11 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott Yeah, the literal reason is obvious. And I know the cash influx is important. But it’s shockingly tone-deaf, right now. Fri Nov 02 11:27:33 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott That’s true. If the city were to find ways to turn over more of the proceeds to relief this year... Fri Nov 02 11:44:05 +0000 2012 Replying to @meganeabbott That, I could get behind. Fri Nov 02 11:46:28 +0000 2012 Replying to @moncia and @jvinopal I will tackle the stairs again today to unpack for evacuation & repack for #dlfforum. Can’t wait to see you, no jokes. Fri Nov 02 11:57:58 +0000 2012 Replying to @ernestopriego, @drcomics and @AtwoodTate Thanks for that, Ernesto! Fri Nov 02 12:37:03 +0000 2012 Replying to @tcarmody I made the big stair climb again. My door guys say power is slowly marching up the east side & that we shd be back on by midnight. Fri Nov 02 21:05:28 +0000 2012 I can see traffic signals on 3rd Ave as far north as 20th or so. Please, please let the power keep marching this way... Fri Nov 02 21:49:57 +0000 2012 RT @GOOD: Can't get to NYC for the Marathon but have a hotel room? Don't cancel your reservation: Donate it to Sandy refugees http://t.c ... Fri Nov 02 21:52:09 +0000 2012 Still trying to decide whether to take a chance on power returning tonight & stay here. Upside: get to hang w kitties for night & wait on + Fri Nov 02 21:54:55 +0000 2012 hauling suitcase down 16 flts. Feed cats in morning & leave fr airport from here. Downside: no certainty of power, water, heat. Also dinner. Fri Nov 02 21:56:35 +0000 2012 Best gifts ever: LED camping lantern, rechargeable USB power pack, hand-crank LEF flashlight. Fri Nov 02 22:01:13 +0000 2012 LES/EVillage, on! Chelsea, on! Come on, WSq, WVillage, Murray Hill! Fri Nov 02 22:03:08 +0000 2012 So close. No cigar. Fri Nov 02 22:06:05 +0000 2012 A festive neighbor across the street. How? And why? Fri Nov 02 22:14:49 +0000 2012 Replying to @mickimcgee You got it! Are you home? Fri Nov 02 22:22:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @cscannella Thank goodness for heat & good neighbors. I'll hope for more very soon. Fri Nov 02 22:24:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @mickimcgee No, so sorry - I meant "you got my 'come on, S Village' wishes." Didn't mean to get yr hopes up too soon. Fri Nov 02 22:26:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @mickimcgee Yup. I'm literally 3 blocks south of the line. Fri Nov 02 22:34:19 +0000 2012 So, Denver: I will be partaking of yr electricity and running water, & also speaking at @CpPress \(aka Counterpath\) tmw at 7pm. Come say hi! Fri Nov 02 22:40:32 +0000 2012 RT @rgfeal: Pleased to have spent day @ACLS1919 with @tcallaghan44, @kfitz and other #MLA colleagues. Doing our best to keep MLA running! Fri Nov 02 22:41:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @lynnegoldstein If my fingers were any more crossed, they'd be fused. :) Fri Nov 02 22:44:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @katinalynn Thanks! Recs would be most gratefully received. I could use a really good meal or two. :) Fri Nov 02 22:52:12 +0000 2012 In case I haven't said: I have the best sister. Not only invited me to stay w her & her husband in their 1BR but insisted when I hesitated. Fri Nov 02 23:06:57 +0000 2012 Holy crap. Madison Square! Lights up to 30th or so! Fri Nov 02 23:08:31 +0000 2012 Lights 3 blocks to my north, 6 or so blocks to my south. SO CLOSE! Fri Nov 02 23:09:18 +0000 2012 Sooooo close. Fri Nov 02 23:21:36 +0000 2012 Power on at Madison Sq means I have viable 3G again, so things are already better. Fri Nov 02 23:29:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself Yeah, I'm sticking it out. Want to be here if the lights go on & hang with them while I can. Fri Nov 02 23:34:14 +0000 2012 Replying to @2xlp Park, sorta. I'm at 36th & 3rd. Fri Nov 02 23:40:57 +0000 2012 Replying to @triproftri Thanks! I'm super hopeful. Fri Nov 02 23:44:05 +0000 2012 Replying to @bighandsome Thank you! I take it you & yours came through well? Fri Nov 02 23:51:17 +0000 2012 Fell asleep a couple of hours ago. Just woke up to find that my power is on! #woohoo! Sat Nov 03 05:27:16 +0000 2012 Weird. A little scary, but a lot empowering. Wed Nov 28 03:26:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni Heh. Apparently not very deep at all. Wed Nov 28 03:28:13 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni You said it! It's a big step, but absolutely the right one. Wed Nov 28 03:36:46 +0000 2012 Replying to @amitorit Thank you! Wed Nov 28 03:47:47 +0000 2012 Replying to @mlmcgill I am trying not to think too hard about what my white whale might be! \(Seriously, thanks\.\) Wed Nov 28 03:51:55 +0000 2012 Replying to @BendProf Thanks, S! Looking forward to saying hi in Boston. Wed Nov 28 03:57:26 +0000 2012 Replying to @footnotesrising Heh. Gotta keep everybody on their toes. Thanks, again! docs.title: 2012 11 01 tweets

docs.text: Saturday. Is. Awesome. I’ve unpacked, gone grocery shopping, done some cleaning. Now I’m making split pea soup. I may even do laundry. Sat Dec 01 18:51:41 +0000 2012 There’s something amazing \(and perhaps a little sad\) about how good it feels to be able to get life at home in order. Sat Dec 01 18:52:14 +0000 2012 Okay, the split pea soup is AWESOME. And the laundry is done. There might be a nap coming soon. #wildsaturday #lookoutworld Sat Dec 01 20:09:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 It’s pretty great, isn’t it? Sat Dec 01 21:47:09 +0000 2012 Remembering with a mild surprise what a beautifully vicious little tune Simon & Garfunkel’s last song was. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1jg8jf9oTjw Sun Dec 02 05:39:05 +0000 2012 RT @MC_IMR: Want to keep up with all the projects of our "parent" site, MediaCommons? If so, please "like" its facebook page & follo ... Mon Dec 03 03:43:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @blros Thanks, Brian! Mon Dec 03 20:07:41 +0000 2012 This day. Oh, man, this day. On the positive side, I just had my Global Entry interview, which was \(a\) next door, and \(b\) five minutes. Mon Dec 03 20:59:24 +0000 2012 On the other side? Databases. Is all I’m saying. Mon Dec 03 21:00:37 +0000 2012 Replying to @naypinya Yes, I do -- enough that this will improve my travel life a good bit. Mon Dec 03 21:01:27 +0000 2012 RT @MurraysCheese: Bill Murray just bought a Murray's tshirt at our store in @GrandCentralNYC and now we have to close because all our s ... And very nice on a killer sore throat. #sadface Wed Dec 05 00:56:46 +0000 2012 Dear Rev. of Higher Education: thanks for the thoughtful review of my book! But how did you get a paper price of $110? http://nyupress.org/books/book-details.aspx?bookId=5008 Wed Dec 05 03:25:23 +0000 2012 Aw, man. All of my people seem to be at #mediaplaces2012, making me nostalgic for my visit to #HUMlab last year. Wed Dec 05 14:19:44 +0000 2012 Replying to @annettemarkham Sadly, I wasn’t invited. I did get to visit last year, though, so I can’t feel too bad about it. Wed Dec 05 14:28:43 +0000 2012 At the launch of Open Utopia, at the NYU Digital Humanities Initiative. Very cool project I supported on Kickstarter! Wed Dec 05 22:40:46 +0000 2012 Duncombe: Realizing that More's Utopia had less to do w/specificity of this Utopia than w/getting readers to imagine what Utopia might be. Wed Dec 05 22:46:06 +0000 2012 Duncombe: Utopia as an imaginal machine, pushing readers to conceive of new worlds; Open Utopia encourages the same. Wed Dec 05 23:03:18 +0000 2012 Replying to @jcmeloni and @amndw2 We waved. Wed Dec 05 23:04:03 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 Briefly. I have a conversation I have to have as soon as the presentation is done, and then have another talk I have to run to. Wed Dec 05 23:13:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @amndw2 \(Rita Raley\!\) Wed Dec 05 23:14:11 +0000 2012 RT @nypl_labs: "It's kinda like Pinterest for the @nytimes." WAIT WHAT?! Seriously!? Awesome! Check out Compendium from @nytlabs http: ... Wed Dec 05 23:18:57 +0000 2012 Now at an absolutely fascinating talk by @ritaraley on disintegrated reading and intermedial textual practices. Thu Dec 06 00:56:44 +0000 2012 Disintegration: a turn away from suturing, away from the assemblage, away from the continuous, toward whatever catches the eye. @ritaraley Thu Dec 06 01:06:02 +0000 2012 No absolute value can be assigned to integrated or disintegrated reading; neither provides any guaranteed benefit or injury. @ritaraley Thu Dec 06 01:07:48 +0000 2012 \(@ritaraley put that way better than I did\.\) Thu Dec 06 01:08:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @brettbobley Dude. Really? I now owe @ritaraley a really nice dinner. Thu Dec 06 01:09:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @elikaortega and @ritaraley Her slides were mostly images; I'm sure the article/chapter will be published soon, though. Thu Dec 06 01:13:51 +0000 2012 Replying to @brettbobley and @ritaraley That's awfully nice. Let's talk! Fri Dec 07 14:22:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @nowviskie That’s huge -- congratulations! Fri Dec 07 20:04:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @adelinekoh Can you email me about this please? Fri Dec 07 20:13:23 +0000 2012 Having one of those "you know, actually putting the coffee in the coffee maker might result in a better cup of coffee" kind of mornings. Mon Dec 10 12:32:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @nancybaym Exactly. I'm more likely to post a screed after the morning 4/5 train, anyhow. Wed Dec 12 04:12:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @dandyroll No, not at all. In fact, I didn't even know they'd posted the ad! Wed Dec 12 12:50:58 +0000 2012 Replying to @dandyroll Having seen it: it's not how I would have defined the position, but it's no longer mine to define. I'm sure it'll get good apps. New post at Planned Obsolescence: "Neoliberal." http://wp.me/p2w8Sh-CN Wed Dec 12 15:45:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @tcarmody Many recent instances have been of the “DH is evidence of neoliberal capitulation” variety. Wed Dec 12 15:53:45 +0000 2012 RT @Ted_Underwood: @tcarmody @kfitz If you want instances, seriously all you need do is search twitter on "neoliberal." It means what "b ... Thu Dec 13 15:38:31 +0000 2012 RT @anildash: Our culture is cowed by extremists who would rather see dead children than let go of their fantasy of armed insurrection. ... Fri Dec 14 18:23:12 +0000 2012 This day has utterly wrecked me. I am so, so sorry, that this is the world that we've made. Fri Dec 14 21:52:12 +0000 2012 Okay, this is crazy: my first book gets a mention in the NYTBR review of Mr. Penumbra. http://www.nytimes.com/2012/12/16/books/review/mr-penumbras-24-hour-bookstore-by-robin-sloan.html?_r=0 Sat Dec 15 14:16:29 +0000 2012 Replying to @s3jones Hey, super cool! Sat Dec 15 14:50:47 +0000 2012 Replying to @tmccormick Very interesting! I might actually have a good platform for such a project… coming soon to #mla13. Sat Dec 15 18:20:58 +0000 2012 Replying to @miriamkp Yay! Little peanut! I hope you all get some sleep. Sat Dec 15 22:19:38 +0000 2012 Replying to @pmhswe and @amandafrench It's fabulous, and very near me! Sun Dec 16 20:52:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @dancohen and @briancroxall Indeed. Saw it yesterday; completely fantastic. Mon Dec 17 00:50:36 +0000 2012 Replying to @kfitz A genuine answer, @ryancordell: Scholarly community. Your work in the place where those you want to reach are also working. #mlacommons Mon Dec 17 23:35:43 +0000 2012 Replying to @ryancordell Trust me, I *completely* understand. Tue Dec 18 00:21:11 +0000 2012 Replying to @peter_garofalo and @MLAnews Personification is indeed a rhetorical device, often used for literary purposes. Some non-literary, too. Tue Dec 18 00:24:53 +0000 2012 Replying to @jonbecker and @ryancordell We're hoping to build a federated network of commonses, allowing both for disciplinary focus and interdisc collab. Tue Dec 18 00:44:38 +0000 2012 Replying to @pfyfe and @ryancordell Absolutely - one of its key purposes. Tue Dec 18 03:44:19 +0000 2012 Replying to @BendProf and @literarychica Also, I hear rumors of a big cake in the MLA booth on Saturday afternoon... Tue Dec 18 03:45:12 +0000 2012 Replying to @BendProf Hmmm. Haven't heard anything, but will pass on in the morning. I hope the rest of the trip was grand, and the travel smooth. So much worth sharing! Thu Dec 20 15:55:34 +0000 2012 Replying to @nowviskie It could be a great idea - showing how we bring our methods to bear on our own processes. Could help so many other conferences. Sat Dec 22 20:02:36 +0000 2012 Oh, but it's such a great thing to be reminded of one's essential stupidity. #sigh Sun Dec 23 10:54:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @ncecire I can always count on you for support. Sun Dec 23 11:05:28 +0000 2012 Replying to @ncecire A mantra that can serve us all well in trying times. Sun Dec 23 11:06:21 +0000 2012 Replying to @LibSkrat I only wish that I could say it were so. Is the essay authored by "Film Crit Hulk"? Or "FILMCRITHULK" as on the blog? Wed Dec 26 15:30:38 +0000 2012 Replying to @jmittell Hmm. I'd probably go with "Film Crit Hulk," in that case. The Handbook supports single-name pseudonyms \(Stendhal, Moliere\) & I'm + Wed Dec 26 15:35:10 +0000 2012 Replying to @jmittell pretty sure that "Hulk" is not intended as a surname. Or possibly I've just overthought what really ought to be a pretty simple thing. SIGH. Sun Dec 30 14:43:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @bonniekyburz I will get out the pruning shears; I want everyone else to get theirs on the table, too! Sun Dec 30 15:01:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @HesterBlum, @mkgold and @MLACommons Yay! Thanks, both of you! Sun Dec 30 20:03:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @samplereality If I cut all the prepositional phrases, I think I can come in under the buzzer. #mla13 Sun Dec 30 20:05:00 +0000 2012 Replying to @eetempleton And breathing. If I eliminate the breathing, I can probably save 30 seconds or so. Sun Dec 30 20:40:59 +0000 2012 Replying to @kakennedy I'd say adjectives are stupid, but that would require caving in to their demands. Sun Dec 30 20:41:45 +0000 2012 Replying to @wynkenhimself It is! I think I can cut enough, though; I really have just overcomplicated this thing. Sun Dec 30 22:15:20 +0000 2012 Replying to @kakennedy Excellent! I was amazed how well it worked. Sun Dec 30 22:17:04 +0000 2012 No no no no. // RT @AP: Secretary of State Hillary Clinton admitted to hospital with blood clot following concussion: http://apne.ws/ZNqbwE Mon Dec 31 01:21:02 +0000 2012 Replying to @kakennedy It's not a bad idea; I'll put that on the list. Mon Dec 31 17:09:39 +0000 2012 Replying to @nancybaym I wish you all better from here. Mon Dec 31 17:25:33 +0000 2012 Replying to @nancybaym Incidentally, I'll be sorry not to be able to pop by & say hi during the convention, but I'm going to be sprinting throughout. Mon Dec 31 17:26:54 +0000 2012 Replying to @samplereality Woohoo! \(And FYI, this tweet produced at least five other new accounts in the minute thereafter\.\) Mon Dec 31 17:30:18 +0000 2012 Uh, you guys? They call it a *silent* phase for a reason. http://commons.mla.org Mon Dec 31 17:36:42 +0000 2012 Replying to @samplereality Excellent indeed. And my last was sarcasm, in case that wasn't clear. + Mon Dec 31 17:37:32 +0000 2012 Replying to @samplereality The more people who log in today, the less likely the server will melt on Thursday. Mon Dec 31 17:38:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @dancohen :) Mon Dec 31 17:38:31 +0000 2012 Replying to @emilyhegarty It's so close to ready until we're very happy to have people stopping in! \(Sorry the joke didn't quite play\.\) Mon Dec 31 17:41:45 +0000 2012 Okay, note to self: sarcasm doesn't always transmit with the tweet. Mon Dec 31 17:54:04 +0000 2012 Replying to @patrick_mj Wise, man. Wise. Mon Dec 31 17:56:17 +0000 2012 Replying to @JenServenti Yeah, really! ... Oh, wait. I get it! Mon Dec 31 18:03:10 +0000 2012 Happy New Year from Paris, all. See you in Boston tomorrow night, for #thatcampmla Wednesday and #MLA13 starting Thursday! Mon Dec 31 23:19:08 +0000 2012 Replying to @jgieseking PHEW. All fingers still crossed, though. Mon Dec 31 23:21:52 +0000 2012 Replying to @katinalynn There was champagne and a lot of raw & smoked fish. We'd met our foie gras quota on Christmas Eve, happily! Missed oysters, tho. Tue Jan 01 00:25:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @MLAconvention The year is looking good so far! Tue Jan 01 00:25:28 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica Excellent - and a happy new year to you! Tue Jan 01 00:26:26 +0000 2013

docs.text: Replying to @jmittell Right there with you, six time zones away. #insomniasucks Tue Jan 01 07:18:18 +0000 2013 Overbooked, overcrowded, overheated plane. 100% underslept me. But here we go. Tue Jan 01 10:14:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @whitneytrettien Just call me Big Sister. \(We're trying to make that stop\.\) Tue Jan 01 19:56:40 +0000 2013 IAD achieved, after the longest flight ever. Tue Jan 01 19:58:24 +0000 2013 Replying to @patrick_mj Nature's perfect food! Tue Jan 01 21:01:10 +0000 2013 The positive: upgraded! The less positive: the 18mo who screamed right behind me all the way across the Atlantic is now 2 rows behind me. Tue Jan 01 21:28:55 +0000 2013 I do not want to be that person who makes with the unsolicited parenting advice. But! Row 21 is not a good place to Ferberize your toddler. Tue Jan 01 21:31:03 +0000 2013 Hello, Boston! I very much look forward to your room service and your hotel bed. Tue Jan 01 23:17:32 +0000 2013 Replying to @readywriting Absolutely! Tue Jan 01 23:20:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @debcha Thanks! Lovely to be here. Wish I were going to see more of the city this time. Wed Jan 02 00:07:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym Why, thank you! Wed Jan 02 00:07:56 +0000 2013 Yay! // RT @briancroxall: Creating my first Group at MLA Commons. Come join us! http://bit.ly/12WEHAO #mla13 #s270 Wed Jan 02 00:57:22 +0000 2013 At the Marriott Copley Place. Trying to figure out when I was here before, and why. Wed Jan 02 00:58:19 +0000 2013 Alternately, the jet lag may be manifesting as deja vu. Having just come from six hours into the future, and all, it sorta makes sense. Wed Jan 02 01:00:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Ooh, good to know. I am actually sitting at the bar & have a salad on the way, but will file this away for future reference. Wed Jan 02 01:01:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @jcmeloni Will do! Very much looking forward to it. Wed Jan 02 01:09:41 +0000 2013 "It's not an upgrade to your phone, it's an upgrade to your self." The amazing things I miss by not watching regular commercial TV anymore. Wed Jan 02 01:17:38 +0000 2013 Replying to @samplereality What did you do now? Wed Jan 02 01:21:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @CapitolClio ::opens eyes:: "I know Ruby." Wed Jan 02 01:22:56 +0000 2013 Replying to @samplereality So sorry! We'll sort it out. Thanks for helping us uncover unexpected behavior, seriously. Wed Jan 02 01:24:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate and @saaorg We want to work with other orgs! Hoping to create a network of networked humanities societies. #mlacommons Wed Jan 02 01:36:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Very interesting. Is it that you're the first to post to those groups that's unnerving? Or something else? Wed Jan 02 01:39:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @briancroxall I get that. I'm hopeful that lots of us will get more comfortable with the audience & mode of address quickly! Wed Jan 02 01:44:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @kegerton and @wynkenhimself I think that's exactly right. Looking forward to getting more folks in and walking around! Wed Jan 02 01:45:42 +0000 2013 This coincident w/ @samplereality's toes has me unnerved. RT @kegerton: MLA Commons looks great, but needs breaking in, just like new shoes. Wed Jan 02 01:47:41 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself It does feel like a new idiom is required. But I think I felt that way about early Twitter, when I had no sense of audience. Wed Jan 02 01:54:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @HesterBlum and @wynkenhimself No, thank you! \(And yes, we are grateful for fine\-grained email notifications\.\) Wed Jan 02 01:55:24 +0000 2013 Replying to @kegerton and @samplereality I am of course happy to hear that, though *somebody else's* toes have been known to be chatty online. Wed Jan 02 01:56:30 +0000 2013 Okay, this has been fun. But given that it's 3am in Paris, and that I slept about an hour last night, the time has come for collapse. Wed Jan 02 02:00:06 +0000 2013 Happy new year, all. See some of you at #thatcampMLA tomorrow, and many more of you at #mla13 thereafter! Fri Mar 08 17:43:34 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn: importance of making the humanities \(and particularly the PhD program\) more outward-facing. Fri Mar 08 17:44:12 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn recounts "more or less accidental" history of the term "alt-ac": acknowledging real intellectual work of non-teaching positions Fri Mar 08 17:49:05 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn recounts early survey work attempting to explore the "alt-ac" community: http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/alt-ac/who-we-are Fri Mar 08 17:51:54 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn Vast majority of survey respondents entered grad school w/high certainty of pursuing a tenure-track professorship. Fri Mar 08 17:55:52 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn Problematic ethics for grad programs of admitting students with increasingly unmeetable expectations. Fri Mar 08 17:56:38 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn Alt-ac graduates feel low degree of satisfaction with career advice received in grad school. Fri Mar 08 17:57:32 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn Compelling range of career trajectories among respondents to survey; many such positions funded in stable, sustainable ways. Fri Mar 08 18:00:04 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn Reasons alt-acs take the positions they do are quite varied, ranging from practical & immediate to more future & goal-oriented. Fri Mar 08 18:01:39 +0000 2013 RT @jvinopal: .@katinalynn from employer responses, most important competencies:collaboration, communication, research, analytical, inte ... Fri Mar 08 18:05:33 +0000 2013 RT @jvinopal: .@katinalynn skills not learned? oral communication, project management, collaboration Fri Mar 08 18:05:38 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn Myth of single job market for humanities PhDs is damaging to grad students; outcomes other than tenure-track often stigmatized. Fri Mar 08 18:06:43 +0000 2013 .@katinalynn giving us a sneak peek at The Praxis Network, to launch very soon! Fri Mar 08 18:08:04 +0000 2013 RT @jvinopal: .@katinalynn Praxis Network first goal is to share info on existing programs rethinking humanities pedagogy Fri Mar 08 18:13:30 +0000 2013 RT @jvinopal: .@katinalynn common among the programs: focus on collaboration. Research output of students doesn't look like traditional ... Fri Mar 08 18:13:50 +0000 2013 RT @jvinopal: .@katinalynn Praxis Network website will also share info about funding. [JV: From my POV this will be key for others looki ...
