Mon Apr 15 19:48:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @sramsay I’m so glad to hear they’re all okay. How terrifying. Mon Apr 15 20:16:20 +0000 2013 Lots of helicopters and sirens in midtown. I’m sure it’s just the usual doing of business here, but it’s freaking me out. Mon Apr 15 20:23:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym So glad to hear that you and yours are all okay. It’s all just awful. Mon Apr 15 20:44:15 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym So glad he’s safe. Sending you virtual hugs until you get home. Mon Apr 15 21:16:34 +0000 2013 Replying to @debcha Oh no! Poor thing. I hope she’s okay -- and that you are, too. Mon Apr 15 21:29:41 +0000 2013 Replying to @debcha Must have been a terrifying few seconds for everyone involved. So glad to know she’s doing okay. Mon Apr 15 21:32:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @sramsay I am sending you one through the matter replicator. Mon Apr 15 21:33:46 +0000 2013 RT @BostonDotCom: Are you a Boston Marathoner who needs a play to stay? http://bit.ly/XD0xYq Have a place to offer runners? http://t.c ... Mon Apr 15 22:16:08 +0000 2013 RT @dpla: A Message from Executive Director Dan Cohen regarding this week's DPLA Launch event: http://hvrd.me/17jrPUH Tue Apr 16 17:13:53 +0000 2013 Replying to @dpla and @dancohen I can only imagine how difficult this must be. Thinking of all of you, and hoping to celebrate with you soon. Tue Apr 16 17:14:48 +0000 2013 Okay, it’s 8.30. I’m not sure what’s sadder: that I’m thinking of going to bed, or that I feel the need to seek absolution for it. Wed Apr 17 00:37:45 +0000 2013 On my way up to @CCSU to talk with @jbj and others about the future of peer review. Come say hi! Wed Apr 17 12:30:29 +0000 2013 Replying to @jbj *whistles eerily* Wed Apr 17 12:54:40 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat I’m a bit behind, having been on the road today, but congratulations! Fabulous news & well deserved. Wed Apr 17 22:57:47 +0000 2013 RT @surlyF: I'm going to go on record and say that today was a pretty suck day in these United States. Wed Apr 17 23:28:56 +0000 2013 Just home after a fun visit to CCSU today. Thanks for having me up, @jbj! Thu Apr 18 00:59:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @katinalynn I had so many days in SoCal where I wanted to phone the set decoration guys and say “dudes, this is totally unrealistic.” Thu Apr 18 01:01:20 +0000 2013 Come join me at the MLA, as director of programs and the Association of Departments of Foreign Languages \(ADFL\) : https://secure.interfolio.com/apply/21460 Thu Apr 18 18:01:40 +0000 2013 I have cracked the code on the custom salad place around the corner, and can now reliably generate the perfect salad on command. Thu Apr 18 18:29:52 +0000 2013 If only I had the patience to purchase, wash, combine, chop, and pack up that salad at home. Thu Apr 18 18:32:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @veek I look forward to the possibility of actually living 2 blocks from an awesome farmer’s market someday... Thu Apr 18 18:42:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @bukuwawa Oh, not even. I still have to get through the coop board interview and, with luck, close thereafter… Thu Apr 18 19:10:29 +0000 2013 Replying to @bukuwawa Indeed! But like really really QUIET potluck social events that would never disturb anyone. Thu Apr 18 19:20:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @jwolman Oh. Uh oh. Thu Apr 18 19:25:24 +0000 2013 Huge congratulations to @dancohen and the entire @dpla crew on today’s launch! So nice to have something unequivocally happy today. Thu Apr 18 19:27:18 +0000 2013 Replying to @jwolman Also very attractive. Thu Apr 18 19:27:38 +0000 2013 Replying to @journalsmith It is an exceedingly valuable skill, when standing in a high-pressure fast-moving custom salad assembly line. Thu Apr 18 19:31:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @profwernimont You have NO idea how much I miss the market there. Cling to that year-round produce, my friend! Thu Apr 18 20:36:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @cscannella Rilly? Congratulations! #OMGindeed Fri Apr 19 19:21:02 +0000 2013 Yay! //RT @jasonrhody: Very pleased to be part of the @Mediacommons survey on DH & media studies: Boolean Logic of DH http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/question/what-are-differentiations-and-intersections-media-studies-and-digital-humanities/response/b Fri Apr 19 19:28:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @nowviskie and @MLAconvention Mwa-ha-ha. Fri Apr 19 20:38:18 +0000 2013 10 good things about a horrifying week, for which, seriously, thanks: http://www.salon.com/2013/04/19/10_good_things_about_a_horrifying_week/ Sat Apr 20 17:15:18 +0000 2013 Either this whole running thing is suddenly going REALLY REALLY well, or I’ve finally managed to unleash the endorphins again. #lookoutworld Sat Apr 20 20:44:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @acavender I like the way you think! Sat Apr 20 23:21:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah If the Kraken allows you to feel like YOU COULD DO ANYTHING, then yeah, totally. At best large public institutions will become a kind of Peoples Express \(and we know how well that worked\) . Germano: “Academe has been in the snow-globe business for years.” http://chronicle.com/article/Do-We-Dare-Write-for-Readers-/138581/is Mon Apr 22 14:15:12 +0000 2013 It’s mornings like these that make me SO HAPPY that everything in the Apple environment JUST WORKS. #grrrr Mon Apr 22 14:24:39 +0000 2013 Replying to @coryb SO MANY THINGS. You would think that trying to get a photo from an iPhone to a Mac was the equivalent of the moon landing. This is unacceptable. Mon Apr 22 18:25:34 +0000 2013 Spinach: a sad, sad substitute. Mon Apr 22 18:26:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @miriamkp and @katinalynn We're thrilled, and really looking forward to cooking up new plans! Mon Apr 22 18:27:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @ranti Thank you for sympathizing with my plight. Tue Apr 23 22:08:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @sramsay And here I was assuming he was coming to join #cunypie. docs.title: 2013 04 01 tweets

docs.text: Whoa, Chronicle redesign. That’s a surprise! Wed May 01 12:55:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @qui_oui Indeed. This one feels a little less underdone, though. Wed May 01 12:57:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @veek GREAT. BIG. TEXT. Wed May 01 12:58:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat Yeah, it’s soooo much cleaner. And my aging eyes are starting to appreciate the larger typefaces... Wed May 01 13:14:17 +0000 2013 In DC for the next 15 hours. Hi, DC friends! Thu May 02 01:11:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @sleonchnm I know. Which explains my not arranging an Irish Whiskey trip. :( Thu May 02 01:13:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @JenServenti Thank you! I am glad the spring sequester seems to have been lifted. Thu May 02 01:24:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @jcmeloni Yes, def. Thu May 02 01:25:14 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal and @brettbobley Close Twitter for one minute to check into a hotel and suddenly all my beauty secrets are being given away. Thu May 02 01:26:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself *waves back* Thu May 02 01:26:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @GeorgeOnline Yes? Thu May 02 01:55:09 +0000 2013 Replying to @GeorgeOnline and @foundhistory Spooky! Thu May 02 02:07:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @bighandsome Alas, yes. I'll be back twice more in the next two weeks, though... Thu May 02 02:26:20 +0000 2013 Replying to @bighandsome Will do! Thu May 02 02:39:11 +0000 2013 Talk achieved. Heading back to Union Station. It’s been… a bit of a blur, DC. See you next week. Thu May 02 14:48:10 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Thank you! Yes, back for another overnight on Monday for a CLIR meeting. Thu May 02 14:53:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself And then the following week for an NAS meeting! \(Speaking of, I should probably make a hotel reservation\!\) Thu May 02 14:54:27 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball and @miriamkp So Completely Wishing For A Multi-Party DM, Because OMG. Thu May 02 23:32:55 +0000 2013 Today’s quest: catch up on all the things. \(Reading, writing, reviews, laundry, all remain undone\.\) Sat May 04 13:06:55 +0000 2013 Began by catching up on sleep. That was pretty successful. Sat May 04 13:07:10 +0000 2013 Replying to @nicholasbirns I do not understand this “summer” to which you refer. I have a 12-month 9-to-5. :) Sat May 04 13:09:30 +0000 2013 Replying to @jenterysayers I was so sorry to hear of Herb’s passing, Jentery. All my best to you and all his loved ones. Sat May 04 13:19:28 +0000 2013 I just had one of those runs that has me certain I COULD DO ANYTHING. A nice way to start wrapping up a gorgeous weekend. Sun May 05 22:40:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @pmhswe Hey, are we at the same meeting tomorrow? Sun May 05 22:41:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @rschon Treadmill, alas. This has been a key problem with Murray Hill: no good running path access. Sun May 05 23:07:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @pmhswe Oh, yay! I’m there too. I am preparing my pantyhose and polishing my sensible pumps. Sun May 05 23:08:40 +0000 2013 Replying to @rschon That sounds fabulous! Mon May 06 00:18:18 +0000 2013 Replying to @rschon Just life. :) Mon May 06 00:18:34 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley and @pmhswe Indeed. My previous employer’s board of trustees used to meet at the California Club, which always gave me the willies. Mon May 06 00:19:32 +0000 2013 Replying to @sshreeves and @pmhswe I guess it’s a good thing my partner isn’t coming with me. He could not abide. Mon May 06 00:23:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym +1000000 Mon May 06 11:56:32 +0000 2013 Another day, another train to DC. Mon May 06 12:05:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison Amazingly, not only did I unpack but I also put the suitcase away! And then wondered why at 6am today… Mon May 06 12:18:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley Hey, do you have any pull there? Mon May 06 12:20:01 +0000 2013 I find it increasingly hilarious that, when I’m stressed and typing too fast, I often accidentally sign off my emails with “All beset.” Mon May 06 14:58:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate w00t Mon May 06 17:16:33 +0000 2013 Pro tip: When involved in a real estate transaction in today’s economy, it’s best to hire a lawyer who has an email account. Mon May 06 21:39:36 +0000 2013 Such a lawyer might find the notion of money flying unassisted through the air from one account to another less bewildering. Mon May 06 21:40:49 +0000 2013 In other news: we’re closing on Wednesday. And I could really use a Xanax. Mon May 06 21:42:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat Paper checks. Certified paper checks. From an in-state bank. Which I do not have. Mon May 06 21:49:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat Fortunately, my lender’s attorney is living in the 21st century. Mon May 06 21:50:14 +0000 2013 Replying to @jcmeloni I’ll let you know around noon on Wednesday, but I certainly hope so. Mon May 06 21:53:31 +0000 2013 Let’s! RT @brettbobley: Let's nominate some humanists. RT @whitehouseostp: Seeking “Open Science” Champions of Change http://www.whitehouse.gov/blog/2013/05/07/seeking-outstanding-open-science-champions-change Tue May 07 14:12:14 +0000 2013 It’s once again been lovely, DC. See you in a few days. Tue May 07 16:51:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @dancohen Works, with no blockage! \(Amazingly, the same seems to be true of the wifi\.\) Tue May 07 18:08:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @dancohen You’re welcome! Sun May 26 15:43:11 +0000 2013 On board at ONT, headed for IAH and EWR. Home about 1am. One last day of packing tomorrow, and then on to the new place on Tuesday! Sun May 26 18:47:44 +0000 2013 And then I leave for the UNT open access symposium on Wednesday. I should get a chance to unpack in August, I think. Sun May 26 18:48:46 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah Undoubtedly. But I do look forward to slowing it down some soon! Sun May 26 18:54:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF I’ll look forward to it! Sun May 26 22:07:53 +0000 2013 EWR. Soooo close. Mon May 27 04:12:10 +0000 2013 Home sweet home - for one more day. Mon May 27 04:56:38 +0000 2013 Q: I’m about to run out to the new apt with the contents of my fridge & some basic cleaning supplies. What other essentials should go today? Mon May 27 15:11:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @jwolman I like the way you think, Jacob. Mon May 27 15:15:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @karikraus Hmmm. Will need here tmw morning, but will be sure to pack in well labeled box! Mon May 27 15:17:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @CapitolClio Ooh yes. Good thought. Mon May 27 15:17:33 +0000 2013 Replying to @FHeimburger Indeed! Mon May 27 15:17:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Ha! I always pack the bedding last & make the bed first thing on the other end, for immediate collapsing purposes. Mon May 27 15:43:25 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench and @jbj Absolute necessities, all. Mon May 27 15:44:28 +0000 2013 The cats, incidentally, are pretty sure they know what’s up, and they are not pleased. Mon May 27 15:50:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Yeah, I’ll cab them over tomorrow night, I think, once the movers are done on the other end. Mon May 27 15:54:16 +0000 2013 Why is it I can live with my own filthy fridge for months but can’t bear putting food in a fridge someone else has left a tiny bit icky? Mon May 27 18:26:38 +0000 2013 Okay, first suitcase full of stuff is in the new apt. Painting is not quite done, but what is done looks awesome. Mon May 27 20:31:16 +0000 2013 Now to finish up packing here & get ready for the movers: 9am tomorrow. Mon May 27 20:32:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah White, all of it. No time to pick colors -- need to live in the place a while. Mon May 27 22:40:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah You saw the pix, right? Several colors, none of them quite right. I liked the olive in the LR but it needed work, so: white for now. Mon May 27 22:43:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah Here’s the set from the inspection again: http://www.flickr.com/photos/kqf/sets/72157632802921265/ Mon May 27 22:46:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah The blue in the BR was particularly problematic, and the 2nd BR was done in this faux-texture thing I hate. Mon May 27 22:47:53 +0000 2013 One last evening on the terrace. It’s been awesome. Mon May 27 23:18:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @lynnegoldstein Yes, that’s been my view for the last two years. The move is tomorrow, at last. Mon May 27 23:22:49 +0000 2013 I have just run out of tape. Alas, I think I ran out of the motivation to go out and get more tape a couple of hours ago. Mon May 27 23:57:40 +0000 2013 Got it together to go out for tape. Can neither confirm nor deny that chocolate ice cream has also been acquired. Tue May 28 00:33:24 +0000 2013 All that remains is the taping up of the last boxes and the waiting for the movers. #letsdothis Tue May 28 13:05:08 +0000 2013 I even got up early this morning and washed my sheets & towels. I am a moving machine. Tue May 28 13:06:18 +0000 2013 Movers are here. 20 minutes in, huge piles of stuff are gone. Totally getting it done! Tue May 28 14:20:27 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF Truly. They’ll be packing the kitchen for me, which I will genuinely enjoy. Tue May 28 14:30:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself No kidding. They’re amazingly efficient. Tue May 28 14:31:13 +0000 2013 The foreman of my moving crew has got to be 6’4”. He just picked up my 50” tv \(flat screen, but not at all a light one\) like it was nothing. Tue May 28 14:39:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @mmwwah It would’ve been cost-efficient for me to have the whole place packed too, but they needed a 2nd day & I only had 1. Tue May 28 14:55:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @mmwwah With each move I acquire one more box that will never get unpacked. Someday I’ll be brave enough just to toss them. Tue May 28 15:05:08 +0000 2013 Cats are closed in 2nd bdrm. I hear weird scratching noise - from upstairs? - & look up to see both cats sauntering across the living room. Tue May 28 15:35:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself I am determined to unpack the boxes of mystery this time. I’ll probably end up tossing the contents. Tue May 28 15:49:38 +0000 2013 Cats are bloody determined to escape, no matter how much I emphasize that they’d be happier where they are. Tue May 28 15:52:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah They wouldn’t. This much activity would just produce frantic attempts to escape. Tue May 28 16:00:50 +0000 2013 2 hrs in. All boxes & most furniture are down & loaded. Kitchen is being packed and tables are being dismantled. I’m betting we’re out by 1. Tue May 28 16:03:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah #singitsister Tue May 28 16:04:32 +0000 2013 1pm may have been a little optimistic but it won’t be far off. Kitchen/china packing nearly complete. Dining rm table disassembly underway. Tue May 28 16:45:42 +0000 2013 Amazing thing number 1: Time-Warner Cable is scheduled to turn on my internet this afternoon between 4 and 7. Tue May 28 17:02:45 +0000 2013 Amazing thing number 2: they just called to see if they could come early, because the tech is in the area. Tue May 28 17:03:50 +0000 2013 \(Alas, I am not\. But he told me to call once I get there\. We’ll see\!\) Tue May 28 17:04:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @stephharves I can’t wait to have you over. How are things on your end? Tue May 28 17:17:28 +0000 2013 Okay, I was *way* over optimistic. I’m now worried about whether they’ll be able to unload before time is up at the new place. #arghmoving Tue May 28 18:13:50 +0000 2013 Out of the apartment with everything except the cats. Now to the new place for unloading, and then back to terrify the kittehs even more. Tue May 28 18:28:07 +0000 2013 Am in the new apt. Painter is still here painting. Truck not yet here but the super \(who is young & fab\) says they can unload late. #breathe Tue May 28 19:25:22 +0000 2013 Before. Tue May 28 19:35:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison Indeed! Tue May 28 19:35:37 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison Oh man yes. Painting will be continuing for another couple of days, I think. Here’s hoping the cats behave. Tue May 28 19:38:30 +0000 2013 Hey guess what! At some point in the coming days I should be able to think about something other than real estate or moving! Tue May 28 19:40:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica Backatcha! Tue May 28 19:45:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica I know you must. And I didn’t have to wrangle a small person! Tue May 28 20:04:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @Ed_Raso I’ve been warned that maintenance will go up, but was quoted a pretty minimal annual increase. We’ll see. Tue May 28 20:05:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica They’re still at the old place. Moving makes them very unhappy. I’ll go back and get them later tonight. Tue May 28 20:07:09 +0000 2013 Okay it’s a total mess right now but this apartment is going to rock. Someday. Tue May 28 21:41:43 +0000 2013 I would pretty much pay cash dollars for somebody to come in here and find me a fork. Tue May 28 23:43:05 +0000 2013 Spent 30 mins on the phone with TWC trying to get my internet to work. Gave up on civilization, @ayjay; am eating leftovers with my fingers. Wed May 29 00:51:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @TWC_Help I’m all set now - thanks for asking, though. Wed May 29 00:55:50 +0000 2013 Holy cats. This faster internet is actually faster. Wed May 29 01:00:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall Are they at least synchronized? Wed May 29 01:02:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @dancohen Thanks! They are right on the cusp of doing so. \(Of course, I’m flying to Dallas tomorrow\. But let’s leave that aside\.\) Wed May 29 01:04:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall Now that’s funny. I would never have known that they were floating rather than embedded but for your experiment. Wed May 29 01:07:14 +0000 2013 Seriously, this extreme internet is so fast your tweets arrive before you’ve even sent them. Wed May 29 01:10:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @john_overholt I *know.* I am waiting for the catch. Wed May 29 01:31:45 +0000 2013 Okay, the internet is working and the bed is made. Time to go pick up two sorely pissed-off cats. Wed May 29 01:39:41 +0000 2013 Cats obtained. Now to survive the cab rise home. Wed May 29 02:58:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Hey, happy birthday! I hope it’s been a great one. Wed May 29 03:33:02 +0000 2013 Mission accomplished. \(Phew\.\) Wed May 29 03:33:44 +0000 2013 The first thing to note about the new place? It’s QUIET. After two years of sirens screaming up 3rd Ave, I’d forgotten what that’s like. Wed May 29 03:36:50 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde Park Slope. Wed May 29 03:38:16 +0000 2013 I have found the down side of having movers pack my kitchen: I have fifteen enormous boxes labeled “Kitchen.” Where’s the coffee grinder? Wed May 29 11:30:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @jasonrhody This is Brooklyn. The coffee grinder is on the back of an antique bicycle pedaled by a mustachioed young man. Wed May 29 15:21:23 +0000 2013 Looking forward to it! // RT @hauntologist: Scurrying off to Dallas for @OASymposium. I get to introduce @kfitz! Wed May 29 16:08:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava Oh no. Is all okay? Wed May 29 20:16:20 +0000 2013 Headed to EWR, on my way to UNT for @OASymposium. What are the odds I go to the right apartment when I get back Saturday? Wed May 29 20:17:18 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava No! I’m so sorry to hear that. I hope things work out. :( Wed May 29 20:25:28 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF Yes, alas. Plane lands about 9. May be seeking a nightcap, though. Wed May 29 20:32:34 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF OH NO. Wed May 29 20:36:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF Good grief. Wed May 29 20:48:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison, @pmhswe and @shanakimball Oh god I’m having flashbacks. \(The unpacking has not yet gotten to start\.\) Thu May 30 03:02:40 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava How miserable! Please feel free to go hang out at my place, which is very quiet other than the painter. Thu May 30 12:58:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava \(If a bit disorganized\.\) Thu May 30 12:58:52 +0000 2013 In front of a hotel here in Dallas there is a blinking sign reading “make your JFK reservations now.” I was a little confused at first. + Thu May 30 13:05:38 +0000 2013 But then it hit me: November is the 50th anniversary. I don’t know whether I’m more alarmed by the passage of time or assassination tourism. Thu May 30 13:05:46 +0000 2013 About to deliver the keynote at @OASymposium. Prepare yourselves for some preaching to the choir! #oa13unt Thu May 30 13:46:14 +0000 2013 Replying to @mjpaulusjr and @micahvandegrift It’s Not Enough Coffee for “collections budget.” My bad. Thu May 30 15:34:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @mjpaulusjr and @micahvandegrift Absolutely. Thu May 30 15:55:08 +0000 2013 Humanities: ever the afterthought. #sigh Thu May 30 16:09:07 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF My work here is done. #oa13unt Thu May 30 16:31:59 +0000 2013 Replying to @RohanMaitzen Yeah, that’s not a book review; that’s a book report. Thu May 30 16:34:29 +0000 2013 Replying to @AnvilAcademic Yes! Thank you! Thu May 30 23:19:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava :) Thu May 30 23:24:09 +0000 2013 Replying to @rogerwhitson Your pw for the Commons is the same as the rest of the website! :) Fri May 31 01:43:33 +0000 2013 Replying to @rogerwhitson You’re welcome! If you really lose the pw, instead of logging in at http://mla.org, there’s a link to have it emailed. Fri May 31 02:01:54 +0000 2013 Congrats! // RT @brettbobley: Wow! Scalar authoring platform receives Editor's Choice from PC Mag! Congrats to @tmcphers & @anvcscalar team. Fri May 31 02:47:46 +0000 2013 Argh, RunKeeper. Did not say to share that with the world. If I didn’t love you, this would be grounds for divorce. Fri May 31 13:00:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @sherah1918 Good luck to you, too! Fri May 31 13:01:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench and @sherah1918 Yeah, that’s the worst weight loss plan ever. I can do one that involves exercise, but a “diet” will not happen. Fri May 31 13:18:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison Indeed, but there’s a whole lot of room way south of “great” that would still be a vast improvement. Fri May 31 13:57:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica, @wynkenhimself, @amandafrench, @m_steph_m and @briancroxall We want roundups of all kinds! We love member curated session lists. Fri May 31 14:47:47 +0000 2013 Hey, #mla14 folks: Think about how you can use @MLACommons to prepare, discuss, promote, and extend your sessions. Contact us for help! Fri May 31 15:27:49 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah and @glovink He has uncovered our dastardly plan. Fri May 31 16:40:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall and @samplereality Notebook and ink pen. Fri May 31 16:41:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison No kidding. It’s heartbreaking to see and imagine what could have been! Fri May 31 17:43:24 +0000 2013 Replying to @dorothyk98 and @MLACommons I know it’s been done by a few sessions with permission of all participants, but it’s not something we can handle. Fri May 31 18:04:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @jbrittholbrook, @m_kahn and @jpkarlsberg Ha! It would be lovely to help foster that vision. Fri May 31 18:30:29 +0000 2013 RT @CampusCopyright: Love it! "Symposiums like this are like church camp. You leave all on fire for Jesus and within a week you're fornicat… Fri May 31 18:50:01 +0000 2013 There may have been a nap this afternoon. If so, it was probably really really good. Fri May 31 22:51:28 +0000 2013 Replying to @ayjay Oh, I so understand. Fri May 31 22:57:04 +0000 2013 Replying to @mamamusings Hey, welcome home! Sat Jun 01 01:18:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @educatemissjax and @nytimes I dunno. I’m missing several pounds that sure went somewhere. Sat Jun 01 02:03:16 +0000 2013
docs.title: 2013 05 01 tweets

docs.text: Saturday morning. 6.20 am. Heading home. \(And really hoping to remember which home to head to\.\) Sat Jun 01 11:25:21 +0000 2013 Aaargh left my laptop power cord in the hotel. Happily I have an extra at home for exactly this reason, but aaargh nonetheless. Sat Jun 01 12:46:40 +0000 2013 And I said to myself, as I packed up the laptop, “don’t forget your power cord!” Sat Jun 01 12:48:13 +0000 2013 The moral of the story is that I am slavishly attached to my routines precisely because I am too dumb to function without them. Sat Jun 01 12:49:32 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Exactly. Have just called them. My checkout routine is totally OCD, but when I don’t do it… Sat Jun 01 12:55:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @jbrittholbrook I believe it! The brain thinks it has solved the problem by reminding itself, and so lets it go. Sat Jun 01 12:55:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal The transition was hard on everybody but they seemed to be settling in well when I left & catsitter reports are good! Sat Jun 01 13:05:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal My routine is a final idiot check - just before I walk out of a hotel room, I do an area by area scan for anything belonging to me. Sat Jun 01 13:06:59 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Totally failed to do it today. sigh. Sat Jun 01 13:07:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Whoops! :( Sat Jun 01 13:20:42 +0000 2013 Replying to @JoVanEvery I usually do, too! Too much recent travel has made me lax, though. Must return to obsessive vigilance. Sat Jun 01 13:21:54 +0000 2013 Happily, I checked my boarding pass before heading to my gate at IAH, as I’m flying back into a different airport than I thought. Huh. Sat Jun 01 15:59:42 +0000 2013 RT @BarackObama: Happy LGBT Pride Month. Look at how far we've come this year in the fight for #MarriageEquality. Sat Jun 01 20:23:28 +0000 2013 RT @tezcatlipoca: I'm not good at Pollyanna, and there's lots of seriously fucked up shit right now, and it's political theater I know but… Sat Jun 01 20:23:31 +0000 2013 RT @tezcatlipoca: …for 1 minute I'm going to be happy our non-white president just tweeted a happy pride month message. Sat Jun 01 20:23:37 +0000 2013 Home. And even went to the right one! Sat Jun 01 21:12:55 +0000 2013 Best part so far: the painting is done, so furniture can be put in the proper rooms, and unpacking can commence! Sat Jun 01 21:14:40 +0000 2013 Least great part so far: it’s freaking hot, and I have not yet had the chance to get air conditioners. Sat Jun 01 21:15:13 +0000 2013 I did, however, find a pair of shorts. So there’s that. Sat Jun 01 21:16:31 +0000 2013 Aw, sadness! // RT @TenuredRadical: Boy the way Glenn Miller played....goodbye, Jean Stapleton/Edith Bunker \("But Ahhh\-chie\!"\) #teeveelegend Sat Jun 01 21:17:32 +0000 2013 The good news! Is I found the coffee grinder! The bad news. Is I have found neither the kettle nor another water-boiling vessel. Sun Jun 02 02:07:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @mkgold and @jvinopal Thanks for answering, Matt; I was offline all yesterday! Mon Jun 03 11:46:50 +0000 2013 Moving update \(because I know you’re dying for one\) : The apartment is about 50% unpacked, I think. It’s still awesome in a prospective way. Mon Jun 03 11:50:39 +0000 2013 However! The heat this weekend was not kind to life on the top floor. Must order air conditioners today. Mon Jun 03 11:51:37 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym No, we’re actually very much the same. This move has been MISERABLE for exactly that reason. + Mon Jun 03 12:09:26 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym I got moved in Tues nt late & had to leave town Weds. Got back Sat afternoon & did nothing but unpack for the rest of the wknd. Mon Jun 03 12:10:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym Kitchen is just about done. Rest of the house is a disaster still. Mon Jun 03 12:10:43 +0000 2013 Replying to @sherah1918 Am hoping so! It’s taking a LONG TIME to cool off in here, though. \(And guess what: I’m traveling tomorrow\!\) Mon Jun 03 12:11:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah More or less, yeah. Mon Jun 03 12:17:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @sleonchnm Yay, congrats! Mon Jun 03 13:38:20 +0000 2013 RT @MLACommons: Hey, #mla14 session organizers: if you’ve created a site on @MLACommons for your session, let us know about it. We’re ga ... Mon Jun 03 20:39:30 +0000 2013 As a colleague of mine said yesterday, if it’s Tuesday, it must be DC. Hello, Amtrak! Tue Jun 04 13:53:37 +0000 2013 How nice to be welcomed home. // RT @Amtrak: @kfitz Hello there! And welcome back to the tracks. Enjoy the ride. Tue Jun 04 13:58:15 +0000 2013 Replying to @jcdrg Thank you, Jean-Claude! Tue Jun 04 14:14:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat Meaning what should the student look at first? This: http://www.mla.org/tenure_promotion Tue Jun 04 15:37:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat If you’re thinking in another direction, though, let me know! Tue Jun 04 15:37:21 +0000 2013 Hello, DC! You’re very shiny today. Tue Jun 04 16:56:34 +0000 2013 Replying to @patrick_mj Srsly. Tue Jun 04 17:26:28 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Not much, though; it’s darn well perfect out there! Tue Jun 04 17:26:49 +0000 2013 Thanks to everybody at #LCI2013 for a great conversation this afternoon! I hope the rest of the week is fruitful for you. Wed Jun 05 01:30:56 +0000 2013 Replying to @noctambulate Do Not Open So We Can Charge You For A Service Call. Wed Jun 05 16:17:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @GeorgeOnline I use Backblaze; it’s been very reliable. Wed Jun 05 17:25:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @dorothyk98 I’m afraid not; it has not been coordinated by us. You might ask around. Wed Jun 05 19:59:47 +0000 2013 RT @MikeTaylor: "The CHORUS strategy is to take OA out of the hands of researchers, so publishers control timetable & infrastructure" http:… Thu Jun 06 17:19:39 +0000 2013 Replying to @backblaze Thank YOU. You’ve got a great product, which has given me great peace of mind! Thu Jun 06 18:36:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @sherah1918 Also that there’s no explanation of the clear po-boy outlier. Thu Jun 06 18:59:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @sherah1918 Indeed. \(Of course, everyone except “crawfish” is just wrong\.\) Thu Jun 06 19:03:15 +0000 2013 Indeed! // RT @tmcphers: Happy to be at Old Dominion with @kfitz and @hollyw discussing the future of grad education. Thanks, Avi Santo. Thu Jun 06 19:57:20 +0000 2013 My new apartment is 2 blocks from an amazing farmers market. Since I moved, I have not been home on a Saturday to take advantage of it. Sad! Sat Jun 08 13:26:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @dandyroll Yes! Someday I’ll actually get to go. Sat Jun 08 17:58:36 +0000 2013 While I remain very sad to have missed #thatcamp yet again this year, I am very happy to have finished unpacking! Sun Jun 09 19:53:38 +0000 2013 Well, everything except hanging the pictures, that is. Sun Jun 09 19:53:50 +0000 2013 And dealing with the 39 boxes of books that should be arriving from California. #uhoh Sun Jun 09 19:54:17 +0000 2013 I will not combust. I will not combust. I will not combust. I will not combust. Mon Jun 10 20:41:30 +0000 2013 Replying to @alicedaer A new mantra, for a new day. Mon Jun 10 20:52:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself FURY. Or really, really deep frustration. Mon Jun 10 21:02:45 +0000 2013 Frustration followed me home. I give up, day. Let's try again tomorrow. Mon Jun 10 23:14:31 +0000 2013 Do you think it’s a coincidence that my Time Capsule died yesterday? Tue Jun 11 11:27:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate Thinking I may just get a new extreme. Am backing up elsewise these days. Tue Jun 11 12:53:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @melissaterras I'm a little behind the curve, but HUGE congratulations, and well deserved! Tue Jun 11 16:22:04 +0000 2013 Aw man. RT @distractify: 1. Lie down 2. Try not to cry 3. Cry a lot :'( Tue Jun 11 16:32:40 +0000 2013 I have done ALL THE THINGS. \(Ok, I have done three of the things\. But it’s the most productive day in a while, so I’m celebrating anyhow\.\) Tue Jun 11 21:24:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Oh, one of my faves! Tue Jun 11 22:09:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @FrostDavis Hey, congratulations! That’s great news. Wed Jun 12 20:10:31 +0000 2013 It's possible that I'm too easily impressed at the end of a long day. But Amazon's systems just made my head explode. + Thu Jun 13 01:00:26 +0000 2013 I was due for a regular subscription shipment today. And I'd placed a totally separate order for a couple of other things two days ago. + Thu Jun 13 01:00:37 +0000 2013 Both arrived today in the same box. It was like crossing the streams, man. - Thu Jun 13 01:00:44 +0000 2013 Got up early this morning to go run. Let a light rain persuade me not to. Wound up writing instead. A reasonable tradeoff, I think. Thu Jun 13 11:29:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym That is a very strange association. Thu Jun 13 13:26:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @samplereality and @surlyF I see your Outlook, and raise you Lotus Notes. (Now replaced, thank goodness. So I can access my email anywhere. + Thu Jun 13 22:49:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @samplereality and @surlyF And so can the NSA!) Thu Jun 13 22:50:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself That... I... I mean... Fri Jun 14 17:55:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself I am still sputtering. Fri Jun 14 17:56:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @jbj That guy is really prolific! Fri Jun 14 17:57:27 +0000 2013 Replying to @CapitolClio and @wynkenhimself "Ladies are everywhere in publishing! At the reception desk, in the copy room, bringing me my coffee..." Fri Jun 14 18:04:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @CapitolClio and @wynkenhimself I'm also really curious what experience led him to the conclusion that The Most Sexist Industry Ever is theater. Fri Jun 14 18:18:31 +0000 2013 So here’s a surprise: when I spend a full week in the office, I manage to get a lot done. I might have to try this more often! Fri Jun 14 19:57:53 +0000 2013 Replying to @CathyNDavidson “Get Things Done… By Having Time to Do Them!” Fri Jun 14 19:59:43 +0000 2013 No, srsly: I accomplished ALL THE THINGS today. Is it planetary alignment? Or just that everybody knows I’m headed out for several weeks? Fri Jun 14 21:43:26 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench Makes all too much sense - and yet seems impossible to induce artificially. Fri Jun 14 21:53:43 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench \(Though several huge pieces of info I was waiting on to close out projects arrived today too\. So it wasn’t just my mindset\.\) Fri Jun 14 21:55:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench I know, right? Fri Jun 14 23:11:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench How very disciplined of you, heh heh. Fri Jun 14 23:11:32 +0000 2013 This summer’s adventure: Geneva, Göttingen, London, Prague. Three conferences, followed by a lot of being still and writing. Starts today! Mon Jun 17 13:37:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @daniellynds Prague is the being still and writing part! Mon Jun 17 13:38:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Thank you! I certainly hope to. Mon Jun 17 13:40:10 +0000 2013 Hoping I’m not forgetting anything crucial. Here we go! Mon Jun 17 17:52:12 +0000 2013 I cannot for the life of me figure out how to get an article linked on FB into Pocket using my iPhone. I am not that dumb, really. Mon Jun 17 18:12:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @finnarne I was afraid of that. You know, if I copy a link and open Pocket, it would be awesome if it would ask if I want a new item w/it. Mon Jun 17 18:21:31 +0000 2013 The suitcases people try to get away with carrying onto planes, I swear. Mon Jun 17 21:28:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @sramsay Just saw a woman go by w/a hard-sided suitcase too wide to fit in the aisle. How she plans to get it in the overhead is a mystery. Mon Jun 17 21:33:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @sramsay I hear you. Like it hasn’t been 12 years or anything. Mon Jun 17 21:33:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @claudiakincaid That Is Perfect. Mon Jun 17 21:37:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @sramsay SERIOUSLY. The fee should apply to overhead bin space, if anything. \(I’d pay it\.\) Mon Jun 17 21:37:50 +0000 2013 Replying to @sleonchnm Now *that’s* packing light. Mon Jun 17 21:38:19 +0000 2013 I hate to mention the status of the seat next to mine on this very full plane, because I’m pretty sure I cannot possibly be this lucky. Mon Jun 17 21:39:44 +0000 2013 But they’re closing the overhead bins. And fussing about electronic devices. #breathheld Mon Jun 17 21:40:55 +0000 2013 There is a duty-free crisis taking place. Someone has someone else’s bourbon. #travelfun Mon Jun 17 21:49:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @sleonchnm Thank you! Mon Jun 17 21:59:41 +0000 2013 As I suspected: delayed incoming flight, and 22B is now occupied. But the fixed armrest means it would only have been magazine space anyhow. Mon Jun 17 22:02:59 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley Nice! Mon Jun 17 22:05:29 +0000 2013 Pulled away from gate 20 mins late. Stopped about 100 meters away. Waited for new flight plan. Now waiting for storm to pass, engines off. Mon Jun 17 22:50:57 +0000 2013 At least I can complain about it. Mon Jun 17 22:51:25 +0000 2013 Ok, out. Fingers crossed. Mon Jun 17 22:58:37 +0000 2013 Thoughts so far: Geneva is pretty! And it’s an easy city to arrive in, with free public transit vouchers. \(And then there was a 4 hour nap\.\) Tue Jun 18 13:03:56 +0000 2013 Replying to @JoVanEvery Ooh, good to know -- thanks! Tue Jun 18 13:10:19 +0000 2013 RT @dancohen: Digital Public Library of America partners with HathiTrust, adds 3.5 million books to its growing collection: http://t.co/k66… Tue Jun 18 15:09:17 +0000 2013 RT @dancohen: PressForward releases a WordPress plugin that's like Google Reader w/ a workflow for editors to select best articles: http://… Tue Jun 18 15:09:52 +0000 2013 New post at Planned Obsolescence: Summer 2013. http://wp.me/p2w8Sh-DG Tue Jun 18 16:23:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @jtheibault It’ll be less tourism than writing retreat, but it should be fantastic nonetheless. Definitely worth trying! Tue Jun 18 17:01:15 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself That guy. I swear to god. Tue Jun 18 18:38:56 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself I assume the latter as well, but the former is more fun to consider. What's the end game in that option? Tue Jun 18 18:48:16 +0000 2013 I have had ALL the caffeine today but still jet lag is kicking my butt, to the extent that I’m not sure I’ll make it through the afternoon. Wed Jun 19 11:40:57 +0000 2013 Felt awful enough that I headed back to the hotel to lie down. Not sure whether I'll make it out for this evening's reception at CERN. Wed Jun 19 14:15:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate Thanks! It's just the usual killer jet lag, about which I am very whiny. Wed Jun 19 14:19:09 +0000 2013 Well, *I* made it out to CERN. The rest of the conference not so much. A nice reception for me, though! Wed Jun 19 16:17:38 +0000 2013 Replying to @shannonmattern How about this: Geneva gives hotel guests free public transit for the duration of their stay. Wed Jun 19 18:45:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate Just in general \(which would be well\-deserved\) or in response to something specific? Wed Jun 19 18:47:38 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate OH! Super cool! Go @ekansa! Wed Jun 19 18:48:01 +0000 2013 Good morning, Geneva! That night’s sleep? That was what I was talking about. Thu Jun 20 03:41:19 +0000 2013 New post at Planned Obsolescence: Getting Back to \(My Own\) Work http://wp.me/p2w8Sh-DL Thu Jun 20 13:15:29 +0000 2013 This picture was taken at 8pm. There was a hailstorm at about 4pm. It’s been about 70 degrees all evening. #crazytown Thu Jun 20 18:20:45 +0000 2013 Will confess to a teeny bit of envy over having to miss attending the WH Champions of Change event, but so happy for @ekansa and @WillNoel! Fri Jun 21 04:52:08 +0000 2013 New post at Planned Obsolescence: Eleven. http://wp.me/s2w8Sh-eleven Fri Jun 21 08:38:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @tezcatlipoca That storm was crazy; the hail here caused a bunch of damage. \(I’m in Geneva\.\) Fri Jun 21 08:58:24 +0000 2013 Replying to @tezcatlipoca Enjoy your vacation! Between it being 93 degrees two days ago and the hail yesterday, it’s a good time to be away. Fri Jun 21 09:07:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @tezcatlipoca Sadly, no; I’m here for OAI 8: http://indico.cern.ch/conferenceDisplay.py?confId=211600 Fri Jun 21 09:07:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @tezcatlipoca Thank you! It’s been lovely being here. I head off to Germany tomorrow, but will hope to come back sometime. Fri Jun 21 09:10:04 +0000 2013 Yes, definitely! // RT @openmarin: @kfitz quotes only UK/US examples in DH. Still work to do to make the whole DH world visible. #oai8 Fri Jun 21 09:19:18 +0000 2013 Replying to @openmarin Absolutely. I’m also very hopeful about the work being done by GO::DH. http://www.globaloutlookdh.org/ Fri Jun 21 09:25:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @openmarin Thanks for the links! I’ll look forward to exploring possible cooperation & collaboration... Fri Jun 21 09:43:25 +0000 2013 Replying to @RLUK_David Thank *you*! I was thrilled to be involved. #oai8 Fri Jun 21 10:01:04 +0000 2013 Replying to @RLUK_David and @tweeterpeter I doubt it has legal status \(yet\) , but here’s a project working to codify: http://opendefinition.org/ Fri Jun 21 10:51:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @PaulaKrebs and @J_C_Hayes Hmmm. Perhaps the folks behind REKn might have a suggestion? http://english.uvic.ca/newsandevents/rekn_joins_world-leading_nines_initiative_arc.html Fri Jun 21 12:29:50 +0000 2013 Thanks to everyone for a great #OAI8. Geneva was lovely and the conversations were excellent. On to Göttingen! Sat Jun 22 07:16:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @tmccormick The conference organizers should be posting them, if they haven’t already. Mon Jun 24 07:00:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @BendProf I can't swear to it, but @MichaelBerube1 implicates me in the history of his decision to blog, and I started in 2002, so... Tue Jun 25 21:24:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @pannapacker, @BendProf and @MichaelBerube1 Absolutely. Also 2002-2003ish. Tue Jun 25 21:41:14 +0000 2013 Instant tears: Equal protection, not states’ rights. #scotus #doma Wed Jun 26 14:07:14 +0000 2013 From @SCOTUSblog: “DOMA is unconstitutional as a deprivation of the equal liberty of persons that is protected by the Fifth Amendment.” Wed Jun 26 14:09:15 +0000 2013 Indeed. // RT @katinalynn: Overjoyed about DOMA. Heartsick about VRA. What a crazy couple of days. Wed Jun 26 14:28:50 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF The ruling is not just that SCOTUS doesn’t have standing, it’s that the 9th didn’t have standing. Wed Jun 26 14:32:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF At least if I’m following the logic, which is not a cinch. Wed Jun 26 14:33:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @JMarkOckerbloom and @surlyF Yes, that. Wed Jun 26 14:36:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @nicolaz and @kelleyeskridge Happy anniversary! Wed Jun 26 14:59:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @ernestopriego Sunday, noonish. Very much looking forward! Wed Jun 26 15:47:51 +0000 2013 Me too! // RT @ernestopriego: Really looking forward to the #OpenAccess monographs in HSS conference @britishlibrary https://www.jisc-collections.ac.uk/JISC-Collections-events/oabooksconf/OAbooksprogramme/ Thu Jun 27 09:55:56 +0000 2013 RT @gwenifill: This. MT @agolis: Within 24 hrs of VRA strike down, 6 of the 9 affected states are pushing new voter ID laws: http://t.co/w… Thu Jun 27 12:38:20 +0000 2013 Replying to @tezcatlipoca My naming system started when I got a 2nd monitor at work & named the computer “starbase.” My home desktop became “homebase.”+ Thu Jun 27 12:50:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @tezcatlipoca My laptop became “voyager.” And so when I got my iPhone it was “rover.” Thu Jun 27 12:51:38 +0000 2013 RT @lkonstan: Check out @LAReviewofBooks's "Distant Reading" symposium, featuring @kfitz, @JimEnglishPenn, & Alexander Galloway. http://t.c… Thu Jun 27 13:47:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @veek Evidence is all anecdotal, as pubs don’t much share sales info. Also, hard to know what sales would have been otherwise. + Thu Jun 27 14:33:41 +0000 2013 Replying to @veek That said, sales of PO were a good bit higher than I think NYUP expected, and I’ve heard the same from other authors/presses. Thu Jun 27 14:34:10 +0000 2013 Replying to @veek I haven’t heard that about books. I have heard claims that going OA with journal articles has resulted in declining subscriptions. Thu Jun 27 14:36:42 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley and @sramsay I’m going to assume that you checked that tweet and the baggage handlers have only just found it. Thu Jun 27 21:05:33 +0000 2013 To all my friends who are currently moving \(e\.g\., @msbonn @ayjay @xina, etc\) , oh, I feel ya. Sending good vibes and fast friendly movers. Thu Jun 27 21:29:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @katinalynn and @mkgold Mind. Blown. Thu Jun 27 22:48:23 +0000 2013 How am I only now figuring out that NYU is an eduroam member? Fri Jun 28 07:35:55 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym and @maximolly Now I feel like I fell down on the job: I’ve been in at least one room with both of you! Glad you’ve finally met. Fri Jun 28 13:55:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @nancybaym and @maximolly Apparently! I was clearly not thinking. Fri Jun 28 13:57:51 +0000 2013 RT @Jisc: Free 2 day conference next week at @britishlibrary - Open Access Monographs in Humanities & Social Sciences http://t.co/cp0FeaNJN… Fri Jun 28 14:06:07 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall No one will miss “of.” Fri Jun 28 14:45:26 +0000 2013 Headed back in for day 2 of Cultures of Obsolescence, including my own talk first thing: http://www.uni-goettingen.de/de/419609.html Sat Jun 29 06:40:56 +0000 2013 Afterward, quick dinner and a train to Frankfurt. Tomorrow morning, a flight to London. Monday & Tuesday, #OAbooks: https://www.jisc-collections.ac.uk/JISC-Collections-events/oabooksconf/OAbooksprogramme/ Sat Jun 29 06:42:34 +0000 2013 Wednesday, a flight to Prague, with no plan other than to be still and get a ton of reading and writing done. #vacation #allieverwanted Sat Jun 29 06:43:26 +0000 2013 Replying to @ahacker Hi! I’m on the 9.55 am Lufthansa flight. Sat Jun 29 12:07:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @ahacker Thanks -- you too! Sat Jun 29 12:55:13 +0000 2013 Running on 3 nights in a row of 4 hours or less sleep. \(Not by choice\.\) Thank goodness for European hotel breakfasts. Sun Jun 30 05:23:31 +0000 2013 Today, FRA > LHR. Tomorrow and Tuesday, @britishlibrary for #OAbooks. http://www.jisc-collections.ac.uk/JISC-Collections-events/oabooksconf/ Sun Jun 30 05:39:19 +0000 2013 Hello, London! You're very shiny and warm today. Sun Jun 30 11:55:39 +0000 2013 My room is on the back side of the hotel \(& so is quiet\!\) , but the view by the elevators on my floor looks like this. Sun Jun 30 15:17:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @ernestopriego Why, thank you! It’s very London-y around here. \(Except for all the sun and warmth, I guess\.\) Sun Jun 30 15:19:25 +0000 2013 Replying to @ernestopriego Seriously cool! Sun Jun 30 15:19:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah Ooh, do tell. Could do with a good dinner. Sun Jun 30 15:33:21 +0000 2013 Replying to @Ed_Raso It is inspiring, isn’t it! I’m here for a conference at the BL. Sun Jun 30 15:37:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @Ed_Raso You know it. Sun Jun 30 15:41:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah OOH. Sold. Sun Jun 30 15:43:27 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah Indeed! I hope things are swell where you are. Sun Jun 30 15:47:53 +0000 2013
docs.title: 2013 06 01 tweets

docs.text: Replying to @shanakimball I missed you arriving here. Quite the crowd! Mon Jul 01 10:30:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Yay, welcome! I hope the trip was okay. Mon Jul 01 10:31:34 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Promise. I finally got a decent night’s sleep last night, so am beginning to emerge from zombiedom myself. Mon Jul 01 10:33:59 +0000 2013 Replying to @carenmilloy Works out very nicely indeed! #OAbooks Mon Jul 01 10:40:30 +0000 2013 RT @ernestopriego: If ECRs don't risk, how will scholarship ever change? Replication of cons methods won't get us there. Risk should be rew… Mon Jul 01 11:10:55 +0000 2013 LS rep at #oabooks says it’s not realistic for societies to run repositories. My response: societies must explore partnering with libraries! Mon Jul 01 11:34:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Say it isn’t so. Mon Jul 01 13:12:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself I have a family full of Italian Americans. Might they count? Mon Jul 01 13:16:09 +0000 2013 Frances Pinter on @KUnlatched model: consortium lowers risks for all; “you have no idea how expensive it is to keep things closed.” #oabooks Mon Jul 01 13:28:49 +0000 2013 Replying to @ernestopriego You are the BEST. Mon Jul 01 15:53:15 +0000 2013 RT @KUnlatched: The Mellon-funded report on open review @kfitz presented about is available at http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/mcpress/open-review/files/2012/06/MediaCommons_Open_Review_White_Paper_final.pdf #oabooks Mon Jul 01 15:55:50 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Awwww! Mon Jul 01 15:56:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @researchremix I was sorry to miss you at #OAI8, but I understand you had other awesome places to be! Mon Jul 01 15:57:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball, @LibSkrat and @researchremix Indeed! Someday soon, I hope. Mon Jul 01 16:01:24 +0000 2013 Replying to @pomonacollege Very nice! The white background opens things up, the menus/sliders are less complex, and I like the use of the new mark. Mon Jul 01 16:34:37 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball, @researchremix and @LibSkrat I like this idea. Mon Jul 01 16:36:30 +0000 2013 Wait, what? // RT @TheAtlantic: First-ever human head transplant is now possible, says neuroscientist http://theatln.tc/15bcjfN via @qz Mon Jul 01 16:41:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @ekansa, @TheAtlantic and @qz But I guess we’d finally answer the question of whether identity is resident in the brain or the body. Mon Jul 01 16:47:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @RLUK_Mike Very nice -- I like that framing of the question. Mon Jul 01 16:50:15 +0000 2013 Replying to @j_w_baker and @tmccormick A very fair point. Expectations for participation in any community of practice need to be established. #oabooks Mon Jul 01 16:52:38 +0000 2013 Replying to @tmccormick But at least in open review communities any homophily that crops up is visible and can be challenged. #oabooks Mon Jul 01 16:53:58 +0000 2013 Hear, hear! // MT @jfwinters: Great first day of #OAbooks… Congratulations to @carenmilloy & colleagues for putting it all together. Mon Jul 01 16:56:46 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley Yeah, it’s pretty mind-blowing. And I’m not trying to be punny. Mon Jul 01 17:01:18 +0000 2013 The thing I can’t wrap my head around \(ha ha\) is who you would be after the transplant. We’d finally know where selfhood is located I guess. Mon Jul 01 17:02:35 +0000 2013 @IntarchEditor Yes -- we’ve definitely found that authors have to participate in the process in very different ways when it’s open. Mon Jul 01 17:03:36 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah No, it’s attachment at the spinal cord. o.O Mon Jul 01 17:04:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @IntarchEditor Some are, it’s true. But it would be great to get the community in that subject area really working together openly! #oabooks Mon Jul 01 17:13:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley Will do -- should see one or the other shortly. Mon Jul 01 17:13:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @ivovolt How did we get people to participate? Twitter, FB, blog posts, email lists - everything we could think of! #oabooks Mon Jul 01 17:16:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde But some recent arguments have suggested that identity inheres in the body rather than strictly in the brain \(e\.g\. muscle memory\) . Mon Jul 01 17:18:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde Such a transplant would at least let us know for sure. Wake the patient up & ask “who are you now?” Mon Jul 01 17:19:10 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde True! Though in that case only the spinal cord has been severed; other systems remain connected, if some function with assistance. Mon Jul 01 17:21:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde \(You realize of course I’m being somewhat tendentious here\. My own personal feeling is that I am in my brain\.\) Mon Jul 01 17:22:33 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde \(But I would say that, wouldn’t I?\) Mon Jul 01 17:22:40 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde Indeed. Mon Jul 01 17:27:25 +0000 2013 Replying to @ivovolt That’s a huge challenge; we talk about it a bit in the full report. Something serious to consider. #oabooks Mon Jul 01 21:32:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @ivovolt It’s downloadable at http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/mcpress/open-review/ Mon Jul 01 22:43:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @ivovolt It got tweeted, but I totally failed to mention it - so you only missed it by paying attention. :) Mon Jul 01 22:50:07 +0000 2013 Replying to @crsbecker I once had one with a telephone-booth-style door, which opened by folding inward. I almost didn’t make it out. Tue Jul 02 11:15:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @crsbecker The best part was when I had your bottle-drop moment, and I had to open the door to bend over, and couldn’t. Tue Jul 02 12:42:24 +0000 2013 You know what I want? A hashtag resolver that tells you what a cryptic acronym stands for. Tue Jul 02 14:15:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava Okay, so then what I want is an API and a Twitter client that pulls up the def on rollover. Tue Jul 02 14:30:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @martin_eve Oh! Sorry I missed you. Tue Jul 02 14:30:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @warnick Hmm. I really want it client-scrapeable, so that I can rollover a hashtag & have the def appear... Tue Jul 02 14:32:20 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava Exactly. Or a tooltip on the desktop. Tue Jul 02 14:32:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava Yes! As you get when you click on a username. Tue Jul 02 14:34:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @warnick It’s true. Wikipedia-style disambiguation would not be out of the question. \(Groups using \#MLA13 include…\) Tue Jul 02 14:36:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @warnick ON IT. Tue Jul 02 14:37:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @jbj People who subtweet deserve what they get. #youknowwhoyouare Tue Jul 02 14:39:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @WmGermano Nice. Messy, but nice. Tue Jul 02 14:40:34 +0000 2013 @ahacker Re your question this morning: http://academicblogs.org might be a good place to find some worthy examples! Tue Jul 02 15:04:15 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall and @billwolff Right. But what I’m really after is a client that could scrape that data & on clicking on a hashtag display both + Tue Jul 02 15:05:07 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall and @billwolff tweets & info about the tag, as many clients do for users. Tue Jul 02 15:05:33 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall and @billwolff My feed is full of them today, I know that for sure. Tue Jul 02 15:07:20 +0000 2013 Now up at #oabooks: @shanakimball on MPublishing, library-based publishing, and library-press collaborations. Tue Jul 02 15:09:04 +0000 2013 Replying to @triplingual, @briancroxall and @billwolff Exactly. Wikipedia-style: #MLA13 is being used by... Tue Jul 02 15:10:29 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley and @shanakimball is on another call right now. Can I take a message and have her get back to you? Tue Jul 02 15:14:16 +0000 2013 Replying to @savasavasava and @shanakimball :) Tue Jul 02 15:21:31 +0000 2013 Wow. Just discovered that my former work email account was deleted July 1 on the dot. Am having a bit of a moment here. #endofanera Tue Jul 02 15:32:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @captain_primate It has been for temporary folk. I know of no 15-year faculty whose accounts have been summarily cut off like this. Tue Jul 02 15:34:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah Who the hell knows? All I got was a form letter, and no indication that the cutoff would be immediate. Tue Jul 02 15:35:18 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah (Frankly, all I’ve been getting there for the last year is stuff I really don’t want, so it’s all good. But there’s a bit of + Tue Jul 02 15:36:28 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah don’t let the door hit you in the ass on the way out that’s a little surprising.) Tue Jul 02 15:37:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall and @captain_primate I’ve been picking up via my gmail acct for a while, which is how I found out authentication was failing. Tue Jul 02 15:37:43 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall and @captain_primate “User unknown.” Snif. Tue Jul 02 15:37:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah I suspect it’s not forwarding; hasn’t appeared yet, anyhow. Let me know if you get an error response. Tue Jul 02 15:41:34 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah Things have been great here. Didn’t make it to StP for bubbles, but am having dinner tonight at a friend’s club. Tue Jul 02 15:42:56 +0000 2013 RT @shanakimball: Really admiring the chutzpah and grace of @CameronNeylon in speaking off the cuff from a deck of 60 randomized slides #OA… Tue Jul 02 15:47:00 +0000 2013 RT @ernestopriego: .@CameronNeylon: |the point of the book, the tweet, the blog post is to engage people, to make people think" #oabooks Tue Jul 02 15:47:57 +0000 2013 Replying to @stephharves I’ve been picking up the PO mail via my Gmail acct for a while, so I have the old stuff. + Tue Jul 02 15:50:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @stephharves And for the last while, the only mail I’ve gotten there was stuff I didn’t want to get anyway. + Tue Jul 02 15:50:28 +0000 2013 Replying to @stephharves But I just want to say: 15 YEARS. Ending with a form email from the dean’s office, and a deleted email address. Tue Jul 02 15:51:16 +0000 2013 .@CameronNeylon: The “unprecedented good” served by open access is not just a public good, but a good for scholarship. #oabooks Tue Jul 02 15:52:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah and @stephharves That checklist is the form email I got from the dean’s office. Wow, this year went fast! Be sure to turn in your keys! Tue Jul 02 16:05:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @mmwwah No, but there was the suggestion that I needed to account for/turn in all materials bought with college or dept funds. Tue Jul 02 16:29:25 +0000 2013 @ernestopriego You should NOT have! Thank you, though it’s really way too much! Tue Jul 02 21:32:58 +0000 2013 Replying to @ernestopriego The good news is that now *I* owe *you* a drink, next time I see you. I will look forward to it. Tue Jul 02 21:40:04 +0000 2013 Replying to @CJ_Daugherty I am so totally it! Tue Jul 02 23:35:50 +0000 2013 Replying to @CJ_Daugherty You look fabulous, too! It was great catching up - I’ll look forward to doing it again soon. Tue Jul 02 23:46:06 +0000 2013 Farewell, London. It was lovely! But I am ready to drag my heavy suitcase somewhere I can unpack and stay put. Wed Jul 03 07:57:21 +0000 2013 Independence Day! My summer rest and writing retreat begins, appropriately, today. Thu Jul 04 05:31:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Woo-hoo! Safe travels, & all my best to Brooklyn. Thu Jul 04 08:46:42 +0000 2013 Replying to @ayjay Looks like oleander, but I'm not really the person to ask. Thu Jul 04 15:23:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball O hai! :) Fri Jul 05 13:28:33 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall 2014?!? That’s insane. @emoryhealthcare Fri Jul 05 14:11:39 +0000 2013 My #oabooks talk: “advan­cing toward its goal with the effi­ciency of a war machine.” I am both flattered and alarmed http://blog.homo-numericus.net/article11208.html Mon Jul 08 08:25:00 +0000 2013 Replying to @piotrr70 Thank you! I did know it was positive; I just had this image of myself crushing everything in my path that was a bit surprising. Mon Jul 08 09:54:49 +0000 2013 Made a turn today in the thing I’m writing. The point I’m now working toward is a bit controversial, which makes me think it’s worth saying. Mon Jul 08 14:37:32 +0000 2013 Replying to @patriksv It’s always nice to figure out that it’s not just empty blather! Mon Jul 08 14:38:33 +0000 2013 I have just discovered that it’s possible to take a good picture of me if I don’t know you’re doing it. New avatars may be forthcoming. Mon Jul 08 15:04:09 +0000 2013 Okay, done. New avatars should be propagating. Imagine me telling an auditorium full of scientists about the digital humanities. Mon Jul 08 15:38:22 +0000 2013 Replying to @wraabe I’ve been fretting a bit about how little my avatar resembled me anymore. \(Okay, I haven’t been fretting about it \*that\* much, but\.\) Mon Jul 08 15:51:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @acavender I was *very* persuasive. Mon Jul 08 16:17:37 +0000 2013 Replying to @nmhouston Thanks! The growing disjuncture between actual me and turquoise-jacket me was taking up far more brain power than it was worth. Mon Jul 08 16:18:41 +0000 2013 @BendProf I was so traumatized by the experience of driving my cats to LAX when we moved to NYC that I may never recover. \(Them too, alas\.\) Tue Jul 09 16:20:39 +0000 2013 Replying to @BendProf Which is to say that the part of moving to my new apt I dreaded most was putting the cats in the carriers to cab them to Brooklyn. Tue Jul 09 16:23:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @BendProf Eh. In the scheme of things, it’s easy. But nothing’s as easy as a pixie that needs no blow-drying. Tue Jul 09 16:27:37 +0000 2013 Replying to @BendProf SO MUCH. And I was busy trying to keep the crazy cab driver from realizing I had not one but two cats with me… Trauma all around. Tue Jul 09 16:28:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @rrkennison Hey thanks! Tue Jul 09 16:29:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @BendProf It’s *okay* if I don’t blow-dry it, but only okay. Much preferable if I do. I’m not sure how long I’ll be able to deal with that! Tue Jul 09 18:33:31 +0000 2013 Replying to @jbj and @wynkenhimself Ooh! **begins to plot** Tue Jul 09 18:36:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @dancohen and @jbj Drop and give me 20, Cohen! Tue Jul 09 18:43:11 +0000 2013 Replying to @dancohen and @jbj Oh wait. That was “Drill Instructor Fitz.” My bad. Tue Jul 09 18:43:29 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @BendProf Alas, my hair has not fully made the move to wavy. It’s 90% straight, with a couple of weird bends. Tue Jul 09 18:53:37 +0000 2013 I’ve just watched the first half of my #OAbooks talk \(http://bit\.ly/12VLnLS\) & am exhausted. Must moderate coffee intake. Tue Jul 09 19:45:43 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Aw, thanks! Tue Jul 09 19:47:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @triplingual It’s Arras. I did a little modification, I think, but honestly I can’t remember what... Wed Jul 10 15:59:25 +0000 2013 Replying to @triplingual My pleasure! Wed Jul 10 16:03:49 +0000 2013 Over the course of the last two days, I have migrated just about everything running at http://machines.pomona.edu to http://machines.plannedobsolescence.net. + Thu Jul 11 14:29:34 +0000 2013 I even managed to get the MarxWiki \(http://machines\.plannedobsolescence\.net/marxwiki\) and the DFW Wiki \(http://machines\.plannedobsolescence\.net/dfwwiki\) running properly again. + Thu Jul 11 14:29:39 +0000 2013 I’m very grateful to the instructional technologists at Pomona, who gave me extra time for the migration and will be redirecting traffic. + Thu Jul 11 14:29:44 +0000 2013 But if you link to machines, please note the update: http://machines.plannedobsolescence.net. Thu Jul 11 14:29:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself It might have come up in the Q&A. The "don't ask" argument comes in part from Susan Bielstein, Permissions: A Survival Guide. Thu Jul 11 20:18:06 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat and @wynkenhimself That's a shame. Bielstein's argument is dead-on: every time we ask, we build precedent, a sense that we should ask. Thu Jul 11 20:22:26 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat and @wynkenhimself And that sense of common practice can bear as much weight as actual law. Thu Jul 11 20:23:22 +0000 2013 A very happy birthday to @JenHoward and @miriamkp! July 12 is an auspicious day. Fri Jul 12 13:37:26 +0000 2013 Replying to @profwernimont ON IT. :) \(cc @wynkenhimself\) Fri Jul 12 15:05:49 +0000 2013 Replying to @profwernimont and @wynkenhimself Not in any detail. But we are hard at work on the future of MLA style and its associated habitus! Fri Jul 12 15:12:41 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall Waffling? WAFFLING?!? I have changed my avatar once in the last 3 years? I think? And tried to prepare everyone for it. Fri Jul 12 16:12:40 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall Really! That one dates back to my pre-MLA sabbatical. Fri Jul 12 16:14:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @briancroxall At least yours still looks something like you. I’ve noted the surprise of late when people first meet me in person. Fri Jul 12 16:14:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @briancroxall Dude. I can’t believe you remember that. It was a total selfie. Paris, summer of 2008! Fri Jul 12 16:16:14 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @briancroxall Getting to work on time is terribly overrated. Fri Jul 12 16:18:09 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @briancroxall Both, I think, but somewhat dizzying! Fri Jul 12 16:23:32 +0000 2013 Replying to @acavender See,@briancroxall? I was not making it up. Fri Jul 12 18:03:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @katinalynn, @wynkenhimself and @briancroxall I’ve got my eye on you, Rogers. Fri Jul 12 18:19:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench, @briancroxall, @eetempleton and @acavender Depends on the goal of the Twitter account. Some intentionally defy identification. Fri Jul 12 18:51:34 +0000 2013 I need a mod_rewrite guru, stat. Sat Jul 13 13:51:42 +0000 2013 RT @eclecticbrotha: The week in review: a woman with tampons and a black kid with Skittles are both more dangerous than a white man with a … Sun Jul 14 06:41:19 +0000 2013 Waking up this morning to utter heartbreak. I am devastated for my country, so far away, in so many ways. Sun Jul 14 06:42:05 +0000 2013 Get Me Rewrite! http://wp.me/p2w8Sh-E0 Sun Jul 14 08:55:22 +0000 2013 Very sorry to be missing ACH & ADHO mtgs, not to mention #DH2013. Couldn’t figure out a Prague-to-Lincoln itinerary that wouldn’t kill me. Mon Jul 15 16:20:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @bestqualitycrab Good grief. I hope it took you weeks — because of stopovers, I mean, not just miserable travel time! Mon Jul 15 17:01:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @sirmies Hi. It would be best to email me: kfitzpatrick at mla dot org. Thanks! Thu Jul 18 07:14:32 +0000 2013 I know there’s something wrong with being this excited because my vacation has been SO PRODUCTIVE, but seriously. SO PRODUCTIVE. #yay #sigh Thu Jul 18 12:16:05 +0000 2013 Replying to @sleonchnm It’s astonishing how much you can get done when you’re away and relaxed.. Thu Jul 18 12:19:08 +0000 2013 +1 // RT @jmcclurken: Wishing I was with so many of my friends and intellectual heroes at #DH2013 this week.... Thu Jul 18 21:04:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @sherah1918 Woo hoo! Fri Jul 19 18:54:45 +0000 2013 Replying to @seth_denbo Congratulations, Seth; I'll look forward to working with you! Tue Jul 23 14:31:32 +0000 2013 Congrats, all! // RT @seth_denbo: I'm very excited to be joining AHA this fall as Director of Scholarly Communication & Digital Initiatives. Tue Jul 23 14:32:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @ernestopriego Lately, I mistype my own name 2 of 3 times... Tue Jul 23 14:42:53 +0000 2013 New post at Planned Obsolescence: On the Working Vacation. http://wp.me/p2w8Sh-F8 Wed Jul 24 13:13:35 +0000 2013 Oh god please. Unless you literally mean technics and mechanics of vision. RT @mariabustillos: stop saying "optics"!!!!!!!!!!! Wed Jul 24 17:38:18 +0000 2013 Replying to @plragde and @mariabustillos Ha. Should have known you’d be there with me. Wed Jul 24 17:39:12 +0000 2013 Rock on, @PomonaCollege. http://www.forbes.com/top-colleges/list/ Wed Jul 24 17:40:19 +0000 2013 \(I choose not to believe that there’s a relationship between my resignation and that ranking\.\) Wed Jul 24 17:40:39 +0000 2013 Replying to @tcarmody I have always known you were the best, Tim Carmody. Wed Jul 24 17:41:48 +0000 2013 Replying to @sleonchnm And you too! Wed Jul 24 17:42:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @mattbucher I do wish that list included financial aid info, esp. those schools with no-loan policies \(ahem, Pomona\) . Wed Jul 24 17:50:13 +0000 2013 Replying to @mattbucher Then again, it’s Forbes. Wed Jul 24 17:50:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @mattbucher Sad, but very likely true. The Pomonas of the world are great, but it’s a nightmarish situation overall. Wed Jul 24 17:55:53 +0000 2013 Replying to @jwolman and @tcarmody Indubitably. Wed Jul 24 18:22:51 +0000 2013 Replying to @ProfM_Lindemann Go you. Thanks for, um, oversharing. Wed Jul 24 18:49:36 +0000 2013 Replying to @ProfM_Lindemann Absolutely! Now to develop a colonoscopy pseudonym generator. Wed Jul 24 18:54:58 +0000 2013 After this week, it’s hard to read the following sentence \(from Giles Slade’s Made to Break\) quite the same way: + Thu Jul 25 14:34:26 +0000 2013 “Stevens’s definitive contribution to this cultural icon was to ‘put the wiener in the bun’” \(151\) . The icon? The Oscar Mayer Wienermobile. Thu Jul 25 14:34:39 +0000 2013 I wonder if I’ll ever feel the same way about wiener puns again. Thu Jul 25 14:34:46 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica There’s an article on audiobooks by Lutz Koepnick in the Jan 2013 PMLA. Thu Jul 25 14:42:27 +0000 2013 Replying to @literarychica I can only imagine. Thu Jul 25 14:43:41 +0000 2013 T-minus 6 days until re-entry, post working vacation. It’s been amazing, on both counts: http://www.plannedobsolescence.net/blog/on-the-working-vacation/ Thu Jul 25 15:52:00 +0000 2013 I am, however, looking forward to finally settling into the apartment I moved into two months ago. Maybe buying a dresser, hanging blinds. Thu Jul 25 15:53:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF NICE. Thu Jul 25 17:18:14 +0000 2013 Replying to @Ed_Raso I bought a dresser from them in like 1995. Gave it away when I left CA two years ago. I miss that dresser. Thu Jul 25 17:19:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal “Boss” is indeed a strange word, esp for one right out of academe. “Senior colleague” works well indeed! Thu Jul 25 17:22:40 +0000 2013 @surlyF Hey, something I’m sure you can use as a blurb somewhere: “Furlough is exactly right.” —Scholarly Kitchen. http://scholarlykitchen.sspnet.org/2013/07/26/dissertation-embargoes-and-the-rights-of-scholars-aha-smacks-the-hornets-nest/ Fri Jul 26 15:47:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF That would make a nice card. That, an email, a Twitter handle — that’s all you need. Fri Jul 26 15:51:44 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF Maybe on the flip side, the inevitable “Furlough is completely wrong.” Fri Jul 26 15:52:36 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley and @loriemerson Hey cool — thanks! Fri Jul 26 15:57:35 +0000 2013 Replying to @theorymeg You did fabulously, but you would have regardless. I hope all is well with you! Mon Jul 29 14:49:24 +0000 2013 Replying to @theorymeg I hear that. Sending you all good wishes! Mon Jul 29 14:57:49 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself Woo hoo! Congrats! Mon Jul 29 16:46:03 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley and @lincolnmullen Completely agreed that this is very desirable, but I’m not sure what’s needed is a tool. A service, yes. And + Mon Jul 29 20:26:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @brettbobley and @lincolnmullen as Brett notes, a name-brand service may go along way toward producing the social change @mia_out points to. Mon Jul 29 20:27:27 +0000 2013 Replying to @lincolnmullen, @brettbobley and @mia_out I would argue for a scholarly org or a consortium thereof... Mon Jul 29 20:30:03 +0000 2013 Packing up to head back to NYC tomorrow. It’s been an absolutely dreamy trip, but I’ll be glad to get home. Tue Jul 30 13:13:43 +0000 2013 Replying to @boone I'd love to hear what you wind up with. My printer/scanner died the day before I left on this trip & I'll have to replace soon. Tue Jul 30 13:34:44 +0000 2013 Okay, packed up, crashing, and bracing for the 3 am cacophony of device alarms. See you tomorrow afternoon, NYC. Tue Jul 30 19:22:25 +0000 2013 Okay: up at 3 am; dragged the suitcases outside just after 4 am; in the lounge at PRG at 5 am. Home around 4 pm. See you in a bit, NYC. Wed Jul 31 03:13:45 +0000 2013 FRA. Lufthansa lounge, sleeping cubicle. Brian Eno, Music for Airports, through noise suppressing headphones. Wake me in a couple of hours. Wed Jul 31 05:39:23 +0000 2013 Hello, Newark! \(A mere 1\.5 hours late\.\) Wed Jul 31 19:48:47 +0000 2013 Hello, Brooklyn. Thanks for the awesome weather. Wed Jul 31 21:55:15 +0000 2013
docs.title: 2013 07 01 tweets

docs.text: Okay. It appears I managed to make it home yesterday. Today begins the process of sorting out the chaos. Thu Aug 01 10:45:01 +0000 2013 \(Which is to say that if I owe you something… I hope I’ll be able to get it to you soon\.\) Thu Aug 01 10:45:59 +0000 2013 Replying to @jmcclurken Thank you! It’s nice to be here, and nice to see an actual stretch of chaos-clearing time ahead. Thu Aug 01 10:46:23 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Ooh, I am trying. Just looking around and saying to myself “one thing at a time. There’s no deadline.” Thu Aug 01 10:49:01 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal That sounds SO nice. Enjoy it! Thu Aug 01 10:53:30 +0000 2013 Ways I know the vacation part of my working vacation is over: I just blow-dried my hair and put on makeup for the first time in weeks. Thu Aug 01 11:17:53 +0000 2013 Also: time to switch everything out of the travel bag and into the work bag. Thu Aug 01 11:27:02 +0000 2013 Replying to @shanakimball Aw shucks. You are The Best. Fri Aug 02 02:17:59 +0000 2013 Replying to @nypl_labs, @tcarmody and @shanakimball Yes, please! Fri Aug 02 03:07:44 +0000 2013 Things that rock today: UC Senate, #owot, New York weather, general Fridayness. Fri Aug 02 20:26:41 +0000 2013 This weekend: major errands, massive unpacking, huge laundry, cooking up a storm. Tonight, sleep. Please oh please, sleep. Mon Aug 05 00:25:53 +0000 2013 \(PS: Thanks again for the awesome weather, NYC\.\) Mon Aug 05 00:26:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @stephharves I hope the jet lag is passing! I slept like a rock, and was thrilled to wake to even more awesome weather. Mon Aug 05 13:44:56 +0000 2013 Replying to @boone Nice! Thanks for the followup. I’m glad to hear it’s working well. Mon Aug 05 15:52:29 +0000 2013 Wha??? Mon Aug 05 20:40:28 +0000 2013 I’m… gobsmacked. // RT @romenesko: The Washington Post has been sold to Jeff Bezos for $250 million. Mon Aug 05 20:40:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Pls do not turn his attention in our direction. Mon Aug 05 20:47:05 +0000 2013 This day has officially just gone crazypants. Mon Aug 05 20:52:08 +0000 2013 Replying to @surlyF and @wynkenhimself Banana snake?!? Tue Aug 06 15:32:50 +0000 2013 Replying to @wynkenhimself and @surlyF Oh, the banana flavor, hands down. Ergh. Tue Aug 06 15:37:33 +0000 2013 RT @mediacommons: Everyday Play, a fantastic new cluster curated by Sam Tobin, is up today at The New Everyday: http://mediacommons.futureofthebook.org/tne/cluster/everyday-play Tue Aug 06 16:39:25 +0000 2013 Replying to @jmcclurken “Try rebooting me and see if that helps!” Tue Aug 06 20:01:20 +0000 2013 Replying to @nowviskie We are very, very sorry for the @scholarslab loss. But there’s more than a little joyful anticipation on this end. @katinalynn Wed Aug 07 15:27:07 +0000 2013 Replying to @katinalynn and @amandafrench The team is super excited about it, too! Wed Aug 07 15:42:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @lmrhody Hey, congrats to you and @chnm both! Wed Aug 07 18:46:54 +0000 2013 RT @MLACommons: We’re happy to announce that MLA Commons has received a grant from the Mellon Foundation to begin phase 2 planning: http://… Wed Aug 07 18:50:12 +0000 2013 Replying to @mkgold, @MLACommons and @cbox Thank you! We’re thrilled to be moving forward with you. Wed Aug 07 19:44:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @wilbanks The crazy thing is that we released that well over a year ago. The Atlantic even blogged it then! http://www.theatlantic.com/technology/archive/2012/03/how-do-you-cite-a-tweet-in-an-academic-paper/253932/ Thu Aug 08 00:18:17 +0000 2013 Replying to @wilbanks Sigh. The 7th edition says that URLs are optional. The format is consistent with that edition. But the 8th… *smiles enigmatically* Thu Aug 08 00:20:52 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat and @wilbanks Oh, now that’s just unfair. Thu Aug 08 00:21:14 +0000 2013 Replying to @wilbanks More meme delay? The shiny new gig is 2 years old. But would love to say hi in DC sometime! Thu Aug 08 01:01:54 +0000 2013 Replying to @wilbanks Avatar and apartment are both sparkly new, yes! And I do still feel like I’m just getting started in the job, so fair enough. Thu Aug 08 01:05:26 +0000 2013 Replying to @amandafrench and @wilbanks Happy MLA Releases a Tweet Citation Format Season! Thu Aug 08 01:08:47 +0000 2013 Replying to @LibSkrat I do not think @KUnlatched is responsible for that story; it looks like they just tweeted it… Fri Aug 09 13:08:11 +0000 2013 Stressful vet trip. One kitty freak out; the other comes home with the cone of shame. One up 4 pounds; the other down 4. Both perturbed. Fri Aug 09 16:11:54 +0000 2013 As R just noted, taking two grouchy 13-year-old kitties to the vet? Not a rainbows and lollipops experience. Fri Aug 09 16:48:07 +0000 2013 Replying to @dancohen Thank you, and backatcha. Mine is heartbreaking to watch, bumping into things & being hissed at by her brother. :( Fri Aug 09 16:52:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @triproftri Oh no! I hope all is better... Fri Aug 09 17:08:33 +0000 2013 Replying to @rgfeal Alice has a bad lick spot - she must have scratched herself & has worried it into infection. :( Alas, @MetVetNYC is Manhattan-only. Fri Aug 09 17:13:19 +0000 2013 Replying to @LizaPotts, @WIRED and @ecshowalter \(a\) Released well over a year ago. \(Check your sources\!\) \(b\) Explained then that in 7th ed URLs are optional. Sat Aug 10 15:14:42 +0000 2013 Replying to @LizaPotts, @WIRED and @ecshowalter Real names are included if the tweeter provides one. URLs are optional because the 7th ed made them so. + Sat Aug 10 15:17:36 +0000 2013 Replying to @LizaPotts, @WIRED and @ecshowalter There’s an explanation in Handbook at 5.6.2. There’s been much discussion since. It’s been heard, trust me. Sat Aug 10 15:18:20 +0000 2013 Replying to @LizaPotts I’ve dealt with the occasional Chicago journal. It’s been… different.
